  wafer_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of wafers in the lot governed by this run card. Tracks lot size for capacity planning and yield calculations.']
  wafer_size_mm varchar [note: 'Diameter of the silicon wafers in this lot, measured in millimeters (e.g., 200mm, 300mm, 450mm). Determines compatible equipment and handling procedures.']

  Note: 'Master traveler document associated with a wafer lot defining the approved process flow, special instructions, engineering deviations, customer-specific requirements, and step-level notes for a given lot run. Captures run card version, approval status, deviation flags, and associated engineering change orders. Sourced from Camstar MES and Oracle Agile PLM.'
}

Table fabrication.equipment_run {
  equipment_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the equipment run. Primary key for this transactional record of a specific tool chamber run executed on a wafer lot or wafer set.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.ar_invoice. Business justification: Equipment Run Usage Billing links each equipment run to the customer invoice that captures per‑run usage fees.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Equipment run cost accounting allocates depreciation and overhead to the cost center responsible for the run.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FAB operator who initiated or supervised this equipment run. Used for accountability and training tracking.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fabrication equipment tool that executed this run. Links to the equipment master record.']
  fabrication_photomask_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the photomask reticle used in lithography exposure. Links to the photomask asset inventory. Applicable only when process_type is lithography.']
  fabrication_process_recipe_id varchar [note: 'FK to fabrication.fabrication_process_recipe.fabrication_process_recipe_id — Equipment runs execute a specific recipe version. Links actual processing to recipe management.']
  fabrication_process_step_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the process step within the overall fabrication flow. Links this run to the routing and process flow definition.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot processed in this equipment run. Links to the wafer lot master record for WIP tracking and genealogy.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Supports Equipment Run Material Consumption Log, associating chemicals/materials used in each run with their master record for SPC and safety compliance.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.recipe. Business justification: Equipment run must reference the exact process recipe applied; essential for audit, yield analysis, and SPC control.']
  tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific process chamber within the equipment tool where this run was executed. Multi-chamber tools may have multiple concurrent runs.']
  abort_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description if the run was aborted or failed. Used for root cause analysis and yield improvement.']
  actual_pressure_torr decimal(12,6) [note: 'Actual measured chamber pressure in Torr during the run. Used for process control and deviation analysis.']
  actual_temperature_celsius decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual measured process temperature in degrees Celsius during the run. Used for process control and deviation analysis.']
  alarm_count varchar [note: 'Number of equipment alarms triggered during this run. Indicator of process stability and equipment health.']
  cmp_removal_rate_angstrom_per_min decimal(10,4) [note: 'Material removal rate during CMP in Angstroms per minute. Critical parameter for planarization control and endpoint detection.']
  cmp_slurry_type varchar [note: 'Type of slurry used in CMP process (e.g., oxide slurry, tungsten slurry, copper slurry). Applicable only when process_type is CMP.']
  cmp_wiwnu_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Within-wafer non-uniformity percentage for CMP process. Measures variation in material removal across the wafer surface. Lower values indicate better planarization quality.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment run record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  deposition_film_material varchar [note: 'Material deposited during CVD, PVD, or ALD runs (e.g., SiO2, Si3N4, TiN, Tungsten, Copper). Applicable for deposition process types.']
  deposition_rate_angstrom_per_min decimal(10,4) [note: 'Film deposition rate in Angstroms per minute. Used for process control and cycle time optimization.']
  deposition_thickness_angstrom decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target or measured film thickness in Angstroms deposited during the run. Critical parameter for film uniformity and device performance.']
  deposition_uniformity_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Film thickness uniformity across the wafer expressed as a percentage. Lower values indicate better uniformity. Critical quality metric for deposition processes.']
  implant_dose_atoms_per_cm2 decimal(18,6) [note: 'Total ion dose implanted in atoms per square centimeter. Critical parameter for doping concentration control in ion implantation processes.']
  implant_energy_kev decimal(10,2) [note: 'Ion beam energy in kiloelectron volts (keV) for ion implantation. Determines the depth of ion penetration into the wafer substrate.']
  implant_species varchar [note: 'Ion species implanted during ion implantation runs (e.g., Boron, Phosphorus, Arsenic, BF2). Applicable only when process_type is implant.']
  implant_tilt_angle_degrees decimal(6,2) [note: 'Wafer tilt angle in degrees during ion implantation. Used to control channeling effects and implant profile.']
  implant_twist_angle_degrees decimal(6,2) [note: 'Wafer twist (rotation) angle in degrees during ion implantation. Used in conjunction with tilt to control channeling effects.']
  lithography_cd_measurement_nm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured critical dimension (CD) in nanometers after lithography exposure and development. Key quality metric for pattern fidelity.']
  lithography_exposure_dose_mj_per_cm2 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Exposure energy dose in millijoules per square centimeter for lithography. Critical parameter for pattern transfer and critical dimension (CD) control.']
  lithography_focus_offset_nm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Focus offset in nanometers applied during lithography exposure. Used for depth-of-focus optimization and process window control.']
  lithography_overlay_x_nm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured overlay error in the X-axis direction in nanometers. Indicates alignment accuracy between successive lithography layers.']
  lithography_overlay_y_nm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured overlay error in the Y-axis direction in nanometers. Indicates alignment accuracy between successive lithography layers.']
  mes_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Transaction identifier from the Manufacturing Execution System (MES) that recorded this equipment run. Provides traceability to the source system.']
  process_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator identifying the category of fabrication process executed in this run. Determines which process-specific parameters are relevant. CVD=Chemical Vapor Deposition, PVD=Physical Vapor Deposition, ALD=Atomic Layer Deposition, CMP=Chemical Mechanical Planarization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CVD|PVD|ALD|CMP|implant|etch|lithography|anneal|clean|wet_clean|diffusion|oxidation|metrology — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  processes_lot varchar [note: 'FK to fabrication.wafer_lot.wafer_lot_id — Equipment run must reference the lot/wafers being processed. Core traceability requirement for process parameter linkage to product.']
  run_duration_seconds decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total duration of the equipment run in seconds, calculated from start to end timestamp. Used for cycle time analysis and equipment utilization metrics.']
  run_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the equipment run completed processing. Marks the end of the process execution cycle.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for this equipment run, typically assigned by the Manufacturing Execution System (MES). Used for operational tracking and traceability.']
  run_processes_lot varchar [note: 'FK to fabrication.fabrication_wafer_lot.fabrication_wafer_lot_id — Equipment runs are executed on wafer lots. This is the core tool-to-WIP linkage.']
  run_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the equipment run started processing. Marks the beginning of the process execution cycle.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the equipment run. Indicates whether the run completed successfully, was aborted, failed due to error, is currently in progress, or was paused.']
  run_uses_recipe varchar [note: 'FK to fabrication.process_recipe.process_recipe_id — Every equipment run executes a specific process recipe. Critical for recipe traceability, excursion investigation, and process control.']
  target_pressure_torr decimal(12,6) [note: 'Target chamber pressure in Torr as specified by the recipe. Critical control parameter for vacuum and deposition processes.']
  target_temperature_celsius decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target process temperature in degrees Celsius as specified by the recipe. Critical control parameter for thermal processes.']
  uses_recipe varchar [note: 'FK to fabrication.process_recipe.process_recipe_id — Every equipment run executes a specific recipe. Required for recipe-to-outcome traceability and process capability analysis.']
  wafer_count varchar [note: 'Number of wafers processed in this equipment run. Batch size for the run.']
  wafer_slot_map varchar [note: 'Mapping of wafer identifiers to slot positions within the carrier or chamber. Structured representation of wafer placement during processing.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a specific equipment tool run (chamber run, process run) executed on a wafer lot or wafer set. Captures tool ID, chamber ID, run start/end timestamps, recipe name and version, process type discriminator (CVD, PVD, ALD, CMP, implant, etch, lithography, anneal, clean, wet clean), actual vs. target parameter deviations, run status, and wafers processed. Process-type-specific parameters stored as structured attributes: implant (species, dose, energy, tilt, twist, beam current), deposition (film material, thickness, rate, precursor flows, uniformity), CMP (slurry type, removal rate, WIWNU, endpoint detection), lithography (reticle ID, exposure dose, focus offset, overlay, CD measurement). Unified model aligned with SEMI E10/E142 equipment event patterns. SSOT for all FAB process execution records.'
}

Table fabrication.photomask {
  photomask_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for photomask']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the IC design project for which this mask was created. Links mask to product design lineage and tapeout records.']
  mask_set_id varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the complete mask set to which this photomask belongs. A mask set comprises all reticles required for a complete wafer fabrication process flow.']
  reticle_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.reticle_set. Business justification: Photomask belongs to a reticle set; adding reticle_set_id FK groups masks for lithography and eliminates reticle_set silo.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Ensures Photomask Supplier Traceability required by mask qualification and export control audits linking each mask to its supplier record.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost paid to acquire this photomask including mask fabrication, OPC, inspection, and shipping. Denominated in USD. Used for asset valuation and depreciation.']
  cd_uniformity_specification decimal(6,3) [note: 'Maximum allowed CD variation across the mask field in nanometers (3-sigma). Tighter specifications required for advanced nodes. Typical range 1-5nm.']
  cleaning_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this mask has undergone wet or dry cleaning to remove particles and contamination. Excessive cleaning can damage mask patterns.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this photomask record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data governance and compliance.']
  critical_defect_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical (killer) defects that will print on wafer and cause yield loss. Subset of total defect count. Mask must be retired or repaired when this exceeds threshold.']
  critical_dimension_target_nm decimal(8,3) [note: 'Target critical dimension (minimum feature size) for this mask layer in nanometers. Used to verify mask pattern fidelity and wafer print quality.']
  cumulative_exposure_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total hours this mask has been exposed to lithography light source (EUV or DUV). Tracks photochemical degradation and pellicle aging.']
  cumulative_usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of wafer exposures performed with this mask since first use. Primary metric for mask wear tracking and lifecycle management.']
  defect_count_last_inspection varchar [note: 'Number of defects detected during the most recent mask inspection. Tracked to monitor mask degradation and determine cleaning or retirement needs.']
  defect_retirement_threshold varchar [note: 'Maximum critical defect count allowed before mask must be retired or sent for repair. Typically 1-5 defects depending on technology node and layer criticality.']
  gds_file_checksum varchar [note: 'MD5 or SHA-256 checksum of the GDSII file used to generate this mask. Ensures mask-to-design traceability and detects unauthorized pattern changes.']
  last_cleaning_date date [note: 'Date when the mask was last cleaned. Used to schedule preventive cleaning and correlate defect growth with cleaning intervals.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent mask inspection performed by KLA or equivalent metrology tool. Used to enforce periodic inspection schedules.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this photomask record was last updated. Tracks data currency and supports change auditing.']
  layer_name varchar [note: 'Process layer designation for which this photomask is used (e.g., POLY, METAL1, VIA2, CONTACT). Corresponds to the GDS layer in the design database.']
  lithography_wavelength varchar [note: 'Exposure wavelength for which this mask is optimized: EUV (13.5nm), DUV ArF (193nm), DUV KrF (248nm), or i-line (365nm).']
  mask_generation varchar [note: 'Generation number of this mask within the mask set. Incremented when a mask is replaced due to defects, pattern revisions, or wear-out. Generation 1 is the original mask.']
  mask_serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the mask shop vendor for traceability and warranty purposes. Engraved on the reticle substrate.']
  mask_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the photomask: qualified (ready for production), in production (actively used), in inspection, in cleaning, in repair, quarantined (defect investigation), retired (end of life), or scrapped (disposed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qualified|in_production|in_inspection|in_cleaning|in_repair|quarantined|retired|scrapped — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mask_type varchar [note: 'Classification of photomask technology: binary (chrome on glass), attenuated phase shift mask (PSM), alternating PSM, EUV with pellicle, EUV without pellicle, or OPC-enhanced mask.']
  meef_value decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mask Error Enhancement Factor quantifying how mask pattern errors are amplified on the wafer. Higher MEEF indicates greater sensitivity to mask defects. Typical range 1.0 to 8.0.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory mask inspection. Calculated based on usage count, exposure hours, or calendar interval per mask management policy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording mask-specific observations, special handling instructions, repair history, or other contextual information not captured in structured fields.']
  opc_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the OPC software and recipe used to generate the mask pattern. Critical for mask-to-mask consistency and reticle enhancement technology tracking.']
  pellicle_installation_date date [note: 'Date when the current pellicle was installed on the mask. Used to track pellicle aging and replacement cycles.']
  pellicle_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the protective pellicle membrane: installed (protecting mask surface), removed (for inspection or repair), damaged (requires replacement), or not applicable (EUV masks without pellicle).']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number under which this mask was procured. Links mask asset to procurement and financial records.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the mask passed final qualification inspection and was approved for production use. Marks the start of the masks production lifecycle.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date when the mask was received from the vendor and logged into the FAB inventory system. Marks the start of the masks asset lifecycle.']
  registration_error_specification_nm decimal(6,3) [note: 'Maximum allowed pattern placement error relative to alignment marks in nanometers. Critical for layer-to-layer overlay accuracy. Typical range 2-10nm.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the mask was retired from production due to reaching usage threshold, excessive defects, or process obsolescence. Null if mask is still active.']
  retirement_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason for mask retirement: usage limit reached, defect limit exceeded, pattern revision required, process obsolescence, physical damage, or lost/missing.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the physical storage location (reticle pod, SMIF pod, or automated reticle storage system slot) where the mask is currently stored when not in use.']
  technology_node varchar [note: 'Semiconductor process technology node for which this mask was designed (e.g., 5nm, 7nm, 14nm, 28nm). Defines minimum feature size and design rules.']
  usage_retirement_threshold varchar [note: 'Maximum cumulative usage count allowed before mask must be retired from production. Defined per technology node and mask type to ensure yield protection.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor warranty for this mask expires. After this date, repair or replacement costs are borne by the FAB.']

  Note: 'Master record for photomasks (reticles) used in EUV and DUV lithography operations, capturing mask set ID, layer name, technology node, mask type (binary, PSM, EUV pellicle), OPC version, MEEF value, mask generation, pellicle status, inspection history summary, usage count, cleaning cycle count, and retirement threshold. SSOT for mask inventory, lithography step qualification, and reticle lifecycle management within the FAB.'
}

Table fabrication.fabrication_lot_genealogy {
  fabrication_lot_genealogy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fabrication lot genealogy relationship record. Primary key for the association entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer for whom the lot genealogy event was performed. Used for customer-specific lot tracking and MPW (Multi-Project Wafer) separation.']
  child_lot_fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the child wafer lot created from the parent lot. References the resulting lot in split, merge, or rework operations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FAB operator who executed the genealogy event. Used for accountability and process audit trails.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FAB equipment or tool used to perform the lot split, merge, or rework operation. Enables equipment-specific traceability.']
  fabrication_process_step_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FAB operation or process step where the genealogy event occurred. Links to the specific manufacturing operation in the process flow.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the semiconductor product or design associated with the lot genealogy event. Links to the IC (Integrated Circuit) design or ASIC (Application-Specific Integrated Circuit) being fabricated.']
  primary_fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent wafer lot from which the child lot originated. References the source lot in split, merge, or rework operations.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the process recipe or procedure used during the genealogy event. Captures the specific process parameters and settings applied.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing work order authorizing the genealogy event. Links the lot transformation to production planning and scheduling systems.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the genealogy event was performed to meet regulatory or customer compliance requirements. True for ITAR, EAR, or customer-mandated lot segregation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this genealogy record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  destination_fab_line varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FAB production line where the child lot was routed after the genealogy event. Tracks lot movement across production lines.']
  is_reversible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the genealogy relationship can be reversed or undone. True for temporary splits or holds that can be re-merged; false for permanent transformations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this genealogy record was last updated. Audit trail for record modifications.']
  mes_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier from the MES (Manufacturing Execution System) that recorded the genealogy event. Enables integration with Camstar or Applied Materials SmartFactory systems.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or special instructions related to the lot genealogy event. Used for capturing context not covered by structured fields.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification assigned to the genealogy event. Influences scheduling and resource allocation for the resulting child lot.']
  process_stage varchar [note: 'Major process stage where the genealogy event occurred. FEOL (Front End of Line) for transistor formation, MOL (Middle of Line) for contacts, or BEOL (Back End of Line) for interconnects.']
  quality_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the genealogy event was triggered by a quality hold or defect isolation requirement. True if the split or rework was quality-driven.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the lot genealogy event. Examples include capacity balancing, defect isolation, customer priority, or process optimization.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual explanation of why the lot split, merge, or rework was performed. Provides context for genealogy analysis and root cause investigation.']
  relationship_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lot genealogy relationship was established. Records the exact moment of the split, merge, or rework event in the FAB.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Type of genealogy relationship between parent and child lots. Defines the nature of the lot transformation event.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when a reversible genealogy relationship was reversed or undone. Null if the relationship has not been reversed or is not reversible.']
  source_fab_line varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FAB production line where the parent lot was located before the genealogy event. Enables cross-line traceability.']
  technology_node varchar [note: 'Process technology node of the wafer lots involved in the genealogy event. Examples include 7nm, 5nm, 3nm FinFET (Fin Field-Effect Transistor) or GAA (Gate All Around) technologies.']
  traceability_code varchar [note: 'Unique traceability identifier linking this genealogy event to upstream and downstream lot history. Enables end-to-end wafer genealogy tracking across the entire FAB process flow.']
  wafer_count_transferred varchar [note: 'Number of wafers transferred from parent lot to child lot during the genealogy event. Critical for WIP (Work in Process) tracking and yield analysis.']
  wafer_size_mm varchar [note: 'Diameter of the wafers involved in the genealogy event, measured in millimeters. Standard values include 200mm, 300mm, or 450mm wafers.']

  Note: 'Association entity capturing parent-child relationships between wafer lots arising from lot splits, lot merges, rework re-entry, and MPW lot separation events. Records relationship type, split/merge timestamp, wafer count transferred, and traceability linkage. Authoritative source for wafer genealogy and lot traceability across the FAB.'
}

Table fabrication.fabrication_technology_node {
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the semiconductor technology node record. Primary key.']
  fab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary fabrication facility where this technology node is manufactured. Links to the fab facility master data.']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this technology node record is currently active and available for use in manufacturing and design operations. False indicates archived or deprecated nodes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this technology node record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  design_rule_complexity varchar [note: 'Complexity level of Design for Manufacturability (DFM) rules required for this node. Higher complexity nodes require more sophisticated Optical Proximity Correction (OPC) and restricted design rules.']
  dfm_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Design for Manufacturability (DFM) checks and optimizations are mandatory for designs targeting this node. True for advanced nodes requiring OPC, dummy fill, and restricted design rules.']
  dft_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Design for Testability (DFT) structures (scan chains, BIST, ATPG patterns) are required for designs on this node. Ensures adequate test coverage for quality assurance.']
  ear_classification varchar [note: 'Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) under US Export Administration Regulations (EAR) for this technology node. Determines export licensing requirements to restricted countries.']
  eol_announcement_date date [note: 'Date when the technology node was officially announced for end-of-life phase-out. Triggers Product Change Notification (PCN) and Last Time Buy (LTB) processes for customers.']
  iatf16949_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this technology node is certified under IATF 16949 Automotive Quality Management standards, required for automotive-grade semiconductor production.']
  iso9001_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the manufacturing process for this technology node is certified under ISO 9001 Quality Management System standards.']
  itar_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this technology node is subject to ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) export controls due to defense or aerospace applications. Restricts international technology transfer.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this technology node record was last updated. Tracks data currency and change history for audit and compliance purposes.']
  lithography_technology varchar [note: 'Primary lithography technology used for patterning in this node. DUV (Deep Ultraviolet) for mature nodes, EUV (Extreme Ultraviolet) for advanced nodes below 7nm.']
  mask_set_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of a full photomask set for this technology node in US dollars. Significant cost driver for advanced nodes using EUV lithography. Business-confidential pricing data.']
  metal_layer_count varchar [note: 'Total number of metal interconnect layers available in the Back End of Line (BEOL) stack for this technology node. Typically ranges from 6 to 15+ layers depending on node complexity.']
  minimum_feature_size_nm decimal(10,3) [note: 'The smallest feature dimension that can be reliably manufactured in this technology node, measured in nanometers. Critical dimension for lithography and patterning capability.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last modified this technology node record. Supports audit trail and accountability requirements.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Internal alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify the technology node in manufacturing execution systems and product lifecycle management systems.']
  node_generation varchar [note: 'Generation classification of the node (e.g., Leading Edge, Mature, Legacy). Indicates the relative maturity and market positioning of the process technology.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the technology node (e.g., 5nm, 7nm, 14nm, 28nm, 65nm, 90nm). Industry-standard designation for the process generation.']
  nre_cost_estimate_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) cost in US dollars for a typical design tapeout on this technology node. Includes mask set costs, design services, and qualification expenses. Business-confidential pricing data.']
  pdk_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Process Design Kit (PDK) that defines the design rules, device models, and technology files for this node. Used by EDA (Electronic Design Automation) tools.']
  power_performance_area_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative or quantitative assessment of the node\'s Power, Performance, and Area (PPA) characteristics relative to prior generations. Critical metric for competitive positioning.']
  process_flow_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the manufacturing process flow (sequence of FEOL, MOL, BEOL steps) defined for this technology node. Tracks process recipe evolution and engineering changes.']
  production_readiness_date date [note: 'Date when the technology node was certified as production-ready and available for high-volume manufacturing (HVM). Null if still in development or qualification.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the technology node. Development: R&D phase. Qualification: undergoing reliability and yield validation. Qualified: approved for production. Production: active manufacturing. Mature: stable high-volume. EOL (End of Life): phase-out planned.']
  reach_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemicals and materials used in this technology node comply with EU REACH Regulation for chemical safety and substance registration.']
  rohs_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the manufacturing process for this technology node complies with EU RoHS Directive restrictions on hazardous substances (lead, mercury, cadmium, etc.).']
  supported_application_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of target application domains optimized for this node (e.g., Mobile, Automotive, AI/ML, IoT, High-Performance Computing, Networking). Guides product design-in decisions.']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of good dies per wafer for this technology node at maturity. Key performance indicator for manufacturing efficiency and cost competitiveness.']
  transistor_architecture varchar [note: 'Type of transistor structure used in this technology node. Planar for older nodes, FinFET (Fin Field-Effect Transistor) for 22nm-7nm, GAA (Gate All Around) for 3nm and below.']
  wafer_size_mm varchar [note: 'Standard wafer diameter supported by this technology node, typically 200mm (8-inch) or 300mm (12-inch). Determines fab equipment compatibility and die yield economics.']

  Note: 'Reference master for semiconductor technology nodes supported in the FAB, capturing node name (e.g., 5nm, 7nm, 28nm, 65nm), node generation, transistor architecture (planar, FinFET, GAA), minimum feature size, metal layer count, PDK version reference, qualification status, and production readiness date. SSOT for node-level process and product classification.'
}

Table fabrication.fab_yield_record {
  fab_yield_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the FAB yield record. Primary key for this transactional yield capture event.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Yield loss financial impact is allocated to the cost center overseeing the wafer lot.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operator or technician who performed the yield measurement. Supports traceability and training effectiveness analysis.']
  fab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the FAB facility where this yield measurement was captured. Enables multi-site yield comparison and benchmarking.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inspection or metrology equipment used to capture this yield measurement (e.g., KLA ICOS system). Enables equipment-specific yield correlation.']
  fab_wafer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific wafer for which yield is being recorded. Links to the wafer master record in the fabrication domain.']
  fabrication_photomask_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the photomask set used for lithography on this wafer. Enables correlation of yield to mask quality and OPC effectiveness.']
  fabrication_process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the process route or recipe used for this wafer. Links yield outcomes to specific process flows.']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.technology_node. Business justification: Yield record captures technology node; add FK to technology_node and remove redundant numeric column.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the wafer lot to which this yield record belongs. Enables lot-level yield aggregation and genealogy tracking.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Enables Yield Dashboard to aggregate yields per IC catalog product.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Yield analysis reports are aggregated per design project; direct FK enables automated project‑level yield dashboards and root‑cause tracking.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'FK to fabrication.wafer.wafer_id — Yield records can be at wafer level for per-wafer yield tracking. Required for wafer-level yield maps and die-level analysis.']
  bin_1_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of die classified into bin 1 (typically highest quality/performance bin). Part of bin-level yield breakdown.']
  bin_2_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of die classified into bin 2 (typically second-tier quality/performance bin). Part of bin-level yield breakdown.']
  bin_3_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of die classified into bin 3 (typically third-tier quality/performance bin). Part of bin-level yield breakdown.']
  checkpoint_code varchar [note: 'The FAB inline checkpoint at which this yield measurement was captured. Distinguishes FEOL, MOL, BEOL, and pre-probe stages. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: POST_FEOL|POST_MOL|POST_BEOL|PRE_PROBE|POST_CMP|POST_LITHO|POST_ETCH|POST_IMPLANT|INLINE_INSPECTION — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes about this yield measurement, including observations, anomalies, or contextual information for root cause analysis.']
  control_limit_lower decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower control limit for yield percentage at this checkpoint. Part of Statistical Process Control (SPC) monitoring.']
  control_limit_upper decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper control limit for yield percentage at this checkpoint. Part of Statistical Process Control (SPC) monitoring.']
  design_loss_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of die lost due to design-related issues (DFM violations, timing failures, functional failures). Part of yield loss pareto analysis.']
  disposition_status varchar [note: 'The disposition decision for the wafer or lot based on this yield measurement. Determines next processing step or lot hold.']
  excursion_severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the yield excursion event. Determines escalation path and response urgency.']
  good_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of die that passed yield criteria at this checkpoint. Represents the actual usable die count after defect screening and binning.']
  gross_die_count varchar [note: 'Total number of die on the wafer before yield screening. Represents the theoretical maximum die count based on wafer size and die layout.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'The reason code if the wafer or lot was placed on hold due to yield excursion. Links to hold reason reference data.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the yield measurement was captured at the inline checkpoint. Represents the business event time of the yield observation.']
  process_loss_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of die lost due to process-related defects (CVD, PVD, ALD, CMP, etch, implant issues). Part of yield loss pareto analysis.']
  random_defect_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of die lost due to random defects (particles, contamination, handling damage). Part of yield loss pareto analysis.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this yield record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this yield record was last updated in the data platform. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  reject_bin_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of die classified into reject bins (failed die that do not meet minimum specifications). Part of bin-level yield breakdown.']
  rework_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that this wafer is eligible for rework based on yield results. Drives rework routing decisions.']
  scrap_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that this wafer should be scrapped based on yield results. Triggers scrap disposition and cost accounting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The Manufacturing Execution System (MES) or inspection system that generated this yield record (e.g., Applied Materials SmartFactory, Camstar, KLA ICOS).']
  specification_limit_lower decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower specification limit for yield percentage at this checkpoint. Represents minimum acceptable yield threshold.']
  systematic_defect_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of die lost due to systematic defects (OPC errors, MEEF issues, lithography hotspots). Part of yield loss pareto analysis.']
  yield_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that this yield record represents an excursion event (yield below control limits or specification). Triggers root cause analysis and corrective action.']
  yield_for_lot varchar [note: 'FK to fabrication.fabrication_wafer_lot.fabrication_wafer_lot_id — Yield records are captured at lot level at key FAB checkpoints.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated yield percentage at this checkpoint (good_die_count / gross_die_count * 100). Primary yield metric for FAB inline performance tracking.']
  yield_record_for_lot varchar [note: 'FK to fabrication.wafer_lot.wafer_lot_id — Yield records reference the lot measured. Required for lot-level yield tracking and excursion detection.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing wafer-level and lot-level yield outcomes at key FAB inline checkpoints (post-FEOL, post-MOL, post-BEOL, pre-probe), recording gross die count, good die count, yield percentage, yield loss pareto by category (process, design, random defect, systematic), bin-level yield breakdown, and yield excursion flags. SSOT for FAB inline yield data distinct from final test yield (owned by test domain) and distinct from SPC/metrology analysis (owned by quality domain).'
}

Table fabrication.mask_set {
  mask_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for mask_set']
  derived_from_mask_set_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on mask_set (derived_from_mask_set_id)']
  application_area varchar [note: 'Primary product domain where the mask set is applied.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance status of the mask set.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the mask set record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the mask set\'s purpose and characteristics.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the mask set is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the mask set is considered valid for production.']
  exposure_tool varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the lithography tool that uses this mask set.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality inspection of the mask set.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the latest inspection.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the mask set record.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the mask set was most recently used in production.']
  lithography_technology varchar [note: 'Lithography technology for which the mask set is designed.']
  mask_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Acquisition cost of the mask set in US dollars.']
  mask_layer_count varchar [note: 'Number of patterned layers contained in the mask set.']
  mask_material varchar [note: 'Material composition of the mask (e.g., quartz, glass, silicon).']
  mask_set_category varchar [note: 'Category indicating whether the mask set is a standard, custom, or prototype design.']
  mask_set_code varchar [note: 'External catalogue or part number assigned to the mask set by the fab.']
  mask_set_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the mask set.']
  mask_set_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for scheduling mask set usage in fab planning.']
  mask_thickness_nm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Physical thickness of the mask substrate in nanometres.']
  mask_type varchar [note: 'Category of mask based on its physical principle.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the mask set used by engineers and planners.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the mask set.']
  quality_grade varchar [note: 'Quality grade assigned after inspection (A‑highest, D‑lowest).']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision of the mask set was released.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for engineering changes to the mask set.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Safety handling class for the mask set according to fab safety standards.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the mask set.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical storage location or vault identifier for the mask set.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of wafer lots processed with this mask set.']
  version varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric version label for the mask set (e.g., V1.2).']
  wafer_size_mm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Diameter of wafers that the mask set is intended for, expressed in millimetres.']
  wavelength_nm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Design wavelength of the lithography exposure system in nanometres.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for mask_set. Referenced by mask_set_id.'
}

Table fabrication.fab_facility {

  capacity_wafer_per_month varchar [note: 'Maximum number of wafers the facility can process in a calendar month.']
  carbon_footprint_kgco2e decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monthly greenhouse‑gas emissions expressed in kilograms CO₂ equivalent.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the facility is located.']
  cleanroom_class varchar [note: 'ISO cleanroom classification of the facility\'s most stringent environment.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing with regulatory and industry standards.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for facility communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for facility communications.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the facility location.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the facility\'s capabilities and role.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the facility ceased operations (null if still active).']
  energy_consumption_mwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average monthly energy consumption measured in megawatt‑hours.']
  environmental_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of environmental certifications (e.g., ISO 14001).']
  equipment_summary varchar [note: 'Free‑text summary of key equipment types and models present.']
  fab_area_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total usable floor area of the fab in square feet.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'External business code or identifier used to reference the facility in enterprise systems.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fabrication facility.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Category of the facility based on its primary function.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the facility (decimal degrees).']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the facility.']
  lithography_type varchar [note: 'Primary lithography technology employed at the fab.']
  location_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the facility.']
  location_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building, etc.).']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the facility (decimal degrees).']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Typical time window (e.g., weekly, monthly) allocated for planned maintenance.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person responsible for overall facility management.']
  next_audit_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance audit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Any supplemental information or remarks about the facility.']
  number_of_cleanrooms varchar [note: 'Count of cleanroom spaces within the facility.']
  number_of_equipment_units varchar [note: 'Total number of major equipment units (e.g., lithography, etch, deposition) installed.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational condition of the facility.']
  process_technology_node varchar [note: 'Primary semiconductor process node supported (e.g., 7nm, 5nm).']
  restricted_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility requires restricted (security‑cleared) access.']
  safety_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of safety certifications held (e.g., ISO 45001).']
  shift_schedule varchar [note: 'Number of production shifts operated per day.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the facility began operations.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the facility location.']
  waste_generated_tons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total waste generated per month measured in metric tons.']
  water_usage_m3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average monthly water usage in cubic meters.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fab_facility. Referenced by fab_facility_id.'
}

  control_system_version varchar [note: 'Software version of the work center\'s control system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work center record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level applied to data generated by this work center.']

  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the work center became operationally active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the work center is scheduled to be retired or decommissioned (nullable).']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average energy usage of the work center measured in kilowatt‑hours per day.']
  equipment_count varchar [note: 'Number of major pieces of equipment installed in the work center.']
  fab_line varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab line to which the work center belongs.']

  manager_contact varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the work center manager.']

  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the work center used in reports and dashboards.']
  next_calibration_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next calibration of the work center\'s equipment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or operational notes.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Real‑time operational condition of the work center.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Manufacturing process step primarily performed at this work center.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety classification of the work center based on internal risk assessments.']
  shift_schedule varchar [note: 'Standard shift pattern assigned to the work center.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the work center.']
  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical ambient temperature inside the work center.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the work center based on the manufacturing process it supports.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the work center record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for work_center. Referenced by work_center_id.'
}

Table fabrication.spc_control_plan {
  spc_control_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for spc_control_plan']

  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the control plan received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or manager who approved the control plan.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent revision of the control plan.']
  control_limit_lower decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical lower control limit used for SPC charting.']
  control_limit_upper decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical upper control limit used for SPC charting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the control plan record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the control plan purpose and scope.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the control plan is retired or superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the control plan becomes active.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control plan is considered critical for product quality.']
  lower_spec_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower acceptable specification limit for the metric.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target metric and limits.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the control plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the control plan, used in manufacturing documentation and MES.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the control plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan based on what is being controlled.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Engineer accountable for maintaining the control plan.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number incremented on each change.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units measured per sampling event.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'How often samples are taken for the control plan.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control plan.']
  target_metric varchar [note: 'Key process metric that the control plan monitors (e.g., wafer thickness).']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Nominal target value for the monitored metric.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the control plan record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the control plan record.']
  upper_spec_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Upper acceptable specification limit for the metric.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the control plan, following the internal versioning scheme.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the control plan record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for spc_control_plan. Referenced by spc_control_plan_id.'
}

Table fabrication.equipment_group {
  equipment_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for equipment_group']
  parent_equipment_group_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on equipment_group (parent_equipment_group_id)']
  parent_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent equipment group, if this group is part of a hierarchy.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the equipment group in MES and ERP systems.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the equipment group.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center code associated with the equipment group for financial tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the equipment group record was first created in the system.']
  default_capacity varchar [note: 'Typical production capacity of the equipment group expressed in wafers per day.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the equipment group.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the equipment group became operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the equipment group is scheduled to be decommissioned (null if open‑ended).']
  fab_location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab site or cleanroom where the equipment group is located.']
  group_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating the position of the group within the equipment hierarchy.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Category of the equipment group indicating its primary function within the fab.']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Preferred maintenance window for the equipment group.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature at which the equipment group can safely operate.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the equipment group.']
  process_technology varchar [note: 'Process node (technology) supported by the equipment group.']
  safety_class varchar [note: 'Safety class assigned to the equipment group according to industry standards.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the equipment group.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the equipment group record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the equipment group record.']
  voltage_rating_v decimal(5,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage rating for the equipment group.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the equipment group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for equipment_group. Referenced by equipment_group_id.'
}

Table fabrication.fab {
  fab_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fab']
  fab_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fab_site. Business justification: Each fab belongs to a fab site; adding fab_site_id FK to fab links fab to its site and resolves fab_site isolation.']
  capacity_wafers_per_month varchar [note: 'Maximum number of wafers the fab can process in a month.']
  cleanroom_class varchar [note: 'Cleanroom class rating of the fab environment.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing with industry regulations.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for fab communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the fab.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fab record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the fab.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the fab ceased operations (null if still active).']
  environmental_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental management certification held by the fab.']
  fab_code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned unique code for the fab.']
  fab_region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region where the fab is located.']
  fab_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fab based on process focus.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity.']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City where the fab is situated.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the fab resides.']
  location_state varchar [note: 'State or province of the fab location.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manager overseeing the fab.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fab.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance date [note: 'Planned date for the next maintenance event.']
  number_of_cleanrooms varchar [note: 'Total count of cleanrooms within the fab.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the fab.']
  power_capacity_mw decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum electrical power capacity of the fab in megawatts.']
  safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of safety incidents recorded at the fab.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the fab began operations.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the fab.']
  technology_node_nm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum feature size of the process technology in nanometers.']
  total_area_sq_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Overall floor area of the fab in square meters.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fab record.']
  waste_treatment_capacity_kg_per_day decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum waste treatment capacity per day in kilograms.']
  water_usage_cubic_m_per_day decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average water consumption per day in cubic meters.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fab. Referenced by fab_id.'
}

Table fabrication.reticle_set {
  reticle_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for reticle_set']
  derived_from_reticle_set_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on reticle_set (derived_from_reticle_set_id)']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent calibration of the reticle set.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or part number assigned to the reticle set.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard the reticle set complies with.']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Acquisition cost of the reticle set in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reticle set record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost field.']
  current_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Accumulated exposure cycles to date.']
  defect_rate_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured defect density in parts per million.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the reticle set and its purpose.']
  expected_lifetime_cycles varchar [note: 'Projected number of exposure cycles before retirement.']
  exposure_tool varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the exposure tool compatible with the reticle set.']
  fab_location varchar [note: 'Fabrication plant or line where the reticle set is stored/used.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reticle set is critical for high‑volume production.']
  is_licensed boolean [note: 'True if the reticle set is covered by a licensing agreement.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the reticle set was last used.']
  lithography_type varchar [note: 'Lithography technology used with the reticle set.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot identifier for traceability.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Current maintenance state of the reticle set.']
  material varchar [note: 'Base material of the reticle substrate.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the reticle set.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or operational notes.']
  number_of_reticles varchar [note: 'Count of individual reticles contained in the set.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when qualification was performed.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Qualification outcome for the reticle set.']
  retire_date date [note: 'Date the reticle set was retired from service.']
  retire_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for retiring the reticle set (e.g., wear, obsolescence).']
  revision varchar [note: 'Version or revision code of the reticle set design.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Safety class based on material and handling requirements.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reticle set.']
  storage_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Controlled storage humidity level for the reticle set.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Controlled storage temperature for the reticle set.']
  total_area_mm2 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Combined active area of all reticles in square millimetres.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the set (e.g., mask, reticle, blank, dummy).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reticle set record.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the reticle set has been used in production.']
  vendor varchar [note: 'Supplier of the reticle set.']
  vendor_part_number varchar [note: 'Vendor‑assigned part number for the reticle set.']
  wavelength_nm varchar [note: 'Design wavelength in nanometers for the reticle set.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for reticle_set. Referenced by reticle_set_id.'
}

Table fabrication.plant {

  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the plant.']
  area_sq_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total built‑up area of the plant in square meters.']
  capacity_wafer_per_month decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum number of wafers the plant can produce in a typical month.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the plant is located.']
  cleanroom_class varchar [note: 'Cleanroom class rating based on particle count standards.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of key compliance certifications held by the plant.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the plant.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plant record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level for data governance purposes.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the plant\'s capabilities and purpose.']
  environmental_rating varchar [note: 'Environmental performance rating of the plant.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which major maintenance was performed.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the plant location.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the plant location.']
  maintenance_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Planned interval in days between routine maintenance activities.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the plant manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the plant manager.']
  manager_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the plant manager.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the plant used in reports and dashboards.']
  number_of_cleanrooms varchar [note: 'Total number of cleanroom suites in the plant.']
  number_of_fabs varchar [note: 'Count of distinct fabrication lines within the plant.']
  operational_shift varchar [note: 'Number of production shifts run per day.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the plant\'s overall governance.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'External business code assigned to the plant, often used in ERP and MES systems.']
  power_capacity_mw decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum electrical power capacity of the plant in megawatts.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad business region where the plant is located.']

  shutdown_date date [note: 'Date when the plant was permanently closed or decommissioned, if applicable.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the plant began operations.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the plant location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the plant within its lifecycle.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the plant based on its primary manufacturing function.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the plant record.']
  waste_treatment_capacity_tons_per_day decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum daily waste treatment capacity in metric tons.']
  water_usage_m3_per_day decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average daily water consumption of the plant.']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'Postal code for the plant\'s address.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for plant. Referenced by plant_id.'
}

Table fabrication.fab_site {
  fab_site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fab_site']
  parent_fab_site_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fab_site (parent_fab_site_id)']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.plant. Business justification: Fab site is part of a plant; adding plant_id FK to fab_site establishes hierarchy and connects plant.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the fab site.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (e.g., suite, building).']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent audit.']
  capacity_wafer_per_month varchar [note: 'Maximum number of wafers the site can process each month.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the fab site is located.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the fab site.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the fab site.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the site was decommissioned or closed (nullable).']
  environmental_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental compliance certifications held by the site.']
  ghs_compliance varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System chemical safety compliance.']
  is_primary_site boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site is the primary manufacturing location for the company.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the fab site.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the fab site.']
  maintenance_window_start varchar [note: 'Daily start time for scheduled maintenance (HH:mm, 24‑hour).']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the site manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the site manager.']
  manager_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the site manager.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fab site.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the upcoming audit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or operational notes.']
  number_of_cleanrooms varchar [note: 'Count of classified cleanroom environments at the site.']
  number_of_employees varchar [note: 'Headcount of staff assigned to the site.']
  number_of_tools varchar [note: 'Total count of lithography, etch, deposition, and other equipment.']
  operational_hours_per_day varchar [note: 'Standard number of production hours each day.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the site.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the fab site address.']
  power_capacity_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum electrical power available to the site in megawatts.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad region (e.g., APAC, EMEA, Americas) where the site resides.']
  safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of recorded safety incidents at the site in the past year.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Physical and logical security classification of the site.']
  site_area_sq_m decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total built‑up area of the fab site in square meters.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the fab site in external systems.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Category of the site (e.g., fab, test, R&D, assembly).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the fab site became operational.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the fab site location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the site.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  waste_treatment_capacity_tons_per_day decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum waste treatment throughput per day.']
  water_capacity_m3_per_day decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily water supply capacity for the fab site.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fab_site. Referenced by fab_site_id.'
}

Table design.ic_design_project {
  ic_design_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the IC design project record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Required for project accounting and billing reports linking each design project to the commissioning customer account.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Project cost accounting requires linking each design project to a cost center for monthly expense reporting and budget control.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the lead IC design engineer responsible for the project, sourced from Workday HCM. Used for design team assignment, accountability tracking, and PLM workflow approvals.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Export license required for each design project before international shipment; regulatory export‑control process mandates linking project to its license.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Manufacturing planning links each design project to the fab tool that will produce it, essential for the \'Production Scheduling\' report.']
  foundry_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Foundry selection contract for each design project; required for manufacturing cost tracking and compliance.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Required for Product Lifecycle Management: design project outcome is recorded as a catalog entry for sales, production planning, and compliance reporting.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Internal orders track project‑specific capital expenditures and commitments in the ERP system.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering team assigned to this IC design project in Workday HCM and Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Supports resource utilization reporting and capacity planning.']
  pdk_id varchar [note: 'FK to design.pdk.pdk_id — Every IC design project targets a specific PDK version. This determines all design rules, standard cells, and device models available. Fundamental design constraint.']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the foundry-specific Process Design Kit (PDK) used for this project (e.g., \'TSMC N5P\', \'Samsung SF3E\'). Links to the PDK library version in the EDA tool environment and governs all DRC/LVS rule sets.']
  process_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_technology_node. Business justification: Technology Node Allocation planning uses this FK to tie a design project to the selected process technology node for yield and performance forecasts.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis assigns each design project to a profit center to capture revenue and cost allocation for product line P&L.']
  research_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.program. Business justification: Program Management Dashboard requires linking each design project to its originating R&D program for reporting and budget tracking.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Linking to a WBS element enables detailed work‑breakdown‑structure budgeting and progress reporting for the design project.']
  block_count varchar [note: 'Number of top-level hierarchical design blocks (partitions) in the IC design. Reflects the block decomposition strategy used for parallel design team assignments and hierarchical physical design in Cadence Innovus.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the IC design project record was first created in the system of record (Siemens Teamcenter PLM or Oracle Agile PLM), propagated to the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  design_phase varchar [note: 'Indicates the primary fabrication phase scope of the design: Front End of Line (FEOL), Middle of Line (MOL), Back End of Line (BEOL), or full-flow. Governs which process modules and DFM rules are active.']
  design_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the integrated circuit design type. Drives NRE cost structure, EDA tool selection, and tapeout process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ASIC|SoC|FPGA|ASSP|MCU|GPU|DSP|RFIC|MEMS|Analog — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  dfm_rule_set_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Design for Manufacturability (DFM) rule set applied to this project. Governs OPC recipe selection, MEEF corrections, and yield-enhancement layout guidelines provided by the foundry PDK.']
  dft_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Design for Testability (DFT) structures (scan chains, BIST, boundary scan) are included in this design. Drives ATPG flow activation, ATE program generation, and KGD qualification planning.']
  eda_tool_suite varchar [note: 'Primary EDA tool suite vendor used for this IC design project (e.g., Cadence Virtuoso/Innovus, Synopsys Design Compiler/PrimeTime, Siemens EDA). Governs tool license allocation and design flow configuration.']
  eda_tool_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the primary EDA tool suite certified for this project (e.g., \'Cadence Innovus 21.1\', \'Synopsys DC 2022.03\'). Ensures design reproducibility and supports audit trails for sign-off certification.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification of the IC design project under US Export Administration Regulations (EAR) or International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR). Governs technology transfer restrictions, foreign national access controls, and license requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EAR99|ECCN_3E001|ECCN_3E002|ECCN_3E003|ITAR|no_restriction — promote to reference product]']
  gate_count_target_k decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target logic gate count in thousands of equivalent gates (Kilo-Gates) for the design. Used for complexity estimation, EDA tool capacity planning, and synthesis runtime forecasting in Synopsys Design Compiler.']
  gds_file_path varchar [note: 'Storage path or URI of the final GDS/GDSII design database file submitted for tapeout. Provides traceability to the authoritative design artifact in the EDA data management system.']
  iatf_automotive_grade boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IC design project targets automotive-grade qualification per IATF 16949 and AEC-Q100/Q101 standards. Triggers additional FMEA, reliability testing, and DPPM requirements in the quality management workflow.']
  ip_core_count varchar [note: 'Total number of licensed or internally developed Intellectual Property (IP) cores integrated into this IC design project. Drives IP license cost tracking, third-party IP compliance review, and BOM management in Oracle Agile PLM.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the IC design project record in the source system of record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  lithography_type varchar [note: 'Primary lithography technology used for critical layer patterning: Extreme Ultraviolet (EUV), Deep Ultraviolet (DUV), multi-patterning DUV, or nanoimprint. Impacts mask cost, OPC complexity, and MEEF considerations.']
  metal_layer_count varchar [note: 'Number of metal interconnect layers in the BEOL stack for this design. Determines routing resource availability, RC parasitics, and mask set cost. Specified in the PDK and foundry process design rules.']
  mpw_shuttle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) shuttle slot assigned to this design for prototype fabrication. Enables cost-sharing across multiple designs on a single wafer run. Null for full-mask production tapeouts.']
  nre_actual_spend_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual NRE expenditure incurred to date for the IC design project in US dollars, as recorded in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO. Compared against nre_budget_usd for variance analysis and financial reporting.']
  nre_budget_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) budget for the IC design project in US dollars. Covers EDA tool licensing, mask set costs, engineering labor, and MPW shuttle fees. Managed in SAP S/4HANA CO cost center planning.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Target die packaging technology for this IC design project (e.g., WLCSP, BGA, CoWoS, InFO, flip-chip). Influences die size constraints, I/O pad ring design, and OSAT partner selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WLCSP|BGA|QFN|CoWoS|InFO|flip_chip|TSV|SiP|LGA — promote to reference product]']
  pdk_version varchar [note: 'Version of the Process Design Kit (PDK) certified for use in this IC design project. Ensures reproducibility of design rule checks (DRC), layout versus schematic (LVS) verification, and parasitic extraction across the design lifecycle.']
  plm_item_reference varchar [note: 'Native item/object identifier in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or Oracle Agile PLM for this IC design project. Enables bidirectional traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer and the authoritative PLM system of record.']
  process_node_nm varchar [note: 'Target semiconductor fabrication process node in nanometers (e.g., \'5nm\', \'7nm\', \'28nm\'). Determines PDK selection, EDA tool configuration, DFM rule sets, and foundry partner assignment. Critical for PPA target-setting and yield estimation.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric project identifier assigned at project initiation in Siemens Teamcenter PLM and Oracle Agile PLM. Used across EDA tool flows, NRE contracts, and cross-functional communications as the canonical project reference.']
  project_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the IC design project scope, target application domain (e.g., AI accelerator, automotive MCU, IoT SoC), and key design objectives. Supports search, discovery, and executive reporting.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the IC design project (e.g., \'Orion SoC Rev2\'). Used in PLM dashboards, design reviews, and executive reporting.']
  project_start_date date [note: 'Official start date of the IC design project, corresponding to project kick-off and initial resource allocation in Siemens Teamcenter PLM and SAP S/4HANA CO.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the IC design project from concept through GDS/GDSII tapeout. Drives milestone tracking, resource allocation, and PLM workflow gates.
