  hast_relative_humidity_pct varchar [note: 'Relative humidity percentage for Highly Accelerated Stress Test, typically 85% RH.']
  hast_temperature_c varchar [note: 'Temperature in degrees Celsius for Highly Accelerated Stress Test, typically 110°C to 130°C.']
  htol_duration_hours varchar [note: 'Duration in hours for High Temperature Operating Life stress test, typically 1000 hours minimum per JEDEC standards.']
  htol_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether High Temperature Operating Life stress test is included in the qualification plan. HTOL accelerates time-dependent failure mechanisms under elevated temperature and voltage bias.']
  htol_temperature_c varchar [note: 'Junction temperature in degrees Celsius for High Temperature Operating Life stress testing, typically 125°C to 150°C.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification program record was last updated.']
  latchup_current_ma varchar [note: 'Trigger current in milliamperes for latch-up testing, typically 100mA minimum per JEDEC standards.']
  latchup_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether latch-up susceptibility test is included in the qualification plan. Latch-up is a CMOS failure mode where parasitic thyristor structures create low-impedance paths.']
  lot_count varchar [note: 'Number of independent fabrication lots from which qualification samples must be drawn to ensure manufacturing process representativeness.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date for completion of all qualification testing and final report approval.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for initiation of qualification testing activities.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the qualification program, typically combining product family code and qualification standard abbreviation.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the qualification program including product identifier and target application domain.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification program. Gate-keeps product lifecycle transition from NPI to volume production.']
  qualification_standard varchar [note: 'Industry or customer-specific reliability qualification standard governing the test plan. JEDEC JESD47 for general semiconductor reliability, AEC-Q100 for automotive ICs, AEC-Q101 for discrete semiconductors, AEC-Q104 for multi-chip modules, MIL-STD-883 for military/aerospace, or customer-specific requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: JEDEC_JESD47|AEC_Q100|AEC_Q101|AEC_Q104|MIL_STD_883|CUSTOMER_SPECIFIC|IEC_60749|IEC_62196 — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Category of qualification program based on trigger event: new product introduction, process node change, package technology change, supply chain change, periodic requalification, or extended temperature range qualification.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name of the reliability engineer responsible for planning, executing, and reporting the qualification program.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Total number of device units required for the complete qualification test plan across all test conditions and lots.']
  tc_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Number of temperature cycles for Temperature Cycling test, typically 500 to 1000 cycles minimum.']
  tc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Temperature Cycling stress test is included in the qualification plan. TC accelerates thermal fatigue failures due to coefficient of thermal expansion mismatch.']
  tc_max_temperature_c varchar [note: 'Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius for Temperature Cycling test, typically 125°C to 150°C.']
  tc_min_temperature_c varchar [note: 'Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius for Temperature Cycling test, typically -55°C or -65°C.']
  test_matrix_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the qualification test matrix document defining the complete set of reliability and environmental stress tests, sample sizes, and acceptance criteria.']
  waiver_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a waiver was granted to allow production release despite qualification test failures or incomplete testing.']
  waiver_justification varchar [note: 'Business and technical justification for granting a qualification waiver, including risk mitigation plan and customer approval documentation.']

  Note: 'Product qualification program records defining the reliability and environmental test plan required before production release gate. Captures qualification standard (JEDEC JESD47, AEC-Q100/Q101/Q104, MIL-STD-883, customer-specific), test matrix, sample plan, stress conditions (HTOL, HAST, TC, ESD, latch-up), duration, pass/fail criteria, qualification status, completion date, and any deviations or waivers granted. Gate-keeps product lifecycle transition from NPI to volume production.'
}

Table product.compliance_cert {
  compliance_cert_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product compliance certification record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Regulatory reporting requires each compliance certification to be linked to the customer account that holds it, enabling audit trails and certification status per client.']
  cert_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Compliance certification records must reference the employee responsible for maintaining the certification.']
  compliance_ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the semiconductor product or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this compliance certification applies.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.ic_catalog.ic_catalog_id — Compliance certifications are obtained for specific products. Must reference the certified product.']
  primary_ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.ic_catalog.ic_catalog_id — Compliance certifications are obtained for specific products or SKUs. Required for export control screening and customer documentation.']
  applicable_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic markets, regions, or countries where this compliance certification is recognized and valid (e.g., USA, EU, CHN, JPN, KOR). Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes.']
  applicable_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory regions or jurisdictions where this certification applies (e.g., North America, European Union, Asia-Pacific, EMEA).']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead auditor or assessor from the certification body who conducted the compliance assessment.']
  automotive_grade_certified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product has achieved automotive-grade qualification certification (e.g., AEC-Q100 for ICs, AEC-Q101 for discrete semiconductors) for use in automotive applications.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited third-party organization or regulatory authority that issued the compliance certification (e.g., TÜV, UL, Intertek, SGS, Bureau Veritas).']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate number or identifier issued by the certification body for this compliance certification.']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope of the certification, including specific product variants, process nodes, package types, or application domains covered by this certification.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compliance certification indicating whether it is active and valid, expired, pending approval, suspended, revoked, or under review.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory or standards certification obtained. RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances), REACH (Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals), TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act), AEC-Q100/Q101 (Automotive Electronics Council qualification standards), ISO 26262 (Automotive Functional Safety), IEC 61508 (Functional Safety), UL (Underwriters Laboratories), CE (Conformité Européenne), FCC (Federal Communications Commission), ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations), EAR (Export Administration Regulations). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RoHS|REACH|TSCA|AEC-Q100|AEC-Q101|ISO 26262|IEC 61508|UL|CE|FCC|ITAR|EAR — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, exceptions, conditions, or special handling instructions related to this compliance certification.']
  conflict_minerals_declaration varchar [note: 'Declaration of conflict minerals (tin, tantalum, tungsten, gold - 3TG) sourcing and compliance status with Dodd-Frank Act Section 1502 and OECD Due Diligence Guidance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance certification record was first created in the system.']
  customer_specific_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of any customer-specific compliance requirements or certifications that this record addresses beyond standard regulatory requirements.']
  ear_controlled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product is subject to EAR (Export Administration Regulations) export controls administered by the Bureau of Industry and Security.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the compliance certification becomes valid and enforceable for the product.']
  environmental_standard varchar [note: 'Environmental management system standard under which this certification was issued (e.g., ISO 14001, ISO 50001 for energy management).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance certification expires and requires renewal or re-certification. Nullable for certifications without expiration.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification code or category assigned to the product under this certification (e.g., ECCN - Export Control Classification Number under EAR, or USML category under ITAR).']
  functional_safety_level varchar [note: 'Functional safety integrity level achieved by the product under ISO 26262 (ASIL A/B/C/D for automotive) or IEC 61508 (SIL 1/2/3/4 for industrial) standards. ASIL (Automotive Safety Integrity Level), SIL (Safety Integrity Level). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ASIL_A|ASIL_B|ASIL_C|ASIL_D|SIL_1|SIL_2|SIL_3|SIL_4|not_applicable — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hazardous_substance_declaration varchar [note: 'Detailed declaration of hazardous substances present in the product, including substance names, CAS (Chemical Abstracts Service) numbers, concentration levels, and compliance status with RoHS, REACH, and TSCA regulations.']
  internal_owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the internal compliance manager or product quality engineer responsible for maintaining this certification.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance certification was officially issued by the certification body.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product is subject to ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) export controls and requires special handling for international shipments.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance certification record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  pcn_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Product Change Notification (PCN) that triggered this certification or recertification, if applicable.']
  quality_management_standard varchar [note: 'Quality management system standard under which this certification was issued (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949, AS9100, ISO 13485).']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product is compliant with REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006 governing the registration, evaluation, authorization, and restriction of chemicals in the European Union.']
  recertification_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required recertification cycles. Null if recertification is not required or is event-driven rather than time-based.']
  recertification_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this certification requires periodic recertification or renewal (True) or is a one-time certification (False).']
  recertification_trigger_events varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of business events that trigger mandatory recertification (e.g., design change, process node migration, package change, supplier change, PCN issuance, material substitution).']
  restricted_countries varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes to which export or sale of this product is restricted or prohibited under applicable export control regulations.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product is compliant with RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) Directive 2011/65/EU restricting the use of specific hazardous materials in electrical and electronic equipment.']
  supporting_documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the repository location containing supporting documentation, test reports, certificates, and compliance declarations for this certification.']
  test_laboratory varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited testing laboratory that performed the compliance testing and analysis supporting this certification.']
  test_report_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the official test report or assessment document that supports this compliance certification.']

  Note: 'Product compliance certification records tracking regulatory and standards certifications obtained for each product or SKU, including RoHS, REACH, TSCA, AEC-Q100/Q101, ISO 26262, IEC 61508, UL, CE, FCC, and ITAR classification. Captures certification body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, applicable markets/regions, and re-certification trigger events. Supports export control screening and customer compliance documentation requests.'
}

Table product.errata {
  errata_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product errata record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who first reported this errata, if discovered externally. Null if discovered internally. Used for customer relationship management (CRM) and design-win tracking.']
  engineering_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Errata tracking assigns an engineering owner employee for root‑cause analysis and customer communication.']
  errata_ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the affected integrated circuit (IC) product, system on chip (SoC), application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC), or field-programmable gate array (FPGA) product.']
  test_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the automatic test equipment (ATE) test pattern, design verification plan test case, or simulation run that reproduces the errata. Used for regression testing and validation.']
  to_ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.ic_catalog.ic_catalog_id — Errata document bugs in specific IC products. Must reference the affected product and revision.']
  affected_revisions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of all silicon revisions affected by this errata (e.g., A0,A1,B0). Used to determine scope of customer impact.']
  affected_use_cases varchar [note: 'Description of customer application scenarios or operating modes impacted by the errata (e.g., high-speed PCIe Gen4 operation, concurrent multi-channel DMA, low-power sleep modes).']
  business_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of commercial and operational impact including potential revenue risk, customer satisfaction impact, warranty exposure, and time to market (TTM) implications.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for closing the errata record. Fixed in Silicon: corrected in new revision; Fixed in Software: resolved via patch; Workaround Sufficient: mitigation acceptable; Product EOL: end-of-life product; Not Reproducible: cannot verify; Working as Designed: not a defect.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this errata record was first created in the product lifecycle management (PLM) system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  customer_disclosure_status varchar [note: 'Status of customer communication regarding this errata. Not Disclosed: internal only; Pending Approval: awaiting management approval; Disclosed NDA: shared under non-disclosure agreement; Disclosed Public: published in public errata sheet.']
  disclosure_date date [note: 'Date when the errata was first communicated to customers via product change notification (PCN), errata sheet update, or technical advisory. Null if not yet disclosed.']
  discovered_by varchar [note: 'Source or method by which the errata was discovered. Internal Validation: found during wafer testing or quality assurance; Customer Report: reported by design-in customer; Field Failure: observed in production deployment; Design Verification: identified during design for testability (DFT) or automatic test pattern generation (ATPG).']
  discovered_date date [note: 'Date when the silicon bug or functional deviation was first identified, either during internal validation, customer testing, or field deployment.']
  engineering_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the design engineer or engineering team responsible for investigating and resolving this errata. Used for accountability and workload tracking.']
  errata_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the errata record, typically formatted as product-family prefix followed by sequential number (e.g., SOC-001234, FPGA-000567). Used in customer communications and product change notifications (PCN).']
  errata_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the errata record. Draft: initial documentation; Under Review: engineering validation; Confirmed: verified silicon bug; Disclosed: communicated to customers; Resolved: fix available; Closed: no longer applicable.']
  fix_plan varchar [note: 'Planned resolution approach for the errata. Next Silicon Revision: will be corrected in next tapeout; Software Patch: addressable via firmware/driver update; No Fix Planned: low priority or end-of-life (EOL) product; Under Evaluation: fix strategy being assessed.']
  fix_target_revision varchar [note: 'Silicon stepping or revision where the errata will be corrected (e.g., C0, D1). Null if fix is software-only or no fix planned.']
  functional_block varchar [note: 'Name of the intellectual property (IP) core, register transfer level (RTL) module, or functional block where the errata occurs (e.g., PCIe Controller, DDR PHY, CPU Core, DMA Engine).']
  impacted_customer_count varchar [note: 'Estimated or actual number of customers affected by this errata based on product shipments and silicon revision distribution. Used for risk assessment and communication prioritization.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential internal notes for engineering team use only. May contain sensitive technical details, customer names, or business strategy information not suitable for external disclosure.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this errata record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this errata record. Used for change tracking and synchronization with downstream systems.']
  pcn_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the product change notification (PCN) document number if this errata was formally communicated to customers. Null if disclosed through other channels or not yet disclosed.']
  regulatory_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the errata affects compliance with industry standards or regulatory requirements such as automotive IATF 16949, functional safety ISO 26262, or export control ITAR/EAR classifications. Null if no regulatory impact.']
  related_errata_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of related errata record identifiers that share common root cause, affect the same functional block, or have dependencies. Used for impact analysis and consolidated fixes.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the errata was resolved either through silicon revision fix, software patch release, or closure decision. Null if still open or under evaluation.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Engineering analysis of the underlying silicon defect or design flaw causing the errata. May reference RTL logic error, timing violation, or process variation sensitivity.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Business impact severity of the errata. Critical: system failure or data corruption; Major: significant functional impact; Moderate: limited functional impact with workaround; Minor: cosmetic or edge-case issue.']
  silicon_revision varchar [note: 'Silicon stepping or revision identifier where the errata was discovered (e.g., A0, B1, C2). Corresponds to mask revision in the fabrication process.']
  symptom_description varchar [note: 'Detailed technical description of the observable behavior or functional deviation caused by the silicon bug. Includes conditions under which the issue manifests.']
  title varchar [note: 'Concise summary title of the silicon bug or functional deviation. Used in errata lists and customer-facing documentation.']
  to_revision varchar [note: 'FK to product.product_revision.product_revision_id — Errata are specific to a silicon revision/stepping. Must link to the affected revision.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when engineering verification of the errata was completed and status changed to verified or cannot reproduce. Null if verification still in progress or not started.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Engineering validation status of the reported errata. Unverified: initial report not yet investigated; In Progress: under engineering analysis; Verified: confirmed as silicon bug; Cannot Reproduce: unable to replicate reported behavior.']
  workaround_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a software, firmware, or operational workaround exists to mitigate the errata impact. True if workaround documented; False if no mitigation available.']
  workaround_description varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions for software, firmware, or operational workaround to avoid or mitigate the errata. May include register configuration changes, driver patches, or usage restrictions. Null if no workaround available.']
  workaround_performance_impact varchar [note: 'Description of any power, performance, or area (PPA) degradation introduced by the workaround (e.g., 5% throughput reduction, 10mW additional power consumption, increased latency). Null if no measurable impact or no workaround.']

  Note: 'Product errata records documenting known silicon bugs, functional deviations, and workarounds for released IC products. Captures errata ID, affected product and revision, silicon stepping, errata title, functional impact description, affected use cases, workaround availability, fix target (next silicon revision or software workaround), customer disclosure status, and severity classification. Critical for customer technical support and design-in risk management.'
}

Table product.configuration_rule {
  configuration_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the configuration rule.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: configuration_rule defines constraints that are scoped to a product family; a foreign key to product_family replaces the free‑text family field, normalizing the model.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.ic_catalog.ic_catalog_id — Configuration rules define valid SKU combinations for a specific catalog product. Product reference is mandatory.']
  overridden_configuration_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on configuration_rule (overridden_configuration_rule_id)']
  rule_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Configuration Rule governance requires a rule owner employee for change approval and audit.']
  applicable_market varchar [note: 'Target market segment (e.g., automotive, consumer, industrial) for which the rule is valid.']
  applicable_process_node varchar [note: 'Process node (e.g., 7nm, 5nm) for which the rule is valid.']
  automotive_qualified boolean [note: 'True if the rule applies to automotive‑qualified SKUs.']
  configuration_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the rule logic and intent.']
  configuration_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  constraint_expression varchar [note: 'Logical expression defining the valid combination of options (e.g., "package_type=\'BGA\' AND speed_grade=\'high\' => voltage_variant=\'1.2V\'").']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO‑3 country code indicating the origin of the component when the rule is applied.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created in the system.']
  ear_eccn_code varchar [note: 'Export Administration Regulations Export Control Classification Number relevant to the rule.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the rule expires; null for open‑ended rules.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the rule becomes effective for SKU creation.']
  hts_code varchar [note: 'Customs classification code applicable to the rule\'s configurations.']
  is_default_rule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule is the default fallback when no other rule matches.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the rule is subject to ITAR export controls.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent formal review of the rule.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the rule.']
  option_dependencies varchar [note: 'Textual description of any dependent options or exclusions related to the rule.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Package option that the rule constrains.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used when multiple rules apply; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  qualification_level varchar [note: 'Qualification level option that the rule constrains.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule enforces REACH‑compliant configurations.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or team that performed the last review.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule enforces RoHS‑compliant configurations.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the rule in downstream systems.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose of the rule.']
  rule_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or team responsible for the rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of rule based on the option it governs.']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management of the rule.']
  speed_grade varchar [note: 'Speed grade option that the rule constrains.']
  temperature_range varchar [note: 'Temperature range option that the rule constrains.']
  voltage_variant varchar [note: 'Voltage variant option that the rule constrains.']

  Note: 'Rules defining valid combinations of package, speed grade, temperature range, voltage variant, and qualification level for orderable SKU creation. Captures constraint expressions, option dependencies, and effectivity dates. Prevents invalid SKU proliferation and powers customer-facing configurator tools for ASIC/FPGA option selection.'
}

Table product.product_sample_request {
  product_sample_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each engineering sample request record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer requesting the sample.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Sample requests incur costs; assigning a cost center allows budgeting and charge‑back.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who approved the request.']
  followup_product_sample_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on product_sample_request (followup_product_sample_request_id)']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the semiconductor product for which the sample is requested.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: Link sample requests to the originating sales order to report conversion rates and manage sample fulfillment tied to specific orders.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the sample request.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request was approved.']
  compliance_itar_flag boolean [note: 'True if the sample is subject to ITAR export controls.']
  compliance_reach_flag boolean [note: 'True if the sample complies with REACH chemical regulations.']
  compliance_rohs_flag boolean [note: 'True if the sample complies with RoHS restrictions.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person for the sample request.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the request record was first created.']
  design_win_conversion_flag boolean [note: 'True if the sample request resulted in a confirmed design win.']
  design_win_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the sample request led to a design‑win for the product.']
  evaluation_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the customer\'s technical evaluation of the sample.']
  evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (e.g., 0‑100) assigned by the customer to the sample.']
  expedited_flag boolean [note: 'True if the request requires accelerated processing.']
  export_control_info varchar [note: 'Export control classification and any restricted country restrictions applicable to the sample.']
  feedback_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the customer is expected to return evaluation feedback.']
  feedback_received_date date [note: 'Date the customer submitted evaluation feedback.']
  feedback_summary varchar [note: 'Brief textual summary of the customer\'s feedback.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the request record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions from the requester.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the sample request.']
  product_sample_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sample request.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units requested.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the sample request, used in communications and tracking.']
  request_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the request within the organization.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request was initially submitted by the customer or internal team.']
  sample_condition varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the sample upon receipt or return.']
  sample_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost associated with providing the sample.']
  sample_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the sample cost.']
  sample_returned_flag boolean [note: 'True if the customer returned the sample after evaluation.']
  sample_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number for traceability.']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Category of sample: engineering silicon, qualification unit, or production‑representative.']
  ship_date date [note: 'Date the sample is scheduled to be shipped to the customer.']
  shipping_address varchar [note: 'Delivery address for the sample shipment.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Logistics method used to deliver the sample.']
  target_application varchar [note: 'Intended end‑use or customer design context for the sample.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for the sample shipment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for quantity, typically "pcs".']

  Note: 'Engineering sample and evaluation unit requests tracking pre-production sample distribution for customer design-in evaluation. Captures sample type (engineering silicon, qualification units, production-representative), quantity, recipient customer, target application, ship/feedback dates, evaluation outcome, and design-win conversion status. Critical for NPI funnel velocity and customer engagement metrics.'
}

Table product.license_agreement {
  license_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the license_agreement association']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account']
  product_ip_core_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the IP core']
  agreement_date date [note: 'Effective date of the licensing agreement']
  licensing_model varchar [note: 'Business model for the license (royalty‑free, per‑unit royalty, NRE)']
  nre_cost_usd decimal [note: 'One‑time non‑recurring engineering cost for licensing and integration']
  per_unit_royalty_usd decimal [note: 'Royalty fee charged per manufactured chip that incorporates the IP core']

  Note: 'This association product represents the licensing contract between an IP core and a customer account. It captures the licensing model, royalty rates, NRE cost, and agreement dates for each IP core licensed to each account.. Existence Justification: IP cores are licensed to customers via formal contracts. Each IP core can be licensed to many accounts, and each account can hold licenses for many IP cores. The license agreement records licensing model, royalty, NRE cost, and effective dates, which are managed as a distinct business entity.'
}

Table product.license_allocation {
  license_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the license_allocation association']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the export license record']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the IC catalog entry']
  authorized_commodities varchar [note: 'List of commodity categories covered by the license for this IC']
  authorized_countries varchar [note: 'ISO country codes where export of this IC is permitted under the license']
  authorized_end_users varchar [note: 'Names of approved end‑user organizations for this IC under the license']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the export license expires for this IC']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the export license was issued']

  Note: 'This association captures the many‑to‑many relationship between IC catalog entries and export licenses. Each record links one ic_catalog to one export_license and stores attributes that are specific to the licensing context, such as authorized commodities, countries, end‑users, and the license validity period.. Existence Justification: An IC catalog entry may be covered by multiple export licenses depending on commodity, destination, and end‑use, while a single export license can apply to many ICs across product families. The licensing team actively creates, updates, and retires these mappings as part of export compliance processes, and the mapping itself carries attributes such as authorized commodities, countries, and validity dates.'
}

Table customer.account {
  account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the account record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation reports assign internal costs to each customer account; enables accurate cost‑to‑customer analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative responsible for the account.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Customer account revenue is posted to a default GL account; required for General Ledger posting per account.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Customer profitability analysis tracks revenue and expense by profit center; required for the Customer Profitability Dashboard.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Normalize address data: move primary address fields from account to address table and reference via FK.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Required for Preferred Supplier selection per account, used in sourcing strategy and procurement reports.']
  account_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the account.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Primary display name of the account (legal or trade name).']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the account.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the account within the semiconductor ecosystem.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent compliance audit for the account.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the account was closed or deactivated, if applicable.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for account communications.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the account with internal and external regulations.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating of the account. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|C|D — promote to reference product]']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code used for billing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|JPY|CNY|KRW|... — promote to reference product]']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level applied to the account record.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet unique identifier for the legal entity.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control regime applicable to the account.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region of the account\'s operations.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'Primary industry vertical the account operates in (e.g., automotive, consumer electronics). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automotive|consumer|industrial|communications|computing|... — promote to reference product]']
  label_requirements varchar [note: 'Labeling format required on products delivered to the account.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent interaction or transaction with the account.']
  last_order_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent order placed by the account.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the corporate entity as registered.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by sales or support teams.']
  packaging_requirements varchar [note: 'Special packaging or labeling requirements for shipments to the account.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the account.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications with the account.']
  primary_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Normalize primary address fields by referencing address table.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Main email address used for account communications.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for the account point of contact.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the account record.']
  revenue_tier varchar [note: 'Revenue bracket of the account based on annual sales.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) based on credit, compliance, and transaction history.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region assigned to the account.']
  strategic_classification varchar [note: 'Strategic importance of the account to the company.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is tax‑exempt.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the account.']
  creation_date date [note: 'Date when the account was first created in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record for all customer accounts in the semiconductor ecosystem, including OEMs, fabless design houses, ODMs, distributors, and automotive Tier-1 suppliers. SSOT for customer identity, legal entity name, DUNS number, account type, industry vertical, revenue tier, strategic classification, credit rating, payment terms, export control classification (EAR/ITAR), account status, preferred communication settings, packaging/labeling requirements, and Salesforce CRM account ID. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA SD module and Salesforce CRM Account object.'
}

Table customer.contact {
  contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the contact.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Every contact must be associated with exactly one customer account. This is the most fundamental FK in the domain — contacts cannot exist without an account parent.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.address']
  contact_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Connect contact to its parent customer account for hierarchy and reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee identifier if the contact is a Semiconductors employee.']
  birth_date date [note: 'Birth date of the contact, used for age verification where required.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level of the contact data.']
  department varchar [note: 'Department within the organization where the contact works.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the contact.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contact in external systems (e.g., Salesforce ID).']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the contact.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the contact person.']
  gdpr_consent_status varchar [note: 'Current GDPR consent status for personal data processing.']
  is_employee boolean [note: 'True if the contact is an employee of Semiconductors.']
  is_key_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact is a primary point of contact for the customer account.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications.']
  last_consent_update timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent GDPR consent status change.']
  last_interaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent interaction with the contact.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the contact.']
  linkedin_profile varchar [note: 'URL to the contact\'s LinkedIn professional profile.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates if the contact has opted in to receive marketing communications.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Contact\'s nationality.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about the contact.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the contact, in international format.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'Preferred method for contacting the individual.']
  profile_picture_url varchar [note: 'URL to the contact\'s profile picture.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contact record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contact record.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the contact\'s functional role.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System where the contact record originated.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Time zone of the contact for scheduling communications.']
  title varchar [note: 'Professional title or position of the contact within their organization.']

  Note: 'Individual contact persons associated with customer accounts, including design engineers, procurement managers, FAE (Field Application Engineers) counterparts, and executive sponsors. Captures full name, title, department, role type (technical, commercial, executive), email, phone, preferred communication channel, LinkedIn profile, GDPR consent status, and active/inactive status. Maps to Salesforce CRM Contact object.'
}

Table customer.account_hierarchy {
  account_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each account hierarchy relationship record.']
  child_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the child corporate account linked to the parent.']
  parent_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent corporate account in the hierarchy.']
  primary_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — The parent_account_id in account_hierarchy must reference the account product. Without this FK, hierarchy records are orphaned.']
  account_hierarchy_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes providing additional context about the parent‑child relationship.']
  account_hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the hierarchy link.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hierarchy record was first created in the source system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the hierarchical relationship became effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the hierarchical relationship ended; null if still active.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the child account within the corporate hierarchy (1 = top‑level).']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'True if the child account is the primary entity under the parent; otherwise false.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the hierarchy record.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hierarchical relationship between parent and child accounts.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the hierarchy record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the hierarchy record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the hierarchy record.']

  Note: 'Defines parent-child corporate hierarchy relationships between customer accounts, enabling roll-up of revenue, design-wins, and engagement metrics across global enterprise accounts. Captures parent account, child account, hierarchy level, relationship type (subsidiary, division, affiliate, distributor branch), effective date, and end date. Critical for global OEM and distributor account management.'
}

Table customer.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the customer segment record. _canonical_skip_reason: Inferred role REFERENCE_LOOKUP; no mandatory minimum categories required.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the segment record was first created.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default discount percentage offered to the segment.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the segment definition becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the segment definition expires or is retired (null if open‑ended).']
  market_share_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired market share percentage for the segment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the segment.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Pricing model applied to the segment.']
  region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region associated with the segment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|CHN|JPN|KOR|DEU|FRA|GBR|IND|BRA|AUS — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  revenue_target_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual revenue target for the segment expressed in US dollars.']
  sales_motion varchar [note: 'Primary sales approach used for the segment.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the market segment within the organization.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the segment purpose and characteristics.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the market segment (e.g., Hyperscaler, Automotive Tier‑1).']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment.']
  strategic_priority_tier varchar [note: 'Internal priority ranking used for resource allocation and go‑to‑market planning.']
  sub_vertical varchar [note: 'More granular market sub‑category within the vertical (e.g., AI Chipmakers, Power Management). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ai_chipmakers|power_management|networking|mobile_devices|automotive_sensors|industrial_automation|defense_systems|smart_home|wearables|edge_computing] ']
  tam_band varchar [note: 'Size band of the total addressable market for the segment.']
  target_customer_type varchar [note: 'Primary type of customers targeted by the segment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the segment record.']
  vertical_market varchar [note: 'High‑level industry vertical to which the segment belongs (e.g., Semiconductor, Automotive, Consumer Electronics). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: semiconductor|automotive|consumer_electronics|industrial_iot|government_defense|healthcare|telecom|energy|finance|retail|education|media] ']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the segment record.']

  Note: 'Reference data defining market segmentation classifications for customer accounts. Each segment represents a strategic grouping used for go-to-market planning, pricing strategy, and resource allocation. Attributes include segment code, segment name (e.g., Hyperscaler, Automotive Tier-1, Consumer Electronics OEM, Industrial IoT, Government/Defense), vertical market, sub-vertical, strategic priority tier, TAM band, and assigned sales motion. Managed by sales strategy and marketing teams as controlled vocabulary.'
}

Table customer.address {
  address_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the address record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Addresses are associated with customer accounts (ship-to, bill-to, HQ). This FK is required for order fulfillment, logistics, and export control screening.']
  address_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Addresses are associated with customer accounts (ship-to, bill-to, HQ). This is a core master data relationship.']
  address_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to reference the address within ERP and PLM systems.']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the address record.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Category of the address indicating its business purpose.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for the most recent address change.']
  change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the most recent address change was recorded.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the address.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Classification indicating whether the address belongs to internal organization, external customer, or partner.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact at the address.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the address.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact at the address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 country code.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the address record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the address becomes valid for business use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the address ceases to be valid (null if open‑ended).']
  export_control_screened boolean [note: 'True if the address has been screened against export control lists.']
  export_control_status varchar [note: 'Result of export‑control screening for the address.']
  geolocation_accuracy varchar [note: 'Indicates the precision level of the latitude/longitude values.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is the primary location for the associated account.']
  label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label identifying the address (e.g., “HQ San Jose”).']
  last_verified date [note: 'Date when the address information was last validated.']
  latitude double [note: 'Latitude coordinate in decimal degrees.']
  lineage_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this address to its predecessor in case of address merges or splits.']
  longitude double [note: 'Longitude coordinate in decimal degrees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the address.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the address.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Business purpose for which the address is used.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region (e.g., APAC, EMEA, AMER) used for reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system where the address originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Salesforce CRM).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the address.']
  street_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address.']
  street_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address (optional).']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction applicable to the address.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier for the address location.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the address record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of address verification.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the record.']

  Note: 'Physical and mailing addresses associated with customer accounts and contacts, including headquarters, ship-to, bill-to, and design center locations. Captures address type, street lines, city, state/province, postal code, country (ISO 3166), region, time zone, and geolocation coordinates. Supports multi-site global accounts with multiple address records per account. Used for logistics, tax jurisdiction, and export control screening.'
}

Table customer.customer_design_win {
  customer_design_win_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the customer design win record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Every design-win is attributed to a specific customer account. This FK is critical for revenue forecasting and account-level reporting.']
  assembly_process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to packaging.assembly_process_flow. Business justification: Each design win selects a packaging process flow; linking enables process planning and yield forecasting in the Process Flow Assignment workflow.']
  channel_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distributor channel partner, if the win originated via a distributor.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.contact.contact_id — Design-wins reference the FAE owner and customer engineering contact. Contact FK enables relationship tracking.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer organization that owns the design win.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Each design win part must have an ECCN classification for export control; used in export license generation and customs documentation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FAE responsible for the design‑win engagement.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Design‑win forecasts and revenue reports must reference the exact catalog entry; part_number is a denormalized duplicate and is removed.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Design win financial tracking attributes revenue to a profit center for margin analysis; appears in Design Win Profitability report.']
  reach_svhc_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.reach_svhc_declaration. Business justification: Design win parts require REACH SVHC declarations for EU chemical compliance; needed for regulatory filing and product safety reporting.']
  research_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.program. Business justification: Design Win Forecast Report links each customer design win to the internal R&D program delivering the technology, enabling revenue attribution and program performance tracking.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Design win must identify the supplier manufacturing the part, essential for supply chain planning and production ramp scheduling.']
  competitive_displacement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the win displaces a competitor\'s product (true) or not (false).']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design win was officially confirmed (won).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  design_project_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal design project associated with the win.']
  design_win_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the design win record.']
  design_win_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the design win (internal development, channel‑sourced, or partner‑sourced).']
  estimated_annual_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected annual revenue from the design win, expressed in US dollars.']
  estimated_annual_unit_volume varchar [note: 'Projected number of units the customer will purchase per year.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the FAE or sales team.']
  nre_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated NRE cost associated with the design win, expressed in US dollars.']
  nre_required_flag boolean [note: 'True if the project includes a non‑recurring engineering (NRE) component.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Package format chosen for the part (e.g., BGA, WLCSP).']
  platform_generation varchar [note: 'Generation of the platform family for which the design win is targeted.']
  process_node varchar [note: 'Technology node (e.g., 7nm, 5nm) selected for the design.']
  production_ramp_date date [note: 'Target date when volume production is expected to ramp.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design win was first registered in the system.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the design‑win lifecycle.']
  tapeout_target_date date [note: 'Planned date for tape‑out of the design.']
  target_application varchar [note: 'Intended end‑product or market application (e.g., smartphone SoC, ADAS ECU, AI accelerator).']

  Note: 'Unified record tracking the full design-in lifecycle from initial registration through confirmed design-win for semiconductor products selected by customers. SSOT for the complete design-in pipeline from early registration through revenue forecasting — distinct from sales-domain opportunity records which track revenue probability and sales stage. Captures design project name, target end product/application (e.g., smartphone SoC, ADAS ECU, AI accelerator), platform generation, process node, package type, device/part number selected, design-win stage (registered, evaluating, sampling, won, lost, on-hold), registration date, design-win confirmation date, tapeout target date, production ramp date, estimated annual unit volume (AUV), estimated annual revenue, competitive displacement flag, associated distributor (if channel-sourced), FAE owner, and NRE requirements. Integrates with Salesforce CRM Opportunity.'
}

Table customer.customer_design_registration {
  customer_design_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each customer design registration record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer owning the design project.']
  channel_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distributor (if any) associated with the registration.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Design registration process records the fab tool that will be qualified for the project; needed for the \'Design Qualification Tool Allocation\' workflow.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Design registration validation uses the catalog to verify process node, package, and qualification; part_number is redundant and removed.']
  packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to packaging.package_type. Business justification: Design registration specifies the intended package type; linking ensures downstream packaging aligns with the registered specifications, used in the Design Registration Review process.']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Design Registration process requires tying the customer\'s registration to the internal research project developing the part for coordination and status reporting.']
  sales_rep_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: DESIGN REGISTRATION: Captures the sales engineer responsible for registering a design win, required for revenue attribution and performance dashboards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the registration record was first created.']
  design_complexity varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of design difficulty.']
  design_project_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the design objectives, features, and scope.']
  design_project_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the customer’s design project.']
  design_start_date date [note: 'Date when the customer officially started the design effort.']
  expected_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected percentage of good dies per wafer for the design.']
  nre_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Non‑Recurring Engineering budget allocated for the design project.']
  nre_budget_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the NRE budget amount.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Physical package style for the final silicon die.']
  platform_generation varchar [note: 'Generation of the semiconductor platform (e.g., next‑gen, legacy) targeted by the design.']
  process_node_nm varchar [note: 'Technology node (in nanometers) selected for the design.']
  production_target_date date [note: 'Target date for first silicon production run.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the qualification status was determined.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the customer design qualification review.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the design registration, used for tracking and external communication.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the design registration.']
  tapeout_target_date date [note: 'Planned date for the final mask set release (tape‑out).']
  target_application varchar [note: 'Primary market or end‑use application for which the design is intended.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the registration record.']

  Note: 'Formal registration of a customer design project that uses a specific semiconductor product, capturing the design project name, target application, platform generation, process node, package type, design start date, tapeout target date, production target date, design registration status, and associated distributor (if applicable). Enables early pipeline visibility and NRE planning. Precedes and feeds into design_win records.'
}

Table customer.qualification_status {
  qualification_status_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each qualification status record.']
  primary_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Qualification status is per-account (or per-site). Must reference the account being qualified.']
  qualification_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer to which this qualification applies.']
  qualification_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: QUALIFICATION OWNERSHIP: Tracks which employee owns each qualification record for audit and compliance reporting, essential for product qualification governance.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific customer site or location being qualified.']
  aec_q_status varchar [note: 'Status of the AEC-Q100/Q101 compliance qualification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification record was created.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code for attached qualification documentation.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the qualification becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the qualification expires or is no longer effective.']
  iatf_16949_status varchar [note: 'Status of the IATF 16949 automotive quality certification.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the qualification record.']
  overall_qualification_status varchar [note: 'Aggregated qualification outcome for the customer.']
  ppap_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Production Part Approval Process qualification.']
  qualification_completion_date date [note: 'Date when qualification was completed.']
  qualification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding the qualification process.']
  qualification_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the qualification record.']
  qualification_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific qualification requirements applicable to the customer.']
  qualification_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent qualification review.']
  qualification_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0-100) representing overall qualification assessment.']
  qualification_start_date date [note: 'Date when qualification activities commenced.']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Category of qualification based on industry segment.']
  qualified_product_families varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product families the customer is qualified to receive.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the qualification record.']

  Note: 'Tracks the qualification and approval status of a customer account or specific customer site for purchasing and receiving semiconductor products, including automotive PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) qualification, AEC-Q100/Q101 compliance acknowledgment, IATF 16949 certification status, customer-specific qualification requirements, qualification start date, qualification completion date, qualified product families, and qualification expiry date. Critical for automotive and industrial customer management.'
}

Table customer.engagement_activity {
  engagement_activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the engagement activity record.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.contact.contact_id — Engagement activities involve specific contacts (meeting participants, email recipients).']
  engagement_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — All engagement activities are logged against a customer account for relationship health tracking.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales opportunity linked to the activity.']
  primary_engagement_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account associated with the activity.']
  related_design_ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the design project referenced in the activity.']
  related_product_ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product discussed or demonstrated.']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Engagement Activity logs include activities tied to a specific research project, needed for project status dashboards and resource planning.']
  activity_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the activity purpose.']
  activity_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Length of the activity measured in minutes.']
  activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the engagement activity occurred.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Category of the engagement activity (e.g., technical visit, design review, product demo, sample request, NDA signing, executive briefing, strategic account designation, tier upgrade, account escalation, at‑risk flag). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: technical_visit|design_review|product_demo|sample_request|nda_signing|executive_briefing|strategic_account_designation|tier_upgrade|account_escalation|at_risk_flag — promote to reference product]']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of files attached to the activity record.']
  budget_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Budgeted monetary amount allocated for the activity.']
  business_impact varchar [note: 'Qualitative impact of the activity on the business relationship.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Medium through which the activity was conducted.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of the activity with internal and external regulations.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center associated with the activity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the activity record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code for the budget amount.']
  engagement_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1‑10) of the overall engagement quality.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next follow‑up action.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the activity is billable to the customer.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the activity contains confidential information.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the activity was escalated to higher management.']
  language varchar [note: 'Language used during the activity.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the activity took place.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Engagement status after the activity.']
  next_action varchar [note: 'Planned follow‑up action after the activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured during the activity.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the result of the activity.']
  participant_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the participant.']
  participant_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the participant.']
  participant_role varchar [note: 'Role of the participant representing the company in the activity.']
  prior_status varchar [note: 'Engagement status before the activity.']
  region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code representing the region of the activity.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the activity must be reported to a regulatory body.']
  risk_flag varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with the engagement.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that captured the activity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the activity record.']

  Note: 'Comprehensive log of all customer engagement activities, touchpoints, and relationship milestone events. Covers FAE technical visits, design review meetings, product demonstrations, sample requests follow-ups, NDA signings, executive briefings, as well as significant relationship milestones (strategic account designation, tier upgrades, account escalations, at-risk flags). Captures activity type, date, participants, account, opportunity reference, outcome, next action, channel, prior/new state for milestone events, and business impact. Integrates with Salesforce CRM Activity/Task/Event objects. Supports customer relationship health scoring and lifecycle tracking.'
}

Table customer.nda_agreement {
  nda_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the NDA agreement record.']
  nda_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Associate NDA agreement with the customer account it concerns.']
  primary_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — NDAs are executed with customer accounts. Must reference the account for access control and roadmap sharing decisions.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: NDAs are executed with suppliers to protect IP; linking supports compliance monitoring and legal audit.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the NDA by the legal or sales organization.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or contractual compliance obligations referenced in the NDA (e.g., ITAR, EAR, GDPR).']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the NDA\'s sensitivity as defined by corporate data governance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the NDA record was first created in the system.']
  document_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the stored NDA document in the document management system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the NDA becomes legally binding.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date the NDA was signed by the authorized signatory.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the NDA expires unless renewed.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code representing the legal jurisdiction governing the NDA.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Official legal name of the customer organization bound by the NDA.']
  nda_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the NDA agreement.']
  nda_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the NDA is mutual (both parties exchange confidential information) or one‑way (only the semiconductor company shares information).']
  renewal_notice_date date [note: 'Date on which a renewal notice must be sent to the customer before expiry.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the technology, roadmap, pricing, or other information covered by the NDA.']
  signatory_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the signatory used for correspondence and notifications.']
  signatory_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual who signed the NDA on behalf of the customer.']
  signatory_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number of the signatory.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for early termination of the NDA, if applicable.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the NDA record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the NDA record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the NDA when amendments are made.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the NDA record.']

  Note: 'Non-Disclosure Agreement records between the semiconductor company and customer accounts, capturing NDA type (mutual, one-way), execution date, expiry date, covered scope (product roadmap, process technology, pricing), signatory contacts, legal entity names, NDA status (active, expired, under renewal), and document reference ID. Required before sharing roadmap, PDK, or pre-production device information with customers.'
}

Table customer.account_team {
  account_team_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each account team assignment record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal employee (sales, FAE, support) assigned to the account.']
  primary_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account to which the team member is assigned.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assignment (e.g., active, inactive, pending, terminated).']
  coverage_region varchar [note: 'Broader geographic region covered by the team member for this account.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s assignment to the account ends or is scheduled to end (null if ongoing).']
  is_primary_team boolean [note: 'True if this assignment belongs to the account\'s primary coverage team (as opposed to secondary support).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any supplemental information about the assignment.']
  primary_contact_flag boolean [note: 'True if this team member is the primary point of contact for the account.']
  role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the employee within the account coverage team.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s assignment to the account becomes effective.']
  territory varchar [note: 'Geographic sales territory associated with the assignment (e.g., NA, EMEA, APAC).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assignment record.']
  workload_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Portion of the employee\'s total workload allocated to this account (percentage).']

  Note: 'Assignment of internal sales, FAE, and support personnel to customer accounts, defining the account coverage team structure. Captures team member employee ID, account ID, role (Account Manager, FAE, Regional Sales Manager, Application Engineer, Customer Success Manager), territory, assignment start date, assignment end date, primary contact flag, and coverage region. Enables account ownership tracking and workload management.'
}

Table customer.distributor_agreement {
  distributor_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the distributor agreement record.']
  channel_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the distributor party associated with this agreement.']
  distributor_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Link distributor agreement to the customer account that holds the distribution relationship.']
  primary_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Distributor agreements reference the distributor\'s account record. The distributor is a type of account.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned by the company to identify the agreement.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement such as distribution, exclusive, or co-marketing.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the agreement received final approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the agreement.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or industry compliance obligations attached to the agreement.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic copy of the signed agreement.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency used for all monetary amounts in the agreement.']
  discount_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard discount percentage applied to the distributor\'s purchases.']
  distributor_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the agreement.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the agreement expires or is terminated; null for open‑ended contracts.']
