  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the agreement becomes legally binding.']
  exclusive_distribution boolean [note: 'True if the distributor has exclusive rights for the territory/product line.']
  mdf_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the MDF entitlement.']
  mdf_entitlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of Marketing Development Fund allocated to the distributor.']
  mdf_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the MDF entitlement expires if not used.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units the distributor must order per transaction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks.']
  price_protection_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of price protection clauses (e.g., minimum advertised price).']
  product_line_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of product line identifiers the distributor is authorized to sell.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required regulatory approvals for the agreement.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the distributor must be notified before automatic renewal.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the agreement renews automatically, manually, or not at all.']
  stock_rotation_rights boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distributor has rights to rotate stock to maintain freshness.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to give notice before terminating the agreement.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for agreement termination, if applicable.']
  territory_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the geographic territory covered.']
  tier varchar [note: 'Level of the distributor based on company‑defined criteria.']

  Note: 'Master records of distribution agreements between the semiconductor company and authorized distributor accounts, capturing distributor tier (authorized, preferred, elite), geographic territory coverage, product line authorizations, stock rotation rights, price protection terms, marketing development fund (MDF) entitlements, agreement effective date, expiry date, and agreement status. Distinct from direct customer accounts — distributors resell to end customers.'
}

Table customer.credit_profile {
  credit_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the credit profile record.']
  credit_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer to which this credit profile belongs.']
  primary_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Credit profiles are per-account. Must reference the account for order-to-cash credit checks.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the credit profile is subject to any regulatory hold or review.']
  credit_analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the credit analyst.']
  credit_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s credit is currently on hold.']
  credit_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for placing the customer\'s credit on hold.']
  credit_limit_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount by which the credit limit was adjusted.']
  credit_limit_adjustment_date date [note: 'Date when the credit limit adjustment took effect.']
  credit_limit_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent credit limit change.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount approved for the customer.']
  credit_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the credit limit (ISO 4217 three‑letter code).']
  credit_limit_effective_from date [note: 'Date when the current credit limit became effective.']
  credit_limit_effective_until date [note: 'Date when the current credit limit expires (null if open‑ended).']
  credit_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit profile.']
  credit_profile_type varchar [note: 'Category of the credit profile based on internal risk segmentation.']
  credit_rating_external varchar [note: 'External credit rating from a third‑party agency.']
  credit_rating_internal varchar [note: 'Internal credit rating assigned by the company.']
  credit_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent credit review.']
  credit_review_notes varchar [note: 'Analyst comments from the latest credit review.']
  credit_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent credit review.']
  credit_score varchar [note: 'Numerical credit score from an external scoring model.']
  credit_score_date date [note: 'Date when the credit score was obtained.']
  credit_score_source varchar [note: 'Source of the credit score (e.g., Equifax, Experian).']
  credit_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the credit limit currently utilized.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency used for all monetary fields (ISO 4217).']
  external_rating_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the external agency providing the credit rating.']
  is_preferred_customer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is marked as a preferred partner.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credit profile.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current unpaid balance owed by the customer.']
  overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount past due beyond the agreed payment terms.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the customer.']
  preferred_customer_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for preferred customer status.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact for credit communications.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the credit profile (e.g., "Standard Credit Profile").']
  rating_date date [note: 'Date when the external credit rating was issued.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit profile record was first created.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification derived from credit metrics.']
  status_date date [note: 'Date when the credit profile status last changed.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the credit profile record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the credit profile record.']

  Note: 'Customer-facing credit and financial risk profile maintained by the customer domain as SSOT for customer creditworthiness assessment. Captures credit limit, credit rating (internal and external Dun & Bradstreet), payment terms (Net 30, Net 60, prepay), credit hold status, credit review date, outstanding balance, overdue amount, credit utilization percentage, currency, and credit analyst notes. Distinct from finance-domain AR aging and collections records — this product owns the customer\'s credit posture used for real-time order-to-cash credit checks. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA FI-AR (Accounts Receivable).'
}

Table customer.customer_sample_request {
  customer_sample_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sample request record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Sample requests originate from a customer account. Required for funnel tracking and account-level engagement metrics.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.contact.contact_id — Sample requests are submitted by a specific contact (design engineer). FK enables follow-up and design-in tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Sample request costs are charged to an internal cost center; required for Sample Cost Allocation reporting.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who submitted the sample request.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FAE who approved the sample request.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Sample fulfillment may be executed via an internal order to capture labor/material spend; needed for Internal Order Cost Tracking.']
  shipping_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Normalize shipping address details into address table, reducing redundancy.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Sample request is sent to a specific supplier; linking enables tracking of sample fulfillment and supplier performance.']
  application_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the customer’s intended application for the sample.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request was approved.']
  cost_adjustment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any additional charge or discount applied to the gross cost (e.g., tax, handling fee).']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base cost of the sample before any adjustments.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the sample cost.']
  cost_net decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final cost after adjustments; amount to be billed to the customer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the request record was first created in the system.']
  customer_sample_request_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the sample request.']
  delivery_requested_date date [note: 'Date by which the customer expects the sample to be delivered.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of sample fulfillment and delivery.']
  fulfillment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the fulfillment status last changed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the requestor or processing team.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Part number (e.g., SKU or device ID) of the sample being requested.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units requested for the sample.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable request number assigned by the system.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request was submitted by the customer.']
  sample_purpose varchar [note: 'Intended use of the sample (e.g., evaluation, design‑in, qualification, testing).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the request record.']

  Note: 'Customer requests for engineering samples, evaluation kits, and pre-production devices prior to volume purchase. Captures requesting contact, account, part number requested, quantity, application description, sample purpose (evaluation, design-in, qualification), requested delivery date, shipping address, approval status, approving FAE, sample cost (if charged), and fulfillment status. Tracks the early-stage design-in funnel from sample to design registration to design-win.'
}

Table customer.price_agreement {
  price_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price agreement record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Customer-specific pricing is negotiated per account. FK to account is mandatory for pricing lookups during order entry.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: PRICE APPROVAL: Links the employee who approves a price agreement, needed for audit trails, compliance with pricing governance, and sign‑off reports.']
  customer_design_win_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated design‑win record, if the price agreement is tied to a winning design.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue posting for each price agreement requires a GL account; used in the Revenue Recognition report.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Price agreements are contractually bound to a catalog item to ensure correct pricing and compliance; part_number is a denormalized duplicate.']
  price_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer to which the price agreement applies.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the price agreement, used in contracts and communications.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the price agreement indicating its purpose or pricing model.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the price agreement was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the internal approval workflow for the price agreement.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the person who approved the agreement.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Special Price Authorization or contract linked to this agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the agreed price.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied on top of the base unit price.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the price agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the price agreement expires; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  is_price_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price agreement is locked from further changes.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier that performed the latest update.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered to qualify for the agreed price.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or internal comments related to the agreement.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'Specific revision or mask set of the part covered by the agreement.']
  price_agreement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, scope, or special conditions of the agreement.']
  price_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the price agreement.']
  price_change_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the approval workflow instance for a price change.']
  price_change_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which a price amendment becomes effective.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative reason for a price amendment or re‑negotiation.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price data (e.g., SAP SD pricing condition, manual entry, external feed).']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the price (e.g., per die, per wafer, per lot).']
  pricing_channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel through which the price is offered.']
  pricing_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region to which the pricing terms apply.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether taxes are already included in the unit price.']
  tier_end_quantity varchar [note: 'Inclusive upper bound of quantity for this volume tier; null if no upper limit.']
  tier_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed unit price applicable for the defined volume tier.']
  tier_start_quantity varchar [note: 'Inclusive lower bound of quantity for this volume tier.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price per unit of the part, expressed in the specified currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the price agreement record.']
  volume_tier varchar [note: 'Label for the volume‑based pricing tier.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier that created the price agreement record.']

  Note: 'Customer-specific pricing agreements and special price authorizations (SPAs) negotiated for specific part numbers. SSOT for customer-negotiated prices distinct from sales-domain standard list pricing and discount schedules. Captures agreed unit price, currency, minimum order quantity (MOQ), volume tiers, effective date, expiry date, approval chain, SPA reference number, and associated design-win or contract. These are customer-committed prices that flow into order entry — not sales pipeline pricing assumptions. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA SD pricing conditions.'
}

Table customer.customer_ltb_notification {
  customer_ltb_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each LTB/EOL notification record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer receiving the LTB/EOL notification.']
  technology_roadmap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.technology_roadmap. Business justification: Last‑Time‑Buy notifications reference the technology roadmap to align product phase‑out with roadmap milestones for compliance reporting.']
  acknowledged_by_contact varchar [note: 'Name of the customer contact who acknowledged the notification.']
  acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'Current response state from the customer.']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the notification is subject to mandatory regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the notification record was first created in the system.']
  cross_reference_notes varchar [note: 'Notes on compatibility between the discontinued part and the replacement.']
  customer_ltb_notification_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the notification record.']
  eol_announcement_date date [note: 'Date the product\'s End‑of‑Life was officially announced to customers.']
  last_shipment_date date [note: 'Date of the final shipment of the discontinued product.']
  ltb_order_deadline date [note: 'Final date by which customers must place a Last‑Time‑Buy order.']
  ltb_order_reference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s internal order number for the LTB purchase.']
  ltb_quantity_committed varchar [note: 'Number of units the customer committed to purchase in the Last‑Time‑Buy.']
  migration_support_fae varchar [note: 'FAE assigned to assist the customer with migration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra information relevant to the notification.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Medium used to deliver the notification.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date the LTB/EOL notification was sent to the customer.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible unique code for the notification, used in communications and tracking.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the notification for handling.']
  product_part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number of the product being discontinued.']
  recommended_replacement_part varchar [note: 'Part number of the suggested replacement product.']
  regulatory_obligation varchar [note: 'Regulatory domain that drives the LTB/EOL requirement (e.g., automotive long‑term supply).']
  transition_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the customer\'s transition plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the notification record.']

  Note: 'Last Time Buy (LTB) and End-of-Life (EOL) customer notification records tracking communication of product discontinuation to affected customer accounts. Captures product part number, EOL announcement date, LTB order deadline, last shipment date, customer account, customer notification date, notification channel, customer acknowledgment status, acknowledged-by contact, LTB quantity committed by customer, LTB order reference, recommended replacement part, cross-reference compatibility notes, migration support FAE assignment, and customer transition plan status. SSOT for customer-facing EOL communication lifecycle. Supports regulatory traceability for automotive long-term supply obligations and customer transition planning.'
}

Table customer.tool_allocation {
  tool_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the tool_allocation association']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fab tool']
  allocation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the allocation ends or is scheduled to end']
  allocation_percentage decimal [note: 'Percentage of the fab tool\'s capacity allocated to the account']
  allocation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the allocation becomes effective']

  Note: 'Represents the allocation of fab tools to customer accounts for capacity planning, capturing the percentage of tool capacity assigned and the allocation period.. Existence Justification: Capacity planning teams actively allocate fab tools to customer accounts, recording the percentage of tool capacity assigned and the allocation period. A single account can be allocated multiple fab tools, and a single fab tool can serve multiple accounts simultaneously. The allocation records are managed as a distinct business entity.'
}

Table sales.opportunity {
  opportunity_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sales opportunity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account linked to the opportunity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing or design‑win campaign linked to the opportunity.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Opportunity Management process requires a primary contact person; linking to customer.contact enables accurate follow‑up and reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative owning the opportunity.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Opportunity pipeline dashboards aggregate by product family to forecast revenue.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Opportunity tracking for a specific chip design project is required for pipeline reporting and resource planning.']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: Opportunity cost estimation uses a specific process flow; linking enables accurate BOM, yield, and cycle‑time forecasts.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Opportunities are owned within a sales territory; FK replaces free‑text field for data integrity.']
  competitive_landscape varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of key competitors and market positioning for the opportunity.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Planned end or expiration date of the contract; nullable for open‑ended agreements.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the contract if the opportunity is won.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the contractual agreement associated with the opportunity.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the primary customer location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  end_market varchar [note: 'Primary market segment the opportunity addresses.']
  expected_close_date date [note: 'Projected date when the opportunity is expected to be closed (won or lost).']
  expected_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated discount or rebate amount applied to the gross revenue.']
  expected_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected gross revenue before discounts, taxes, or adjustments.']
  expected_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected net revenue after discounts and adjustments.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the opportunity record.']
  nre_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Potential NRE revenue associated with custom design or engineering services.']
  opportunity_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the opportunity, often reflecting the target product or project.']
  opportunity_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier assigned to the opportunity (e.g., OPP‑2024‑00123).']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price used for revenue calculations, expressed in the selected currency.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Pricing structure applied to the opportunity.']
  probability_percent varchar [note: 'Estimated probability (0‑100) that the opportunity will be won, based on stage and historical data.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region for the opportunity.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the sale is pursued.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current pipeline stage of the opportunity.']
  stage_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity last moved to its current stage.']
  target_application varchar [note: 'Intended end‑use or application for the product (e.g., automotive ADAS, AI accelerator).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., units, chips).']
  win_loss_date date [note: 'Date on which the opportunity outcome (win or loss) was recorded.']
  win_loss_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative reason why the opportunity was won or lost.']

  Note: 'Master record: Core sales opportunity tracking design-win campaigns, pipeline stages (Prospecting→Design-In→Design-Win→Production Ramp→Won/Lost), probability, and expected revenue for semiconductor IC, SoC, ASIC, and FPGA engagements. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Opportunity object. Captures end market (automotive, mobile, AI, IoT, computing), product family, target application, competitive landscape, NRE potential, design-win status, campaign grouping (campaign type, target segment, effectiveness metrics), and stage transition history for pipeline velocity analysis. SSOT for all sales pipeline activity including campaign-level aggregation for design-in programs, product launches, and competitive displacement initiatives.'
}

Table sales.quote {
  quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the quote record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or prospect receiving the quote.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Quote generation must record the exact customer contact authorizing the quote; this FK supports compliance and audit trails.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales rep who created the quote.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Quote header must be tied to a product family for contract and pricing governance.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Quotes are generated for design projects; linking enables financial forecasting of design spend.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — Every quote is generated in context of an opportunity — this is the core pipeline-to-commercial conversion link. Engineers need this to trace quote→opportunity for pipeline reporting.']
  packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to packaging.package_type. Business justification: Quote generation requires selecting a package type; linking ensures accurate pricing, compliance, and production planning.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.price_list.price_list_id — Quotes reference the applicable price list for base pricing. Essential for pricing compliance and margin analysis.']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: Quotes are generated based on a defined process flow; the link supports pricing, lead‑time, and qualification status.']
  quote_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — Quotes are generated in the context of an opportunity. This is the core pipeline-to-commercial link. Required for quote-to-win conversion tracking.']
  conversion_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote was converted to an order, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote record was first created in the system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the quote amounts.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'Additional delivery conditions beyond Incoterms, such as FOB destination or DAP warehouse.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the gross amount.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FOB|CFR|CIF|DAP|DDP — promote to reference product]']
  is_converted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quote has been converted to a sales order.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of calendar days from order receipt to delivery.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final amount payable after discount and tax.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition attached to the quote.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Catalog or part number of the quoted product.']
  quote_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote was issued to the customer.']
  quote_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the scope, special conditions, or notes for the quote.']
  quote_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the quotation, often used in communications and tracking.']
  quote_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the quote.']
  reason_lost varchar [note: 'Textual reason provided when a quote is marked as lost.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code representing the primary sales region for the quote.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tax calculated on the gross amount after discount.']
  total_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quoted amount before discounts, taxes, or adjustments.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per individual unit for the quoted product at the selected volume tier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the quote record.']
  valid_until date [note: 'Date after which the quote is no longer valid.']
  volume_tier varchar [note: 'Quantity bracket that determines unit pricing.']
  win_loss_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the quote after the decision period.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which the quoted terms become effective.']

  Note: 'Transaction record: Commercial price quotation issued to a customer or prospect for semiconductor products including ICs, packaged devices, wafer-level products, and IP licensing. Captures quoted unit price, volume tiers, lead time, validity period, incoterms, currency, and SAP SD quotation reference. Tracks quote-to-order conversion and win/loss outcomes.'
}

Table sales.quote_line {
  quote_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quote line record.']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.certification. Business justification: Quote lines must reference specific certifications (e.g., RoHS, ITAR) required by the customer; enables automated compliance checks during quoting.']
  design_ip_core_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ip_core. Business justification: Quote lines often represent IP core licensing; linking tracks IP revenue and compliance.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Enables quote‑level reporting and margin analysis by product family.']
  finished_good_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_good. Business justification: Quote line pricing and availability require linking to the specific finished good inventory record for SKU, enabling real-time stock checks during quoting.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Quote line items must reference the material master to obtain accurate part numbers, cost, and lead‑time for procurement planning, used in the Quote‑to‑Order workflow.']
  primary_quote_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent sales quote to which this line belongs.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Required for accurate pricing, inventory and compliance tracking in quote lines.']
  tertiary_quote_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.quote.quote_id — Header-detail relationship: quote_line cannot exist without its parent quote. Critical for quote value calculation and line-level pricing analysis.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Each line item (SKU) is associated with the test program used for its qualification; required for compliance reporting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center to which the line\'s revenue is allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quote line was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the quote (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  customer_requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the customer expects delivery of the quoted items.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the unit price.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the quoted terms become effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the quoted terms are no longer valid.']
  internal_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of internal sales or finance approval for the line.']
  is_custom boolean [note: 'True if the quoted SKU is a custom or engineered product.']
  is_price_locked boolean [note: 'True if the quoted price is locked and cannot be changed without re‑quote.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of calendar days from order to shipment.']
  line_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form comment entered by the sales rep for this line.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the quote for ordering.']
  net_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Unit price after discount, before tax.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Physical packaging of the die (e.g., BGA, QFN, WLCSP).']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier classification that determines discount levels.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the product requested in this line.']
  quote_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quote line.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the sale is pursued.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region associated with the quote.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the quote line (e.g., SAP, Salesforce).']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Any customer‑specific handling, packaging, or shipping notes.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary tax calculated for this line.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  total_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Gross amount for the line (unit_price * quantity) before tax.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Base price per unit before discounts, in the quote currency.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for quantity, typically \'pcs\' for pieces.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the quote line.']
  warranty_years varchar [note: 'Number of years of warranty offered for the product.']

  Note: 'Detail record: Individual line item within a sales quotation specifying a single semiconductor SKU, die, or IP core with its quoted quantity, unit price, discount, package type, lead time, and applicable pricing tier. Supports multi-line quotes covering mixed product families and end-market configurations.'
}

Table sales.sales_design_win {
  sales_design_win_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the design win record.']
  finished_good_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_good. Business justification: Design win commits to manufacturing a specific finished good; linking design win to the finished‑good record ties sales commitment to inventory production planning.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — A design win is the successful outcome of an opportunity. This link is critical for pipeline conversion metrics and is the most important FK in semiconductor sales.']
  primary_sales_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who selected the semiconductor device.']
  primary_sales_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the record.']
  primary_sales_sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the semiconductor device that won the design.']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: Design win records the process flow used for the winning design, required for revenue attribution and capacity planning.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales quote associated with the design win.']
  sales_nre_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Non-Recurring Engineering agreement linked to the design win.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Design‑win record must capture the test program that validated the winning design for warranty and support purposes.']
  competitor_displacement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0-100) representing the impact of displacing the competitor.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework applicable to the design win. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ITAR|EAR|RoHS|REACH|ISO9001|ISO14001|ISO45001 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the design win record.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Contract end date.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Contract start date.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of the contract in months.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design win record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for revenue amounts.']
  design_win_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the design win record.']
  displaced_competitor_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the competitor whose device was displaced.']
  displaced_device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the competitor\'s device that was displaced.']
  displacement_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the competitor\'s device was displaced.']
  estimated_annual_revenue_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected gross revenue per year from the design win before discounts or adjustments.']
  estimated_annual_revenue_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected net revenue after adjustments.']
  estimated_annual_unit_volume varchar [note: 'Projected number of units the customer will purchase per year.']
  export_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates if the device is subject to export control regulations.']
  forecast_accuracy decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage indicating forecast accuracy of the revenue estimate.']
  forecasted_ramp_rate_per_month decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected increase in unit volume per month during the ramp period.']
  is_key_account boolean [note: 'Indicates if the customer is a strategic key account.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the design win was last reviewed for compliance or forecast updates.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Industry segment of the end product.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about the design win.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Pricing approach applied to the design win.']
  region varchar [note: 'Global region of the customer.']
  revenue_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any discount, rebate, or adjustment applied to the gross revenue.']
  revenue_ramp_end_date date [note: 'End date of the revenue ramp period.']
  revenue_ramp_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the revenue ramp period.']
  sales_design_win_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the design win.']
  sales_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic or market territory responsible for the design win.']
  target_application varchar [note: 'End product or application where the winning device will be used (e.g., smartphone, automotive ECU).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the design win record.']
  win_source varchar [note: 'System or source where the design win was recorded.']
  win_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the design win was officially confirmed.']

  Note: 'Transaction record: Confirmed design-win record capturing the moment a customer selects a semiconductor device for integration into their end product (PCB, module, or SoC platform). Records the winning device, customer design reference, target application, estimated annual unit volume (AUV), revenue ramp timeline, and competitive displacement details (displaced competitor, displaced device, displacement reason). Critical KPI for semiconductor commercial teams. Represents the key conversion event from opportunity to committed design.'
}

Table sales.sales_nre_agreement {
  sales_nre_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the NRE agreement record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer party for whom the NRE work is performed.']
  chips_act_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.chips_act_obligation. Business justification: NRE agreements may be subject to CHIPS Act obligations; linking records the specific obligation tied to each agreement.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: NRE agreements are allocated to product families for cost recovery and reporting.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — NRE agreements are commercial vehicles arising from ASIC/custom IC opportunities. Required for NRE pipeline tracking.']
  primary_sales_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative owning the agreement.']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: NRE agreements are scoped to a specific process flow; linking enables milestone tracking against that flow.']
  research_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.program. Business justification: NRE Agreement Management ties each agreement to the specific research program funding the development effort.']
  actual_revenue_recognized decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue recognized to date according to accounting standards.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the NRE agreement, used in contracts and invoicing.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the NRE agreement type.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the milestone deliverable.']
  change_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a change order has been issued for the agreement.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the change order associated with the agreement.']
  completed_milestones varchar [note: 'Number of milestones that have been completed.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the agreement content.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the related SAP SD contract number.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the NRE amount.']
  deliverable_type varchar [note: 'Type of deliverable associated with the milestone.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the NRE agreement ends or expires; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the NRE agreement becomes binding.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'True if the agreement grants exclusive rights to the customer for the developed IP.']
  forecasted_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected revenue from the NRE agreement for forecasting purposes.']
  invoice_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone triggers an invoice generation.']
  ip_ownership_clause varchar [note: 'Specifies IP ownership rights granted by the agreement.']
  last_milestone_completed_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent milestone that has been completed.']
  milestone_actual_date date [note: 'Actual completion date recorded for the milestone.']
  milestone_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee associated with the milestone, invoiced upon completion.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Name of a specific NRE milestone (e.g., RTL Freeze, Tapeout).']
  milestone_planned_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for the milestone.']
  milestone_sequence varchar [note: 'Order of the milestone within the agreement schedule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments regarding the agreement.']
  nre_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contracted NRE fee for the agreement.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms governing invoicing of NRE fees.']
  risk_assessment_score varchar [note: 'Internal risk rating (1‑5) for the NRE project.']
  sales_nre_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the NRE agreement.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region associated with the agreement.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement was terminated.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided if the agreement is terminated before completion.']
  total_milestones varchar [note: 'Total number of milestones defined in the agreement.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the agreement record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the agreement record.']

  Note: 'Master record: Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) agreement governing custom IC or ASIC development engagements. Captures NRE fee structure, milestone payment schedule with individual billing/delivery milestones (RTL freeze, tapeout, first silicon, qualification), deliverables (RTL, GDS, PDK, tapeout), IP ownership terms, exclusivity clauses, and SAP SD contract reference. Contains milestone detail records tracking milestone name, planned/actual completion dates, milestone amounts, invoice trigger flags, approval status, and completion workflow. Enables NRE revenue recognition scheduling and project tracking. SSOT for all NRE commercial terms and milestone execution.'
}

Table sales.price_list {
  price_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price list record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or role that approved the price list.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Price lists are defined per product family to enforce consistent pricing policies.']
  packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to packaging.package_type. Business justification: Price list entries are defined per package type; FK replaces denormalized package_type column for consistency and audit.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Price lists are defined for specific market segments; FK to customer_segment supports pricing strategy and segment‑based pricing reports.']
  superseded_by_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a newer price list that supersedes this one.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Monetary amount of the price adjustment.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or justification for the price adjustment.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Type of price adjustment applied to the base price.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price list was approved.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Append-only log of changes to the price list record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price list record was created.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the price becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the price expires or is superseded.']
  end_market varchar [note: 'Target market segment (e.g., automotive, mobile, data center).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this price list is the default for its scope.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about the price list.']
  price_list_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the price list purpose and scope.']
  price_list_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the price list.']
  price_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price list.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Category of pricing defined by the list (e.g., standard list, distributor, OEM, spot).']
  pricing_condition_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD pricing condition record identifier.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the semiconductor product (e.g., part number).']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region for which the price list applies.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applicable to the price.']
  unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Base price per unit for the defined tier and product.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price list record.']
  validity_end date [note: 'End date of the price list\'s validity window.']
  validity_start date [note: 'Start date of the price list\'s validity window.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the price list.']
  volume_tier_max varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity for the volume pricing tier.']
  volume_tier_min varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity for the volume pricing tier.']

  Note: 'Master record: Master pricing repository defining standard and customer-specific pricing for semiconductor products by product family, package type, volume tier, and end market. Supports list price, distributor price, OEM price, and spot price variants. Contains price adjustment records capturing negotiated deviations, special bids, volume rebate adjustments, design-win incentives, and distributor markdowns with adjustment type, approved deviation amount, approval authority, validity windows, and SAP SD pricing condition override references. Integrates with SAP SD condition records and Salesforce CRM pricing engine. Tracks effective dates, supersession history, and full adjustment audit trail. SSOT for all semiconductor product pricing and price deviations.'
}

Table sales.customer_contract {
  customer_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the customer contract.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer party that holds the contract.']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.certification. Business justification: Contracts often stipulate mandatory product certifications; linking ensures contractual obligations are tracked against the certification registry.']
  customer_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — Long-term supply contracts often originate from won opportunities. Links contract terms back to the originating sales engagement.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Sales representative responsible for the contract.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — Contracts are the commercial outcome of won opportunities. Traceability needed for contract-to-opportunity lineage.']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Customer Contract records reference the R&D project that will deliver the custom chip, required for delivery planning and cost allocation.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Contracts often stipulate required test programs for delivered parts; linking ensures contractual compliance tracking.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the contract has been amended.']
  annual_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average yearly revenue expected from the contract.']
  arbitration_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether disputes are resolved via arbitration.']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the contract will auto‑renew at the end of its term.']
  confidentiality_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a confidentiality clause is present.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the contract for easy identification.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned by the customer or sales organization.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of the contract such as supply, service, license, or NRE.']
  contract_value_total decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of the contract over its full term.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure allowed for the customer.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal credit rating of the customer.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for all monetary amounts.']
  customer_contract_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the contract purpose and special conditions.']
  customer_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the base unit price.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or is terminated; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  eol_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an End‑of‑Life provision is part of the contract.']
  invoicing_frequency varchar [note: 'How often invoices are issued under the contract.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent contract amendment.']
  ltb_provision boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Last‑Time‑Buy clause is included.']
  max_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity per order allowed under the contract.']
  min_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity per order required by the contract.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition agreed with the customer.']
  pcn_obligation boolean [note: 'Specifies if the supplier must notify the customer of product changes.']
  pricing_terms varchar [note: 'Narrative description of pricing structure, discounts, and rebates.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Family of semiconductor products covered by the contract.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the contract renews automatically, manually, or not at all.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region associated with the contract.']
  supply_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of supply coverage: exclusive, multi‑source, or global.']
  technology_node varchar [note: 'Process technology node (e.g., 7nm, 5nm) applicable to the supplied products.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract is formally terminated.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days notice required before contract termination.']
  unit_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Base price per unit of product or service covered by the contract.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  volume_commitment varchar [note: 'Total quantity of units the customer commits to purchase over the contract term.']

  Note: 'Master record: Long-term commercial contract with a semiconductor customer covering supply commitments, pricing agreements, volume guarantees, last-time-buy (LTB) provisions, product change notification (PCN) obligations, and end-of-life (EOL) terms. Distinct from NRE agreements — governs ongoing product supply rather than development services. Linked to SAP SD outline agreements.'
}

Table sales.territory {
  territory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the sales territory.']
  parent_territory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent territory in the hierarchy, if any.']
  territory_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary sales representative responsible for the territory.']
  territory_fae_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the field application engineer covering the territory.']
  channel_tier varchar [note: 'Sales channel classification for the territory.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the primary country of the territory. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|CHN|JPN|KOR|... — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the territory record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the territory definition expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the territory definition becomes effective for sales activities.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the quota targets apply.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Delimited path representing the territory\'s position in the global→regional→country→district hierarchy (e.g., "Global>EMEA>Germany>Berlin").']
  is_overlay boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the territory is an overlay (true) used for specialist coverage in addition to primary territories.']
  multi_rep_coverage boolean [note: 'True if the territory is covered by multiple sales representatives or FAEs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the territory.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the broader region (e.g., EMEA, APAC, AMER) to which the territory belongs.']
  revenue_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fiscal period revenue quota assigned to the territory (in corporate currency).']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to uniquely reference the territory in external systems.']
  territory_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the territory.']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the sales territory.']
  territory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the territory.']
  territory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory based on its purpose or focus.']
  unit_target_quantity varchar [note: 'Target number of units (e.g., die, wafers) to be sold from the territory for the fiscal period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the territory record.']

  Note: 'Master record: Sales territory definition and quota management assigning geographic regions, end markets, or named accounts to sales representatives and field application engineers (FAEs). Captures territory code, region hierarchy (global→regional→country→district), assigned sales rep, FAE coverage, revenue and unit targets by fiscal period (quarterly, half-year, annual), quota amounts, stretch targets, attainment tracking, and effective dates. Includes account-to-territory assignment details with assignment type (direct, distribution, rep firm), primary/secondary coverage flags, channel tier, and multi-rep coverage models. Supports territory realignment, overlay models (product specialists, strategic account managers), quota assignment workflows, and sales performance management common in semiconductor distribution channels. Used for compensation planning and quarterly business review (QBR) reporting.'
}

Table sales.sales_forecast {
  sales_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the forecast record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer for whom the forecast is created.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the forecast.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Capacity Planning compares forecasted demand with each fab tool\'s capacity; the Forecast‑vs‑Capacity report needs this FK.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Forecasts are produced per product family for capacity planning and financial reporting.']
  process_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_technology_node. Business justification: Demand forecasts are created per technology node; linking provides node‑specific capacity and yield assumptions.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Forecasts are created for a specific sales territory; replace string with FK to territory for referential integrity.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the forecast was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the forecast.']
  bias decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average signed deviation of forecast from actuals.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Confidence rating assigned to the forecast.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  effective_end date [note: 'Last day of the period the forecast covers.']
  effective_start date [note: 'First day of the period the forecast covers.']
  end_market varchar [note: 'Target market segment of the forecast.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period identifier (e.g., FY2025Q1).']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the forecast (demand, supply, or capacity).']
  forecast_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the forecast (e.g., F2025Q1-001).']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the forecast.']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast scenario (commit, upside, or best‑case).']
  geography varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the forecast geography.']
  horizon_months varchar [note: 'Length of the forecast horizon expressed in months.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the forecast is locked from further edits.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of the forecast.']
  last_reviewer varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the last review.']
  mape decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical measure of forecast accuracy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale for the forecast.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Projected unit volume for the forecast period.']
  revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected revenue amount for the forecast period.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Named scenario associated with the forecast.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the forecast data.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the forecast was submitted for review.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for forecast_quantity.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the forecast.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the forecast record.']
  variance_to_actual decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between forecast and actual results.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for the forecast record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the forecast.']

  Note: 'Transaction record: Sales demand forecast capturing expected unit volume and revenue by product family, customer, end market, geography, and fiscal period. Supports rolling 12-month and 18-month forecast horizons used in semiconductor S&OP and wafer-start authorization processes. Tracks forecast version (commit, upside, best-case), submission date, confidence level, and variance against actuals. Integrates with SAP APO/IBP demand planning. Enables forecast accuracy KPI tracking (MAPE, bias) critical for fab capacity planning decisions.'
}

Table sales.booking {
  booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the booking record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who placed the booking.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination location for shipment.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.ar_invoice. Business justification: REQUIRED: Booking‑to‑invoice reconciliation report; invoices are generated from bookings for order fulfillment and revenue recognition.']
  booking_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — Bookings fulfill opportunities. This link closes the pipeline-to-revenue loop essential for forecast accuracy measurement.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost accounting allocates each sales booking to a finance cost center to track expenses and calculate profitability.']
  customer_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing sales contract or NRE agreement.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal sales representative owning the booking.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Required for export‑control compliance; each shipment (booking) must be tied to the export license covering the commodities and destination.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Production Order Scheduling assigns each booking to a specific fab tool for wafer fabrication; required for the Production Allocation Report.']
  final_test_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.final_test_run. Business justification: Production booking is released only after a final test run passes; FK records which test run cleared the batch.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Booking must reference the exact IC catalog entry to drive manufacturing and compliance checks.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Booking of customer orders triggers allocation of a wafer lot; linking booking to the wafer lot enables production scheduling and traceability.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — Bookings represent the commercial commitment arising from won opportunities. Essential for opportunity-to-revenue traceability.']
  packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to packaging.package_type. Business justification: Booking (order fulfillment) must specify the package type to be produced; needed for manufacturing scheduling and regulatory reporting.']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: Bookings are fulfilled using a defined process flow; the link drives production scheduling and yield tracking.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit‑and‑loss reporting ties booking revenue to a profit center for accurate profit analysis.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for Order Fulfillment process: each sales booking generates a purchase order to a supplier, enabling end‑to‑end order‑to‑cash and procure‑to‑pay tracking.']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Production bookings are linked to a tapeout to schedule wafer fabrication and track yield.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Bookings are tied to a sales territory; FK enables consistent territory hierarchy and reporting.']
  backlog_flag boolean [note: 'True if the booking is currently in backlog (unfulfilled).']
  booked_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units (dies) committed in the booking.']
  booked_revenue_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue amount before discounts and taxes, in the booking currency.']
  booked_revenue_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue after discounts and before tax.']
  booking_number varchar [note: 'External business number assigned to the booking, used in sales and finance processes.']
  booking_source varchar [note: 'Origin system that created the booking record.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the booking.']
  booking_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the booking was created in the source system.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by sales or operations.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the booking with internal and regulatory rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the booking amounts.']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Preferred logistics mode for shipment.']
  design_win_fk varchar [note: 'FK to sales.design_win.design_win_id — In semiconductor sales, bookings trace to the design-win that generated the demand. Critical for design-win revenue attribution.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the gross revenue.']
  external_order_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the original sales order in SAP SD.']
  forecast_flag boolean [note: 'True if the booking is included in the sales forecast.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the booking is deemed critical for product launch or revenue targets.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the booking (e.g., standard sale, NRE, design‑win).']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the customer.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Pricing approach applied to the booking.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the booking.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'High‑level product family (e.g., ASIC, FPGA, SoC) for the booked part.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the booking must be reported under specific regulatory frameworks (e.g., CHIPS Act).']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the customer expects the shipment to be delivered.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date when the booked revenue is recognized for accounting.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region for the booking.']
  ship_to_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the ship‑to location.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the net revenue.']
  total_tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code used for calculating tax_amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the booking record.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Warranty duration provided to the customer, in months.']

  Note: 'Transaction record: Confirmed sales booking representing a firm customer purchase order commitment for semiconductor devices. Captures booking date, booked revenue, booked units, device part number, customer account, ship-to location, requested delivery date, backlog status, and SAP SD sales order reference. Represents the commercial booking event — distinct from shipment or invoice. SSOT for bookings-to-billings (B2B) ratio, book-to-ship analysis, and revenue waterfall reporting critical to semiconductor quarterly earnings.'
}

Table sales.lead {
  lead_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lead record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account associated with the lead.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign associated with the lead.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative owning the lead.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Lead qualification and routing depend on the product family of interest.']
  lead_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — Leads convert to opportunities upon qualification. This FK enables lead-to-opportunity conversion funnel analysis.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.opportunity.opportunity_id — Leads convert to opportunities — this link tracks the conversion funnel. Essential for lead source ROI analysis.']
  process_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_technology_node. Business justification: Leads are qualified against a target technology node; the link supports early feasibility analysis.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Leads are assigned to a sales territory; FK provides proper linkage to territory hierarchy.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the lead\'s organization address.']
  company_industry varchar [note: 'Industry classification of the lead\'s organization.']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization associated with the lead.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the lead is subject to any regulatory compliance considerations (e.g., ITAR).']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the lead contact.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person for the lead.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number of the lead contact.']
  conversion_date date [note: 'Date when the lead was converted to an opportunity or closed.']
  conversion_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the lead conversion attempt.']
  country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the lead\'s organization.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lead was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the estimated revenue.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level of the lead data as per corporate policy.']
  device_interest varchar [note: 'Specific type of semiconductor device the lead is interested in (e.g., ASIC, FPGA, SoC).']
  estimated_quantity varchar [note: 'Projected number of units the lead may purchase.']
  estimated_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected monetary value of the lead opportunity.']
  expected_close_date date [note: 'Projected date by which the lead is expected to be closed.']
  is_nre boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the lead is for a Non‑Recurring Engineering (NRE) project.']
  itar_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the lead involves technology subject to ITAR regulations.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the lead record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the lead record.']
  lead_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the lead as used in sales systems.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the lead was captured.']
  lead_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lead within the sales pipeline.']
  lead_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lead based on sales process stage.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Target market segment for the lead (e.g., Consumer, Automotive, Data Center).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or observations about the lead.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the lead for follow‑up.']
  rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the lead\'s potential.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region of the lead (e.g., APAC, EMEA, AMER).']
  score varchar [note: 'Numerical score representing lead quality based on qualification criteria.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the lead originated (e.g., Salesforce, web portal).']
  target_application varchar [note: 'Intended application area for the semiconductor product (e.g., mobile, automotive, AI).']
  technology_node varchar [note: 'Process technology node of interest (e.g., 7nm, 5nm).']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the lead\'s organization address.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the lead record.']

  Note: 'Transaction record: Early-stage sales lead representing an unqualified potential customer or design opportunity. Captures lead source, contact information, target application, device interest, estimated opportunity size, lead status, and conversion outcome. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Lead object. Tracks lead-to-opportunity conversion funnel for semiconductor design-in campaigns.'
}

Table sales.channel_partner {
  channel_partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the channel partner record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Partner‑related operational costs are tracked in a dedicated finance cost center for cost allocation.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Distribution agreements require linking each channel partner to its upstream supplier(s) for inventory allocation and contractual compliance.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the partner\'s primary business address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the partner\'s primary business address (optional).']
  authorized_product_lines varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of product families the partner may sell.']
  channel_partner_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the partner relationship.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the partner\'s primary address.']
  contract_effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the partner contract becomes effective.']
  contract_effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the partner contract expires or is scheduled to terminate (null if open‑ended).']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the master agreement governing the partner relationship.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the partner contract.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the partner\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the channel partner record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  external_partner_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used for the partner in external systems (e.g., third‑party distributor portal).']
  hub_consignment_level varchar [note: 'Level of consignment inventory maintained at partner hubs.']
  inventory_reporting_obligation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner must provide regular inventory visibility to Semiconductors.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the record was last reviewed for compliance or data quality.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal entity name as registered for contracts and compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for internal comments, observations, or special handling instructions.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the channel partner organization.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Category of the partner based on its business model and relationship to Semiconductors.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the partner\'s primary address.']
  price_protection_claims_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if the partner can submit price‑protection claims under the agreement.']
  pricing_agreement_type varchar [note: 'Pricing model governing the partner\'s purchase terms.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address used for official communications with the partner.']
