  delivery_date_revised date [note: 'The new confirmed delivery date after the amendment is applied. Populated for date_push_out and date_pull_in amendment types. Drives updated production scheduling, wafer start authorization, and customer delivery commitments.']
  dispute_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of any associated customer dispute or claim raised in Salesforce CRM that prompted or resulted from this amendment. Links the amendment to the customer dispute resolution workflow for escalation tracking.']
  export_control_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the amendment (particularly product substitution or destination change) requires re-review under export control regulations (EAR/ITAR). Ensures compliance with BIS/EAR and ITAR before the revised order is fulfilled.']
  original_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of the amended field before the change was applied. Stored as STRING to accommodate heterogeneous amendment types (quantity, date, price, product code). Paired with revised_value and amended_field_name to form a complete before/after audit record.']
  pcn_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Product Change Notification (PCN) that triggered this amendment, if applicable. Links the order amendment to a formal PCN issued under JEDEC JESD46 for product or process changes affecting customer orders.']
  quantity_original decimal(18,4) [note: 'The ordered quantity on the sales order line before the amendment was applied. Populated specifically for quantity_change amendment types. Enables direct numeric comparison and delta calculation for backlog management and wafer start authorization adjustments.']
  quantity_revised decimal(18,4) [note: 'The ordered quantity on the sales order line after the amendment is applied. Populated for quantity_change amendment types. Used for backlog recalculation, wafer start authorization updates, and allocation management.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity fields on this amendment. Common semiconductor units include EA (each), KU (thousands of units), WAFER, DIE, and REEL for tape-and-reel packaged devices.']
  requested_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the individual (internal sales representative, customer service agent, or customer contact) who initiated the amendment request. Supports audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  revenue_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net change in order value resulting from this amendment, expressed in the order currency. Positive for upward revisions (pull-in, price increase), negative for reductions (push-out, cancellation, price decrease). Used directly in revenue recognition and backlog reporting.']
  revenue_recognition_period varchar [note: 'The accounting period (YYYY-MM) in which the revenue impact of this amendment is recognized under ASC 606 / IFRS 15. Assigned by the finance team during amendment approval and used for period-close revenue reporting.']
  revised_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The new value of the amended field after the change is applied. Stored as STRING to accommodate heterogeneous amendment types. Paired with original_value and amended_field_name to form a complete before/after audit record.']
  so_line_item_number varchar [note: 'The specific line item number within the parent sales order that this amendment affects. An amendment may target a single line (e.g., quantity change on one SKU) or the entire order header (e.g., cancellation). Null if the amendment applies to the full order.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this amendment record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking across SAP SD, Oracle Agile PLM, Salesforce CRM, and manual entry channels.']
  substitute_product_code varchar [note: 'The product part number or material code of the replacement product, populated only when amendment_type is \'product_substitution\'. Captures the new IC, SoC, or packaged device being substituted in place of the original ordered product.']
  to_sales_order varchar [note: 'FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Amendments modify a specific sales order. Required for change management audit trail.']
  unit_price_original decimal(18,6) [note: 'The unit selling price on the sales order line before the price revision amendment. Populated for price_revision amendment types. Supports revenue recognition delta calculation and customer dispute resolution.']
  unit_price_revised decimal(18,6) [note: 'The new unit selling price after the price revision amendment is applied. Populated for price_revision amendment types. Used for revenue recognition recalculation and updated order value reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to this amendment record in the data platform. Supports incremental load detection and change data capture in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version counter for amendments on the same sales order line, starting at 1. Enables tracking of iterative changes (e.g., multiple date push-outs on the same line) and supports optimistic concurrency control in the Silver layer.']
  wafer_start_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment requires a corresponding update to the Wafer Start Authorization (WSA) in the fab. True when quantity changes or cancellations affect in-flight WIP lots in the fabrication facility.']

  Note: 'Record of all changes made to a sales order after initial entry, capturing the amendment type (quantity change, date push-out, pull-in, cancellation, price revision, product substitution), original value, revised value, amendment date, requester, approver, and business justification. Supports change order management workflows and provides a complete audit trail of order modifications for revenue recognition and customer dispute resolution.'
}

Table order.nre_order {
  nre_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the NRE order record. Primary key for the NRE order entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who contracted this NRE engagement. The party responsible for NRE payment and acceptance.']
  fabrication_photomask_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the photomask set fabricated as part of this NRE engagement. Links NRE to specific mask tooling costs.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IC design project that this NRE order funds. Links NRE charges to specific design engineering work.']
  nre_sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated production sales order that this NRE order supports. Links NRE charges to the recurring product order.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'FK to order.order.order_id — NRE orders are linked to associated production orders for the same customer engagement.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal engineering team assigned to execute this NRE project. Used for resource allocation and capacity planning.']
  primary_nre_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project manager responsible for NRE execution and customer communication. Primary point of contact for project status.']
  sales_nre_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_nre_agreement. Business justification: NRE orders are executed under an NRE agreement; linking is required for compliance and revenue recognition.']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Contractually defined criteria that must be met for customer acceptance of NRE deliverables. Used for milestone signoff and dispute resolution.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when all NRE deliverables were completed and accepted. Triggers final milestone billing and project closure.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when engineering work began. Used to track schedule adherence and project performance.']
  cancellation_clause varchar [note: 'Contractual terms governing NRE order cancellation, including notice periods, cancellation fees, and work-in-progress settlement.']
  chips_act_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NRE project qualifies for CHIPS Act funding or incentives. Used for government reporting and subsidy tracking.']
  completed_milestone_count varchar [note: 'Number of milestones that have been completed and accepted by the customer. Used to calculate project completion percentage.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of confidentiality requirements for NRE deliverables and design information. Determines access controls and disclosure restrictions.']
  contract_date date [note: 'Date when the NRE contract was signed and became legally binding. Establishes the start of contractual obligations.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Commercial structure of the NRE contract. Determines billing method and risk allocation between customer and supplier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NRE order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for NRE amounts. Defines the monetary unit for all financial values in this order.']
  customer_po_number varchar [note: 'Customer\'s purchase order number authorizing the NRE engagement. Used for invoice reconciliation and payment tracking.']
  deliverable_list varchar [note: 'Comprehensive list of engineering deliverables committed in the NRE contract. Includes design files, documentation, test reports, and IP cores.']
  design_complexity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative measure of IC design complexity based on gate count, IP blocks, and verification requirements. Used for NRE pricing and resource estimation.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export Administration Regulations classification for NRE deliverables. Determines export licensing requirements and destination restrictions.']
  invoiced_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount invoiced to date across all completed milestones. Used to track billing progress and outstanding receivables.']
  ip_core_list varchar [note: 'List of third-party or internal IP cores integrated as part of this NRE engagement. Tracks IP licensing and royalty obligations.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NRE engagement involves ITAR-controlled defense articles or technical data. Triggers strict export and access controls.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this NRE order record. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  milestone_count varchar [note: 'Total number of engineering milestones defined in this NRE contract. Used for progress tracking and billing schedule management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about the NRE engagement. Used for knowledge capture and handoff.']
  nre_order_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the NRE order. Externally communicated reference number used in contracts and invoices.']
  nre_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the NRE order. Tracks progression from contract approval through engineering completion and invoicing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|in_progress|milestone_review|completed|invoiced|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nre_to_sales_order varchar [note: 'FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — NRE orders are associated with a customer engagement that typically has a parent sales order or contract reference.']
  nre_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the NRE engagement by engineering deliverable category. Determines the type of one-time engineering work being performed.']
  paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount received from customer to date. Used to track payment status and outstanding balances.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code defining milestone payment schedule and due dates. References master payment terms configuration.']
  pdk_version varchar [note: 'Version of the Process Design Kit used for this NRE design work. Ensures design-manufacturing compatibility.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Contractual penalties for late delivery or non-conformance to acceptance criteria. Defines financial consequences of schedule or quality failures.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Contractually committed date for completion of all NRE deliverables. Used for customer expectation management and penalty clauses.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for engineering work to commence. Used for resource planning and project scheduling.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to recognize NRE revenue. Determines timing of revenue booking based on contract structure and completion criteria.']
  technology_node varchar [note: 'Semiconductor process technology node for this NRE engagement (e.g., 7nm, 5nm, 3nm). Determines design rules and manufacturing process.']
  total_nre_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contracted value of the NRE engagement across all milestones. Represents the full one-time engineering charge.']

  Note: 'Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) order record capturing one-time engineering charges associated with custom IC design, mask set fabrication, or process qualification for a specific customer. Captures NRE type (mask NRE, design NRE, qualification NRE), total NRE amount, deliverable list, acceptance criteria, and linkage to the associated production order or design project. Includes milestone records defining specific engineering deliverables and payment triggers: milestone name, description, planned completion date, actual completion date, milestone amount, billing status, customer acceptance date, and acceptance signoff reference. Supports milestone-based revenue recognition for NRE contracts and tracks engineering project progress against contractual commitments. Distinct from recurring product orders due to milestone-based billing and engineering deliverable tracking.'
}

Table order.order_nre_milestone {
  order_nre_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the NRE milestone record. Primary key for the order_nre_milestone product.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project manager or technical lead responsible for coordinating and delivering this milestone.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent NRE order under which this milestone is defined. Links milestone to the contractual order.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the engineering team or organizational unit responsible for delivering this milestone.']
  acceptance_signoff_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the customer representative who provided formal acceptance signoff for the milestone.']
  acceptance_signoff_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal customer acceptance document, email, or system record confirming milestone approval.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the milestone deliverable was completed and submitted for customer review.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when engineering work on this milestone commenced.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current billing and payment status of the milestone, tracking progression from acceptance through payment receipt.']
  contract_modification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone has been modified from the original contract terms through a formal change order or amendment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this milestone record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the milestone amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).']
  customer_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date when the customer formally accepted the milestone deliverable, triggering payment and revenue recognition.']
  deliverable_location varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or repository location where the milestone deliverable artifacts are stored.']
  dependency_milestones varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of prerequisite milestone numbers or identifiers that must be completed before this milestone can begin.']
  effort_hours_actual decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual engineering effort in hours expended to complete this milestone, tracked for cost accounting and future estimation.']
  effort_hours_planned decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated engineering effort in hours required to complete this milestone, used for resource planning and cost estimation.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date when the invoice for this milestone was issued to the customer.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice document generated for this milestone billing.']
  milestone_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual payment amount associated with this milestone in the order currency. Represents the revenue to be recognized upon milestone acceptance.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the engineering deliverable, acceptance criteria, and scope of work associated with this milestone.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Short descriptive name of the milestone deliverable (e.g., RTL Design Completion, Tapeout Readiness, First Silicon Validation).']
  milestone_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the milestone within the NRE contract. Typically sequential or hierarchical (e.g., M1, M2.1, MS-001).']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the milestone indicating progress and acceptance state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|in_progress|completed|accepted|rejected|on_hold|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the milestone by engineering phase or deliverable category.']
  modification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract change order or amendment document that modified this milestone terms, scope, or payment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, clarifications, or context regarding milestone execution, issues, or customer interactions.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment for this milestone is contractually due from the customer.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Actual date when payment for this milestone was received from the customer.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Contractually committed or planned date by which the milestone deliverable is expected to be completed.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when engineering work on this milestone is planned to begin.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation provided by the customer if the milestone deliverable was rejected, including specific deficiencies or gaps.']
  revenue_recognition_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual amount of revenue recognized for this milestone, which may differ from milestone_amount due to contract modifications or adjustments.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date when revenue for this milestone was recognized in the financial system, typically aligned with customer acceptance date per ASC 606.']
  rework_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether engineering rework is required to address customer feedback or rejection reasons.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of technical or schedule risk associated with achieving this milestone on time and meeting acceptance criteria.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this milestone within the NRE order, defining the execution sequence and dependencies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this milestone record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Individual milestone record within an NRE order, defining specific engineering deliverables and associated payment triggers. Captures milestone name, description, planned completion date, actual completion date, milestone amount, billing status, customer acceptance date, and acceptance signoff reference. Supports milestone-based revenue recognition for NRE contracts and tracks engineering project progress against contractual commitments.'
}

Table order.rma {
  rma_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for rma']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer initiating the return. Links RMA to the customer master record.']
  failure_analysis_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the failure analysis record if FA was performed. Links RMA to detailed root cause investigation and corrective action.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality inspector or technician who performed the incoming inspection of the returned material.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing lot from which the returned units originated. Critical for traceability and yield impact analysis.']
  rma_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to order.order.order_id — RMAs reference the original sales order for the returned product. rma.sales_order_id → order.sales_order_id.']
  rma_replacement_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the replacement sales order created to ship substitute product to the customer. Nullable if disposition is not replacement.']
  rma_sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original sales order from which the returned product was shipped. Links RMA to the originating order transaction.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product master record for the returned semiconductor product (IC, SoC, ASIC, FPGA, or packaged die).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the RMA was approved by the quality or customer service team. Nullable if RMA is rejected or still pending.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the RMA was fully closed after disposition action completed (credit issued, replacement shipped, or material scrapped).']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal corrective action (CAPA) is required based on the RMA findings. True triggers CAPA workflow per ISO 9001 and IATF 16949.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RMA record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the credit issued to the customer in the transaction currency. Nullable if no credit is issued.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the credit memo issued to the customer for the returned material. Nullable if no credit is issued (e.g., customer error returns).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, CNY).']
  customer_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the customer contact for RMA correspondence, status updates, and resolution communication.']
  customer_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the customer representative who initiated or is managing the RMA on the customer side. Used for communication and escalation.']
  customer_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the customer contact for urgent RMA inquiries or escalation.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the defect or issue reported by the customer. Captures detailed failure symptoms, visual observations, or functional anomalies.']
  disposition_instruction varchar [note: 'Action to be taken with the returned material. Determines downstream workflow (credit memo, replacement order, FA request, or scrap authorization).']
  dppm_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this RMA should be counted in DPPM calculations. True if the defect is attributable to manufacturing or design (excludes customer-induced damage).']
  export_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned product is subject to export control regulations (ITAR, EAR). True requires special handling and documentation.']
  failure_analysis_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal failure analysis (FA) has been requested for the returned units. True triggers FA workflow and lab assignment.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the incoming inspection. Determines whether the return claim is valid and informs disposition and credit decisions.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and comments for cross-functional coordination (quality, engineering, customer service). Not shared with customer.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RMA record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking and data lineage.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned product is REACH compliant. Affects material handling, disposal, and rework procedures.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date when the returned material was physically received at the receiving facility or OSAT partner. Nullable until material arrives.']
  receiving_facility_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the facility (FAB, OSAT, warehouse, or service center) where the returned material is received and inspected.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the customer initiated the RMA request. Principal business event timestamp for the RMA lifecycle.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the reason for the return. Used for root cause analysis, DPPM tracking, and warranty claim classification.']
  return_shipping_carrier varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier used to ship the returned material (e.g., FedEx, UPS, DHL, regional freight forwarder).']
  return_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier tracking number for the return shipment. Used for shipment visibility and proof of receipt.']
  returned_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units (dies, packaged chips, or wafers) returned under this RMA. Used for DPPM calculation and inventory adjustment.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the RMA. Used in customer communications, shipping labels, and cross-system tracking.']
  rma_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RMA. Tracks progression from customer request through receipt, inspection, and final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|approved|rejected|in_transit|received|inspected|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned product is RoHS compliant. Affects disposition options (scrap vs. rework) and environmental handling requirements.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the root cause identified during inspection or FA. Used for DPPM trending and corrective action prioritization.']
  to_original_order varchar [note: 'FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — RMAs reference the original sales order for the returned product. Required for credit processing and quality traceability.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the returned quantity. EA=each (individual die/chip), WFR=wafer, LOT=production lot, KIT=assembly kit.']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this RMA is being processed as a warranty claim. True if the return is within warranty period and defect is covered.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the warranty coverage for the returned product expires. Used to validate warranty claim eligibility.']

  Note: 'RMA (Return Material Authorization) record managing customer returns of semiconductor products for quality issues, shipping damage, or end-of-life returns. Captures RMA number, return reason code, defect description, returned product and quantity, original order reference, disposition instruction (scrap, rework, credit, replacement), credit memo reference, and failure analysis request flag. SSOT for customer return workflows and DPPM tracking inputs.'
}

Table order.blanket_order {
  blanket_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the blanket purchase agreement record in the Databricks Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer (OEM, automotive Tier-1, or distributor) who has entered into this blanket purchase agreement.']
  customer_design_win_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the design win record in Salesforce CRM that originated this blanket order. Links the long-term supply commitment back to the customer design-in event and program qualification milestone.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the account manager or field sales representative who owns the customer relationship and negotiated this blanket agreement.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP sales organization responsible for managing and fulfilling this blanket order agreement.']
  agreed_unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'The contractually agreed price per unit for the product under this blanket order. May be a single fixed price or the base tier price when pricing tiers are defined. Expressed in the contract currency.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the blanket order agreement type. Scheduling agreements define delivery cadence; quantity contracts commit to total volume; value contracts commit to total spend; MPW (Multi-Project Wafer) blankets cover shuttle run commitments; distributor frame agreements govern stocking distributor relationships.']
  allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking (lower value = higher priority) used during constrained supply allocation to determine which blanket orders receive preferential wafer starts and finished goods inventory. Automotive and safety-critical programs typically receive highest priority.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the blanket order agreement was formally approved by the authorized signatory (e.g., VP of Sales or Finance Controller) and transitioned from draft to active status. Required for SOX revenue recognition controls.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this blanket order agreement is configured to automatically renew for an additional contract period upon expiry if neither party issues a termination notice within the agreed notice window.']
  blanket_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric identifier for the blanket purchase agreement, used in customer-facing communications, SAP SD scheduling agreements, and ERP cross-references. Follows the BO- prefix convention.']
  calloff_frequency varchar [note: 'The agreed cadence at which the customer issues individual release orders (call-offs) against this blanket agreement. Drives wafer start planning, fab scheduling, and inventory build strategy.']
  chips_act_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product or program covered by this blanket order qualifies for US CHIPS and Science Act incentives or domestic content requirements. Relevant for US government-funded programs and domestic semiconductor supply chain initiatives.']
  committed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total quantity of units (dies, packaged ICs, or wafers) the customer has committed to purchase over the full contract period. This is the binding volume commitment underpinning the blanket agreement.']
  contract_duration_months varchar [note: 'Total duration of the blanket order agreement expressed in calendar months. Standard semiconductor blanket agreements run 12 or 24 months; automotive long-term supply agreements may extend to 36 months.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this blanket order record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA SD). Represents the business event of agreement entry, not the ETL load time. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this blanket order (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, KRW, TWD). Semiconductor contracts are predominantly denominated in USD.']
  customer_contract_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s own purchase contract or frame agreement reference number as provided on the customer\'s procurement documentation. Used for cross-referencing with the customer\'s ERP or procurement system.']
  customer_part_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s own part number or device designation for the product covered by this blanket agreement. Required for cross-reference on customer purchase orders and shipping documents.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which this blanket order is fulfilled. Direct: sold directly to end customer; Distributor: fulfilled via authorized stocking distributor; Rep Firm: managed through manufacturer\'s representative; OEM: original equipment manufacturer direct program; EMS: electronics manufacturing services partner.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the blanket order agreement becomes legally binding and call-off releases may begin. Marks the start of the contract period, typically aligned with the customer\'s fiscal year or program start.']
  end_market_segment varchar [note: 'The primary end-market application segment for the product covered by this blanket order. Used for revenue segmentation, demand planning, and strategic reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automotive|mobile|computing|industrial|iot|ai_datacenter|aerospace_defense|consumer|networking|medical — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the blanket order agreement expires and no further call-off releases may be issued. Typically 12 to 24 months from the effective date for semiconductor OEM and automotive Tier-1 agreements.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export Administration Regulations (EAR) Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) for the product covered by this blanket order. Determines whether an export license is required for shipment to the customer\'s country. Critical for semiconductor products subject to BIS/EAR controls.']
  export_license_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an export license is required to ship product under this blanket order to the customer\'s destination country based on the ECCN and end-use classification. Triggers compliance review workflow when True.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between Semiconductors and the customer for shipments under this blanket agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product or technology covered by this blanket order is subject to International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) controls. When True, additional compliance approvals and end-use certificates are required before shipment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent update to this blanket order record in the source system. Tracks amendments to pricing, quantities, dates, or terms. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  last_release_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent call-off release order issued against this blanket agreement. Used to monitor customer ordering activity, identify dormant blankets, and trigger account management follow-up.']
  lead_time_weeks varchar [note: 'The agreed manufacturing and delivery lead time in weeks from call-off release order placement to customer delivery. Encompasses wafer fab cycle time, assembly and test (OSAT), and logistics. Critical for wafer start authorization scheduling.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum quantity per individual call-off release order as agreed in the blanket contract. Enforces production lot efficiency and wafer start economics. Expressed in the same unit of measure as committed_quantity.']
  ncnr_coverage_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the committed quantity or blanket value that is subject to NCNR terms. A value of 100 means the entire commitment is NCNR; partial NCNR coverage is common in distributor agreements.']
  ncnr_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the blanket order is subject to Non-Cancellable Non-Returnable (NCNR) terms. When True, the customer cannot cancel or return product once a release order is issued. Standard for automotive and high-volume OEM semiconductor supply agreements.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the blanket order agreement. Draft: under negotiation; Active: binding and open for call-offs; On Hold: temporarily suspended; Partially Released: some call-off orders issued; Fully Released: all committed quantity has been called off; Closed: contract period ended; Cancelled: terminated before expiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|active|on_hold|partially_released|fully_released|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms code defining the invoice due date, early payment discount conditions, and payment schedule applicable to release orders issued under this blanket agreement (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30).']
  pricing_tier_structure varchar [note: 'Defines the pricing model applied to this blanket order. Fixed: single price for all quantities; Volume Tiered: unit price decreases at defined quantity thresholds; Time Tiered: price adjusts at defined calendar intervals; Milestone Tiered: price adjusts upon reaching program milestones such as design-in or production ramp.']
  product_part_number varchar [note: 'The Semiconductors internal part number (device identifier) for the IC, SoC, ASIC, or FPGA product covered by this blanket order. Aligns with the product master in SAP MM and Oracle Agile PLM.']
  quantity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the committed and released quantities. EA = each (packaged unit); WFR = wafer; DIE = bare die; REEL = tape-and-reel packaging unit; TRAY = tray packaging unit. Aligns with SAP MM unit of measure configuration.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product and its packaging materials comply with EU REACH Regulation (EC No 1907/2006) requirements for chemical substance registration and restriction. Required for EU market access.']
  release_order_to_blanket varchar [note: 'FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Individual sales orders (release orders) are created against blanket orders. The sales order must reference its parent blanket for volume tracking.']
  released_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative quantity that has been called off against this blanket order through individual release orders to date. Used to track consumption of the committed volume and compute remaining open quantity.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days prior to the expiry date by which either party must provide written notice to prevent automatic renewal or to initiate renegotiation of the blanket agreement terms.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product shipped under this blanket order is compliant with the EU RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) restricting hazardous substances in electronic equipment. Mandatory for EU market shipments.']
  sales_order_to_blanket varchar [note: 'FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Individual sales orders are call-offs against blanket orders. This link is critical for blanket quantity consumption tracking and volume commitment management.']
  ship_to_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary customer delivery destination under this blanket agreement. Used for export control classification, logistics routing, and trade compliance screening.']
  total_blanket_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the blanket order agreement, calculated as committed_quantity multiplied by agreed_unit_price (or sum of tier values). Represents the maximum revenue commitment from this customer for the contract period. Used for revenue forecasting and SOX financial reporting.']

  Note: 'Long-term blanket purchase agreement with a customer defining total committed quantity, pricing tiers, and delivery cadence over a contract period (typically 12-24 months). Captures blanket order value, product mix, agreed unit prices, minimum order quantities, call-off frequency, remaining open quantity, and NCNR (non-cancellable non-returnable) terms. Supports high-volume OEM, automotive Tier-1, and distributor relationships where customers commit to annual volumes with periodic release orders against the blanket.'
}

Table order.order_hold {
  order_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each hold record placed on a sales order or order line within the SAP S/4HANA SD order block management module. Primary key for the order.hold data product.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality lot or wafer lot under disposition that triggered a QUALITY hold on the order. Links to the quality domain for full traceability between order holds and quality management workflows. Null for non-quality holds.']
  mpw_shuttle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) run associated with the held order, if applicable. Enables traceability between order holds and MPW shuttle schedules, which have fixed tapeout and delivery dates that may be impacted by hold duration.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order line item on which this hold is placed. Null if the hold applies to the entire sales order header rather than a specific line. Enables line-level hold granularity for partial shipment scenarios.']
  primary_order_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or team responsible for managing and resolving this hold. Typically the credit analyst, export compliance officer, quality engineer, or customer service representative who initiated or owns the hold. Links to the workforce domain for accountability tracking.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sales order on which this hold has been placed. Links the hold record to the order.order data product for full order-to-cash lifecycle traceability.']
  tertiary_order_escalation_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the senior manager or compliance officer to whom the hold has been escalated. Null if not escalated. Enables accountability tracking for escalated holds and management reporting.']
  trade_compliance_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_compliance_hold. Business justification: Order holds can be triggered by a trade compliance hold; linking enables root‑cause analysis.']
  chips_act_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hold requires a CHIPS Act compliance review, particularly for orders involving customers or end-uses that may conflict with CHIPS Act guardrail provisions restricting expansion of semiconductor manufacturing capacity in countries of concern.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hold record was first created in the data platform. Represents the audit trail creation timestamp for the Silver layer record, distinct from the hold_date which captures the business event time.']
  credit_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The customer credit exposure amount in the order currency at the time the credit hold was placed. Applicable only for CREDIT hold type. Represents the outstanding accounts receivable balance plus open order value that triggered the credit block in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The approved credit limit for the customer at the time the credit hold was placed. Applicable only for CREDIT hold type. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR credit management. Used to calculate credit utilization and assess hold severity.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the credit exposure and credit limit amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency semiconductor customer accounts.']
  customer_request_reference varchar [note: 'Customer-provided reference number, purchase order amendment number, or communication reference that authorized or requested the hold. Applicable for CUSTOMER_REQUEST hold type. Enables traceability between the hold and the customer\'s formal request documentation in Salesforce CRM.']
  die_bank_impacted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hold has blocked shipment from the die bank inventory associated with the sales order. True = die bank order is impacted; False = die bank order is not affected. Used for die bank inventory management and allocation planning.']
  dppm_threshold varchar [note: 'The Defective Parts Per Million (DPPM) threshold that was exceeded and triggered the quality hold. Captures the specific quality metric breach driving the hold for QUALITY hold types. Used for quality performance analysis and customer quality reporting.']
  escalation_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was escalated to senior management or a compliance officer. Null if the hold has not been escalated. Used to measure escalation response time and management intervention frequency.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hold has been escalated to senior management or a compliance officer due to complexity, duration, or business impact. True = escalated; False = not escalated. Triggers escalation workflow notifications in SAP S/4HANA.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) or USML category of the product on the held order that triggered the export compliance review. Captures the specific classification driving the hold for EXPORT_COMPLIANCE hold types. Aligns with EAR/BIS and ITAR regulatory requirements.']
  export_license_number varchar [note: 'The export license or authorization number being verified or awaited when the hold type is EXPORT_COMPLIANCE. References the specific BIS/EAR license, ITAR authorization, or equivalent export control document required before shipment can proceed. Null for non-export-compliance holds.']
  hold_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was placed on the sales order or order line. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the hold lifecycle. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD order block creation timestamp.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the hold record as assigned by SAP S/4HANA SD order block management. Used in customer communications, credit team workflows, and export compliance tracking.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the hold record. ACTIVE = hold is in effect and order processing is blocked; RELEASED = hold has been resolved and order processing may resume; ESCALATED = hold has been escalated to senior management or compliance officer; CANCELLED = hold was voided without formal release (e.g., duplicate entry).']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hold category that determines the workflow and approval path required for release. CREDIT = credit limit exceeded or payment overdue; EXPORT_COMPLIANCE = pending export license or ITAR/EAR review; QUALITY = quality disposition pending; CUSTOMER_REQUEST = customer-initiated pause; ALLOCATION = insufficient die bank or wafer inventory; REGULATORY = RoHS/REACH/CHIPS Act compliance verification pending.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold is related to International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) controlled technology or products. True = ITAR-controlled item requiring State Department authorization; False = not ITAR-controlled. Drives export compliance workflow routing and documentation requirements.']
  order_value_at_risk decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net value of the sales order or order line blocked by this hold, expressed in the order currency. Represents the revenue at risk due to the hold and is used for business impact assessment, management reporting, and prioritization of hold resolution efforts.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for resolving the hold. Drives workflow routing and SLA assignment. CREDIT_MGMT = credit and collections team; EXPORT_COMPLIANCE = trade compliance and ITAR/EAR team; QUALITY = quality assurance and reliability team; CUSTOMER_SERVICE = order management team; SUPPLY_CHAIN = allocation and inventory team; FINANCE = accounts receivable.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee or team responsible for managing and resolving this hold at the time it was placed. Denormalized for operational reporting and audit trail purposes without requiring a join to the workforce domain.']
  quality_disposition_status varchar [note: 'Current disposition decision status for the quality lot associated with a QUALITY hold. PENDING = disposition not yet determined; USE_AS_IS = lot approved for shipment as-is; REWORK = lot requires rework before shipment; SCRAP = lot scrapped, order must be re-sourced; RETURN_TO_SUPPLIER = material returned to supplier. Null for non-quality holds.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the hold was placed on the order. Captures the specific business condition triggering the block, such as credit limit exceeded amount, export license number pending, quality lot disposition reference, or customer-requested delivery pause reason. Free-text field sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD order block reason text.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized SAP S/4HANA SD order block reason code (e.g., 01 = Credit Block, 02 = Export Block, 03 = Quality Block) used for systematic reporting and workflow routing. Complements the free-text hold_reason field with a machine-readable classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|02|03|04|05|06|07|08|09|10 — promote to reference product]']
  release_approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who approved the release of the hold at the time of release. Denormalized for audit trail and compliance reporting without requiring a join to the workforce domain.']
  release_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was formally released and order processing was authorized to resume. Null if the hold is still active. Used to calculate hold duration and measure order management cycle time performance.']
  release_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the resolution that enabled the hold to be released. Captures the specific action taken, such as credit limit increase approved, export license number obtained, quality lot disposition completed, or customer confirmation received. Free-text field for audit and compliance documentation.']
  requested_release_date date [note: 'The date by which the hold is expected or requested to be released, as agreed with the customer or set by the hold owner. Used for proactive hold management, customer commitment tracking, and delivery schedule impact assessment.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Extended free-text notes documenting the full resolution process, including any corrective actions taken, approvals obtained, reference documents reviewed, and follow-up actions required. Supports post-hold analysis and process improvement initiatives.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold resolution exceeded the defined SLA target hours. True = SLA was breached; False = resolved within SLA. Used for order management performance reporting and customer escalation management.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Target number of business hours within which the hold must be resolved per the order management SLA policy. Varies by hold type: credit holds typically 24 hours, export compliance holds 72 hours, quality holds 48 hours. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and customer commitment management.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated or triggered the hold. SAP_SD = automatically triggered by SAP S/4HANA SD order block rules; SALESFORCE = initiated from Salesforce CRM by customer service; MANUAL = manually entered by order management team; EXPORT_COMPLIANCE_SYSTEM = triggered by export compliance screening system; QUALITY_SYSTEM = triggered by SAP QM or MES quality disposition workflow.']
  source_system_hold_reference varchar [note: 'The native hold or order block identifier as recorded in the originating operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA SD order block number). Enables reconciliation between the Silver layer data product and the source system of record for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hold record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks the most recent update to any hold attribute, supporting change data capture, audit trail requirements, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  wafer_start_impacted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hold has blocked or delayed a wafer start authorization (WSA) associated with the sales order. True = wafer start is impacted; False = wafer start is not affected. Critical for fab capacity planning and WIP management in semiconductor manufacturing.']

  Note: 'Record of holds placed on a sales order or order line that prevent further processing or shipment. Captures hold type (credit hold, export compliance hold, quality hold, customer request hold), hold reason, hold date, hold owner, release date, release approver, and resolution notes. Supports order management workflows where orders must be paused pending credit approval, export license verification, or quality disposition. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD order block management.'
}

Table order.delivery_confirmation {
  delivery_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the delivery confirmation record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the delivery_confirmation data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who received and confirmed the semiconductor shipment. Used for downstream invoice release and revenue recognition attribution.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales order associated with this delivery confirmation. Enables traceability from proof of delivery back to the originating customer order in the order-to-cash lifecycle.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment record for which this delivery confirmation is being captured. Links the proof of delivery (POD) event back to the originating shipment.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'The Advance Shipment Notice number from the customer\'s EDI 856 ASN acknowledgement message. Used to correlate the delivery confirmation with the inbound EDI transaction from the customer\'s warehouse management or ERP system.']
  carrier_pod_reference varchar [note: 'The carrier\'s own proof-of-delivery document reference number as provided in the carrier POD data feed. Used to reconcile the internal delivery confirmation with the carrier\'s logistics records and resolve freight disputes.']
  carrier_tracking_number varchar [note: 'The carrier\'s shipment tracking number associated with this delivery confirmation. Enables direct lookup in carrier portals for POD document retrieval and dispute resolution.']
  confirmation_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel through which the delivery confirmation was received from the customer or carrier. \'edi\' = Electronic Data Interchange (EDI 856/861); \'portal\' = customer self-service portal; \'api\' = direct API integration; \'email\' = email-based confirmation; \'manual\' = manually entered by customer service representative.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this delivery confirmation record. Communicated to customers and logistics partners as the official proof-of-delivery (POD) reference number. Format: DC- followed by 10 digits.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the delivery confirmation record. \'pending\' indicates awaiting customer acknowledgement; \'confirmed\' indicates full receipt acknowledged; \'disputed\' indicates customer raised a discrepancy; \'partially_confirmed\' indicates partial quantity acknowledged; \'cancelled\' indicates confirmation voided.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of semiconductor units (dies, packaged ICs, wafers, or reels) confirmed as received by the customer at the destination facility. May differ from shipped quantity in cases of shortage, overage, or partial delivery. Used as the basis for invoice quantity and revenue recognition.']
  confirmed_receipt_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the customer confirmed physical receipt of the semiconductor shipment at the destination facility. This is the principal business event date for the delivery confirmation and serves as the trigger date for invoice release and revenue recognition per ASC 606 / IFRS 15.']
  confirmed_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the customer confirmed receipt of the shipment, including timezone offset. Used for SLA measurement, carrier performance analytics, and precise revenue recognition event logging.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery confirmation record was first created in the system, either via carrier POD data feed or customer EDI 856 ASN acknowledgement ingestion. Audit trail field for data lineage.']
  customer_acceptance_status varchar [note: 'The customer\'s formal acceptance decision for the delivered semiconductor product. \'accepted\' = unconditional acceptance; \'rejected\' = full rejection, return initiated; \'conditionally_accepted\' = accepted with noted discrepancy pending resolution; \'pending_inspection\' = customer incoming quality inspection in progress.']
  customer_po_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s purchase order number as referenced on the delivery confirmation. Cross-references the customer\'s procurement system to the semiconductor manufacturer\'s order-to-cash records. Critical for invoice matching and accounts receivable reconciliation.']
  customer_signoff_name varchar [note: 'Name of the customer representative who signed off on the delivery confirmation at the receiving facility. Captured from carrier POD document or EDI 856 acknowledgement. Classified as confidential PII as it identifies an individual at the customer organization.']
  customer_signoff_reference varchar [note: 'The reference identifier of the customer\'s authorized signoff on the delivery confirmation, such as a digital signature ID, customer dock receipt number, or customer ERP goods receipt document number. Required for invoice release authorization and revenue recognition event triggering.']
  days_late varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the confirmed receipt date exceeded the committed delivery date on the sales order. Negative values indicate early delivery. Zero indicates on-time delivery. Used for carrier performance scorecards and customer SLA compliance reporting.']
  delivery_performance_code varchar [note: 'Classification of delivery performance relative to the confirmed delivery date on the sales order. \'on_time\' = delivered within the committed window; \'early\' = delivered before committed date; \'late\' = delivered after committed date; \'partial\' = partial quantity delivered; \'failed\' = delivery not completed. Used for OTIF (On-Time In-Full) KPI reporting.']
  device_revision varchar [note: 'The silicon revision or stepping of the delivered semiconductor device (e.g., A0, B1, C2). Captured at delivery confirmation to ensure the customer received the correct device revision as specified in the purchase order and product change notification (PCN).']
  discrepancy_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the discrepancy reported by the customer. Captures details not covered by the discrepancy_type enumeration, such as specific damage descriptions, lot number mismatches, or partial damage to a reel. Used in quality notification and RMA processing.']
  discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator set to TRUE when the customer has reported any discrepancy between the shipment contents and the delivery confirmation. Triggers downstream discrepancy resolution workflow, quality notification in SAP QM, and potential RMA initiation.']
  discrepancy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discrepancy reported by the customer. \'quantity_shortage\' = fewer units received than shipped; \'wrong_part\' = incorrect part number or device type received; \'damaged_goods\' = physical damage to ICs, packaging, or wafers; \'wrong_lot\' = incorrect lot number or traceability mismatch; \'labeling_error\' = incorrect label or marking; \'none\' = no discrepancy.']
  export_control_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator set to TRUE when export control compliance has been verified for this delivery, confirming the shipment reached the authorized end-destination and end-user as specified in the export license. Required for BIS/EAR and ITAR compliance audit trails.']
  goods_receipt_document_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s ERP goods receipt document number (e.g., SAP MIGO GR document) confirming the semiconductor shipment has been posted into the customer\'s inventory. Provides the strongest form of delivery confirmation for invoice matching and dispute resolution.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code governing the transfer of risk and title for this shipment. Determines the point at which delivery is legally confirmed and revenue recognition is triggered. Critical for export compliance and financial reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_release_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator set to TRUE when this delivery confirmation has triggered the downstream invoice release event in SAP S/4HANA FI/SD. Enables monitoring of the order-to-cash cycle and identification of confirmed deliveries pending invoice generation.']
  invoice_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the invoice release event was triggered in SAP S/4HANA FI following delivery confirmation. Used for order-to-cash cycle time analytics and revenue recognition timing compliance under ASC 606 / IFRS 15.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator set to TRUE when the delivered semiconductor product is subject to ITAR controls. Triggers mandatory end-use verification and delivery confirmation audit requirements under ITAR Part 123.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The semiconductor wafer lot number associated with the delivered product. Enables full traceability from delivery confirmation back to the fabrication lot in the MES (Camstar/Applied Materials SmartFactory). Critical for quality escapes, field failure analysis, and DPPM tracking.']
  package_count_confirmed varchar [note: 'The number of physical packages (boxes, reels, trays, tubes) confirmed as received by the customer. Compared against the shipped package count to identify missing packages in transit. Used for carrier claims and freight audit.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturer\'s part number (MPN) of the semiconductor product delivered. Used to verify correct part delivery against the customer PO and to cross-reference with the product master in Oracle Agile PLM and SAP MM.']
  pod_source varchar [note: 'The originating data source from which this delivery confirmation was captured. \'carrier_pod\' = carrier electronic POD feed; \'edi_856\' = customer EDI 856 ASN acknowledgement; \'customer_portal\' = customer self-service portal submission; \'manual\' = manually entered by customer service; \'email_confirmation\' = email-based confirmation.']
  quantity_variance decimal(18,4) [note: 'The arithmetic difference between shipped_quantity and confirmed_quantity (shipped minus confirmed). A positive value indicates a shortage; a negative value indicates an overage. Used to trigger discrepancy workflows and credit/debit memo processing in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  receiving_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination country where the semiconductor shipment was received. Used for export control compliance verification, trade compliance reporting, and geographic revenue analytics.']
  receiving_location_code varchar [note: 'The customer\'s facility or dock location code where the semiconductor shipment was physically received. Sourced from the customer EDI 856 ASN or carrier POD data. Used for logistics analytics and customer facility mapping.']
  revenue_recognition_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator set to TRUE when this delivery confirmation has satisfied the performance obligation criteria and triggered a revenue recognition event in the financial system. Critical for SOX-compliant revenue reporting.']
  rma_reference_number varchar [note: 'The Return Merchandise Authorization number issued when a discrepancy results in a product return. Populated only when discrepancy_flag is TRUE and a return has been authorized. Links the delivery confirmation to the downstream RMA and reverse logistics process.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator confirming that the delivered semiconductor product complies with the EU RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) restricting hazardous substances. Captured at delivery confirmation for regulatory compliance documentation.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of semiconductor units originally shipped as recorded on the shipment record. Compared against confirmed_quantity to identify shortages or overages at delivery confirmation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the confirmed delivery quantity. EA = each (individual packaged IC or die); REEL = tape-and-reel packaging; WAFER = bare or processed wafer; TRAY = JEDEC tray; TUBE = IC tube packaging; LOT = wafer lot.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this delivery confirmation record, such as discrepancy updates, status changes, or customer signoff amendments. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Customer delivery confirmation record capturing proof of delivery (POD) for semiconductor shipments. Records confirmed receipt date, confirmed quantity, receiving location, customer PO reference, discrepancy flag, discrepancy type (quantity shortage, wrong part, damaged goods), and customer signoff reference. Triggers downstream invoice release and revenue recognition events. Sourced from carrier POD data and customer EDI 856 ASN acknowledgements.'
}

Table order.lot_assignment {
  lot_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a specific lot-to-order-line assignment record within the order-to-cash fulfillment process.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer for whom this lot assignment is made. Supports customer-level lot traceability reporting required by automotive and aerospace customers.']
  assignment_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Lot assignment ownership links a responsible employee to each wafer lot for traceability and quality audits.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: REQUIRED: Lot assignment process records which wafer lot is assigned to a specific order/line; FK provides direct lookup for assignment audits.']
  mpw_shuttle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MPW (Multi-Project Wafer) shuttle run from which this lot originates, applicable for MPW lot types. Links the assignment back to the specific MPW shuttle for design-to-delivery traceability.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order line item within the sales order for which this lot is being assigned. Enables line-level lot traceability for multi-line orders.']
  osat_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) vendor responsible for packaging and assembly of the lot, if applicable. Relevant for packaged goods lots and KGD lots sourced from external assembly houses.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sales order header to which this lot assignment belongs. Links the lot assignment back to the customer order for order-to-cash traceability.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outbound shipment record under which this lot assignment was physically dispatched. Links the lot assignment forward to the shipment for end-to-end order-to-cash traceability.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ATE (Automatic Test Equipment) test program used to qualify the lot prior to assignment. Enables traceability from the customer order back to the specific test program version used for lot qualification.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the assigned lot was physically shipped to the customer. Populated upon shipment confirmation from the MES or SAP SD goods issue posting. Used for on-time delivery (OTD) performance measurement.']
  allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank governing the order in which this lot assignment is fulfilled when supply is constrained. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used by supply chain planners for backlog management and allocation decisions.']
  assigned_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of units from this lot assigned to the order line. For wafer lots this represents wafer count; for die lots this represents die count; for packaged goods lots this represents unit count. Must not exceed the available lot quantity.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the lot was formally assigned to the customer order line. Used for backlog management, delivery schedule tracking, and IATF 16949 lot traceability audit trails.']
  assignment_source varchar [note: 'Indicates how the lot assignment was created. Manual indicates a planner manually assigned the lot; atp_auto indicates the SAP ATP (Available-to-Promise) engine automatically assigned it; allocation_run indicates it was created by a batch allocation run; planner_override indicates a planner overrode an automatic assignment; system_transfer indicates the assignment was transferred from another order.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the lot-to-order assignment. Tentative indicates a preliminary reservation pending confirmation; confirmed indicates a firm allocation; allocated indicates inventory has been committed; shipped indicates the lot has been dispatched; cancelled indicates the assignment was voided; on_hold indicates the assignment is suspended pending quality disposition or export review.']
  backlog_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot assignment is part of the open order backlog, meaning the order has not yet been fully shipped and revenue has not been recognized. Used for backlog reporting and revenue forecasting.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code or description explaining why a lot assignment was cancelled. Populated only when assignment_status is cancelled. Used for root cause analysis and supply chain performance reporting.']
  certificate_of_conformance_number varchar [note: 'The certificate of conformance document number issued for this lot assignment, confirming the lot meets all specified quality and regulatory requirements. Required by automotive (IATF 16949) and aerospace customers.']
  chips_act_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lot assignment is associated with production eligible for US CHIPS and Science Act incentives or reporting requirements. Relevant for domestic semiconductor manufacturing compliance and subsidy tracking.']
  confirmed_ship_date date [note: 'The confirmed date on which the assigned lot is scheduled to ship to the customer. Drives delivery commitment and ATP (Available-to-Promise) calculations in SAP SD.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the country where the lot was manufactured. Required for customs declarations, tariff classification, and CHIPS Act domestic content reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lot assignment record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation timestamp required for SOX compliance and IATF 16949 traceability records.']
  customer_po_number varchar [note: 'The customer-provided purchase order number associated with this lot assignment. Required for invoice matching, customer receiving, and lot traceability documentation provided to the customer.']
  date_code varchar [note: 'The manufacturing date code of the lot in YYYYWW format (year and work week), indicating when the lot was fabricated. Required for component traceability, shelf-life management, and customer receiving inspection.']
  dppm decimal(12,4) [note: 'The defective parts per million rate measured for the assigned lot. Key quality metric reported to automotive customers under IATF 16949 and AIAG quality reporting requirements.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'The Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) applicable to the lot being assigned, as required under EAR (Export Administration Regulations). Determines whether an export license is required for shipment to the customer destination country.']
  export_license_number varchar [note: 'The export license number issued by BIS or DDTC authorizing shipment of this lot to the customer, where required. Must be documented on shipping documentation for EAR/ITAR compliance.']
  export_license_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an export license is required for shipment of this lot to the customer destination country based on the ECCN and destination. Drives export compliance workflow in SAP GTS.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'The SAP QM inspection lot number associated with the quality inspection results for this manufacturing lot. Links the lot assignment to the formal quality inspection record for audit traceability.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lot being assigned is subject to ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) controls. ITAR-controlled lots require additional compliance review and documentation before shipment.']
  kgd_grade varchar [note: 'The KGD (Known Good Die) quality grade assigned to the lot, applicable for die lots and advanced packaging orders. Grade A represents highest quality fully tested die; Grade B represents die meeting relaxed specifications; engineering sample and qualification grades apply to pre-production lots.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturing lot identifier assigned by the Camstar MES or SAP PP module. Uniquely identifies the wafer lot, die lot, or packaged goods lot being assigned to the order line. This is the primary traceability key linking customer orders back to wafer fabrication.']
  lot_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lot being assigned to the order line. Distinguishes between wafer lots (silicon substrates in fabrication), die lots (singulated Known Good Die), packaged goods lots (finished assembled units), KGD (Known Good Die) lots for advanced packaging, and MPW (Multi-Project Wafer) shuttle lots.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'The finished goods or intermediate part number (SAP material number) associated with the lot being assigned. Identifies the specific IC, die, or wafer product being fulfilled against the order line.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'The revision level of the part number associated with the assigned lot. Critical for automotive and aerospace customers who require revision-level traceability per IATF 16949 and AS9100 requirements.']
  process_node varchar [note: 'The semiconductor fabrication process node (technology node) used to manufacture the lot, expressed in nanometers (e.g., 7nm, 5nm, 28nm, 65nm). Identifies the manufacturing technology generation for the assigned lot.']
  quality_disposition varchar [note: 'The quality disposition status of the lot at the time of assignment. Accepted indicates the lot passed all quality gates; conditionally accepted indicates the lot is approved with documented deviations; rejected indicates the lot failed quality inspection; under review indicates quality evaluation is in progress; quarantined indicates the lot is isolated pending investigation.']
  quality_disposition_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting any lot-specific quality disposition details, deviations, waivers, or special handling instructions associated with this assignment. Required for automotive customers under IATF 16949 non-conforming product management.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned lot is compliant with EU REACH Regulation (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals). Required for EU market shipments and chemical substance reporting.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned lot is compliant with the EU RoHS Directive (Restriction of Hazardous Substances), restricting the use of specific hazardous materials in electronic equipment. Required for EU market shipments.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure applicable to the assigned quantity. Wafers for wafer lots, dies for die lots, units for packaged goods, reels or trays for tape-and-reel or tray-packaged finished goods.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lot assignment record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to the assignment for audit trail and data lineage purposes in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  wafer_fab_site varchar [note: 'The fabrication facility site code where the wafer lot was manufactured. Identifies the specific FAB (Fabrication Facility) for supply chain traceability, export control compliance, and CHIPS Act reporting.']
  yield_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'The die yield percentage of the assigned lot, representing the proportion of good dies per wafer that passed all test and inspection criteria. Critical quality metric for automotive and aerospace customers requiring lot-level quality data.']

  Note: 'Association record linking a specific wafer lot or finished goods lot to a customer order line for fulfillment traceability. Captures assigned lot number, lot type (wafer lot, die lot, packaged goods lot), assigned quantity, assignment date, assignment status (tentative, confirmed, shipped), and any lot-specific quality disposition notes. Enables full lot traceability from customer order back to wafer fabrication and forward to shipment. Critical for automotive (IATF 16949) and aerospace customers requiring lot-level traceability.'
}

Table quality.inspection_lot {
  inspection_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection lot record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Required for the Inspection Lot Customer Assignment report, tying each lot’s inspection results to the purchasing customer for traceability, billing, and compliance.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inspection lot cost allocation required for budgeting and internal accounting; finance cost_center tracks these expenses.']
