  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Export compliance verification requires each inspected lot to be linked to the export license authorizing its shipment.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_wafer_lot. Business justification: Inspection lot records are tied to a specific wafer lot for lot‑level quality inspection; linking enables lot‑level inspection reports and traceability.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Inspection lot reports are generated per IC part; linking to ic_catalog enables traceability for compliance and lot‑by‑lot quality reports.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Required for Inspection Lot Traceability Report linking each lot to its originating IC design project for root‑cause analysis and compliance.']
  inspection_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Inspection Lot Report requires linking each lot to the Fab Tool used for inspection to ensure traceability and compliance.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Inspection Lot Traceability Report: links each lot to the inspector employee who performed the inspection.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Opportunity‑to‑Lot traceability report ties production lot quality outcomes back to the originating sales opportunity for revenue impact analysis.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Inspection lot is generated per order line; linking enables traceability for warranty and inspection reporting.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: Inspection lot tracking ties each lot to the specific process step being inspected, required for lot‑by‑step quality dashboards.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Inspection Lot‑PO traceability required for Supplier Performance Review reports linking inspection results to the originating purchase order.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier providing the material.']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Maximum defect count allowed for acceptance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection lot record was created.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Total number of defects detected in the lot.']
  defect_density decimal(10,4) [note: 'Defects per unit area metric for the lot.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition assigned to the lot after inspection.']
  disposition_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the chosen disposition.']
  external_lot_code varchar [note: 'External reference code used by suppliers or customers.']
  iatf_16949_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with IATF 16949 automotive quality standards.']
  inspection_lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection lot.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the inspection.']
  inspection_stage varchar [note: 'Process stage at which the inspection occurs.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Category of inspection performed on the lot.']
  iso_9001_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with ISO 9001 quality management.']
  jedec_reliability_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with JEDEC reliability specifications.']
  kgd_certification_date date [note: 'Date when KGD certification was granted.']
  kgd_certified boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the lot contains KGD‑certified dies.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User name of the person who last modified the record.']
  lot_size varchar [note: 'Total number of units (wafers, dies, or packaged parts) in the lot.']
  lot_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lot based on its position in the workflow.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Type of material the lot consists of.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the primary measurement taken during inspection.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the measured value.']
  measurement_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the key measurement (e.g., critical dimension).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the inspection lot.']
  quality_engineer varchar [note: 'Name of the quality engineer responsible for the lot.']
  rejection_criteria varchar [note: 'Defect count threshold that triggers rejection.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units sampled from the lot.']
  sampling_plan_aql varchar [note: 'AQL value defined for the sampling plan.']
  technology_node varchar [note: 'Process technology node used for the lot.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used to quantify the lot size.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  wafer_size_mm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Diameter of wafers in the lot, expressed in millimetres.']
  yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of good units produced from the lot.']

  Note: 'Master record for a quality inspection lot representing a batch of wafers, dies, or packaged units submitted for quality inspection at any stage. Covers all inspection types: incoming material (IQC for raw wafers, chemicals, photomasks, gases, OSAT subassemblies with supplier and PO reference), in-process (FEOL, BEOL, packaging), and final outgoing. Captures lot origin, supplier ID (for IQC), inspection type, inspection stage, lot size, sampling plan parameters (AQL, sample size, acceptance/rejection numbers), inspection results, measured parameters, and disposition decision (accept, reject, hold, rework). Integrates with SAP QM and Camstar MES for lot status management and goods receipt inspection. SSOT for all quality inspection activities across the fab-to-finish flow including incoming quality control.'
}

Table quality.defect_record {
  defect_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the defect record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Enables the Defect Ownership Tracking process, linking each defect to the responsible customer account for warranty claims and customer notifications.']
  booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.booking. Business justification: Warranty claim analysis requires linking each defect to the original customer booking for liability and cost recovery.']
  cluster_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking defects that belong to the same spatial cluster.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Defect handling expenses are charged to a cost center for financial tracking of quality loss costs.']
  defect_wafer_map_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the generated wafer defect map.']
  design_ip_core_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ip_core. Business justification: Needed for Defect Attribution Report to associate each defect with the specific IP core block used in the design, enabling targeted remediation.']
  detection_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Defect Analysis Report ties each defect to the detecting Fab Tool for root‑cause analysis and corrective action planning.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the inspection.']
  experimental_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_lot. Business justification: Research defect analysis ties defect records to the experimental lot they originated from for root‑cause analysis of new process nodes.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot containing the wafer.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.inspection_lot.inspection_lot_id — Every defect is detected within an inspection lot. This is the fundamental parent-child relationship for defect tracking. Without this FK, defects cannot be traced to their inspection context.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Defect Tracking & Recall Management uses the inventory lot ID to locate and quarantine affected stock across the supply chain.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Defect records must be tied to the originating order line for root‑cause analysis, warranty claims, and corrective actions.']
  primary_wafer_map_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.wafer_map.wafer_map_id — Defects with X/Y coordinates must link to the wafer map for spatial analysis. This is critical for systematic defect pattern detection.']
  process_recipe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.recipe. Business justification: Defect analysis reports need the recipe used during the step to correlate process parameters with defect types.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: Root cause analysis requires linking each defect record to the exact process step where the defect originated, used in RCA reports.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Defect Record linked to purchase order for Supplier Defect Rate reports used in corrective action planning.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer on which the defect was observed.']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Logical zone on the wafer used for density calculations.']
  bin_assignment varchar [note: 'Pass/fail bin code assigned to the die (e.g., P, F1, F2).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes from the inspector or analyst.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed action to remediate the defect.']
  defect_area_um2 double [note: 'Calculated area of the defect in square micrometers.']
  defect_classification varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the defect (e.g., particle, patterning, etch).']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the defect type (e.g., D001, D002).']
  defect_density_per_zone double [note: 'Number of defects per unit area within the zone.']
  defect_layer varchar [note: 'Process layer where the defect was found.']
  defect_severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating indicating impact on yield.']
  defect_size_nm double [note: 'Measured size of the defect in nanometers.']
  defect_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the defect record.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to detect the defect.']
  die_x varchar [note: 'Column index of the die containing the defect on the wafer grid.']
  die_y varchar [note: 'Row index of the die containing the defect on the wafer grid.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final handling decision for the defect.']
  edge_exclusion_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the defect lies within the defined edge exclusion area.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the defect was detected during inspection.']
  flat_notch_orientation varchar [note: 'Orientation of wafer flat or notch relative to the defect.']
  inspection_recipe varchar [note: 'Name of the inspection recipe or parameter set used.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the defect record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the defect record.']
  repeatability_flag boolean [note: 'True if the defect pattern repeats across multiple wafers.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the identified root cause.']
  x_coordinate double [note: 'Horizontal position of the defect on the wafer map (millimeters).']
  y_coordinate double [note: 'Vertical position of the defect on the wafer map (millimeters).']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing individual defect events, wafer-level defect maps, and spatial bin distributions detected during wafer inspection, die sort, or final test. At the defect event level: stores defect classification code, defect coordinates (X/Y on wafer map), defect size, defect layer (FEOL/MOL/BEOL), detection method (KLA ICOS optical, e-beam, SEM), severity rating, and disposition (scrap, rework, accept). At the wafer map level: captures per-wafer die grid coordinates, per-die pass/fail bin assignment, defect density per zone, edge exclusion zone, flat/notch orientation, and map generation timestamp. Enables yield spatial analysis, systematic defect pattern detection (repeater analysis, cluster detection), and wafer-level bin map visualization. Integrates directly with KLA ICOS inspection system output and ATE wafer probing systems. SSOT for all defect events, spatial defect analysis, and wafer-level quality maps.'
}

Table quality.wafer_map {
  wafer_map_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for wafer_map']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator or system user who initiated the map generation.']
  experimental_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_lot. Business justification: Wafer maps are associated with experimental lots to evaluate new wafer designs in research projects.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspection tool that produced the map.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fabrication lot containing the wafer.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Wafer Map Archive ties each map to the inventory wafer lot for yield analysis and historical compliance audits.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Costing and pricing analysis links wafer map data to the original sales quote to validate pricing assumptions against actual silicon usage.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer associated with this map.']
  wafer_probe_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.wafer_probe_run. Business justification: REQUIRED: Test execution (wafer_probe_run) creates the wafer map; traceability report links map to its probe run for root‑cause analysis.']
  bin_count_total varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct bins used in the map.']
  compliance_iatf16949 boolean [note: 'Flag indicating compliance with IATF 16949 for this wafer map.']
  compliance_iso9001 boolean [note: 'Flag indicating compliance with ISO 9001 for this wafer map.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation timestamp in the data lake.']
  defect_density_per_sqmm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average defect density across the wafer measured in defects per square millimeter.']
  defect_type varchar [note: 'Primary defect type identified on the wafer.']
  defect_zone varchar [note: 'Region of the wafer where defects are concentrated.']
  die_grid_columns varchar [note: 'Number of die columns in the wafer grid.']
  die_grid_rows varchar [note: 'Number of die rows in the wafer grid.']
  die_yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Yield percentage calculated from passing dies over total dies.']
  edge_exclusion_zone_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Width of the edge exclusion zone in millimeters where dies are not counted.']
  fail_bin_code varchar [note: 'Bin code representing failing (defective) dies.']
  failing_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of dies classified as failing.']
  flat_orientation varchar [note: 'Orientation of the wafer flat/notch relative to the map coordinate system.']
  is_kgd_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wafer passed Known Good Die certification.']
  kgd_certification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when KGD certification was applied.']
  map_checksum varchar [note: 'Checksum (e.g., SHA-256) of the map file for integrity verification.']
  map_file_path varchar [note: 'File system path or URI where the wafer map image/file is stored.']
  map_generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the wafer map was generated.']
  map_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the wafer map.']
  map_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the map generation algorithm or software.']
  pass_bin_code varchar [note: 'Bin code representing passing (good) dies.']
  passing_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of dies classified as passing.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes about the wafer map.']
  total_die_count varchar [note: 'Total number of dies evaluated on the wafer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record last update timestamp.']

  Note: 'Spatial defect and bin distribution map for an individual wafer within an inspection lot. Captures wafer ID, lot ID, die grid coordinates, per-die pass/fail bin assignment, defect density per zone, edge exclusion zone, flat/notch orientation, and map generation timestamp. Sourced from KLA ICOS and ATE wafer probing systems. Enables yield spatial analysis and systematic defect pattern detection.'
}

Table quality.yield_record {
  yield_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the yield record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator or technician overseeing the measurement.']
  experimental_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_lot. Business justification: Yield data from quality is linked to experimental lots to assess the success of research‑driven process changes.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment that performed the measurement.']
  final_test_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.final_test_run. Business justification: REQUIRED: Yield records reference the final test run that produced pass/fail counts; needed for yield vs. test performance dashboards.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.inspection_lot.inspection_lot_id — Yield measurements are taken at quality gates associated with inspection lots. This link enables yield-to-defect correlation analysis.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Yield Reporting aggregates yield percentages per inventory lot for financial reporting and customer yield guarantees.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Yield records are reported per order line; FK supports performance metrics and customer yield reports.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: Yield records are aggregated per process step; linking provides step‑level yield analysis for continuous improvement.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Yield performance is reported per profit center to assess profitability of product lines.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Yield analysis per purchase order supports Yield Loss Category reports and supplier yield KPI tracking.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the process recipe applied to the wafer.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Yield records are produced per SKU for production performance dashboards; FK to sku replaces denormalized product_sku column.']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Supports Yield Analysis Dashboard linking yield data to the corresponding tapeout, essential for performance tracking and cost forecasting.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the individual wafer.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data acquisition batch.']
  bin_distribution_summary varchar [note: 'Compact representation (e.g., JSON) of die bin counts across test bins.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement equipment was calibrated.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or observations related to the measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the yield record was first created in the system.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Number of defects detected for the specified defect type.']
  defect_density_per_cm2 decimal(8,4) [note: 'Number of defects per square centimeter of wafer area.']
  defect_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dominant defect observed.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the yield measurement was captured.']
  fab_line varchar [note: 'Production line identifier within the fab.']
  good_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of dies that passed all quality tests.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail outcome of the inspection.']
  inspection_system varchar [note: 'Name of the inspection/metrology system used (e.g., KLA ICOS).']
  lot_humidity_percent decimal(4,2) [note: 'Relative humidity of the fab environment during measurement.']
  lot_origin varchar [note: 'Fab location code where the wafer lot originated.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the wafer lot.']
  lot_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average temperature of the wafer lot during measurement.']
  measurement_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Stated accuracy of the measurement as a percentage.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Physical method used to obtain the yield measurement.']
  measurement_stage varchar [note: 'Quality gate at which the yield was measured.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the primary measurement (e.g., percent, count).']
  measurement_variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical variance observed across repeated measurements.']
  process_node varchar [note: 'Technology node (e.g., 7nm, 5nm) used for the wafer.']
  quality_gate varchar [note: 'Specific quality gate where the measurement was recorded.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the measurement was taken.']
  source_file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the source file or data feed that supplied the measurement.']
  test_time_seconds decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total time taken to perform the yield test.']
  tool_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the measurement tool.']
  total_die_count varchar [note: 'Total number of dies on the wafer lot.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the yield record.']
  yield_gap_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Difference between actual yield and target yield.']
  yield_loss_category varchar [note: 'Root cause classification for yield loss.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Yield expressed as a percentage of good dies over total dies.']
  yield_record_status varchar [note: 'Current validation status of the yield record.']
  yield_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target yield percentage defined for the product/process.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing yield measurement outcomes at each quality gate: wafer probe (die sort yield), final test yield, and packaged unit yield. Stores wafer lot ID, process node, product SKU, total die count, good die count, yield percentage, bin distribution summary, yield loss category (random defect, systematic, parametric, test escape), and measurement timestamp. SSOT for yield tracking across the fab-to-finish flow.'
}

Table quality.spc_chart {
  spc_chart_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SPC chart record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity does not fit standard role categories and is modeled as a custom data product.']
  control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the control plan that defines the SPC parameters for this chart.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the measurement or logged the data.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot associated with the measurement.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.inspection_lot.inspection_lot_id — SPC measurements are taken during inspection activities. Out-of-control SPC signals trigger inspection lot holds. This is the process control to inspection feedback loop.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: SPC Dashboard monitors process control metrics linked to the specific inventory lot to trigger corrective actions.']
  opc_rule_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.opc_rule_set. Business justification: OPC rule set defines target CD for the step; SPC chart must reference it for model‑based control limits per JEDEC standards.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: SPC charts are generated per process step; linking enables automated retrieval of step metadata for control‑limit calculations.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: SPC Chart‑PO association enables statistical process control monitoring tied to specific supplier purchase orders.']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: SPC charts created during research runs are stored with the associated research project for statistical analysis.']
  tool_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: SPC Dashboard links each chart to the Fab Tool that generated the data, enabling tool‑level performance monitoring.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific wafer within the lot.']
  assignable_cause_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the identified root cause for an out‑of‑control signal.']
  chart_type varchar [note: 'Statistical chart type used for the measurement series.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes or observations related to the measurement.']
  control_limit_center decimal(12,6) [note: 'Center line value (process mean) for the chart.']
  control_limit_lower decimal(12,6) [note: 'Calculated lower control limit for the chart.']
  control_limit_upper decimal(12,6) [note: 'Calculated upper control limit for the chart.']
  corrective_action_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corrective action taken to address the assignable cause.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SPC chart record was created in the system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the measurement data.']
  die_position varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual die (chip) measured.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this SPC chart configuration was retired or superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this SPC chart configuration became effective.']
  is_baseline boolean [note: 'True if this chart serves as the baseline for comparative analysis.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Technique by which the measurement was obtained.']
  measurement_subgroup varchar [note: 'Logical grouping identifier for the sample (e.g., lot number, wafer ID).']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the measurement was recorded.']
  measurement_tool varchar [note: 'Equipment or system that performed the measurement.']
  measurement_value decimal(12,6) [note: 'Observed value of the monitored parameter for the sample.']
  out_of_control_flag boolean [note: 'True when the measurement falls outside control limits.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the process parameter being monitored (e.g., CD, Thickness, Resistivity).']
  parameter_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the monitored parameter (e.g., nm, µm, %).']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units in each sample subgroup.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'How often samples are taken for this SPC chart.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the measurement was taken.']
  spc_chart_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the SPC chart record.']
  spc_to_metrology varchar [note: 'FK to quality.metrology_measurement.metrology_measurement_id — SPC charts are populated by metrology measurements. This is the fundamental data flow from measurement to statistical control.']
  spc_to_spec varchar [note: 'FK to quality.spec.spec_id — SPC control limits are derived from product/process specifications. The spec product defines the acceptance criteria that SPC monitors against. This link is essential for automated OOC detection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the SPC chart record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the control plan applied to this chart.']

  Note: 'Statistical Process Control record encompassing both the control plan definition and ongoing chart measurements for a specific process step and tool combination. Captures control plan parameters (monitored parameters, chart type, sampling frequency, sample size, reaction plan), control chart data (X-bar/R, CUSUM, EWMA values), UCL/LCL/CL values, out-of-control signal flags, assignable cause codes, and corrective action references. Supports IATF 16949 and ISO 9001 SPC requirements for process stability monitoring. SSOT for SPC configuration and measurement tracking.'
}

Table quality.fmea_record {
  fmea_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) record.']
  fabrication_process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key hint to the manufacturing process step entity.']
  sales_design_win_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_design_win. Business justification: Design‑win risk management requires associating the FMEA with the winning design to monitor failure modes for the sold product.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key hint to the product master record associated with this FMEA.']
  action_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person accountable for implementing the recommended action.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the FMEA was formally approved by quality management.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who granted final approval.']
  cause_of_failure varchar [note: 'Identified underlying cause(s) that could lead to the failure mode.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Open text field for any supplemental information or notes.']
  compliance_standards varchar [note: 'Enumerated list of quality or industry standards governing the FMEA.']
  control_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the FMEA to the relevant control plan document.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who entered the FMEA record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the FMEA record was initially entered into the system.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Technique or inspection method employed to discover the failure during manufacturing or testing.']
  detection_rating varchar [note: 'Numerical rating (1‑10) indicating how likely the failure will be detected before it reaches the customer.']
  detection_technique varchar [note: 'Specific detection approach (e.g., visual inspection, automated metrology).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the FMEA record is considered active for risk management.']
  failure_mode_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing how the failure can occur within the product or process.']
  fmea_code varchar [note: 'Business‑level code used to reference the FMEA in reports and change management systems.']
  fmea_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name that identifies the FMEA, often reflecting the product or process it evaluates.']
  fmea_record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the FMEA record, tracking its progression from creation to retirement.']
  fmea_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative defining the boundaries (product, process, geography) covered by the analysis.']
  fmea_to_qualification varchar [note: 'FK to quality.qualification_program.qualification_program_id — FMEAs feed into qualification programs. IATF 16949 requires FMEA linkage to qualification activities.']
  fmea_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record is a Design FMEA (DFMEA) or Process FMEA (PFMEA).']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the failure mode is deemed critical to product safety or performance.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who performed the most recent update.']
  occurrence_rating varchar [note: 'Numerical rating (1‑10) representing the probability that the failure mode will occur.']
  post_action_rpn varchar [note: 'Re‑calculated risk priority number after corrective actions have been applied.']
  potential_effect varchar [note: 'Explanation of the impact on the customer if the failure mode materializes.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Text describing the action(s) proposed to mitigate the identified failure mode.']
  related_design_item varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific IC design element, IP core, or component associated with the failure.']
  related_process_step varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manufacturing/process step where the failure is observed.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier tracking changes to the FMEA over time.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the failure mode origin.']
  risk_priority_number varchar [note: 'Calculated risk priority number derived from severity, occurrence, and detection ratings.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Numerical rating (1‑10) of the seriousness of the failure effect on the end customer.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the recommended action should be finished.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the latest modification to the FMEA record.']

  Note: 'Failure Mode and Effects Analysis record documenting potential failure modes for a product design or manufacturing process step. Captures failure mode description, potential effect on end customer, severity rating (S), occurrence probability (O), detection rating (D), RPN (Risk Priority Number = S×O×D), recommended actions, responsible owner, target completion date, and post-action revised RPN. Supports IATF 16949 DFMEA and PFMEA requirements.'
}

Table quality.reliability_test {
  reliability_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the reliability test record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer associated with the qualification.']
  certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.certification. Business justification: Reliability test results are required evidence for product certification; linking ties each test to its certification record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Reliability test costs are allocated to a cost center for cost‑of‑quality accounting.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the test equipment used (e.g., KLA ICOS system).']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot from which samples were taken.']
  fmea_record_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.fmea_record.fmea_record_id — Reliability test failures validate or invalidate FMEA failure mode predictions. FMEA recommended actions often include reliability testing. qualification_program description states \'Links to reliabili']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Reliability test results are tied to a specific IC part; required for qualification compliance and customer reliability reports.']
  reliability_test_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.reliability_test_run. Business justification: REQUIRED: Reliability test plan must be associated with each execution run to satisfy JEDEC reliability compliance and generate post‑run reports.']
  analysis_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to analyze the failed device.']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'Industry standard governing the qualification (e.g., AEC‑Q100, JEDEC JESD47).']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the person/committee approving the qualification.']
  compliance_iatf_16949 boolean [note: 'True if the test complies with automotive quality standard IATF 16949.']
  compliance_iso_9001 boolean [note: 'True if the test complies with ISO 9001 quality management requirements.']
  compliance_jedec boolean [note: 'True if the test follows applicable JEDEC reliability standards.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that supplied the reliability test data (e.g., KLA ICOS).']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Identifier for the device family or SKU under test.']
  failure_mechanism varchar [note: 'Physical mechanism responsible for the failure.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Observed mode of failure for the device.']
  failure_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the device that failed during testing.']
  failure_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Time elapsed until failure, measured in hours.']
  fit_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated failure rate expressed in FIT (10⁹ hours).']
  fit_rate_confidence decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level for the reported FIT rate.']
  is_kgd_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device is Known Good Die (KGD) certified.']
  milestone_schedule varchar [note: 'Key milestones and dates for the qualification program.']
  operator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the technician who ran the test.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification program.']
  pass_fail_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rule defining pass or fail for the test (e.g., max failure rate).']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the reliability qualification program.']
  qualification_plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the qualification plan document.']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualification driving the reliability test.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reliability test record was created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reliability test record.']
  reliability_grade varchar [note: 'Qualitative reliability grade assigned to the device.']
  reltest_to_qualification varchar [note: 'FK to quality.qualification_program.qualification_program_id — Reliability tests are executed as part of qualification programs. This link is essential for tracking qualification completeness.']
  root_cause_classification varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the root cause.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of devices subjected to the test.']
  test_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total duration of the stress test, in hours.']
  test_execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test was executed.']
  test_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity level during the test, expressed as a percentage.']
  test_location varchar [note: 'Facility or fab where the test was performed.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail outcome of the test.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current execution status of the test.']
  test_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature set point for the stress test, in degrees Celsius.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Stress test methodology applied.']
  test_voltage_v decimal(5,2) [note: 'Voltage applied during the stress test, in volts.']
  weibull_scale_parameter decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scale parameter of the Weibull reliability model.']
  weibull_shape_parameter decimal(6,3) [note: 'Shape parameter of the Weibull reliability model.']

  Note: 'Master record for reliability qualification programs and individual test execution, encompassing program definition, test planning, stress test execution, and failure event tracking. At the program level: captures qualification type (new product, process change, OSAT qualification, PCN-driven), applicable standards (AEC-Q100, JEDEC JESD47, ISO 9001), qualification plan version, milestone schedule, overall qualification status, and approval authority. At the test level: captures test type (HTOL, HAST, TC, ESD, JEDEC stress tests), test conditions (temperature, voltage, humidity, duration), sample size, device type, pass/fail criteria, and individual failure details including failed unit serial number, failure time (hours), failure mode, failure analysis method (SEM, FIB, EMMI, curve trace), root cause classification, and failure mechanism (electromigration, TDDB, HCI, NBTI). SSOT for JEDEC JESD47 and AEC-Q100/Q101 qualification lifecycle from program initiation through test execution to final disposition, FIT rate calculations, and Weibull reliability modeling.'
}

Table quality.quality_qualification_program {
  quality_qualification_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the qualification program record.']
  product_qualification_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.qualification_program. Business justification: Quality qualification programs map one‑to‑one with product qualification programs for tracking regulatory and internal compliance.']
  program_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Qualification Program Management requires linking each program to its owner employee for governance reporting.']
  related_fmea_fmea_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Failure Mode and Effects Analysis linked to this program.']
  related_test_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed test plan used during qualification.']
  research_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.program. Business justification: Qualification programs certify new technologies and are managed under the corresponding research program.']
  actual_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured wafer yield achieved during qualification.']
  applicable_standards varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of standards governing the program (e.g., AEC‑Q100, JEDEC JESD47, ISO 9001).']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual/committee that approves the program.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification program was formally approved.']
  change_request_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the change request that triggered the qualification (if PCN‑driven).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture against applicable standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification program record was first created.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers must be notified of qualification outcomes.']
  documentation_link varchar [note: 'URL or path to the primary qualification program documentation.']
  dppm_actual decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured DPPM observed during qualification.']
  dppm_target decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable DPPM for the qualification program.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the qualification program becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the qualification program expires or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended).']
  final_disposition varchar [note: 'Outcome after program completion indicating product release eligibility.']
  kgd_certification_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if Known Good Die certification is mandatory for the program.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of the qualification program.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the last review.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the qualification program.']
  milestone_schedule varchar [note: 'Serialized representation (e.g., JSON) of key milestones and target dates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the qualification program.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Aggregated result of the qualification program.']
  program_budget_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for the qualification effort expressed in US dollars.']
  program_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the program for reporting and governance.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the program in ERP/PLM systems.']
  program_manager varchar [note: 'Person responsible for day‑to‑day management of the qualification program.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the qualification program.']
  qualification_plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the detailed qualification plan document.']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Category of qualification: new product, process change, OSAT qualification, or PCN‑driven.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required regulatory clearances.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the qualification program.']
  statistical_process_control_included boolean [note: 'True if SPC data collection is part of the qualification activities.']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired wafer yield expressed as a percent.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the qualification plan, e.g., V1.0, V2.1.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for a product or process qualification program governing the full set of qualification activities required before production release. Captures qualification type (new product, process change, OSAT qualification, PCN-driven), applicable standards (AEC-Q100, JEDEC JESD47, ISO 9001), qualification plan version, milestone schedule, overall qualification status, approval authority, and final disposition. Links to reliability tests, FMEA records, and PCN events.'
}

Table quality.quality_kgd_certification {
  quality_kgd_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the KGD certification record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Supports the Customer‑Specific KGD Certification Management workflow, replacing free‑text customer_name with a reference to the customer account for accurate certification tracking.']

  research_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.program. Business justification: KGD certifications are required for outputs of specific research programs to meet industry standards.']
  test_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: KGD Certification records must reference the Fab Tool used for testing to satisfy JEDEC reliability audit requirements.']

  burn_in_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether burn‑in testing was performed and its outcome.']

  certification_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the certification was performed.']
