  certification_reference varchar [note: 'External reference code used by customers or partners to track the certification.']
  certification_standard varchar [note: 'Standard or specification against which the die lot was certified.']
  compliance_iatf16949 boolean [note: 'True if the certification complies with automotive quality standards.']
  compliance_iso9001 boolean [note: 'True if the certification complies with ISO 9001 quality standards.']
  compliance_jedec boolean [note: 'True if the certification meets JEDEC requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was created.']
  customer_requirements varchar [note: 'Any customer‑specific KGD criteria or notes.']
  expiration_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the certification expired or was terminated.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the certification becomes invalid.']
  kgd_version varchar [note: 'Version of the KGD certification schema applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the certification.']
  parametric_screen_result varchar [note: 'Result of the parametric screening step.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number of the product associated with the die lot.']
  quality_kgd_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the certification.']
  reliability_grade varchar [note: 'Overall reliability classification assigned to the die lot.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision count of the certification record.']
  test_coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of required tests that were successfully completed.']
  test_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Length of time the test was run.']
  test_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the test was conducted.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Category of test performed for the certification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']

  Note: 'Known Good Die certification record attesting that a specific die lot has passed all required electrical, parametric, and reliability screens for bare-die shipment to customers. Captures die lot ID, product part number, certification standard (JEDEC JESD49), test coverage percentage, burn-in status, parametric screen results, certification date, expiry date, certifying engineer, and customer-specific KGD requirements. Critical for advanced packaging and MCM customers.'
}

Table quality.dppm_record {
  dppm_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the DPPM record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who received the shipment and reported the defect.']
  capa_record_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.capa_record.capa_record_id — Customer quality issues (DPPM events, 8D reports) trigger CAPA records. This link is required for customer complaint resolution tracking per IATF 16949.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: DPPM records track defective units per part number; linking to ic_catalog enables part‑level defect analytics.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DPPM record was first created in the system.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the DPPM record.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Current closure state of the quality notification.']
  compliance_iso9001 varchar [note: 'Compliance status of the record with ISO 9001 quality management requirements.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate actions taken to contain the defect and prevent further impact.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Corrective steps implemented to eliminate the root cause.']
  defective_units varchar [note: 'Number of units returned by the customer that were identified as defective.']
  dppm_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated DPPM value for the shipment period (defective_units / total_units_shipped * 1,000,000).']
  eight_d_report_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated 8‑D problem‑solving report.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the primary quality event (e.g., notification creation).']
  failure_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the observed failure mode or defect.']
  is_kgd_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned die was a Known Good Die (KGD) certified part.']
  kgd_certification_date date [note: 'Date on which the KGD certification was granted.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the DPPM record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quality notification associated with the record.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number (MPN) of the semiconductor product associated with the DPPM record.']
  preventive_action varchar [note: 'Preventive measures introduced to avoid recurrence of similar defects.']
  record_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the DPPM record, used for external reference and tracking.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the customer or supplier must respond to the notification.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause analysis result identifying the underlying reason for the defect.']
  shipment_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the shipment period covered by this DPPM record.']
  shipment_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the shipment period covered by this DPPM record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system for the record (e.g., SAP QM, KLA ICOS).']
  total_units_shipped varchar [note: 'Total number of units shipped to the customer during the shipment period.']

  Note: 'Customer quality performance and communication record encompassing DPPM tracking, formal quality notifications (8D, SCAR, customer complaints), field return management, and customer-facing quality communications. Captures product part number, customer account, shipment period, total units shipped, defective units returned, DPPM value, notification type, failure description, containment actions, root cause, corrective/preventive actions, 8D report reference, response due date, and closure status. SSOT for all customer-facing quality metrics, notifications, and communications per IATF 16949 customer satisfaction monitoring. Integrates with Salesforce CRM for customer communication tracking.'
}

Table quality.capa_record {
  capa_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the CAPA record.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.audit.audit_id — Audit findings (now merged into audit) trigger CAPAs. audit_finding description states \'linked CAPA record\'. This is a mandatory operational link for ISO 9001 clause 10.2.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: CAPA implementation costs are allocated to a cost center to monitor corrective action spending.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for implementing the CAPA.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer or lot linked to the issue.']
  nonconformance_report_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.nonconformance_report.nonconformance_report_id — CAPAs are triggered by nonconformances. This is a critical traceability link for ISO 9001 clause 10.2 compliance.']
  related_audit_finding_quality_audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the audit finding associated with the CAPA.']
  related_customer_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer complaint that triggered the CAPA.']
  related_defect_defect_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the defect record linked to this CAPA.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or design associated with the CAPA.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the action was actually completed.']
  attachment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., URL or file ID) to supporting documents or images.']
  capa_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the CAPA for tracking and reference.']
  capa_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CAPA record.']
  capa_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the action is corrective or preventive.']
  closure_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the CAPA closure after verification.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the CAPA was formally closed.']
  compliance_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific compliance clause or standard (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949) governing the CAPA.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Specific actions planned or taken to eliminate the identified root cause.']
  cost_actual decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual financial cost incurred after implementation.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost to implement the corrective or preventive action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA record was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost fields.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department owning the CAPA (e.g., Quality, Engineering).']
  detection_date date [note: 'Date when the problem was first detected.']
  detection_phase varchar [note: 'Phase of the product lifecycle where the issue was detected.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Source of detection (e.g., inspection, customer, audit).']
  effectiveness_verification_criteria varchar [note: 'Criteria and metrics used to verify that the CAPA was effective.']
  impact varchar [note: 'Description of the business or technical impact of the non‑conformance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the CAPA.']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Actions designed to prevent recurrence of similar issues in the future.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking to schedule the CAPA work.']
  problem_statement varchar [note: 'Clear description of the problem or non‑conformance that triggered the CAPA.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for when the record was first captured in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for the most recent modification of the record.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment of the issue before mitigation.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the identified root cause.']
  root_cause_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to determine the root cause (e.g., 5‑Why, Ishikawa, FTA, Pareto).']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the issue addressed by the CAPA.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the corrective or preventive action should be completed.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the effectiveness of the CAPA was verified.']
  verification_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the effectiveness verification.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the CAPA record.']

  Note: 'Corrective and Preventive Action record managing the full lifecycle of a quality improvement action triggered by a defect, customer complaint, audit finding, or reliability failure. Captures problem statement, root cause analysis method (5-Why, Ishikawa, FTA), root cause description, corrective action plan, preventive action plan, implementation owner, target and actual completion dates, effectiveness verification criteria, and closure approval. Supports ISO 9001 clause 10.2 and IATF 16949 CAPA requirements.'
}

Table quality.nonconformance_report {
  nonconformance_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the non‑conformance report.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Feeds the Customer Impact Reporting procedure, associating each NCR with the affected customer account to trigger notifications and compliance reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Nonconformance handling expenses are tracked against a cost center for financial impact analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality engineer responsible for the report.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Nonconformances affecting export‑controlled items must be reported against the specific export license for regulatory tracking.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot impacted by the non‑conformance.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: NCMRs are issued for a specific IC part; linking to ic_catalog supports root‑cause analysis and customer notifications.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.inspection_lot.inspection_lot_id — NCRs are typically discovered during inspection. This link provides traceability from the non-conformance back to the inspection event where it was detected.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: NCR handling requires linking the report to the inventory lot to enforce holds, quarantine, and corrective action tracking.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: NCRs reference the process step where non‑conformance was detected, essential for corrective‑action workflow.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: NCR‑PO linkage needed for root‑cause analysis and supplier accountability in Nonconformance Reporting.']
  related_mrb_meeting_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the MRB meeting linked to this report.']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: NCRs generated from prototype runs are recorded against the research project that produced the lot.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the wafer where the issue was detected.']
  attached_document_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of document identifiers attached to the report.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes capturing audit trail details.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard applicable to the report.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completion of corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned corrective actions to prevent recurrence.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action implementation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the customer must be notified.']
  customer_notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the customer notification was sent.']
  detection_point varchar [note: 'Process step or system where the non‑conformance was first detected.']
  die_range varchar [note: 'Range of die numbers on the wafer affected (e.g., "100-200").']
  disposition_action_required varchar [note: 'Specific actions required to implement the chosen disposition.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision on how the non‑conforming material will be treated.']
  hold_initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the quality hold was placed.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the lot was placed on hold.']
  hold_release_condition varchar [note: 'Condition(s) that must be satisfied to release the hold.']
  hold_released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the quality hold was lifted (null if still active).']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Category of quality hold applied to the lot.']
  impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary impact of the non‑conformance.']
  impact_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the financial impact.']
  is_customer_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the non‑conformance impacts a customer.']
  mrb_decision varchar [note: 'Decision made by the MRB regarding the non‑conformance.']
  nonconformance_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the deviation from specification.']
  nonconformance_report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the report.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority assigned for handling the report.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier (NCR number) assigned to the report.']
  report_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the non‑conformance was initially recorded.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Analysis identifying the underlying cause of the non‑conformance.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the non‑conformance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the report (e.g., Camstar MES, KLA ICOS).']
  specification_violated varchar [note: 'Name or code of the specification that was not met.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']

  Note: 'Non-Conformance Report (NCR) and quality hold management record documenting product or process deviations from specification, managing lot hold/release lifecycle, and tracking material review board dispositions. Captures NCR number, affected lot/wafer/unit range, non-conformance description, specification violated, detection point, hold type (process excursion, SPC out-of-control, customer complaint, reliability failure), hold initiation and release timestamps, hold reason, hold release conditions with approver, disposition decision (use-as-is, rework, scrap, return to supplier), material review board (MRB) decision, responsible quality engineer, required disposition actions, and financial impact assessment. SSOT for all in-process quality escapes, quality holds, MRB dispositions, and lot release management. Integrates with Camstar MES and SAP QM for lot status management.'
}

Table quality.quality_audit_finding {
  quality_audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each quality audit finding record.']
  audit_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_event. Business justification: Quality audit findings originate from compliance audit events; linking provides traceability to the audit that generated the finding.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Audit finding remediation costs are charged to a cost center for budgeting corrective actions.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment involved in the finding, if applicable.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_wafer_lot. Business justification: Audit findings are associated with the wafer lot under audit; linking provides lot‑level audit traceability.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: Audit findings often cite the specific process step; linking supports audit traceability and remediation tracking.']
  related_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documentation (e.g., inspection report, test log).']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Audit findings related to research processes are linked to the originating research project for compliance tracking.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product (IC, ASIC, etc.) related to the finding.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer associated with the finding.']
  audit_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit was performed or the observation was recorded.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the audit finding, used for tracking and reference.']
  audit_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the audit was conducted.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the audit (e.g., Process, Design, Supplier).']
  audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score assigned to the audit based on internal rating criteria.']
  audit_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the audit team responsible.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Category of audit that generated the finding: internal, customer, third‑party, or regulatory.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the person who performed the audit.']
  capa_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPA record that resolves this finding.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the finding was formally closed.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the finding\'s resolution lifecycle.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance outcome of the audit finding.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which corrective actions must be completed.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to address the finding and prevent recurrence.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to detect the finding (e.g., inspection, test, review).']
  finding_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the finding: major non‑conformance, minor non‑conformance, observation, or improvement opportunity.']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the observation, non‑conformance, or opportunity identified.']
  impact_estimate varchar [note: 'Qualitative or quantitative estimate of the potential impact on yield, cost, or schedule.']
  is_repeat_issue boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the same issue has been observed previously.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information.']
  observation_location varchar [note: 'Physical location (fab, cleanroom, design office) where the finding was observed.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit finding record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit finding record.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Standard or regulatory framework referenced (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949).']
  repeat_issue_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this specific issue has been recorded.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the finding based on severity and impact.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified underlying cause of the finding after analysis.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Impact severity of the finding on product quality, safety, or compliance.']
  standard_clause varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific clause of a standard (e.g., ISO 9001:8.5.1) that the finding relates to.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the effectiveness of corrective action was verified.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of verification of the corrective action.']

  Note: 'Quality audit finding record capturing observations, non-conformances, and opportunities for improvement identified during internal quality audits, customer audits, or third-party certification audits (ISO 9001, IATF 16949). Stores audit type, audit date, auditor name, audited process/area, finding classification (major NC, minor NC, observation), finding description, applicable standard clause, linked CAPA record, and closure status.'
}

Table quality.audit {
  audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for audit']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the lead auditor responsible for the audit.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the audit was completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the audit actually started.']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard against which the audit is performed.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the audit (e.g., QA-2024-001).']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Textual description of the functional or product scope covered by the audit.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audit record.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit: internal, customer‑conducted, third‑party certification, or other.']
  certificate_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the certification issued (if any) as a result of the audit.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Latest date by which all corrective actions must be closed.']
  findings_total varchar [note: 'Aggregate number of findings recorded for the audit.']
  major_nonconformances varchar [note: 'Count of major non‑conformances identified.']
  minor_nonconformances varchar [note: 'Count of minor non‑conformances identified.']
  observations varchar [note: 'Number of observations (non‑conformance but not a defect).']
  opportunities_for_improvement varchar [note: 'Count of OFI (Opportunity for Improvement) findings.']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the audit after evaluation of all findings.']
  plan_document varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., file name or URL) to the detailed audit plan.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit record.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for the audit to commence.']
  team varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of auditor identifiers or names participating in the audit.']

  Note: 'Master record for quality management system audit events and their findings, covering internal, customer-conducted, and third-party certification body audits. At the audit level: captures audit program reference, audit type (system, process, product), audit scope, applicable standard (ISO 9001, IATF 16949, JEDEC), lead auditor, audit team, scheduled and actual dates, audit plan document reference, overall audit result, and certificate reference. At the finding level: captures individual observations, non-conformances, and opportunities for improvement with finding classification (major NC, minor NC, observation, OFI), finding description, applicable standard clause, corrective action linkage, responsible owner, due date, and closure status. SSOT for all quality audit planning, execution, and finding management per ISO 19011 and IATF 16949 clause 9.2.'
}

Table quality.quality_metrology_measurement {
  quality_metrology_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each metrology measurement event.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the personnel who initiated or supervised the measurement.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the metrology equipment that performed the measurement.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Metrology measurements are taken for a specific IC part; FK supports process control dashboards and traceability.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Metrology measurements must be associated with the inventory lot for compliance audits and traceability of measurement data.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the process recipe or step definition associated with this measurement.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the individual wafer on which the measurement was performed.']
  gauge_rr_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the gauge repeatability and reproducibility study linked to this measurement.']
  measurement_comment varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text comments or observations captured by the operator or system.']
  measurement_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement was performed automatically by the tool or manually by an operator.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the metrology reading was taken on the wafer or test structure.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Category of metrology measurement (e.g., CD-SEM, ellipsometry, profilometry, overlay, film thickness, resistivity, particle count).']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(12,4) [note: 'Estimated uncertainty (±) associated with the measured value.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the measured value (e.g., nm, µm, Å, %).']
  measurement_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric result of the metrology reading expressed in the associated unit.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Result of the measurement comparison against specification limits.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Name or code of the fabrication step (e.g., lithography, etch, CMP) during which the measurement was taken.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the measurement record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the measurement record.']
  site_coordinates varchar [note: 'Encoded X/Y coordinate(s) of the measurement location(s) within the wafer map.']
  site_map_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the measurement site layout used (e.g., 5‑point, 9‑point, 49‑point, full wafer map).']
  spec_lower_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for the measurement before it is considered out‑of‑spec.']
  spec_upper_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the measurement before it is considered out‑of‑spec.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Desired nominal value for the measurement as defined by the process specification.']
  tool_qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification state of the metrology tool (e.g., qualified, unqualified, under maintenance).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of individual metrology measurements taken on wafers or test structures during semiconductor processing for quality verification and process control. Captures measurement type (CD-SEM, ellipsometry, profilometry, overlay, film thickness, resistivity, particle count), tool ID and tool qualification status, wafer lot ID, process step, measurement site coordinates (5-point, 9-point, 49-point maps), measured value, target value, upper/lower spec limits, pass/fail status, measurement uncertainty, gauge R&R reference, and measurement timestamp. Sourced from KLA, ASML, Onto Innovation, and other metrology tools. Feeds SPC control charts and process control loops. SSOT for all quality-critical dimensional and parametric measurements.'
}

Table quality.supplier_quality_scorecard {
  supplier_quality_scorecard_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each supplier quality scorecard record.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.inspection_lot.inspection_lot_id — Supplier scorecards aggregate incoming inspection results. The scorecard description states \'aggregating incoming inspection results\'. This is the data source link.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier evaluated in the scorecard.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the supplier\'s quality liaison.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code for monetary values in the scorecard.']
  evaluation_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the reporting period covered by the scorecard.']
  evaluation_period_start date [note: 'First day of the reporting period covered by the scorecard.']
  improvement_action_plan varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the corrective action plan associated with this scorecard.']
  kpi_cost_of_poor_quality decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost associated with defects, rework, and warranty claims for the period.']
  kpi_delivery_conformance decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries that meet agreed‑upon quality criteria.']
  kpi_incoming_quality_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of incoming parts that meet quality specifications.']
  kpi_responsiveness decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average time (in days) for the supplier to address corrective actions.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next supplier quality review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations from the reviewer.']
  overall_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated quality score for the supplier (0‑100 scale).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scorecard record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scorecard record.']
  scorecard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scorecard record.']
  scorecard_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the scorecard for change‑tracking purposes.']
  supplier_development_program varchar [note: 'Name or code of the development program linked to the supplier.']
  supplier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supplier based on business relationship (e.g., tier level).']
  tier_classification varchar [note: 'Overall classification of the supplier based on the scorecard outcome.']
  trend_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of quality performance trend compared to the previous period.']

  Note: 'Periodic quality performance scorecard for a supplier, aggregating incoming inspection results, NCR counts, DPPM, on-time delivery quality, and CAPA responsiveness over a defined reporting period. Captures supplier ID, evaluation period, overall quality score, individual KPI scores (incoming quality rate, delivery conformance, responsiveness, cost of poor quality), scorecard tier classification (preferred, approved, conditional, probation, disqualified), trend direction, improvement action plan references, supplier development program linkage, and next review date. Supports supplier qualification decisions, approved vendor list management, and IATF 16949 clause 8.4 external provider monitoring requirements.'
}

Table quality.quality_spec {
  quality_spec_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality specification record.']
  fabrication_process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.process_step. Business justification: Specification documents are applied to a particular process step; linking supports step‑specific spec compliance checks.']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.technology_node. Business justification: Specs often depend on the technology node; linking allows node‑specific parameter validation.']
  measurement_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Specification Measurement Traceability links each spec to the Fab Tool that performs the measurement, required for ISO 9001 compliance.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Spec Ownership Register requires linking each specification to the responsible engineer for accountability.']
  packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to packaging.package_type. Business justification: Required for Package Design Specification process where each quality spec must be linked to its package type to enforce dimensional and electrical limits.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Quality specifications are defined per SKU; FK replaces the denormalized product_sku field for precise version control.']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the acceptance criteria.']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Level of the product hierarchy the spec applies to.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the specification was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the specification.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved the specification.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Chronological log of changes made to the specification.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent change to the specification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification record was created.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed specification document.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the specification becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the specification expires (null if open‑ended).']
  iatf_16949_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with automotive quality standard IATF 16949.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to inspect or measure the parameter.']
  iso_9001_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specification complies with ISO 9001 quality standards.']
  jedec_reliability_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with JEDEC reliability specifications.']
  last_validated_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent validation activity.']
  lower_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for the parameter.']
  measurement_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Stated accuracy of the measurement equipment as a percentage.']
  measurement_variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed variance of repeated measurements expressed as a percentage.']
  nominal_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target nominal value for the parameter.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the specification.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the measured parameter (e.g., Vth, line width).']
  process_node varchar [note: 'Technology node (e.g., 7nm, 5nm) relevant to the specification.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Family or series to which the product belongs.']
  quality_spec_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the specification.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the specification is reviewed.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number of the specification.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the specification record.']
  spec_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code assigned to the specification.']
  spec_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the quality specification.']
  spec_type varchar [note: 'Category of the specification such as electrical, dimensional, visual, or reliability.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Category of test associated with the specification.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the parameter (e.g., mV, nm).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification record.']
  upper_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the parameter.']
  validated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specification has been validated against actual parts.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the specification.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the quality specification for a product SKU or process step, including all acceptance criteria, parametric limits, visual inspection standards, and test coverage requirements. Captures spec version, applicable product family, process node, specification type (electrical, dimensional, visual, reliability), parameter name, nominal value, upper and lower spec limits, measurement units, and approval status. SSOT for quality acceptance criteria referenced by inspection lots and test programs.'
}

Table quality.quality_hold {
  quality_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the quality hold record.']
  customer_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer complaint that caused the hold, if applicable.']
  defect_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the defect record linked to this hold, when the hold originates from a defect inspection.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who approved the hold release.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_wafer_lot. Business justification: Quality holds are frequently placed on entire wafer lots; linking enables lot‑wide hold management and reporting.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: Holds are often placed due to issues at a particular process step; linking enables traceability to the step causing the hold.']
  related_spc_rule_spc_control_chart_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the statistical process control rule that triggered the hold, if applicable.']
  trade_compliance_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_compliance_hold. Business justification: When a quality hold is placed for trade‑compliance reasons, it must reference the corresponding trade compliance hold record.']
  affected_entity_ref varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific lot, die bank, or inventory batch under hold.']
  affected_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity that the hold applies to.']
  affected_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units (dies, wafers, or pieces) impacted by the hold.']
  compliance_iatf_16949_flag boolean [note: 'True if the hold complies with automotive industry quality standard IATF 16949.']
  compliance_iso_9001_flag boolean [note: 'True if the hold complies with ISO 9001 quality management requirements.']
  compliance_jedec_flag boolean [note: 'True if the hold meets applicable JEDEC reliability criteria.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold record was first created in the system.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Optional date/time after which the hold is automatically cleared if not released.']
  hold_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the hold for reporting and compliance.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the hold for tracking across systems.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hold.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the reason for the hold.']
  initiated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality engineer who placed the hold.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was placed.']
  is_kgd_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the held lot requires Known Good Die (KGD) certification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by the quality engineer.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level indicating urgency of resolution.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the business or technical reason for the hold.']
  release_reason_description varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the hold was released.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was officially released.']
  required_disposition_actions varchar [note: 'Specific actions required to resolve the hold (e.g., re‑work, inspection, scrap).']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the disposition process for the hold.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for the hold record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the hold record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record placing a quality hold on a wafer lot, die bank, or finished goods inventory pending quality disposition. Captures hold type (process excursion, SPC out-of-control, customer complaint, reliability failure), affected lot or unit range, hold initiation timestamp, hold reason description, responsible quality engineer, required disposition actions, hold release conditions, and actual release timestamp with approver. Integrates with Camstar MES and SAP QM for lot status management.'
}

Table quality.failure_analysis_report {
  failure_analysis_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the failure analysis report.']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Failure Analysis Report requires linking the analyst employee to the report for accountability and skill tracking.']
  capa_record_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.capa_record.capa_record_id — Failure analysis conclusions drive corrective actions. This is a critical link in the closed-loop quality system.']
  failure_nonconformance_report_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.nonconformance_report.nonconformance_report_id — FA reports are triggered by non-conformances. FA description explicitly states \'Links to... nonconformance_report\'. This is a critical investigation-to-disposition link.']
  failure_related_ncr_nonconformance_report_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the linked non‑conformance report, when the failure triggers a quality NCR.']
  related_failure_failure_analysis_report_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated reliability failure record, if the analysis originates from a reliability test.']
  reliability_test_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.reliability_test.reliability_test_id — Failure analysis reports investigate failures detected during reliability testing. FA report description states \'Links to reliability_failure\' which is now merged into reliability_test.']
  analysis_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the failure analysis investigation was completed.']
  analysis_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the failure analysis investigation began.']
  analysis_technique varchar [note: 'Primary analytical method(s) employed during the failure investigation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the final review of the report.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the report was formally approved or rejected.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or observations added by the analyst.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the report record was first created.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standard defect code identifying the defect class (e.g., D1234).']
  failure_analysis_report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the report.']
  failure_mechanism varchar [note: 'Descriptive classification of the underlying failure mechanism (e.g., electromigration, latch‑up, oxide breakdown).']
  failure_severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating assigned to the failure based on impact and recurrence risk.']
  failure_site_location varchar [note: 'Physical location on the die/wafer where the failure was observed (e.g., metal layer 3, die X12Y7).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the report record.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the report (e.g., FA‑2023‑0012).']
  report_title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the failure analysis report.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Category indicating the origin of the failure analysis request.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative of the root cause conclusion derived from the analysis.']
  sample_description varchar [note: 'Brief description of the physical sample(s) examined (e.g., wafer lot, die coordinates, package type).']
  supporting_evidence_refs varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of file paths or identifiers for images, data files, and test logs that support the analysis.']

  Note: 'Formal failure analysis report documenting the investigation of a failed device, wafer, or component. Captures FA request source (reliability test, customer return, in-process failure), sample description, analysis techniques used (SEM, FIB cross-section, EMMI, TEM, EDX), failure site location, identified failure mechanism, root cause conclusion, supporting evidence references, analyst name, and report approval status. Links to reliability_failure, nonconformance_report, and capa_record.'
}

Table quality.quality_notification {
  quality_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for quality_notification']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or department that reported the quality issue.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or component impacted by the quality issue.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: A quality notification is always generated for a specific inspection lot; linking notification to its lot enables traceability and eliminates redundant lot reference columns.']
  related_quality_notification_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on quality_notification (related_quality_notification_id)']
  related_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work order created to address the issue.']
  review_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person responsible for reviewing the notification.']
  actual_resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the issue was actually closed or resolved.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'True if supporting documents or images are attached to the notification.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch identifier associated with the affected units.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by users regarding the notification.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Quality management standard(s) applicable to the notification.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed actions to remediate the quality problem.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the notification record was first created in the system.']
  customer_impact_description varchar [note: 'Details on how customers are impacted by the defect.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Number of defective units identified in the notification.']
  defect_rate_ppm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Defects per million units calculated for the affected lot.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Level of escalation required for the issue.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the inspection that generated the notification was performed.']
  inspection_tool varchar [note: 'Equipment used to perform the quality inspection.']
  is_customer_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quality issue affects external customers.']
  is_repeat_issue boolean [note: 'True if the same defect has been reported previously.']
  kgd_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is KGD (Key Generic Device) certified.']
  location varchar [note: 'Factory or site code where the defect was observed.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for the wafer or component lot.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the measured value.']
  measurement_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric measurement captured during inspection (e.g., line width).']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Planned steps to mitigate the identified risk.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier assigned to the notification (e.g., QN‑2024‑000123).']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the origin or nature of the quality issue.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business‑defined priority level for handling the notification.']
  repeat_issue_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this defect has been reported in the past.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the quality issue was initially reported.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the notification was reviewed.']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk rating assigned to the issue.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the underlying cause of the quality issue.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Impact level of the quality issue based on risk and potential loss.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the quality notification.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the notification.']
  target_resolution_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the issue should be resolved.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the notification record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for quality_notification. Referenced by quality_notification_id.'
}

Table quality.qualification_report {
  qualification_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for qualification_report']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product that the qualification report pertains to.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot identifier associated with the qualification.']
  qualification_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_qualification_program. Business justification: Each qualification report documents the outcome of a qualification program; linking the report to its program provides proper hierarchy and prevents the report table from being isolated.']
  superseded_qualification_report_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on qualification_report (superseded_qualification_report_id)']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the test program used for the qualification.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the report was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved the qualification report.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes added by the author.']
  compliance_standards varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of standards the qualification adheres to. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ISO 9001|JEDEC|IATF 16949|ASTM|MIL‑STD|CUSTOM — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the qualification report record was first created in the system.']
  customer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the customer requesting or receiving the qualification.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or tool that supplied the qualification data.']
  dppm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Defect density measured as defects per million units.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the qualification results are considered effective.']
  engineer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer responsible for the qualification.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the qualification report is no longer valid (nullable).']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for quantitative results in the report.']
  qualification_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the qualification (pass, fail, or conditional).']
  report_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the qualification report was issued.']
  report_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the qualification report.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the qualification report, used for tracking and reference.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Category of the qualification report indicating the subject of qualification.']
  report_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the qualification report (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number of the qualification report.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the report.']
  test_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the qualification testing completed.']
  test_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the qualification testing began.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the qualification report record.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of units meeting qualification criteria.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for qualification_report. Referenced by qualification_report_id.'
}

Table quality.customer_complaint {
  customer_complaint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for customer_complaint']

  cost_net decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net cost after adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the complaint record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative provided by the customer describing the issue.']
  dppm_impact decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured impact of the complaint expressed in DPPM.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'True if the complaint has been escalated to higher management.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Level of escalation applied to the complaint.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Result of the quality inspection related to the complaint.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot identifier associated with the affected product.']
  manufacturing_site_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the plant or fab where the product was produced.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by quality engineers.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the complaint for handling urgency.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'True if the complaint must be reported to a regulatory body.']
  regulatory_report_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory report filed for this complaint.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the complaint was resolved.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective or remedial action.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified underlying cause of the defect or failure.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the root cause category.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating indicating the impact of the complaint on product performance or safety.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the complaint was received.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the complaint.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the complaint record.']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complaint resulted in a warranty claim.']
  warranty_claim_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the associated warranty claim.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for customer_complaint. Referenced by related_customer_complaint_id.'
}

  typical_defect_density decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average defect density historically observed in this zone, expressed as defects per square centimeter.']

  zone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the zone\'s purpose, geometry, and typical usage.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the wafer zone (e.g., Center, Edge A).']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the zone within the fab workflow.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the logical location of the zone on the wafer.']
  zone_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the zone definition, incremented on each change.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for wafer_zone. Referenced by zone_id.'
}

  associated_product_family varchar [note: 'Product family code to which the defect cluster is most relevant.']

  is_customer_reported boolean [note: 'True if the defect cluster has been reported by a customer.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of the defect cluster definition.']

  measurement_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Typical measured size or intensity value for defects in this cluster.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the defect cluster.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the latest review process.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Primary root‑cause category for the defects in this cluster.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the defect cluster.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the defect cluster.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the defect cluster record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the defect cluster definition.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for defect_cluster. Referenced by cluster_id.'
}

  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classified confidentiality level of the document content.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the document record was first created in the system.']

  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the document must be retained according to policy.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person who reviewed and approved the document.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier indicating the iteration of the document (e.g., A, B, C, 1, 2).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system where the document originated (e.g., KLA ICOS, internal QA portal).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the document.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the quality document.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the document record.']
  version_number decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric version of the document, used for tracking major/minor changes.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for quality_document. Referenced by related_document_id.'
}

Table quality.test_plan {
  test_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for test_plan']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the test equipment or platform used for this plan.']
  related_test_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on test_plan (related_test_plan_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the test plan received formal approval.']
  approver varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved the test plan for release.']
  author varchar [note: 'Name of the person who originally authored the test plan.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard that the test plan adheres to (e.g., JEDEC, ISO 9001).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test plan record was first created in the system.']
  defect_threshold varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable number of defects per million parts (DPPM) for the plan.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the test plan purpose, scope, and methodology.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the test plan expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the test plan becomes operationally effective.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test plan was most recently run.']
  last_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent execution of the test plan.']
  measurement_units varchar [note: 'Units of measurement for key test parameters (e.g., V, A, Ohm).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the test plan.']
  pass_fail_criteria varchar [note: 'Textual definition of the conditions that determine pass or fail outcomes.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External code or number used to reference the test plan in manufacturing systems.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the test plan.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the test plan.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Product family or line to which the test plan applies.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for change control of the test plan.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the test plan.']
  target_device varchar [note: 'Identifier of the semiconductor device or die that the test plan validates.']
  test_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the test plan (e.g., qualification, production, debug).']
  test_duration_estimate_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated total execution time for the test plan, in minutes.']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'Physical or logical environment where the test plan is executed (e.g., cleanroom, probe station).']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Category of testing performed by the plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the test plan record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric version label (e.g., v1.2, R3) for the test plan.']
  wafer_size varchar [note: 'Standard wafer diameter (e.g., 200mm, 300mm) for which the plan is defined.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for test_plan. Referenced by related_test_plan_id.'
}

Table quality.control_plan {
  control_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for control_plan']
  superseded_control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on control_plan (superseded_control_plan_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the control plan.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or team that authored the control plan.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or justification for the most recent change to the control plan.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Quality or industry standard(s) the control plan adheres to.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the control plan record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the control plan purpose and scope.']
  dppm_actual decimal(10,4) [note: 'Observed defect rate measured under the control plan.']
  dppm_target decimal(10,4) [note: 'Target defect rate expressed in parts per million.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the control plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the control plan expires or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended plans).']
  inspection_tool varchar [note: 'Equipment or system used to perform the inspection (e.g., KLA ICOS).']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control plan is considered critical for product quality.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the control plan was last reviewed for relevance.']
  measurement_criteria varchar [note: 'Key measurement or characteristic monitored by the control plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or observations.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External code or number used to reference the control plan in manufacturing systems.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the control plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the control plan.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Manufacturing process step(s) covered by the control plan.']
  related_product_family varchar [note: 'Product family or line to which the control plan applies.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for reviewing the control plan.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the control plan.']
  sampling_rate varchar [note: 'Frequency or method of sampling for the control plan.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the control plan.']
  target_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Target numeric value for the measured characteristic.']
  tolerance_lower decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable deviation below the target value.']
  tolerance_upper decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable deviation above the target value.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used for the target and tolerance values.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the control plan record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the control plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for control_plan. Referenced by control_plan_id.'
}

Table test.test_program {
  test_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each test program record.']
  ate_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.ate_configuration. Business justification: Test programs are executed on specific ATE configurations; many programs can share one configuration, so add FK from test_program to ate_configuration.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budgeting process: each test program is funded by a specific cost center; required for cost allocation reports.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or manager who approved the program release.']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.technology_node. Business justification: Required for Test Program Definition: each test program is created for a specific technology node to ensure correct test parameters and compliance reporting.']
  finished_good_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_good. Business justification: Test program is defined for a specific finished‑good SKU to ensure test coverage aligns with product specifications.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Test Program Management requires linking each test program to the IC catalog entry it validates for release tracking.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Required for Test Program Planning: links each test program to the specific material (chip) defined in the Material Master, enabling traceability of test coverage to product specifications.']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: Test Engineering defines a test program per manufacturing flow; linking enables the Test Flow Specification report that maps each program to its process flow.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability tracking: test program results are evaluated against profit center performance for ROI analysis.']
  research_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.program. Business justification: Required for R&D Program Test Alignment Report linking each test program to its originating research program for budget and compliance tracking.']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Needed for Test Program Management to associate test results with the specific research project that defines the chip design, enabling milestone reporting.']
  actual_coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured fault‑coverage achieved after program execution.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the program was approved.']
  ate_platform varchar [note: 'Automatic Test Equipment platform compatible with the program.']
  atpg_tool varchar [note: 'ATPG software used to generate the test vectors (e.g., Synopsys TestMAX, Mentor Tessent).']
  bin_mapping_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bin‑mapping table associated with this program.']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of modifications to test flows, limits, or parameters.']
  correlation_study_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer‑to‑package correlation study associated with the program.']
  coverage_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired fault‑coverage percentage the program aims to achieve.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the program record was first created in the system.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date on which the program version was marked as deprecated.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Narrative assessment of the program\'s impact on yield, cost, and schedule.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the program version is deprecated.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or team responsible for the program.']
  parametric_test_data_reference varchar [note: 'Link to the parametric test data set used for limit verification.']
  program_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the test program purpose.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned code that uniquely identifies the test program across systems.']
  related_pcn_eco varchar [note: 'Reference to the Product Change Notification or Engineering Change Order linked to this program version.']
  release_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the program version was officially released for production use.']
  target_device varchar [note: 'Specific device part number or SKU the program validates.']
  target_device_family varchar [note: 'Family or series of devices the program is intended for (e.g., FinFET‑45nm, GAA‑7nm).']
  test_coverage_metric varchar [note: 'Metric used to express coverage (e.g., fault, code, timing).']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'Physical environment where the program is executed.']
  test_flow_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the test flow sequence executed by the program.']
  test_flow_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the underlying test flow definition.']
  test_limit_units varchar [note: 'Units for numeric test limits (e.g., mV, mA, ns).']
  test_limit_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the primary test limit associated with the program.']
  test_program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the test program.']
  test_program_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the test program.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Category of testing performed by the program (e.g., wafer probe, die sort, final test, burn‑in, system level test).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the program record.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation state of the program version.']
  version_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of changes introduced in this version.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier for the program (e.g., 1.0.3).']

  Note: 'Master record for ATPG-generated and manually authored test programs used on ATE platforms, with full version lifecycle management. Captures program name, target device family, test type (wafer probe, die sort, final test, burn-in, SLT), ATE platform compatibility, ATPG tool used, coverage targets, and program lifecycle status. Includes version-level tracking: version number, change description, changed test flows or limits, validation status, release date, approved-by engineer, associated PCN/ECO reference, and change impact assessment. SSOT for all test program definitions and their complete evolution history across test operations.'
}

Table test.ate_configuration {
  ate_configuration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the ATE configuration record.']
  ate_configuration_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying this ATE configuration.']
  bin_mapping_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the binning map used in this configuration.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the configuration must be re‑calibrated.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration state of the configuration.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason describing why the configuration was changed.']
  compliance_ear_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the configuration complies with Export Administration Regulations.']
  compliance_itar_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the configuration complies with International Traffic in Arms Regulations.']
  configuration_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the configuration in work orders and test plans.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration record was first created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the configuration is retired (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the configuration becomes valid for use.']
  hardware_revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for the ATE hardware bundle (e.g., Rev A, Rev B).']
  instrument_resource_allocation varchar [note: 'List of instrument resources (e.g., power supplies, signal generators) allocated to this configuration.']
  last_calibration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent calibration activity.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the configuration.']
  load_board_qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification status of the load board for production use.']
  load_board_revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier of the load board / Device Interface Board used.']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Scheduled maintenance window description (e.g., weekly Saturday 02:00‑04:00).']
  max_parallel_site_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of test sites that can be exercised simultaneously.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the configuration.']
  parametric_test_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parametric testing is enabled for this configuration.']
  pin_electronics_card_map varchar [note: 'Mapping description of pin‑to‑electronics card assignments for the configuration.']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'Type of test platform the configuration supports (wafer probe, final test, or reliability).']
  supported_device_families varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of device families (e.g., ASIC, SoC, FPGA) that this configuration can test.']
  test_coverage_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of functional test coverage provided by this configuration.']
  test_yield_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target yield percentage that the configuration aims to achieve during test runs.']
  tester_model varchar [note: 'Model of the Automatic Test Equipment platform (e.g., Advantest V93000).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the configuration record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the configuration record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier for the configuration (e.g., 1.2.0).']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the hardware warranty for this configuration expires.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the configuration record.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record for Automatic Test Equipment (ATE) platform setups used across wafer probe and final test operations. Captures tester model and platform type (e.g., Advantest V93000, Teradyne UltraFlex), hardware configuration revision, pin electronics card map, load board/DIB (Device Interface Board) revision and qualification status, instrument resource allocation, calibration status and due date, supported device families, maximum parallel site count, and configuration effective dates. SSOT for ATE setup definitions that test runs reference for full equipment traceability. Links to equipment domain for physical asset and maintenance tracking.'
}

Table test.probe_card {
  probe_card_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the probe card.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prober equipment to which the probe card is currently assigned.']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.technology_node. Business justification: Probe cards are qualified per technology node; linking ensures traceability of card usage to node specifications for yield and reliability analysis.']
  card_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the probe card used for identification in test engineering.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance classification applicable to the probe card.']
  contact_resistance_ohm decimal(6,3) [note: 'Measured electrical resistance of needle contacts in ohms.']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Acquisition cost of the probe card in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the probe card record was first created in the system.']
  die_site_layout varchar [note: 'Description of the die site arrangement on the wafer for this probe card.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the probe card last underwent scheduled maintenance.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent test run that utilized the probe card.']
