  maintenance_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Planned interval in months between preventive maintenance activities.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that manufactured the probe card.']
  needle_count varchar [note: 'Total number of probing needles/pins on the card.']
  needle_replacements varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of needle replacement events performed on the card.']
  next_maintenance_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next preventive maintenance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Any additional remarks or observations about the probe card.']
  pitch_um decimal(5,2) [note: 'Center‑to‑center spacing of needles in micrometers.']
  probe_card_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing special features, notes, or remarks about the probe card.']
  probe_card_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the probe card within test operations.']
  probe_card_type varchar [note: 'Design classification of the probe card (e.g., cantilever, vertical, MEMS, advanced).']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the probe card was qualified for production use.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the probe card qualification process.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Safety classification level for handling the probe card.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number that uniquely identifies the physical probe card.']
  supplier varchar [note: 'Supplier from which the probe card was procured.']
  touchdown_count varchar [note: 'Number of successful needle contacts recorded per test cycle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the probe card record.']
  usage_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total operational hours the probe card has been used in testing.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer\'s warranty for the probe card expires.']

  Note: 'Master record for probe cards used in wafer probing operations, owned by test engineering for qualification, assignment, and performance tracking. Captures probe card type (cantilever, vertical, MEMS, advanced), needle/pin count, pitch, technology node compatibility, die site layout, touchdown count, maintenance cycle, current condition status, and assigned prober tool. Tracks probe card lifecycle from incoming qualification through active use to retirement, including needle replacement history and contact resistance trending. Links to equipment domain for physical asset management while test domain owns operational qualification and assignment decisions.'
}

Table test.bin_definition {
  bin_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each bin definition record.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Bin definitions belong to a test program; many bins per program, so add FK from bin_definition to test_program.']
  bin_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the bin outcome according to JEDEC taxonomy.']
  bin_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code used in test programs to reference the bin.']
  bin_definition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the bin purpose and usage.']
  bin_definition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bin definition.']
  bin_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose of the bin.']
  bin_number varchar [note: 'Numeric identifier assigned to the test bin within a test program.']
  bin_sort_order varchar [note: 'Display order for bins in reports and UI.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bin definition record was first created.']
  device_family varchar [note: 'Product family or platform to which the bin definition applies.']
  disposition_rule varchar [note: 'Action to be taken for devices falling into this bin.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the bin definition becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the bin definition is retired (null if indefinite).']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Root cause or failure mechanism associated with the bin.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this bin is the default for its category.']
  parameter_set varchar [note: 'Set of parametric test parameters associated with the bin.']
  test_level varchar [note: 'Stage of testing where the bin is applied.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the bin definition record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bin definition.']
  yield_impact_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates how the bin affects overall wafer or lot yield.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the bin definition record.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining all test bin codes used in wafer sort and final test. Captures bin number, bin name, bin category (pass, functional fail, parametric fail, contact fail, leakage fail), disposition rule (ship, scrap, rework, hold), yield impact classification, and associated failure mode. Provides standardized bin taxonomy across all test programs and device families per JEDEC and internal standards.'
}

Table test.wafer_probe_run {
  wafer_probe_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each wafer probing execution event.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Billing & Reporting: wafer probe runs are billed to the customer account that owns the wafer lot, required for financial and compliance reports.']
  ate_configuration_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.ate_configuration.ate_configuration_id — Each probe run executes on a specific ATE configuration. Required for traceability and equipment correlation analysis.']
  correlation_study_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the correlation study linking wafer‑level and package‑level test data.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who operated the probe equipment for this run.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer prober equipment used.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Wafer Probe Run records must be associated with the IC catalog entry to trace probe results to the specific product version.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Wafer probe runs are performed on wafers from a given design project; yield analysis links probe data to the originating IC design project.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Internal order captures labor and equipment cost for each wafer probe run; used in cost‑of‑goods‑sold calculations.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Probe Yield Report requires linking each wafer probe run to the inventory wafer lot to update lot-level yield and inventory status.']
  probe_card_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.probe_card.probe_card_id — Probe card assignment is critical for wafer probe operations — probe card condition directly impacts yield and contact quality.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: Probe runs are scheduled after a specific process step; the Probe Run Scheduling dashboard needs the step reference to track yield impact.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.test_program.test_program_id — Every wafer probe execution must reference the specific test program (and version) being run. This is the fundamental operational link between test execution and test definition.']
  wafer_probe_card_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the probe card installed on the prober for this run.']
  wafer_test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Wafer probe runs are executed under a specific test program; replace version string with FK to test_program.']
  ate_configuration varchar [note: 'Configuration identifier for the Automatic Test Equipment used in the run.']
  bin_map_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the binning map used to classify die test results.']
  contact_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of dies that made electrical contact during probing.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wafer probing run completed or was terminated.']
  fail_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of dies that failed one or more test criteria.']
  gross_die_count varchar [note: 'Total die count before any filtering (e.g., before known‑good die certification).']
  parametric_test_data_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parametric test measurements were captured for this run.']
  pass_die_count varchar [note: 'Number of dies that passed all test criteria.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this run record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this run record.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments entered by the operator or quality engineer.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the probe run, typically following the pattern RUN-######.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wafer probing run started.']
  test_coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of functional tests executed relative to the total test plan.']
  total_die_count varchar [note: 'Total number of dies present on the wafer.']
  wafer_probe_run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the probe run.']
  wafer_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the wafer within the lot.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each wafer probing execution event. Records wafer lot ID, wafer sequence number, probe card used, ATE configuration, test program version, prober tool, start and end timestamps, total die count, pass die count, fail die count, contact yield, gross die count, and operator ID. Core operational event for wafer-level electrical test and die sort operations.'
}

Table test.unit_test_result {
  unit_test_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for unit_test_result']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Each die test result is linked to the device\'s ECCN classification for export‑control compliance reporting and analytics.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator who oversaw the test.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the test equipment (ATE) used.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot associated with the unit.']
  final_test_run_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.final_test_run.final_test_run_id — Package-level test results must link back to the final test run event. Required for lot disposition and DPPM tracking.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Unit test results must be traceable to the inventory wafer lot for quality tracking and stock accounting.']
  netlist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.netlist. Business justification: Die test results are correlated to the netlist version used for synthesis; debugging reports require the netlist_id reference.']
  primary_bin_definition_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.bin_definition.bin_definition_id — Every unit test result is assigned a bin code from the bin_definition master. This is the core yield classification link.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: Die test results are tied to the manufacturing step that produced the die; required for the Step‑Level SPC analysis report.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for Test Result Traceability to Procurement: links die test results to the purchase order that supplied the wafer lot, enabling warranty claims and quality audits.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Unit test results are produced by a test program; add FK to capture the originating program.']
  unit_bin_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bin definition record describing bin codes.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer containing the unit.']
  wafer_probe_run_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.wafer_probe_run.wafer_probe_run_id — Die-level test results from wafer probing must link back to the probe run event. Required for wafer map generation and lot traceability.']
  assembly_position varchar [note: 'Position identifier for multi-die or chiplet assemblies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result record was created in the system.']
  device_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the packaged device, if applicable.']
  hard_bin_code varchar [note: 'Hard bin classification assigned to the unit based on test outcome.']
  kgd_status varchar [note: 'Qualification status of the unit as a Known Good Die.']
  measurement_summary varchar [note: 'Concise summary of parametric measurement values for the unit.']
  measurement_units varchar [note: 'Units of the parametric measurements reported. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: C|V|Ohm|nA|pF|%|dB — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  parametric_flags varchar [note: 'Flags indicating which parametric tests were executed or passed.']
  pass_fail varchar [note: 'Overall pass or fail result for the unit.']
  retest_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the unit has been retested.']
  retest_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the unit was retested after an initial failure.']
  soft_bin_code varchar [note: 'Soft bin classification for detailed failure analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that generated the test result record.']
  test_condition varchar [note: 'Descriptive condition of the test environment.']
  test_result_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes associated with the test result.']
  test_result_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the test result format or schema.']
  test_stage varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating the test insertion stage where the result was captured.']
  test_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the unit was tested, in degrees Celsius.']
  test_time_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total duration of the test execution for the unit, in seconds.']
  test_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test was performed.']
  test_voltage_v decimal(6,2) [note: 'Voltage level applied during testing, in volts.']
  test_yield_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the unit is counted towards lot yield calculations.']
  unit_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the tested unit, expressed as wafer X/Y coordinates for wafer-level or serial number for package-level.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the test result record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing individual unit-level test results from all test insertions including wafer probing, die sort, final test, and system-level test (SLT). Records unit identifier (X/Y die coordinates for wafer-level or device serial/unit position for package-level), test insertion stage discriminator, assigned hard-bin and soft-bin codes per bin_definition, pass/fail status, total test time, parametric measurement summary flags, retest indicator and retest count, KGD (Known Good Die) qualification status, multi-die/chiplet assembly position, and test temperature/voltage condition. Serves as the single source of truth for all unit-level test outcomes across the entire test flow, enabling wafer map generation, unit-level yield analysis, full lot traceability, and die-to-package correlation. Aligns with STDF PTR/FTR/MPR record concepts.'
}

Table test.final_test_run {
  final_test_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique internal identifier for the package-level test execution event.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Delivery Acceptance: final test results are associated with the customer account to confirm product acceptance and trigger invoicing.']
  ate_configuration_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.ate_configuration.ate_configuration_id — Final test runs execute on specific ATE configurations. Required for equipment utilization and correlation analysis.']
  device_architecture_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.device_architecture. Business justification: Final Test Run must reference the device architecture defined in research to ensure test data is tied to the correct architecture for yield analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the operator who initiated the test run.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Final test of devices slated for export must be tied to the governing export license to satisfy USML/EAR control reporting.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific ATE equipment unit used.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot associated with the devices under test.']
  final_test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Final test runs are generated by a specific test program; add FK to capture this relationship.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Final test run outcomes are reported per IC product to support qualification, release, and compliance documentation.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Final test execution costs are booked to an internal order for expense tracking and profitability reporting.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Final test run outcomes are recorded against the inventory wafer lot to update inventory status and yield metrics.']
  packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to packaging.package_type. Business justification: Final test results are reported per package type; linking to package_type enables yield analysis and compliance reporting per JEDEC package family.']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: Final test runs validate a completed process flow; the Final Test Yield Summary links each run to its originating flow.']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Final test is executed on taped‑out silicon; the Final Test Yield report must reference the tapeout_id for traceability.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.test_program.test_program_id — Every final test execution references a specific test program version. Same fundamental link as wafer_probe_run to test_program.']
  ate_name varchar [note: 'Name or model of the ATE system executing the test.']
  bin_distribution varchar [note: 'Compact representation of test bin counts (e.g., JSON or delimited string).']
  boot_success_count varchar [note: 'Number of devices that successfully booted during SLT.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the operator or system.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the primary failure mode when the run fails.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test execution completed.']
  fail_count varchar [note: 'Number of devices that failed one or more test criteria.']
  final_test_run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test run.']
  handler_name varchar [note: 'Identifier of the handler module that loads devices into the ATE.']
  parametric_test_fail varchar [note: 'Count of parametric test points that failed.']
  parametric_test_pass varchar [note: 'Count of parametric test points that passed.']
  pass_count varchar [note: 'Number of devices that passed all test criteria.']
  power_consumption_mw decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured average power consumption during system‑level test, in milliwatts.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test run record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this test run record.']
  slt_board_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the board used for system‑level testing, if applicable.']
  socket_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the socket used to hold the device during testing.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test execution started.']
  test_location varchar [note: 'Code of the laboratory or floor where the test was performed.']
  test_program_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the ATE test program used for this run.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Aggregated result of the test run.']
  test_run_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the test run, used for tracking across systems.']
  test_shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the test run was executed.']
  test_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the test was performed, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  test_time_seconds decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total duration of the test execution for the device.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether the run is a final test or system‑level test (SLT).']
  total_sites varchar [note: 'Number of parallel test sites active on the ATE for this run.']
  yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Yield of the test run expressed as a percentage of passed devices.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for package-level test execution events on ATE including final test and system-level test (SLT). Captures device lot ID, package type, test type discriminator (final_test, SLT), test configuration (socket, ATE, handler, SLT board), test program version, test temperature, test time, pass/fail counts, bin distribution, power consumption (SLT), boot success (SLT), and test site count. Covers all post-packaging test operations distinct from wafer-level probing.'
}

Table test.parametric_measurement {
  parametric_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each parametric measurement record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Quality Compliance: parametric measurements are reported back to the owning customer account for quality assurance and regulatory compliance.']
  bin_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.bin_definition. Business justification: Parametric measurements reference a bin definition; replace ad‑hoc bin fields with FK to bin_definition.']
  calibration_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the calibration record applied to the equipment for this measurement.']
  die_wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual die on which the measurement was performed.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the test equipment (ATE, probe card, etc.) that generated the measurement.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Parametric measurements need the IC catalog reference to correlate measured values with product specifications.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Parametric measurements are associated with the inventory wafer lot for SPC and statistical analysis.']
  parametric_review_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the review.']
  primary_parametric_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the human operator overseeing the test run.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: Parametric measurements are captured after a defined process step; required for the Process Step Parameter Trending report.']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Parametric measurements are taken on final silicon; regulatory and reliability reports link each measurement to the tapeout that produced the device.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the test program or ATPG pattern set used for the measurement.']
  test_result_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the overall test result set for the die.']
  test_step_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific test step within the program.']
  unit_test_result_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.unit_test_result.unit_test_result_id — Parametric measurements are captured per unit under test. Must link to the parent unit result for traceability.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer containing the die measured.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the measurement event was captured by the test system.']
  lower_spec_limit decimal(20,6) [note: 'Lower acceptable bound for the parameter as defined by the product spec.']
  measured_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Numeric value recorded for the test parameter.']
  measurement_average_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Average of repeated measurements for the parameter.']
  measurement_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form comment entered by the operator or analyst.']
  measurement_condition_frequency_mhz decimal(12,3) [note: 'Frequency condition during the measurement, expressed in megahertz.']
  measurement_condition_temperature_c decimal(12,3) [note: 'Temperature condition during the measurement, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  measurement_condition_voltage_mv decimal(12,3) [note: 'Voltage condition applied during the measurement, expressed in millivolts.']
  measurement_evaluated_against_limit varchar [note: 'FK to test.test_limit.test_limit_id — Each parametric measurement is evaluated against specification limits defined in test_limit. This link enables pass/fail determination and SPC analysis.']
  measurement_flagged boolean [note: 'Indicates if the measurement was flagged for outlier review.']
  measurement_for_unit_result varchar [note: 'FK to test.die_test_result.die_test_result_id — Parametric measurements are captured for specific die/device test results. This is the detail-level link from measurement to the unit being tested.']
  measurement_location varchar [note: 'Physical location on the die where the measurement was taken.']
  measurement_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of the test, indicating whether the measurement is parametric or functional.']
  measurement_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality assessment of the measurement data.']
  measurement_repeat_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the parameter was measured in this test cycle.']
  measurement_review_status varchar [note: 'Current review status of the measurement.']
  measurement_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the measurement review was completed.']
  measurement_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this measurement within the test program.']
  measurement_source varchar [note: 'Source of the measurement data, e.g., probe card or automatic test equipment.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the measurement record.']
  measurement_std_dev decimal(12,6) [note: 'Statistical standard deviation of repeated measurements.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the physical measurement occurred (may differ from ingestion time).']
  measurement_tool_version varchar [note: 'Software/firmware version of the test tool that generated the measurement.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement is analog or digital.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(12,6) [note: 'Estimated uncertainty of the measured value.']
  parametric_against_limit varchar [note: 'FK to test.test_limit.test_limit_id — Each parametric measurement is evaluated against a test limit specification. Required for pass/fail determination and SPC.']
  parametric_checked_against_limit varchar [note: 'FK to test.test_limit.test_limit_id — Each parametric measurement is evaluated against a test limit. This FK enables limit-vs-actual analysis and SPC trending.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Result of the measurement against spec limits.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the measurement record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the measurement record.']
  test_parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific parametric test parameter (e.g., Vth, Idsat).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Engineering unit associated with the measured value. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: V|mV|A|mA|Ohm|C|K|Hz — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  upper_spec_limit decimal(20,6) [note: 'Upper acceptable bound for the parameter as defined by the product spec.']

  Note: 'Transactional record storing individual parametric test measurements captured during wafer probe and final test. Records test parameter name, measured value, lower specification limit (LSL), upper specification limit (USL), pass/fail status, measurement unit, test condition (voltage, temperature, frequency), and associated die or device result. Enables SPC analysis, parametric yield optimization, and process-test correlation studies.'
}

Table test.limit {
  limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the test limit record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Test limits are defined per IC catalog entry to ensure each product revision meets its specification envelope.']
  pdk_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.pdk. Business justification: Test limits are defined per process node; the Test Limit Specification requires the PDK identifier to ensure correct corner definitions.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ATPG or ATE program that consumes this limit.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the limit record.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the limit.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Chronological log of actions performed on the limit record.']
  bin_mapping_code varchar [note: 'Code linking the limit to a specific binning scheme.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why the limit was changed.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard governing the limit.']
  created_by_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for creating the limit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the limit record was first created.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the measurement data used for the limit.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the semiconductor device (e.g., ASIC, SoC, FPGA) to which the limit applies.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the limit becomes active for production testing.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the limit is no longer valid (null for open‑ended).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the limit is currently active in the test flow.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of the limit.']
  limit_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the limit.']
  limit_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the limit definition.']
  lower_spec_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable measured value for the parameter.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Category of measurement the limit belongs to.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the limit.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the parametric test parameter (e.g., Vdd, Idd).']
  product_revision varchar [note: 'Internal revision identifier of the product (e.g., A1, B2) linked to the limit.']
  regulatory_flag boolean [note: 'True if the limit is mandated by a regulatory requirement.']
  related_pcn_number varchar [note: 'Product Change Notification identifier that triggered the limit change.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for limit review.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the limit deviation.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of samples used to calculate the limit.']
  statistical_method varchar [note: 'Statistical approach used to derive the limit.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Nominal target value that the process aims to achieve.']
  test_condition_set varchar [note: 'Named set of test conditions (temperature, voltage, frequency) under which the limit is valid.']
  test_flow_name varchar [note: 'Name of the test flow or sequence where the limit is applied.']
  tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allowed percentage deviation from the target value.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the limit values. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: V|mV|A|mA|Ohm|kOhm|nF|pF|C|% — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the limit record.']
  upper_spec_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable measured value for the parameter.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the limit record, incremented on each change.']
  version_history varchar [note: 'JSON or delimited text capturing historical versions of the limit.']

  Note: 'Master record defining parametric test limits for each test parameter by device type, product revision, and test condition. Captures parameter name, LSL, USL, target value, measurement unit, test condition set, limit revision history, effective date, and approval status. SSOT for all test specification limits used across ATPG programs and ATE test flows. Supports limit change management and PCN-driven limit updates.'
}

Table test.coverage {
  coverage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each test coverage record.']
  device_type_ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key linking to the device type master data.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer or analyst who performed the last review.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Test coverage reports are generated per IC product to assess validation completeness before tape‑out.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Test coverage metrics are reported per design project; the Design Coverage Dashboard aggregates coverage by ic_design_project_id.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key linking to the master test program entity.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or observations about the coverage run.']
  correlation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score indicating correlation between wafer‑level and package‑level test results.']
  coverage_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of coverage measured (functional, parametric, structural, timing).']
  coverage_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the coverage metrics were measured.']
  coverage_method varchar [note: 'Method used to obtain coverage metrics.']
  coverage_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the coverage record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the coverage record was first created in the system.']
  defect_density decimal(10,4) [note: 'Number of defects per unit area observed during testing.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Logical classification of the device (e.g., ASIC, SoC, FPGA) for which coverage is measured.']
  dft_structure_coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Coverage of DFT structures such as scan chains, BIST, and boundary‑scan.']
  fault_coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall percentage of detectable faults covered by the test program.']
  iddq_coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of IDDQ (quiescent current) tests covered.']
  is_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the coverage record has been formally approved.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the coverage level is critical for tapeout readiness.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent formal review of the coverage data.']
  path_delay_coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of timing paths meeting delay specifications.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the coverage data (e.g., MES, PLM).']
  stuck_at_fault_coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of stuck‑at faults covered.']
  tapeout_ready boolean [note: 'True if the coverage meets the tapeout release criteria.']
  test_program_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the test program associated with this coverage record.']
  tool varchar [note: 'Name of the software tool used to generate coverage metrics.']
  tool_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the coverage analysis tool.']
  total_faults varchar [note: 'Total number of fault models considered for coverage analysis.']
  transition_fault_coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transition‑faults covered.']
  units varchar [note: 'Units for coverage metrics, typically percent.']
  untestable_fault_count varchar [note: 'Number of faults that cannot be tested with the current program.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the coverage record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for the coverage record.']
  yield_estimate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected silicon yield based on current coverage levels.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking test coverage metrics for each test program version by device type. Captures fault coverage percentage, stuck-at fault coverage, transition fault coverage, path delay coverage, IDDQ coverage, DFT structure coverage (scan chains, BIST, boundary scan), and untestable fault count. Links test program to design DFT intent and supports tapeout readiness reviews.'
}

Table test.reliability_test_run {
  reliability_test_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each reliability test run record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Warranty & Reliability Reporting: reliability test outcomes are linked to the customer account for warranty claims and reliability guarantees.']
  device_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot from which the tested devices were drawn.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the personnel who initiated or supervised the test run.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the test equipment (ATE, probe station) used for the run.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Reliability test runs must be linked to the IC catalog entry to track long‑term performance and warranty metrics per product.']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Reliability test runs are executed on a specific inventory wafer lot; linking enables lot-level reliability tracking.']
  primary_test_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.test_program.test_program_id — Reliability test executions (including burn-in) reference test programs for pre/post stress testing.']
  reliability_test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Reliability test runs are associated with the test program they validate; add FK to link them.']
  acceleration_factor double [note: 'Factor used to extrapolate stress results to normal operating conditions.']
  bias_condition varchar [note: 'Electrical bias applied to the device (e.g., Vdd=1.2V, ground), described in free text.']
  burn_in_board_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the burn‑in board or fixture used for the test.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Applicable industry standard or specification (e.g., JEDEC JESD47) governing the test.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test run record was first created in the system.']
  data_capture_rate_hz double [note: 'Sampling frequency of test data acquisition.']
  duration_hours double [note: 'Total time the device is subjected to the stress conditions, expressed in hours.']
  failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of devices that failed during the test run.']
  humidity_control_method varchar [note: 'Method used to maintain humidity (e.g., saturated salt solution, humidifier).']
  infant_mortality_rate double [note: 'Percentage of early‑life failures observed during the test.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to capture test data (e.g., ATE, probe).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the operator.']
  post_stress_yield_percent double [note: 'Yield percentage measured after stress exposure.']
  pre_stress_yield_percent double [note: 'Yield percentage measured before applying stress.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the qualification assessment per JEDEC standards.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of individual devices (dies) included in the test run.']
  screen_effectiveness_percent double [note: 'Effectiveness of the stress screen in removing latent defects, expressed as a percentage.']
  stress_humidity_percent double [note: 'Relative humidity level maintained during the stress test, expressed as a percentage.']
  stress_mode varchar [note: 'Pattern of stress application during the run.']
  stress_temperature_c double [note: 'Maximum temperature applied during the stress phase, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  stress_voltage_v double [note: 'Voltage level applied to the device under test during stress, expressed in volts.']
  temperature_ramp_rate_c_per_min double [note: 'Rate at which temperature was increased during ramp‑up.']
  test_batch_code varchar [note: 'Identifier grouping multiple test runs executed as a batch.']
  test_data_file_path varchar [note: 'File system path or URI to the raw test data file.']
  test_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the stress phase completed.']
  test_failure_mode varchar [note: 'Category of failure observed during the test.']
  test_failure_rate_percent double [note: 'Percentage of devices that failed out of the sample.']
  test_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or fab where the test was performed.']
  test_program_version varchar [note: 'Version of the test program or script executed.']
  test_result_file_hash varchar [note: 'Hash (e.g., SHA‑256) of the test result file for integrity verification.']
  test_result_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level textual summary of the test outcome (e.g., PASS, FAIL, PARTIAL).']
  test_run_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier assigned to the test run (e.g., TRT‑20230915‑001).']
  test_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the stress phase began.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the test run.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the category of reliability stress applied during the run.']
  test_yield_improvement_percent double [note: 'Improvement in yield attributable to the stress screening.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the test run record.']
  voltage_ramp_rate_v_per_sec double [note: 'Rate at which voltage was increased during ramp‑up.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for reliability, qualification, and burn-in stress screening test executions. Covers HTOL, HAST, ESD, latch-up, electromigration, and burn-in stress screens. Captures test type, stress conditions (temperature, voltage, humidity, bias), duration, sample size, device lot, burn-in board ID, failure count, acceleration factor, pre/post-stress test results, infant mortality rate, screen effectiveness, and qualification status per JEDEC standards. Consolidates all reliability and stress screening operations into a single transactional record.'
}

Table test.correlation_study {
  correlation_study_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the correlation study record.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Correlation studies compare test data to design simulations; they must reference the originating IC design project for accurate analysis.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Required for Correlation Study Traceability: associates each study with the Material Master record of the device under test, supporting regulatory reporting and root‑cause analysis.']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Correlation studies are conducted within a research project; linking provides traceability for analysis reports and IP filing decisions.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.test_program.test_program_id — Correlation studies compare results between wafer probe and final test programs. Must reference both program versions being correlated.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the study was formally approved for use.']
  confidence_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level (e.g., 95.00) associated with the study results.']
  correlation_coefficient decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric coefficient (e.g., Pearson r) representing strength of correlation for each parameter.']
  correlation_methodology varchar [note: 'Statistical or analytical method used to derive the correlation.']
  correlation_study_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the study.']
  cpk_impact decimal(10,4) [note: 'Impact on process capability index (Cpk) resulting from the correlation adjustment.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the study record was first created in the system.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the study must be revalidated or retired.']
  guard_band_recommendation varchar [note: 'Suggested guard‑band values for test limits based on study findings.']
  insertion_pair varchar [note: 'Description of the two test insertions being correlated (e.g., wafer‑probe vs final‑test).']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study contains confidential information that requires restricted handling.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the study record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments, observations, or special considerations captured by the study owner.']
  parameter_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct test parameters included in the correlation analysis.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of devices or test points used in the study.']
  study_code varchar [note: 'External business code or identifier assigned to the study (e.g., SOP‑CS‑2024‑001).']
  study_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the study used for reporting and search.']
  study_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the study methodology: parametric, functional, or combined.']
  systematic_offset decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured systematic offset between the two test insertions.']
  test_platform varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the ATE platform used (e.g., Advantest V93000).']
  test_site varchar [note: 'Physical location or fab where the test was performed.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number of the study record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the study record.']

  Note: 'Master record for test correlation studies comparing results across different test insertions, ATE platforms, or test sites. Primarily supports wafer-probe-to-final-test correlation for KGD qualification and test cost reduction, but also covers tester-to-tester correlation, site-to-site correlation, and multi-insertion guard-band optimization. Captures study name, device type, correlation methodology (parametric, functional, combined), insertion pair under study, correlation coefficient by parameter, systematic offset values, Cpk impact analysis, guard-band recommendations, sample size and confidence level, study status, approval date, and expiration/revalidation schedule. Enables test program optimization, probe-only shipping decisions, and OSAT transfer qualification.'
}

Table test.insertion {
  insertion_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the test insertion definition.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: Test Insertion Planning requires assigning a specific Fab Tool; the Insertion Schedule report lists the Fab Tool needed for each insertion.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.test_program']
  ate_platform_type varchar [note: 'Type of Automatic Test Equipment platform required.']
  bin_mapping_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the binning scheme applied to test results at this insertion.']
  conditional_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates the insertion is executed only when specific pre‑conditions are met.']
  correlation_study_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data from this insertion is used in wafer‑to‑package correlation studies.']
  cost_per_unit_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated cost incurred for each unit processed at this insertion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the insertion record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the insertion definition becomes active in the test flow.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the insertion definition is retired; null if still active.']
  handler_requirement varchar [note: 'Specifies the handler or prober needed for the insertion.']
  insertion_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used to reference the insertion within the test flow.']
  insertion_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the test insertion step (e.g., wafer probe, die sort).']
  insertion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the insertion definition.']
  insertion_type varchar [note: 'Category of the insertion step within the manufacturing test flow.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the insertion must be executed for every device.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest temperature allowed during the insertion test.']
  min_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest temperature allowed during the insertion test.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the insertion.']
  optional_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the insertion can be skipped without affecting product qualification.']
  sequence_order varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the insertion in the overall test flow.']
  skip_rule_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable rule that defines when this insertion may be skipped.']
  test_coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of functional or parametric coverage provided by this insertion.']
  test_parameter_set varchar [note: 'Name of the parameter set applied during the test program execution.']
  test_time_budget_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed test time for this insertion, in seconds.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the insertion record.']
  yield_gate_criteria_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum pass‑rate percentage required to allow progression to the next step.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the complete test insertion architecture for each device type within the manufacturing flow. Captures insertion name (wafer probe, die sort, pre-burn-in, burn-in, post-burn-in, final test, SLT, outgoing QA), sequence order in the flow, mandatory vs. optional vs. conditional flag, assigned test program reference, required ATE platform type, handler/prober requirement, yield gate criteria (minimum pass rate to proceed), test time budget allocation, cost per unit at this insertion, and insertion-level skip rules. SSOT for the test flow architecture governing which tests are performed at which manufacturing stage, in what sequence, and under what conditions. Critical for test cost modeling and flow optimization.'
}

Table test.adaptive_test_flow {
  adaptive_test_flow_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each adaptive test flow configuration.']
  correlation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.correlation_study. Business justification: Adaptive test flow references a correlation study; replace string reference with FK to correlation_study for referential integrity.']
  fallback_adaptive_test_flow_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on adaptive_test_flow (fallback_adaptive_test_flow_id)']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: Adaptive test flows are generated per process flow to optimize test time; the Adaptive Flow Configuration tool needs this FK.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to test.test_program.test_program_id — Adaptive test flows modify the execution of a base test program. Must reference which program the adaptive rules apply to.']
  adaptive_flow_at_insertion varchar [note: 'FK to test.test_insertion.test_insertion_id — Adaptive test rules are applied at specific test insertion points in the manufacturing flow.']
  adaptive_rule_set varchar [note: 'JSON or XML representation of the adaptive rules (skip rules, limit adjustments, ordering logic).']
  adaptive_test_flow_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the flow logic, purpose, and scope.']
  adaptive_test_flow_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the flow configuration.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON log of significant changes to the flow configuration for compliance auditing.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Applicable industry or regulatory standard governing the test flow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the flow record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level for the flow record as defined by corporate data policy.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the flow becomes active for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the flow is retired or superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  effectiveness_metric varchar [note: 'Primary metric used to evaluate the adaptive flow\'s success.']
  flow_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the flow in systems and reports.']
  flow_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the adaptive test flow.']
  flow_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the flow is fully adaptive, static, or a hybrid of both.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the flow has been deprecated and should no longer be used.']
  last_evaluation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent performance evaluation of the flow.']
  limit_adjustment_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy used to tighten or loosen test limits based on inline data.']
  ml_model_name varchar [note: 'Name of the predictive model referenced for test optimization.']
  ml_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the referenced ML model.']
  multi_site_optimization_params varchar [note: 'Parameters governing test flow distribution across multiple test sites.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  predictive_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to the predictive analytics model used.']
  quality_escape_risk_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable risk of defective dies escaping test, expressed as a percentage.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing overall risk associated with the flow configuration.']
  rule_evaluation_logic varchar [note: 'Description of how adaptive rules are evaluated (e.g., priority order, conflict resolution).']
  skip_rule_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of conditions under which tests may be skipped.']
  test_order_optimization varchar [note: 'Parameters that define the optimal sequencing of test items.']
  test_time_reduction_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage reduction in overall test time compared to baseline.']
  trigger_conditions varchar [note: 'Logical conditions that activate the adaptive flow (e.g., prior bin result, parametric thresholds).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the flow record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the flow record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for change management and release tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the flow record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining adaptive and intelligent test flow configurations that dynamically adjust test content based on prior test results, inline parametric data, or predictive models. Captures adaptive rule sets (skip rules, test ordering optimization, limit tightening/loosening), triggering conditions, ML model references for predictive test, multi-site optimization parameters, test time reduction targets, quality escape risk thresholds, and effectiveness metrics. Supports modern test methodologies including good-die-bad-die classification, adaptive limit setting, and AI-driven test optimization programs. SSOT for all dynamic test flow logic that modifies standard test program execution based on real-time or historical data.'
}

Table test.program_assignment {
  program_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the test_program_assignment association']
  inventory_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the wafer lot']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the test program']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date the test program was scheduled for the wafer lot']
  program_assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the test program assignment for the wafer lot']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment of a test program to a wafer lot. It captures when the program was scheduled for the lot and the current status of that assignment. Each record links one test_program to one inventory_wafer_lot.. Existence Justification: In semiconductor fabs, each wafer lot can be tested with multiple test programs over its lifecycle, and each test program is applied to many wafer lots. The scheduling of these programs is managed as a distinct assignment record that captures the assignment date and status. Humans create, update, and delete these assignment records as part of the test planning process.'
}

Table test.test_case {
  test_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for test_case']
  parent_test_case_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on test_case (parent_test_case_id)']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Test cases belong to a test program; adding test_program_id FK eliminates silo and enables navigation.']
  bin_mapping_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the binning scheme associated with this test case.']
  category varchar [note: 'Level at which the test is applied, such as wafer‑level, die‑level, package‑level, or system‑level.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the test case within the test management system.']
  coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of functional or parametric specifications covered by this test case.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test case record was first created in the system.']
  default_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Standard execution time expected for the test case, in seconds.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the test purpose, methodology, and any special considerations.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the test case becomes valid for use in production.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the test case is no longer valid; null if open‑ended.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test case is executed automatically by ATE without manual intervention.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the test case used in reports and dashboards.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name of the engineering group or individual responsible for maintaining the test case.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority indicating the importance of the test case for production release.']
  required_equipment varchar [note: 'List of ATE or probe stations required to execute the test case.']
  specification_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed test specification document stored in the PLM system.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the test case definition.']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired yield percentage that the test case aims to achieve.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of test performed, indicating the nature of the measurement or stimulus.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the test case record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for test_case. Referenced by test_case_id.'
}

Table test.test_step {
  test_step_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for test_step']
  prerequisite_test_step_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on test_step (prerequisite_test_step_id)']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the test program that includes this step.']
  bin_mapping_required boolean [note: 'True if the step\'s result must be mapped to a yield bin.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the test step record was first created in the system.']
  default_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Typical execution time for the step when run under standard conditions.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the test step definition is valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the test step definition is retired (null if indefinite).']
  equipment_required varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ATE or fixture needed to execute the step.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if failure of this step causes the entire test to be aborted.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the primary measurement captured by the step.']
  pass_fail_criteria varchar [note: 'Rule or threshold that determines whether the step passes or fails.']
  step_category varchar [note: 'Phase of the overall test flow where the step occurs.']
  step_code varchar [note: 'Compact alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the step within a test program.']
  step_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose, methodology, and expected outcome of the step.']
  step_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the test step as used in test programs and reports.']
  step_order varchar [note: 'Zero‑based sequence number indicating the step\'s position in the test program.']
  step_type varchar [note: 'Category of the test step based on its functional purpose.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the test step record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for test_step. Referenced by test_step_id.'
}

Table equipment.fab_tool {
  fab_tool_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fab_tool']
  capex_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_request. Business justification: CAPEX approval links each purchased tool to its request for audit and funding traceability.']
  certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.certification. Business justification: Equipment certification management (ISO, safety) mandates a FK from fab tool to the certification record for auditability.']
  chips_act_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.chips_act_obligation. Business justification: CHIPS Act reporting ties each capital equipment purchase to a specific obligation record for domestic production compliance.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for OEE and maintenance cost reporting per cost center; finance tracks tool expenses by cost center, obvious for budgeting.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Export classification (ECCN) of a fab tool is required for export‑license determination and regulatory filing.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Export Control process requires each fab tool to be linked to its export license for legal shipment and usage tracking.']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.technology_node. Business justification: REQUIRED: Tool‑node compatibility matrix used in capacity planning and regulatory compliance reporting; linking each fab_tool to its supported technology_node is standard in semiconductor fabs.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Fixed‑asset register must reference each fab tool for depreciation schedules and asset audits.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Depreciation and expense posting need a GL account per tool; finance GL accounts are used in asset accounting reports.']
  oem_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: PROCUREMENT: OEM supplier needed for purchase contracts and warranty compliance; experts track OEM supplier per tool.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis attributes tool usage to profit centers; required for product line margin calculations.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Asset Management assigns a responsible engineer to each fab tool; required for ownership, accountability, and compliance reporting.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the asset within the enterprise.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Internal asset tag used for inventory and tracking within the enterprise.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent calibration activity.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next calibration.']
  capacity_wafer_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum number of wafers the tool can process per hour.']
  capital_expenditure_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial capital cost recorded for the asset.']
  cleanroom_class varchar [note: 'Cleanroom classification where the tool operates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the data lake.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Date depreciation of the asset ends (end of useful life).']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date depreciation of the asset begins.']
  energy_consumption_kwh_per_year decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated electricity usage per year.']
  fab_site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the fab location where the tool is installed.']
  fab_tool_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the equipment, including special features.']
  fab_tool_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the equipment (e.g., "EUV Scanner 1").']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware version installed on the tool.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the equipment was first installed in the fab.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which preventive maintenance was performed.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle phase of the equipment.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Planned interval between preventive maintenance activities.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model designation assigned by the OEM.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average operating time between failures.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair the tool after a failure.']
  oee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated OEE expressed as a percentage.']
  power_rating_kw decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum electrical power consumption of the tool.']
  process_node_compatibility varchar [note: 'Semiconductor process nodes the tool can support.']
  purchase_date date [note: 'Date the equipment was purchased or capitalized.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status with applicable regulations.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number uniquely identifying the physical unit.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Current version of the tool\'s control software.']
  tool_subtype varchar [note: 'Technology subtype describing the process method. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: euv|duv|cvd|pvd|ald|plasma|chemical — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tool_type varchar [note: 'High‑level functional category of the equipment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lithography|deposition|etch|cmp|metrology|ate|ion_implanter — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the manufacturer warranty ends.']

  Note: 'Master record for all semiconductor manufacturing equipment assets including lithography scanners (EUV/DUV), CVD/PVD/ALD deposition systems, etch chambers, CMP tools, ion implanters, ATE platforms, and metrology/inspection systems. Authoritative SSOT for tool identity, classification, OEM specifications, process node compatibility, FAB location, install date, and asset lifecycle status. Sourced from Applied Materials SmartFactory MES and SAP S/4HANA Fixed Assets.'
}

Table equipment.tool_chamber {
  tool_chamber_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tool chamber.']
  fab_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab where the chamber is installed.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Chamber operation schedule tracks the primary operator; needed for shift planning and OEE attribution.']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the multi‑chamber tool that houses this chamber.']
  process_integration_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.process_integration_run. Business justification: Process Integration Run includes chamber‑specific parameters; linking to tool chamber enables performance and maintenance impact tracking.']
  vendor_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: PROCUREMENT: Chamber vendor is a supplier; linking enables vendor performance reporting and spare‑part sourcing.']
  audit_last_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit for the chamber.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the chamber was last calibrated.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration condition of the chamber.']
  chamber_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the chamber by the manufacturer or fab.']
  chamber_lifetime_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative operating hours the chamber has logged.']
  chamber_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the process chamber.']
  chamber_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status (e.g., fault code, maintenance activity).']
  chamber_type varchar [note: 'Category of the chamber based on its primary process function.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory and internal compliance state of the chamber.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the chamber record was first created in the system.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of the embedded firmware controlling the chamber.']
  gas_flow_rate_sccm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard cubic centimeters per minute flow rate for process gases.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the chamber was first installed in the fab.']
  last_calibration_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent calibration (e.g., pass, fail, deviation values).']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the chamber was last inspected for wear or damage.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance event.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the chamber record.']
  location varchar [note: 'Specific location within the fab (e.g., line, bay, floor).']
  maintenance_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Standard interval in days between scheduled maintenance events.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model designation of the chamber.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average operating hours between failures for the chamber.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair the chamber after a failure.']
  next_maintenance_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next maintenance activity based on the maintenance cycle.']
  oee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated OEE metric for the chamber (percentage).']
  pressure_setpoint_pa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target pressure setting for the chamber during operation.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the chamber was last qualified.']
  qualification_result varchar [note: 'Detailed outcome of the qualification test (e.g., pass, fail, notes).']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent qualification run for the chamber.']
  safety_lock_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the chamber\'s safety interlock.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number for the chamber.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the control software used by the chamber.']
  temperature_setpoint_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target temperature setting for the chamber during operation.']
  throughput_pph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum parts‑per‑hour the chamber can process under nominal conditions.']
  tool_chamber_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the chamber\'s purpose, capabilities, or special notes.']
  tool_chamber_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the chamber.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer\'s warranty for the chamber ends.']

  Note: 'Individual process chamber or module within a multi-chamber fab tool (e.g., a CVD cluster tool with 4 deposition chambers, or an etch system with multiple etch modules). Tracks chamber-level qualification status, process recipe assignments, chamber-specific utilization, and maintenance history independently from the parent tool. Essential for chamber-level SPC and yield correlation in multi-chamber tools.'
}

Table equipment.tool_qualification {
  tool_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each tool qualification record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer responsible for the qualification.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: QUALIFICATION COMPLIANCE report requires linking each tool qualification to the specific product family it supports, enabling audit of qualification status per family.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Tool qualification reports must reference the specific design project they validate, required for the \'Design Qualification Management\' process.']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment (lithography scanner, deposition system, etc.) being qualified.']
  primary_tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific chamber or module within the tool that was qualified.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the process recipe used during qualification.']
  tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.tool_chamber.tool_chamber_id — Chamber-level qualification is standard practice for multi-chamber tools. A specific chamber may be disqualified while others remain active.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the qualification was formally approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the process engineer or manager who approved the qualification.']
  baseline_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target baseline value that the tool must meet or exceed.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the measurement tool was last calibrated.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration status of the measurement tool.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the change control request linked to this qualification.']
  compliance_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance sign‑off status for the qualification.']
  compliance_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance document associated with the qualification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification record was created in the system.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to detailed qualification documentation or report.']
  equipment_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number of the equipment.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tool is on the critical production path.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance on the equipment.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment maintenance is current.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the qualification metric.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations captured during qualification.']
  oee_impact decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated impact of the qualification on Overall Equipment Effectiveness, expressed as a percentage.']
  process_node varchar [note: 'Technology node (e.g., 7nm, 5nm) for which the tool is qualified.']
  qualification_end_date date [note: 'Date when the qualification activity completed.']
  qualification_location varchar [note: 'Plant or fab location where the qualification was performed.']
  qualification_protocol varchar [note: 'Name or code of the protocol used to qualify the tool.']
  qualification_reason varchar [note: 'Business driver for performing the qualification.']
  qualification_start_date date [note: 'Date when the qualification activity began.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification process.']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Category of qualification performed on the tool.']
  qualification_validity_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the qualification remains valid from the start date.']
  qualification_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the qualification record, incremented on re‑qualifications.']
  result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the qualification.']
  result_metric_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the result metric.']
