  result_metric_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured value of the primary qualification metric (e.g., overlay error).']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with the qualification outcome.']
  tolerance decimal(12,4) [note: 'Acceptable deviation from the baseline value.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the qualification record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the qualification expires and must be renewed.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the qualification is considered valid for production.']

  Note: 'Qualification and certification records for fab tools and chambers against specific process nodes, recipes, and product families. Captures qualification type (initial qual, re-qual, process change qual), qualification protocol, pass/fail criteria, baseline metrology results, approving process engineer, and qualification validity period. A tool must be qualified before it can run production wafer lots. Sourced from Oracle Agile PLM and SmartFactory MES.'
}

Table equipment.pm_schedule {
  pm_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the preventive maintenance schedule record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the schedule record.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — PM schedules must reference the tool they apply to. This drives work order generation and maintenance planning.']
  pm_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab tool or chamber to which the schedule applies.']
  required_spare_part_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the spare part that must be available for the maintenance.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard that the maintenance activity must satisfy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule record was first created in the system.']
  estimated_downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned equipment downtime in minutes for the maintenance activity.']
  interval_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the interval value (day, week, month, wafer, or run).']
  interval_value varchar [note: 'Numeric value defining the interval between maintenance events (e.g., 500 wafers, 1000 runs, 30 days).']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment is classified as critical for production (true) or not (false).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the schedule record.']
  last_performed_date date [note: 'Date on which the maintenance activity was last executed.']
  maintenance_window_end timestamp [note: 'Planned end time of the maintenance window.']
  maintenance_window_start timestamp [note: 'Planned start time of the maintenance window.']
  next_scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for the next execution of the maintenance.']
  oee_impact_estimate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated impact of the maintenance on Overall Equipment Effectiveness expressed as a percentage.']
  pm_procedure_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or document ID for the detailed preventive maintenance procedure.']
  pm_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the maintenance activity and its purpose.']
  pm_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the schedule.']
  pm_type varchar [note: 'Category of preventive maintenance activity (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly, wafer‑count‑based, run‑count‑based).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the maintenance activity.']
  required_technician_skill_level varchar [note: 'Skill level required of the technician performing the maintenance.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to uniquely reference the schedule across systems.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the maintenance schedule.']
  scheduling_constraint varchar [note: 'Business rule or production window constraint that influences when the PM can be run.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the schedule record.']
  work_order_template_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work‑order template used to generate PM work orders.']

  Note: 'Preventive maintenance (PM) schedule master defining planned PM activities for each fab tool or chamber. Specifies PM type (daily, weekly, monthly, wafer-count-based, run-count-based), PM procedure reference, estimated downtime window, required spare parts, required technician skill level, and scheduling constraints relative to production WIP. Drives the PM work order generation cadence in SAP PM module.'
}

Table equipment.maintenance_event {
  maintenance_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the maintenance event record.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.ar_invoice. Business justification: Chargeback for maintenance services requires linking each maintenance_event to the generated invoice for cost allocation and audit compliance.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary technician who performed the work.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab floor or site where the equipment resides.']
  maintenance_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the fab tool or chamber undergoing maintenance.']
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scheduled maintenance plan governing this event.']
  maintenance_spare_part_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.spare_part.spare_part_id — Maintenance events consume spare parts. This link enables parts consumption tracking and TCO calculation.']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.pm_schedule.pm_schedule_id — Scheduled PM events must link back to the PM schedule that triggered them, enabling PM compliance tracking.']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — Every maintenance event must reference the tool serviced. Core operational FK for maintenance history and MTTR calculation.']
  primary_spare_part_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.spare_part.spare_part_id — Maintenance events consume spare parts. This link enables parts consumption tracking and inventory depletion.']
  baseline_change_flag boolean [note: 'True if the maintenance altered the equipment baseline for qualification.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by technicians or supervisors.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework applicable to the maintenance activity.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  downtime_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total equipment downtime measured in minutes.']
  eco_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ECO that drove the modification, if applicable.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the maintenance work was completed.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier or ticket number assigned to the maintenance event.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance activity.']
  labor_cost_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of labor based on labor hours and rate.']
  labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total technician labor time recorded for the event.']
  maintenance_category varchar [note: 'Strategic classification of the maintenance approach.']
  maintenance_event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the maintenance event.']
  oee_impact_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated impact on Overall Equipment Effectiveness expressed as a percent.']
  parts_cost_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate cost of all spare parts and consumables used.']
  performance_improvement_target varchar [note: 'Planned performance gain (e.g., throughput increase) resulting from the event.']
  post_config_version varchar [note: 'Snapshot or version identifier of equipment configuration after maintenance.']
  pre_config_version varchar [note: 'Snapshot or version identifier of equipment configuration before maintenance.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the maintenance event record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the maintenance event record.']
  repair_action_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of the corrective actions performed during the event.']
  requalification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal re‑qualification is needed after the event.']
  requalification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required re‑qualification process.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the underlying cause of failure.']
  root_cause_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the specific root cause identified.']
  safety_incident_description varchar [note: 'Details of any safety incident associated with the maintenance.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'True if the maintenance event involved a safety incident.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the maintenance work actually began.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the technician responsible for the maintenance.']
  total_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Combined cost of parts, labor, and any additional charges.']
  trigger_source varchar [note: 'Origin that caused the maintenance event to be created.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier that last modified the record.']
  upgrade_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the event involved a hardware, software, or firmware upgrade.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every maintenance activity executed on a fab tool or chamber, including preventive maintenance (PM), corrective maintenance (CM), emergency breakdown repair, engineering-driven modifications, tool upgrades/retrofits, and hardware/software modifications. Captures event type, trigger (scheduled PM, alarm, operator-reported, FDC event, ECO), start/end timestamps, actual downtime duration, root cause classification (5-why, fishbone), repair actions taken, spare parts and consumables consumed (part ID, quantity, unit cost, planned vs unplanned), technician IDs, upgrade/retrofit details (upgrade type, ECO reference, pre/post configuration, performance improvement targets, baseline change flag), and return-to-service sign-off including re-qualification requirements. SSOT for tool downtime attribution, MTTR/MTBF calculation, parts consumption tracking, upgrade history, and total cost of ownership. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA PM and SmartFactory MES.'
}

Table equipment.tool_downtime {
  tool_downtime_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each downtime event record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator on duty when the downtime was reported.']
  maintenance_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.maintenance_event.maintenance_event_id — Downtime events caused by maintenance must link to the maintenance event for root cause tracing and planned vs unplanned analysis.']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the fab tool or chamber that experienced the downtime.']
  tool_maintenance_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order associated with the downtime, if any.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or observations related to the downtime event.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Brief description of the corrective action performed to resolve the issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the downtime record was first created in the system.']
  downtime_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the downtime event expressed in whole minutes.']
  downtime_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the tool exited the downtime state.']
  downtime_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the root cause of the downtime.']
  downtime_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of why the tool was down.']
  downtime_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the tool entered the downtime state.']
  downtime_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the downtime according to the SEMI E10 state model.']
  impact_wip_lot_count varchar [note: 'Number of work‑in‑process lots affected by the downtime.']
  oee_impact_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated reduction in Overall Equipment Effectiveness caused by the downtime, expressed as a percentage.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Team or role accountable for handling the downtime event.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category describing the underlying cause of the downtime.']
  scheduled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime was planned (true) or unplanned (false).']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the downtime event based on impact and urgency.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Production shift during which the downtime occurred.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the downtime data.']
  state_after varchar [note: 'Equipment state after the downtime event concluded.']
  state_before varchar [note: 'Equipment state immediately prior to the downtime event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the downtime record.']

  Note: 'Granular downtime records for each fab tool and chamber classified per SEMI E10 equipment state model (Productive, Standby, Engineering, Scheduled Downtime, Unscheduled Downtime, Non-Scheduled). Captures state transition timestamps, downtime reason code, responsible party (maintenance, engineering, facilities, scheduling), and impact on WIP lots. Feeds OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation and capacity planning models.'
}

Table equipment.calibration_record {
  calibration_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each calibration record.']
  calibration_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the technician who performed the calibration.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — Calibration records must reference the tool or instrument being calibrated. Required for ISO 9001 traceability.']
  primary_calibration_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the fab equipment that was calibrated.']
  tertiary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — Calibration records must reference the tool/instrument calibrated. Required for ISO 9001 traceability.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the wafer on which the calibration measurement was taken.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Planned interval in days between successive calibrations for this equipment.']
  calibration_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the calibration.']
  calibration_record_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the calibration record.']
  calibration_report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed calibration report stored in the document repository.']
  calibration_result_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the calibration outcome for downstream analytics.']
  calibration_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the record (e.g., KLA ICOS, SmartFactory MES).']
  calibration_standard varchar [note: 'Reference standard used for calibration (e.g., NIST traceable, SEMI E30).']
  calibration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the calibration activity was performed.']
  calibration_type varchar [note: 'Method of calibration applied to the equipment.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or observations captured by the technician.']
  compliance_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance clause (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949) satisfied by this calibration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calibration record was first created in the system.']
  location varchar [note: 'Plant or fab line where the equipment resides (e.g., FAB1, LineA).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot associated with the calibration (if applicable).']
  measured_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Value recorded by the instrument during calibration.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(12,4) [note: 'Estimated uncertainty of the measured value.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the measured and nominal values (e.g., nanometers, volts).']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next calibration must be performed.']
  nominal_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target or nominal value that the equipment should meet.']
  pass_fail_result varchar [note: 'Pass/Fail outcome of the calibration activity.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the calibration technician.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the calibration record.']

  Note: 'Calibration and metrology verification records for fab tools and measurement instruments. Tracks calibration type (in-situ, offline, periodic), calibration standard used, measured vs. nominal values, calibration pass/fail result, calibration interval, next due date, calibrating technician, and traceability to NIST or SEMI standards. Mandatory for ISO 9001 and IATF 16949 compliance. Sourced from KLA ICOS and SmartFactory MES calibration modules.'
}

Table equipment.tool_alarm {
  tool_alarm_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each alarm event generated by semiconductor manufacturing equipment.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key of the operator who acknowledged or acted on the alarm.']
  maintenance_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance work order or corrective action triggered by the alarm, if any.']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key of the equipment (e.g., lithography scanner, CMP tool) that generated the alarm.']
  tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.tool_chamber.tool_chamber_id — Chamber-level alarm sourcing is critical for multi-chamber tools where one chamber may fault while others continue.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when an operator or system acknowledged the alarm.']
  alarm_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the alarm type for reporting and analytics.']
  alarm_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific alarm condition as defined by the equipment vendor.']
  alarm_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in seconds from alarm onset to clearance.']
  alarm_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping identifier for related alarms (e.g., temperature excursions, pressure drops).']
  alarm_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the alarm condition provided by the equipment.']
  alarm_origin varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the alarm was generated automatically by the tool or manually triggered.']
  alarm_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority assigned to the alarm for routing and escalation (higher = more urgent).']
  alarm_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the alarm indicating impact on production (critical, major, minor, warning, info).']
  alarm_source varchar [note: 'Logical source within the tool that raised the alarm (e.g., chamber, subsystem, sensor, control system).']
  alarm_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the alarm event.']
  alarm_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alarm condition was first detected on the tool.']
  clearance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alarm condition was cleared or resolved.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed corrective action taken to resolve the alarm condition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alarm record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  equipment_location varchar [note: 'Plant or fab line location where the equipment resides (e.g., FAB1_LINEA).']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Category of the equipment (e.g., lithography_scanner, etcher, CMP_tool).']
  oee_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alarm had a measurable impact on Overall Equipment Effectiveness.']
  predicted_failure_flag boolean [note: 'True if the alarm is identified by predictive models as a precursor to equipment failure.']
  repeat_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the same alarm code has recurred within a defined time window.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Textual description of the underlying cause determined after investigation.']
  severity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the quantitative severity of the alarm as calculated by vendor logic.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Shift during which the alarm occurred (e.g., A, B, C, D).']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or module that supplied the alarm (e.g., MES, KLA ICOS, Camstar).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the alarm record.']

  Note: 'Real-time and historical alarm events generated by fab tools and process equipment. Captures alarm ID, alarm code, alarm severity (critical, major, minor, warning), alarm source (chamber, subsystem, sensor), alarm message text, onset timestamp, acknowledgment timestamp, clearance timestamp, and associated maintenance event if escalated. Feeds fault detection and classification (FDC) workflows and predictive maintenance models.'
}

Table equipment.equipment_process_recipe {
  equipment_process_recipe_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the equipment process recipe record.']
  change_notification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the change request that introduced or modified this recipe version.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Process recipes are classified under ECCN codes; linking enables recipe‑level export control compliance checks.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator or system that last executed the recipe.']
  equipment_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fab_tool. Business justification: A recipe is owned by a specific fab tool; adding fab_tool_id enables direct navigation and removes redundant attributes that can be derived from the fab_tool record.']
  equipment_recipe_for_tool varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — Recipes are tool-specific. Each recipe must reference compatible tools for recipe-to-tool assignment and execution tracking.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — Recipes must reference compatible tool types. Required for MES dispatching - lots can only be dispatched to tools with qualified recipes.']
  pdk_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.pdk. Business justification: Each process recipe is defined for a specific PDK version; needed for the \'Recipe Management\' report linking recipes to PDKs.']
  tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific equipment chamber where the recipe is executed.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the recipe within the change control process.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest audit (passed, failed, pending).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the recipe (e.g., SEMI, ISO).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recipe record was first created in the system.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed recipe documentation or PDF.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the recipe version is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the recipe version is valid for production.']
  exposure_dose_mj_cm2 decimal(6,2) [note: 'Energy dose applied during lithography exposure.']
  focus_offset_nm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Focus adjustment offset for the lithography tool, in nanometers.']
  gas_flow_rate_sccm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard cubic centimeters per minute flow rate for process gases.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipe version is currently active for scheduling.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipe has been superseded and should no longer be used.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance audit of the recipe.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of this recipe version.']
  oee_actual_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured OEE percentage achieved during the most recent execution.']
  oee_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage for the recipe.']
  parameter_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct process parameters defined in the recipe.']
  parameter_set_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the key process parameters defined in the recipe.']
  pressure_setpoint_pa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target chamber pressure for the process, in pascals.']
  process_node_target varchar [note: 'Technology node the recipe is intended for (e.g., 7nm, 5nm).']
  process_step_count varchar [note: 'Number of discrete process steps defined in the recipe.']
  recipe_category varchar [note: 'High‑level process domain the recipe belongs to.']
  recipe_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the recipe used in change management and scheduling.']
  recipe_hash varchar [note: 'Checksum or hash value used to verify recipe integrity.']
  recipe_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the process recipe.']
  recipe_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or group responsible for the recipe.']
  rf_power_watts decimal(6,2) [note: 'Radio‑frequency power setting for plasma processes, in watts.']
  safety_status varchar [note: 'Safety classification of the recipe based on hazardous materials or process risk.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the recipe record (e.g., SmartFactory MES).']
  spin_speed_rpm varchar [note: 'Rotational speed for spin‑coat steps, in revolutions per minute.']
  temperature_setpoint_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target temperature setting for the process step, in degrees Celsius.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the recipe record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version string of the recipe (e.g., v1.2, rev3).']
  version_history_note varchar [note: 'Free‑text note describing changes made in this version.']
  yield_actual_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual yield percentage observed in the latest run.']
  yield_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target good‑die yield percentage for the recipe.']

  Note: 'Master and execution records for process recipes on fab tools. Defines complete recipe parameters (temperature, pressure, gas flows, RF power, spin speed, exposure dose, focus offset, etc.) for each process step and tool type, including recipe name, version, process node target, tool compatibility, recipe owner, approval status, and effective date range. Also captures each recipe execution instance linking a specific wafer lot run to the recipe version used, actual as-run vs as-set parameter values, execution start/end timestamps, operator ID, chamber used, and pass/fail outcome. SSOT for recipe configuration, version management, recipe execution history, and recipe-to-yield correlation analysis. Sourced from SmartFactory MES and Camstar recipe management.'
}

Table equipment.recipe_execution {
  recipe_execution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each recipe execution event.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Run‑time execution records must reference the ECCN of the recipe used to satisfy audit trails for controlled technologies.']
  equipment_process_recipe_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.equipment_process_recipe.equipment_process_recipe_id — Each recipe execution must reference the recipe version used. Core traceability for yield correlation and process control.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — Recipe executions must reference the tool where processing occurred for tool-specific performance analysis.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot that was processed in this execution.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Recipe execution on a wafer lot must be tied to the design project it produces, used in the \'Production Tracking\' dashboard.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.invoice_line. Business justification: Each recipe execution billed as a line item; linking to invoice_line captures execution details for revenue recognition and traceability.']
  primary_recipe_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the human operator who initiated or supervised the execution.']
  recipe_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab tool (e.g., lithography scanner, deposition system) where the recipe was executed.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the process recipe definition used for this execution.']
  tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific chamber or module inside the tool used for the execution.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the individual wafer within the lot (e.g., Wafer 001).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch number associated with the lot run.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the execution met all applicable process compliance rules.']
  duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time of the execution in seconds (derived from start and end timestamps).']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the recipe execution completed or was terminated.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Category of equipment (e.g., \'Lithography\', \'CVD\', \'Etcher\').']
  execution_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the recipe execution: pass, fail, or abort.']
  gas_flow_actual_sccm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured gas flow rate during the run.']
  gas_flow_setpoint_sccm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target gas flow rate in standard cubic centimeters per minute.']
  is_calibrated boolean [note: 'True if the tool was calibrated immediately before execution; otherwise false.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the execution is for a critical process step (true) or not (false).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for operator comments or observations.']
  oee_availability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Availability component of Overall Equipment Effectiveness for this execution.']
  oee_performance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Performance component of Overall Equipment Effectiveness for this execution.']
  oee_quality_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quality component of Overall Equipment Effectiveness for this execution.']
  power_actual_w decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured power delivered during execution.']
  power_setpoint_w decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target power level for plasma or laser processes, in watts.']
  pressure_actual_pa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured chamber pressure during execution.']
  pressure_setpoint_pa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target chamber pressure for the recipe, in pascals.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Logical name of the process step within the overall flow (e.g., \'Etch\', \'Deposition\').']
  recipe_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the recipe (e.g., v3.2.1) applied during the run.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this execution record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this execution record.']
  result_code varchar [note: 'Machine‑generated code providing detailed reason for pass/fail/abort.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the execution within the batch.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the recipe execution started.']
  temperature_actual_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured temperature achieved during execution.']
  temperature_setpoint_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target temperature for the process step, in degrees Celsius.']
  time_actual_sec decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured duration the step actually ran.']
  time_setpoint_sec decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target duration for the process step, in seconds.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each process recipe execution on a fab tool or chamber, linking a specific wafer lot run to the recipe version used, actual process parameter values recorded (as-run vs. as-set), execution start/end timestamps, operator ID, and pass/fail outcome. Enables recipe-to-yield correlation analysis and supports SPC control chart population. Sourced from SmartFactory MES and Camstar lot history.'
}

Table equipment.spc_control {
  spc_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for spc_control']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the fab tool (e.g., lithography scanner, etcher) where the violation occurred.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot being processed when the SPC event was recorded.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: SPC violations are tracked per design project to analyze yield, needed for the \'Yield Improvement\' analysis.']
  primary_spc_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator who acknowledged or entered the violation.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the process recipe applied to the lot at the time of the violation.']
  spc_chart_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SPC control chart configuration associated with this violation.']
  tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific chamber or module within the tool that generated the out‑of‑control signal.']
  analysis_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the violation was analyzed for root cause.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer batch associated with the violation.']
  chart_type varchar [note: 'Statistical chart type used for monitoring (X‑Bar/R, EWMA, or CUSUM).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the violation triggers a regulatory or internal compliance requirement.']
  control_limit_lcl_at_time decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower control limit value that was in effect at the moment of detection.']
  control_limit_ucl_at_time decimal(12,4) [note: 'Upper control limit value that was in effect at the moment of detection.']
  corrective_action_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or document identifier for the corrective action plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the violation record was first created in the system.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System that supplied the measurement data.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Duplicate of event_timestamp for legacy reporting compatibility.']
  disposition_action varchar [note: 'Immediate action taken in response to the violation.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the SPC violation was detected on the fab floor.']
  is_false_alarm boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the violation was later determined to be a false alarm.']
  is_repeat_violation boolean [note: 'Indicates if this violation is a repeat occurrence within a defined time window.']
  lcl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical lower control limit for the monitored parameter at the time of chart configuration.']
  measured_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Actual measured value that triggered the SPC violation.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the monitored parameter (e.g., nm, µm, sec).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the operator or analyst.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the process parameter (e.g., film thickness, critical dimension) that is being controlled.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Name of the fab process step (e.g., etch, deposition, CMP) during which the violation occurred.']
  regulatory_reported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the violation has been reported to a regulatory body (e.g., SEMI, ISO).']
  remediation_deadline date [note: 'Target date by which corrective actions must be completed.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation effort.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the identified root cause for the violation.']
  rule_set varchar [note: 'Specific Western Electric rule(s) that were violated.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'How often samples are taken for the monitored parameter.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined severity rating of the SPC breach.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Shift during which the violation occurred.']
  spc_control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the violation record.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Desired target value for the monitored parameter.']
  trend_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of the observed trend when a trend rule is violated.']
  ucl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical upper control limit for the monitored parameter at the time of chart configuration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the violation record.']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the out‑of‑control condition (UCL breach, LCL breach, run‑rule, trend, or outlier).']

  Note: 'Statistical Process Control (SPC) monitoring configuration and violation records for fab tool process parameters. Defines control chart setup (chart type: Xbar-R, EWMA, CUSUM; monitored parameter; UCL/LCL/target values; sampling frequency; Western Electric response rules) and captures out-of-control signals. Violation records include violation type (UCL breach, LCL breach, run rule violation, trend), measured value, control limits at time of violation, detection timestamp, affected tool and chamber, associated wafer lot, disposition action (lot hold, tool quarantine, false alarm), and corrective action reference. SSOT for all SPC chart configuration and process excursion management in the FAB. Sourced from SmartFactory MES SPC module.'
}

Table equipment.oee_record {
  oee_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each OEE measurement record.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot being processed during the measurement period.']
  oee_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the fab tool for which the OEE record is captured.']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — OEE records must reference the measured tool. This is the fundamental relationship for equipment productivity reporting.']
  availability_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of scheduled time the equipment was available for production.']
  available_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total scheduled time the equipment was available for production.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or observations related to the OEE measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OEE record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  downtime_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of downtime type.']
  downtime_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code describing the root cause of downtime.']
  engineering_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Hours spent in engineering or setup state (non‑productive).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the OEE measurement was recorded.']
  idle_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Hours the equipment was idle (no work) while available.']
  measurement_period varchar [note: 'Granularity of the OEE aggregation (e.g., shift, day, week).']
  oee_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to compute OEE (e.g., SEMI E10 standard or custom algorithm).']
  oee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite OEE metric calculated as availability × performance × quality.']
  performance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of actual production speed to ideal speed, expressed as a percentage.']
  productive_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total hours the equipment spent in productive state during the measurement period.']
  quality_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of good units produced out of total units processed.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OEE record.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Team or function accountable for the recorded downtime or state.']
  scheduled_downtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Planned downtime (e.g., maintenance, changeover) duration in hours.']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the production shift associated with the OEE record.']
  shift_name varchar [note: 'Label of the shift (e.g., A, B, C, D) during which the OEE data was collected.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the OEE data.']
  state_current varchar [note: 'Equipment state at the end of the measurement period.']
  state_last_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent state transition within the period.']
  state_transition_count varchar [note: 'Number of equipment state changes recorded in the period.']
  unscheduled_downtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Unplanned downtime (e.g., breakdown) duration in hours.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the OEE record.']
  wafer_throughput decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of good wafers produced per hour (WPH) during the period.']

  Note: 'Periodic equipment productivity measurement and state tracking records for each fab tool combining SEMI E10 equipment state model data and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) metrics. Captures granular state transitions (Productive, Standby, Engineering, Scheduled Downtime, Unscheduled Downtime, Non-Scheduled), state transition timestamps, downtime reason codes, responsible party attribution (maintenance, engineering, facilities, scheduling), WIP lot impact, measurement period (shift, day, week), availability rate, performance rate, quality rate, composite OEE percentage, productive hours, available hours, idle hours, engineering hours, wafer throughput (WPH), and downtime breakdown. SSOT for all equipment state tracking, downtime attribution, productivity reporting, capacity consumption analysis, bottleneck identification, and fab scheduling inputs. Sourced from SmartFactory MES run data and OEE calculation engine.'
}

Table equipment.spare_part {
  spare_part_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each spare part record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory cost allocation requires linking each spare part to the cost center that budgets its purchase.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — Spare parts must reference compatible tool types/models for PM planning and emergency repair part identification. This is a many-to-many but critical for operations.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: INVENTORY/RISK: Each spare part has a primary supplier for lead‑time, quality, and risk assessment reports.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required calibrations for the part.']
  calibration_required_flag boolean [note: 'True if the part must be calibrated periodically (e.g., sensors, metrology tools).']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of regulatory or industry certifications applicable to the part (e.g., RoHS, REACH).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the spare part record was first created in the system.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Business impact rating if the part is unavailable (high = production‑stop risk).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the unit cost.']
  current_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Number of units of the part currently available in the fab stockroom.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for disposing of excess or expired parts.']
  equipment_compatible varchar [note: 'List of equipment models or tool families that can use this spare part.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is classified as hazardous under safety regulations.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent inspection activity.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date the part was most recently inspected for quality or safety compliance.']
  last_received_date date [note: 'Date the most recent shipment of this part was received into inventory.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'Date the part was last issued for equipment maintenance or repair.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of calendar days from order placement to receipt of the part.']
  lifecycle_end_date date [note: 'Date the part was officially retired or marked obsolete.']
  lifecycle_start_date date [note: 'Date the part was first introduced into the inventory catalog.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the specific storage bin or area within the fab stockroom.']
  min_stock_level varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be on hand to avoid stock‑out situations.']
  part_category varchar [note: 'High‑level equipment domain the part supports. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lithography|deposition|etch|cmp|metrology|ate|assembly|test|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  part_image_url varchar [note: 'Link to a digital image or drawing of the spare part.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the spare part used in inventory and work orders.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned part number that uniquely identifies the component across suppliers.']
  part_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the part based on its usage and criticality to fab operations.']
  part_volume_cm3 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical volume of a single unit of the part in cubic centimeters.']
  part_weight_kg decimal(8,3) [note: 'Physical weight of a single unit of the part in kilograms.']
  reorder_point varchar [note: 'Inventory quantity threshold that triggers a replenishment order.']
  safety_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Buffer quantity kept to protect against demand variability and supply delays.']
  spare_part_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the part, including function and key specifications.']
  spare_part_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the part within the inventory system.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Environmental condition required for safe storage of the part.']
  supplier varchar [note: 'Primary external supplier or vendor from which the part is sourced.']
  unit_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard purchase cost per individual unit of the part.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the spare part record.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer\'s warranty for the part expires.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for spare parts and consumables inventory specific to fab tool maintenance, including critical spare parts (e.g., focus rings, edge rings, quartz liners, lamp assemblies, pump kits), their OEM part numbers, compatible tool types, minimum stock levels, lead times, and storage location in the FAB stockroom. Supports PM planning and emergency repair readiness. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM module.'
}

Table equipment.metrology_run {
  metrology_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each metrology run record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician who initiated the run.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — Metrology runs must reference the metrology tool used for tool-specific measurement bias tracking and calibration correlation.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Lot identifier of the wafer(s) measured in this run.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Metrology measurements are associated with a design project for CD verification, required by the \'Design Quality Assurance\' report.']
  metrology_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key of the equipment asset that performed the metrology run.']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — Metrology runs must reference the metrology tool that performed the measurement. Required for tool-to-tool matching and measurement system analysis.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the measurement recipe used for this run.']
  site_map_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the site‑map layout used for site‑specific measurements.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the individual wafer within the lot.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration state of the metrology tool at run time.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the operator or engineer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the run record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System that supplied the metrology data.']
  humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity inside the tool during the run (%).']
  lot_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the wafer lot for the run.']
  mean_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical mean of the measured values across all sites.']
  measured_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Raw measurement value recorded for the primary parameter.']
  measurement_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement was performed inline with production or offline.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the physical parameter measured during the run.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure associated with the measurement type.']
  metrology_run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the run.']
  oee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated OEE for the tool during the run, expressed as a percentage.']
  pass_fail varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail result of the run against spec limits.']
  run_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the metrology run in seconds.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the metrology run, e.g., MR000123.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the metrology run was executed on the tool.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the run was performed.']
  sigma_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Sigma (3‑sigma) statistic derived from mean and standard deviation.']
  spec_limit_high decimal(12,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the acceptable specification window for the measurement.']
  spec_limit_low decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the acceptable specification window for the measurement.']
  std_dev decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard deviation of the measured values across all sites.']
  temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature of the tool chamber during the run (°C).']
  tool_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the metrology tool (e.g., KLA‑ICOS CD‑SEM 1234).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the run record.']

  Note: 'Metrology and inspection run records capturing in-line and off-line measurement results from metrology tools (CD-SEM, OCD, ellipsometer, profilometer) and inspection systems (KLA ICOS, brightfield/darkfield inspection). Records measurement recipe, wafer lot and site map, measured parameter (CD, film thickness, overlay, defect density), measured values per site, mean/3-sigma statistics, and pass/fail disposition against spec limits. SSOT for process control metrology data. Sourced from KLA ICOS and SmartFactory MES.'
}

Table equipment.tool_warranty {
  tool_warranty_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each tool warranty record.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.ar_invoice. Business justification: Warranty claim settlements are invoiced; linking tool_warranty to ar_invoice tracks warranty costs and payment status.']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preventive maintenance schedule linked to this warranty.']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab tool covered by this warranty.']
  claim_history_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warranty claims filed under this warranty.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the warranty as OEM, third‑party, or extended coverage.']
  covered_failure_modes varchar [note: 'List or description of failure modes that are covered under the warranty.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warranty record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the warranty cost.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the warranty coverage becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the warranty coverage ends (null for open‑ended contracts).']
  escalation_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the escalation contact.']
  escalation_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person to contact for warranty escalation.']
  extended_warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty is an extended (post‑OEM) warranty.']
  last_claim_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent warranty claim.']
  last_claim_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent warranty claim.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the warranty agreement.']
  oem_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer that issued the warranty.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Part number of the equipment model covered by the warranty.']
  response_time_sla_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum response time in hours promised by the service provider for warranty incidents.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number of the equipment.']
  service_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third‑party service organization responsible for warranty service.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the warranty record.']
  warranty_contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned by the OEM or service provider.']
  warranty_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount paid for the warranty agreement.']
  warranty_terms_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the warranty terms and conditions.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty covers parts, labor, or full equipment.']

  Note: 'Warranty and service contract records for fab tools covering OEM warranty terms, extended warranty agreements, and third-party service contracts. Captures warranty type (parts, labor, full coverage), coverage start/end dates, OEM service provider, covered failure modes, response time SLAs, escalation contacts, and warranty claim history. Supports maintenance cost management and make-vs-buy decisions for repair. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA contract management.'
}

Table equipment.tool_installation {
  tool_installation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each tool installation record.']
  certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.certification. Business justification: Installation compliance certifications (safety, environmental) are tracked per installation project for regulatory reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name of the lead engineer or OEM field specialist responsible for the installation.']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the semiconductor tool being installed.']
  acceptance_signoff_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person (e.g., fab manager) who signed off the installation.']
  acceptance_signoff_date date [note: 'Date when the final acceptance sign‑off was recorded.']
  capital_expenditure_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Capital cost incurred for the tool installation.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of certifications (e.g., ISO, SEMI) applicable to the installed tool.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for the installation expense.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the installation record was created in the system.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date the tool was delivered to the fab site.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation of the installed asset ends.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation of the installed asset begins.']
  fab_bay varchar [note: 'Specific bay or cleanroom area where the tool resides.']
  fab_building varchar [note: 'Building identifier within the fab site.']
  fab_floor varchar [note: 'Floor level of the installation location.']
  fab_site_code varchar [note: 'Code of the fabrication site where the tool is installed.']
  installation_code varchar [note: 'Business code or number assigned to the installation for tracking in ERP/MES.']
  installation_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual completion date of the installation work.']
  installation_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the installation event (e.g., "Fab A Lithography Scanner Install").']
  installation_project_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or code of the project governing the installation.']
  installation_start_date date [note: 'Planned or actual start date of the installation work.']
  installation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the installation.']
  installation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the installation activity.']
  measurement_capacity_wafer_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rated processing capacity of the tool after installation.']
  new_fab_site_code varchar [note: 'Site code of the fab where the tool is now installed after relocation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations captured during installation.']
  oee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated OEE percentage for the tool post‑installation.']
  previous_fab_site_code varchar [note: 'Site code of the fab where the tool was previously located.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Administrative status of the record for data‑governance purposes.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance status of the installation.']
  relocation_date date [note: 'Date when the tool relocation was executed.']
  relocation_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for moving the tool to a new location.']
  requalification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tool must undergo re‑qualification after installation.']
  sat_report_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Site Acceptance Test report.']
  site_acceptance_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the SAT protocol after installation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the installation record.']
  utility_exhaust_hookup_completed_date date [note: 'Date when exhaust/ventilation hookup was completed.']
  utility_gas_hookup_completed_date date [note: 'Date when process gas hookup (e.g., N2, Ar) was completed.']
  utility_power_hookup_completed_date date [note: 'Date when electrical power hookup was completed.']
  utility_vacuum_hookup_completed_date date [note: 'Date when vacuum system hookup was completed.']
  utility_water_hookup_completed_date date [note: 'Date when de‑ionized water hookup was completed.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the tool\'s warranty expires after installation.']

  Note: 'Installation, commissioning, relocation, and site acceptance records for fab tools covering the full physical placement lifecycle. Captures initial delivery, installation project reference, delivery date, source and destination FAB location (bay, building, facility), installation start/end dates, installation team (OEM field engineers, FAB facilities), utility hookup completion (power, gases, DI water, exhaust, vacuum), relocation events (reason: capacity rebalancing, fab consolidation, technology transfer; move dates; decommission/recommission steps), site acceptance testing (SAT) protocol results, re-qualification requirements triggered, and final acceptance sign-off. SSOT for tool physical placement lifecycle, asset location history, and relocation tracking. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Fixed Assets and SmartFactory MES.'
}

Table equipment.fdc_event {
  fdc_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each fault detection and classification event.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer who reviewed or acted on the fault.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.fab_tool.fab_tool_id — FDC events must reference the affected tool for fault containment and engineer notification routing.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot being processed when the fault occurred.']
  fdc_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab tool that generated the fault event.']
  fdc_tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific chamber or module within the tool where the fault originated.']
  maintenance_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance order created as a result of the fault.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the process recipe active on the tool at the time of the event.']
  related_event_fdc_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a preceding or related fault event, if applicable.']
  sensor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific sensor that reported the out‑of‑range value.']
  tool_chamber_id varchar [note: 'FK to equipment.tool_chamber.tool_chamber_id — FDC events are often chamber-specific. Chamber-level fault detection is standard in multi-chamber tools.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when an operator or system acknowledged the alarm.']
  alarm_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific alarm condition as defined by the tool vendor or SEMI E116.']
  alarm_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the alarm.']
  alarm_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record is an alarm (pre‑emptive) or a fault (critical failure).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or run number associated with the wafer lot for traceability.']
  classification_rule varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule set used to classify the event according to SEMI E116 logic.']
  clearance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alarm was cleared or resolved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fault event record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'System‑determined action taken automatically in response to the fault.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds between onset and clearance of the alarm.']
  engineer_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the engineer\'s investigation and corrective action.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fault was escalated to a maintenance work order.']
  is_simulated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record represents a simulated test event rather than a real fault.']
  lot_step varchar [note: 'Name of the process step (e.g., lithography, etch) being executed when the fault occurred.']
  message varchar [note: 'Descriptive text supplied by the tool explaining the nature of the alarm or fault.']
  normal_range_high decimal(12,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the normal operating range for the monitored parameter.']
  normal_range_low decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the normal operating range for the monitored parameter.']
  oee_impact_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated impact of the fault on Overall Equipment Effectiveness expressed as a percentage.']
  onset_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fault or alarm was first detected on the equipment.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the monitored sensor or process parameter that exceeded its threshold.']
  parameter_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured value of the parameter at the moment the alarm was raised.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used for scheduling remediation actions.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the underlying cause of the fault.']
  root_cause_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing detailed analysis of the root cause.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the event, used for prioritization and escalation.']
  source_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the source that raised the alarm (e.g., sensor, subsystem).']
  source_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the specific sensor, subsystem, or software component that generated the event.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating MES or inspection system that supplied the fault data.']
  threshold_high decimal(12,4) [note: 'Configured high threshold that, when crossed, triggers a fault condition.']
  threshold_low decimal(12,4) [note: 'Configured low threshold that, when crossed, triggers a fault condition.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Engineering unit associated with the parameter value (e.g., °C, V, mA).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fault event record.']

  Note: 'Fault Detection and Classification (FDC) monitoring configuration, real-time alarm management, and fault event records for fab tools per SEMI E116. Defines monitored sensor parameters (signal name, data type, engineering units, sampling rate, normal operating range, fault thresholds, classification rules) and captures all tool alarms and fault events. Records include alarm/fault ID, alarm code, type, severity (critical, major, minor, warning), alarm source (chamber, subsystem, sensor), alarm message text, affected tool and chamber, triggering parameter and value, onset/acknowledgment/clearance timestamps, associated wafer lot and recipe, automated disposition (lot hold, tool stop, alert only), escalation to maintenance event, and engineer review outcome. SSOT for real-time FDC configuration, tool alarm management, fault classification, and process excursion containment. Feeds predictive maintenance models. Sourced from SmartFactory MES FDC module.'
}

Table equipment.tool_capex {
  tool_capex_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each tool capital expenditure record.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Capital expenditure for a tool requires an associated export license to ensure legal acquisition and deployment.']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab tool (equipment) that this capex record pertains to.']
  service_provider_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the third‑party service organization responsible for maintenance contracts.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the OEM or supplier providing the equipment.']
  capex_date date [note: 'Date when the capex transaction was recorded or approved.']
  capex_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the capex project for tracking and audit.']
  capitalization_date date [note: 'Date when the asset is capitalized on the balance sheet.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center identifier charging the capex expense.']
  covered_failure_modes varchar [note: 'List of failure modes that are covered under the warranty or service contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capex record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate the tool over its useful life.']
  escalation_contact varchar [note: 'Contact information (name/email) for escalation of critical warranty issues.']
  extended_warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an extended warranty beyond the standard period has been purchased.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the capital funds used for the purchase.']
  installation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost incurred to install and commission the tool at the fab site.']
  make_vs_buy_repair_decision varchar [note: 'Strategic choice for repairing the tool: performed in‑house (make), outsourced (buy), or hybrid.']
  nre_charges decimal(18,2) [note: 'One‑time engineering fees associated with tool customization or integration.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number associated with the acquisition of the tool.']
  purchase_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final agreed purchase price paid to the vendor, excluding taxes and additional charges.']
  quoted_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Price quoted by the vendor before negotiation, in the transaction currency.']
  response_time_sla_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum response time promised by the service provider for a warranty claim.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA FI‑AA).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the purchase price.']
  third_party_service_contract_flag boolean [note: 'True if a separate service contract exists with a third‑party provider.']
  tool_capex_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, scope, or special notes of the capex project.']
  tool_capex_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the capex record. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|approved|funded|purchased|installed|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax and any discounts, representing the final cost to the company.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the capex record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated economic life of the tool for depreciation purposes.']
  warranty_claim_count varchar [note: 'Number of warranty claims filed for this tool.']
  warranty_claim_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value of all warranty claims settled.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when warranty coverage expires.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when warranty coverage becomes effective.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Scope of warranty coverage provided by the vendor.']

  Note: 'Capital expenditure, warranty, and service contract records for fab tool commercial and financial lifecycle. Captures capex project reference, tool asset reference, purchase order, OEM vendor, quoted and actual purchase price, installation cost, NRE charges, depreciation method, useful life, capitalization date, funding source (internal capex, CHIPS Act grant, JV contribution), warranty type (parts, labor, full coverage), warranty coverage start/end dates, OEM service provider, covered failure modes, response time SLAs, escalation contacts, warranty claim history with claim amounts and resolution, extended warranty agreements, third-party service contracts, and make-vs-buy repair decisions. SSOT for equipment financial management, warranty lifecycle, service contract management, and total cost of ownership. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AA and contract management.'
}

Table equipment.tool_safety_cert {
  tool_safety_cert_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each safety certification record.']
  primary_fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab tool (lithography, deposition, etc.) to which this certification applies.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date the certification becomes invalid unless renewed.']
  certification_issue_date date [note: 'Date the certification was originally issued.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the safety certification (e.g., "Chemical Safety – SEMI S2").']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'External certification number or code issued by the certifying body.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the certification.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of safety certification covering chemical, electrical, ergonomics, or environmental aspects.']
  compliance_standards varchar [note: 'Applicable safety and environmental standards satisfied by the certification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the system.']
  identified_hazards varchar [note: 'List of safety hazards identified for the tool (e.g., chemical exposure, electrical shock).']
  next_recertification_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next safety recertification activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments, observations, or special instructions related to the certification.']
  oee_impact_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated impact of the certification status on Overall Equipment Effectiveness, expressed as a percentage.']
  recertification_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required recertifications.']
  required_controls varchar [note: 'Mitigation measures or engineering controls required to address the identified hazards.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating for the tool based on hazard assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the certification record.']

  Note: 'Safety certification and compliance records for fab tools covering chemical safety (SEMI S2), electrical safety (SEMI S22), ergonomics (SEMI S8), and environmental compliance (ISO 14001, RoHS, REACH). Captures certification type, certifying body, certification date, expiry date, compliance status, identified hazards, required safety controls, and re-certification schedule. Mandatory for FAB operating permits and regulatory audits.'
}

Table equipment.part_substance_composition {
  part_substance_composition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the part_substance_composition association']
  spare_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the spare part master record']
  substance_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the substance inventory master record']
  concentration decimal [note: 'Percentage of the substance present in the spare part']
  usage_volume_kg decimal [note: 'Annual quantity of the substance used in the context of this part (kg)']

  Note: 'This association captures the composition relationship between a spare part and a regulated chemical substance. It records the concentration of the substance in the part and the annual usage volume needed for compliance reporting (REACH, RoHS, TSCA, etc.). Each row links one spare_part to one substance_inventory.. Existence Justification: Each spare part can contain multiple regulated chemical substances, and each substance can be present in many spare parts. The company actively tracks the concentration of each substance in a part and the annual usage volume for compliance reporting, which is managed as a distinct record.'
}

Table equipment.sensor {
  sensor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sensor']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant/facility where the sensor is installed.']
  parent_sensor_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sensor (parent_sensor_id)']
  accuracy decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical measurement error expressed as a percentage of full scale.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent calibration performed on the sensor.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next calibration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sensor record was first created in the system.']
  data_rate_hz decimal(10,2) [note: 'Frequency at which the sensor reports data.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the sensor\'s purpose, mounting location, or special notes.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the sensor\'s embedded firmware.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the sensor was installed on equipment.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IPv4 address used for network communication with the sensor.']
  last_maintenance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent maintenance activity.']
  mac_address varchar [note: 'Hardware MAC address of the sensor\'s network interface.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that produced the sensor.']
  measurement_range_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Highest value the sensor can accurately measure.']
  measurement_range_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lowest value the sensor can accurately measure.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the sensor\'s primary reading.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑provided model designation for the sensor.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label assigned to the sensor.']
  next_maintenance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date‑time for the upcoming maintenance.']
  power_rating_watts decimal(10,2) [note: 'Electrical power consumption of the sensor.']
  resolution decimal(5,2) [note: 'Smallest distinguishable change in the measured value.']
  sensor_type varchar [note: 'Category of physical quantity measured by the sensor.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number stamped on the sensor hardware.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the sensor.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sensor record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for sensor. Referenced by sensor_id.'
}

Table equipment.maintenance_plan {
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for maintenance_plan']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the equipment asset linked to this maintenance plan.']
  superseded_maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on maintenance_plan (superseded_maintenance_plan_id)']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required calibration activities.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard governing the maintenance activities.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the cost estimate; default is USD.']
  cost_estimate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected monetary cost to execute the maintenance plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the maintenance plan record was created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance plan expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance plan becomes effective.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of equipment the plan applies to.']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Time unit for the frequency value.']
  frequency_value varchar [note: 'Numeric interval defining how often the maintenance activity recurs.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is considered critical for production continuity.']
  last_performed_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance plan was last executed.']
  maintenance_task_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the primary maintenance activities covered by the plan.']
  maintenance_window_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical duration of a maintenance event expressed in hours.']
  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average operating time between equipment failures, used for reliability analysis.']
