  quantity_out decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of child entity material produced by the transformation. Measured in units appropriate to child entity type (wafers, dies, or packaged units).']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Type of transformation relationship between parent and child entities. Defines the manufacturing operation that created the genealogy link: split (one-to-many), merge (many-to-one), rework, retest, package, assembly, die attach, wire bond, molding, or singulation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: split|merge|rework|retest|package|assembly|die_attach|wire_bond|molding|singulation — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rework_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Number of rework cycles applied to the material. Tracks cumulative rework history for quality risk assessment and customer notification requirements.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material scrapped during the transformation operation. Used for yield loss analysis and cost accounting.']
  scrap_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the reason for material scrap. Links to defect classification taxonomy for root cause analysis and FMEA.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that created the genealogy record. Identifies whether data originated from MES, ERP, or OSAT partner systems for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  test_program_version varchar [note: 'Version of the test program used during transformation (for retest operations). Critical for correlating test results across genealogy and validating test program changes.']
  transformation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transformation operation occurred. Critical for forward and backward traceability in automotive PPAP documentation and field return investigations.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for quantity tracking. Specifies whether quantities are counted in wafers, dies, packaged units, kilograms, or liters depending on material type.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing work order number authorizing the transformation operation. Links genealogy to production planning and scheduling systems.']
  yield_at_transformation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Yield percentage achieved during the transformation operation. Calculated as (quantity_out / quantity_in) * 100. Critical for process capability analysis and SPC monitoring.']

  Note: 'Association record capturing parent-child relationships between wafer lots, die banks, and finished goods to enable full forward and backward traceability across split, merge, rework, retest, and packaging transformations. Tracks genealogy record ID, parent entity type and ID, child entity type and ID, relationship type (split/merge/rework/retest/package), transformation date, quantity in, quantity out, yield at transformation, process step at split/merge, and operator reference. Supports IATF 16949 traceability, automotive PPAP documentation, customer complaint root cause analysis, and field return investigation.'
}

Table inventory.reservation {
  reservation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inventory reservation record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer associated with the reservation, if applicable.']
  consuming_goods_movement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the goods movement that will consume the reservation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who performed the reservation.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the raw material or finished‑good SKU associated with the reservation.']
  split_from_reservation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on reservation (split_from_reservation_id)']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer or material lot that is being reserved.']
  bin_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory bin (e.g., A, B, C) used for yield or quality segregation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reservation record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  demand_source_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating demand record (e.g., sales order, production order).']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reservation automatically expires if not consumed.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the reserved inventory (e.g., available, on_hold).']
  is_kgd boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reservation is for KGD‑qualified inventory.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority used for allocation sequencing.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why the reservation was created.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Customer‑or‑production requested date for the reserved inventory to be delivered.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible reservation code used in communications and reporting.']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reservation.']
  reservation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reservation was created in the source system.']
  reservation_type varchar [note: 'Category of demand source that triggered the reservation.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Amount of inventory units earmarked by the reservation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP/MES system that generated the reservation record.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the storage location where the reserved inventory resides.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the reserved quantity (e.g., pcs, wafers, kg).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the reservation record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for inventory allocation and reservation events that earmark specific quantities of WIP lots, die bank dies, raw materials, or finished goods for a designated demand source (customer order, production order, packaging run, engineering sample request). Captures reservation ID, material/lot reference, reserved quantity, UoM, demand source type and ID, reservation date, requested delivery date, priority, reservation status (active/consumed/cancelled/expired), consuming goods_movement reference, and reserving user. Enables ATP (available-to-promise) calculations, prevents double-allocation of scarce KGD inventory, and supports OSAT packaging run scheduling.'
}

Table packaging.assembly_order {
  assembly_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for assembly_order']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who requested the assembly order.']
  assembly_osat_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the outsourced assembly partner responsible for execution.']
  assembly_package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type.package_type_id — Every assembly order specifies a package type. This FK is essential for filtering orders by package format, capacity planning, and routing to qualified OSAT sites.']
  assembly_packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type']
  assembly_process_flow_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.assembly_process_flow.assembly_process_flow_id — Each assembly order references a specific process flow revision that governs how the lot will be assembled. Critical for traceability and process control.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assembly Order Cost Tracking report requires associating each order with the responsible cost center for budgeting and expense allocation.']
  customer_design_win_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_design_win. Business justification: Required for linking each assembly order to the specific customer design win that authorizes production, used in the Order Fulfillment process.']
  die_bank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.die_bank. Business justification: Needed for Assembly Order Cost Allocation and Regulatory Traceability linking the order to the die bank supplying the dies.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: ECCN classification determines export control category for each order; needed for compliance reporting and customs filings.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Required for Export License Verification Report; each assembly order must be linked to the export license governing its shipment.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary equipment used for the assembly.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Needed for Production Planning to associate each assembly order with the exact IC design.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Assembly Order is created for a specific IC design project; linking enables traceability, cost allocation, and schedule alignment in the packaging execution process.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Needed for Order Planning report that uses inspection lot results to set expected yield and schedule packaging of wafers.']
  mpw_shuttle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.mpw_shuttle. Business justification: MPW shuttle schedule drives packaging lead times; linking the order to the shuttle provides coordinated planning across design and packaging.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Custom packaging projects are driven by sales opportunities; linking ties assembly orders to the originating opportunity for forecasting.']
  order_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Sales order assignment to a sales rep is used in order‑to‑cash reporting and commission calculations.']
  osat_vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.osat_vendor.osat_vendor_id — Assembly orders are issued to a specific OSAT vendor or internal site. Critical for vendor performance tracking and capacity allocation.']
  package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type.package_type_id — Every assembly order specifies a package type — this is the primary classification that determines process flow, substrate, and equipment. Critical for filtering orders by package family.']
  packaging_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific packaging line used.']
  price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.price_agreement. Business justification: Ensures the assembly order uses the correct pricing terms defined in the customer\'s price agreement, critical for billing and contract compliance.']
  primary_assembly_process_flow_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.assembly_process_flow.assembly_process_flow_id — Each assembly order executes against a specific process flow revision. Required for traceability and change control.']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: REQUIRED: Order must reference the exact fab process flow used to produce dies for traceability and handoff reports.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Assembly order fulfillment depends on a purchase order; linking supports order‑PO status reconciliation and financial reporting.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Assembly orders are created from approved sales quotes; linking provides traceability from quote to manufacturing.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: Required for Assembly Order creation based on a Sales Order; the assembly planning report must trace each assembly order back to its originating sales order.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Required for Assembly Order Management report linking each order to the specific SKU being produced.']
  substrate_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the substrate lot used in the packaging process.']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Packaging must reference the tapeout record to ensure the correct mask set and timing closure status are used for assembly.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Required for Assembly Order Planning: each order must specify the test program that will validate the dies before packaging, used in the Assembly Order Execution Report.']
  actual_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Yield percentage achieved after assembly completion.']
  assembly_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the assembly order.']
  assembly_osat_vendor_fk varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.osat_vendor.osat_vendor_id — Assembly orders are issued to a specific OSAT vendor or internal site. This FK is essential for OSAT capacity planning, scorecard reporting, and order routing.']
  assembly_site varchar [note: 'Code of the facility where the assembly is performed.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the assembly work was completed.']
  cost_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any cost adjustments (e.g., discounts, fees) applied to the gross amount.']
  cost_gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost before any adjustments.']
  cost_net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final cost after adjustments, representing the amount to be billed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assembly order record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost amounts.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Number of defects recorded during assembly.']
  expected_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target yield percentage expected for the assembly run.']
  hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is currently on hold.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the final inspection for the assembled package.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent status transition.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks related to the order.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible alphanumeric identifier assigned to the assembly order.']
  order_placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assembly order was initially placed.']
  order_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the order request.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the assembly order.']
  quantity_ordered varchar [note: 'Number of die units requested for assembly.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the order was released for execution.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Any additional instructions required for the assembly (e.g., cleanroom level, moisture sensitivity).']
  target_ship_date date [note: 'Planned date for shipment of the completed assembly.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the assembly order record.']

  Note: 'Primary transactional record for die packaging and assembly work orders issued to OSAT partners or internal assembly lines. Captures assembly order number, package type (WLCSP, InFO, CoWoS, flip-chip, wire bond, BGA, leadframe), die lot reference, substrate lot, quantity ordered, target ship date, assembly site, process flow revision, and order status lifecycle (released, in-process, completed, on-hold). SSOT for all assembly execution events in the packaging domain. Shipment logistics and delivery tracking are owned by the order domain; cost accounting is owned by the finance domain.'
}

Table packaging.package_type {
  package_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for package_type']
  process_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_technology_node. Business justification: REQUIRED: Package qualification and compliance depend on the technology node; the link drives qualification reports.']
  ball_count_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of solder balls for BGA‑type packages.']
  ball_count_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of solder balls for BGA‑type packages.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the package type record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the package type becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the package type is no longer valid (null if indefinite).']
  is_3d_ic boolean [note: 'True if the package incorporates through‑silicon vias or other 3‑D stacking.']
  is_advanced_package boolean [note: 'True for cutting‑edge or specialty package formats.']
  is_ball_grid_array boolean [note: 'True if the package is a ball‑grid array type.']
  is_flip_chip boolean [note: 'True if the package utilizes flip‑chip interconnect technology.']
  is_heterogeneous_integration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package supports heterogeneous integration (e.g., chip‑on‑wafer).']
  is_itar_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the package is subject to International Traffic in Arms Regulations.']
  is_leadframe boolean [note: 'True if the package uses a traditional leadframe.']
  is_osat_supported boolean [note: 'True if the package type is commonly offered by outsourced assembly providers.']
  is_reach_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the package complies with REACH chemical safety regulations.']
  is_rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the package meets RoHS restriction requirements.']
  is_wire_bond boolean [note: 'True if the package uses wire‑bond interconnects.']
  jedec_outline_code varchar [note: 'Official JEDEC outline identifier for the package (e.g., 5x5‑1mm).']
  max_current_a decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum continuous current rating for the package.']
  max_die_pitch_um decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum center‑to‑center spacing between dies for multi‑die packages.']
  max_operating_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest temperature at which the package can reliably operate.']
  max_power_dissipation_w decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum power the package can dissipate safely.']
  max_voltage_v decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum voltage rating the package can withstand.']
  min_die_pitch_um decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum center‑to‑center spacing between dies for multi‑die packages.']
  min_operating_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest temperature at which the package can reliably operate.']
  moisture_sensitivity_level varchar [note: 'JEDEC‑defined moisture sensitivity classification.']
  package_category varchar [note: 'Primary category of the package format; limited to six most common values.']
  package_dimensions_height_um decimal(8,2) [note: 'External height (thickness) dimension of the package.']
  package_dimensions_length_um decimal(8,2) [note: 'External length dimension of the package.']
  package_dimensions_width_um decimal(8,2) [note: 'External width dimension of the package.']
  package_family varchar [note: 'Higher‑level family grouping (e.g., Wafer‑Level, Flip‑Chip, 3D‑IC).']
  package_material varchar [note: 'Primary material used for the package substrate.']
  package_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the package format (e.g., WLCSP, CoWoS).']
  package_type_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the package’s mechanical and electrical characteristics.']
  package_type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the package type.']
  pin_count_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of electrical pins/balls the package can support.']
  pin_count_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of electrical pins/balls the package can support.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification state of the package format.']
  reference_document varchar [note: 'Identifier or URL of the primary specification document for the package type.']
  thermal_resistance_c_per_w decimal(10,4) [note: 'Thermal resistance from die to ambient, expressed in degrees Celsius per Watt.']
  typical_thickness_um decimal(6,2) [note: 'Nominal vertical thickness of the finished package.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the package type record.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all semiconductor package formats supported by Semiconductors, including WLCSP, InFO, CoWoS, TSV-based 3D-IC, flip-chip BGA, wire-bond QFN, leadframe DIP/QFP, and advanced heterogeneous integration formats. Stores package family, body dimensions, pin/ball count range, thermal resistance specs, moisture sensitivity level (MSL), JEDEC outline code, and qualification status. SSOT for package format classification used across assembly, product catalog, and qualification domains.'
}

Table packaging.osat_vendor {
  osat_vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for OSAT vendor record.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: REQUIRED: OSAT vendor is a supplier; linking enables unified supplier performance, audit, and regulatory reporting.']
  aec_q100_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the vendor holds AEC-Q100 qualification.']
  audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score from the latest audit (0-100).']
  capacity_tier varchar [note: 'Capacity tier classification for the vendor\'s assembly lines.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor record was first created.']
  dppm_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Historical DPPM rate of the vendor\'s assembly processes.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet unique identifier for the vendor.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control regime applicable to the vendor.']
  iatf_16949_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the vendor holds IATF 16949 certification.']
  iso_9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the vendor holds ISO 9001 certification.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality audit performed on the vendor.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the vendor record.']
  nda_ip_protection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a non-disclosure agreement and IP protection are in place.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes regarding the vendor.']
  package_capabilities varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of package types the vendor is qualified to assemble (e.g., WLCSP, InFO, CoWoS, TSV, flip-chip, BGA).']
  partnership_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification status of the vendor within the OSAT program.']
  preferred_package_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of package types the vendor is preferred for.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the vendor.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact.']
  quality_certifications varchar [note: 'Semicolon-separated list of quality certifications held by the vendor (e.g., IATF 16949, ISO 9001, AEC-Q100).']
  standard_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical lead time in days for standard package orders.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government tax identifier for the vendor.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the vendor record.']
  vendor_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the vendor\'s primary site.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Unique vendor code used in ERP and PLM systems.']
  vendor_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the vendor\'s headquarters. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CHN|KOR|JPN|TWN|DEU|... — promote to reference product]']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the OSAT vendor as registered.']
  vendor_short_name varchar [note: 'Commonly used short name or abbreviation for the vendor.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor based on ownership and relationship.']

  Note: 'Master record for Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test (OSAT) partners and internal assembly sites used by Semiconductors. Captures vendor legal name, site locations, approved package type capabilities, quality certifications (IATF 16949, ISO 9001, AEC-Q100), historical DPPM performance, capacity tiers, standard lead times, NDA/IP protection status, export control classification (EAR/ITAR), and approved/preferred/probation status. SSOT for OSAT partner identity and qualification status within the packaging domain.'
}

Table packaging.assembly_process_flow {
  assembly_process_flow_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each assembly process flow definition.']
  assembly_package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type.package_type_id — Process flows are defined per package type. Required for routing and DFM constraint validation.']
  assembly_packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Audit: Process flow creation must be signed by a process engineer; required for compliance reports and traceability.']
  package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type.package_type_id — Process flows are defined for specific package types. Critical for routing assembly orders to the correct flow.']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: REQUIRED: Packaging flow is tied to a specific fab flow for end‑to‑end yield and DFM coordination.']
  process_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: Process Flow Definition uses a specific process node; required for Process Flow Specification report.']
  assembly_process_flow_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the flow, its purpose, and scope.']
  assembly_process_flow_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the flow definition.']
  bond_type varchar [note: 'Primary interconnect technology employed in the flow.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard that this flow must satisfy.']
  dfm_constraints varchar [note: 'Free‑text list of DFM rules or constraints that must be obeyed for this flow.']
  die_attach_method varchar [note: 'Method used to attach the die to the substrate.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the flow becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the flow is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  equipment_class varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of equipment required for the flow (e.g., "Flip‑Chip Bonder", "Mold Press").']
  final_inspection_type varchar [note: 'Primary inspection technique performed after assembly completion.']
  flow_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the assembly process flow (e.g., "Advanced 3D‑IC Flip‑Chip Flow").']
  flow_to_package_type varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.packaging_package_type.packaging_package_type_id — Process flows are defined per package type. A package type may have multiple flow revisions but each flow targets a specific package format.']
  is_default_flow boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this flow is the default for its package type.']
  marking_method varchar [note: 'Method used to apply identification marks to the finished package.']
  molding_material varchar [note: 'Encapsulation material applied during the molding step.']
  process_time_target decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target total processing time for the flow, expressed in minutes.']
  process_yield_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target yield percentage for the flow (e.g., 98.75).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the flow record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the flow record.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number of the flow definition.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Safety level assigned to the flow (e.g., "Class A – Low Hazard").']
  singulation_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to separate individual packages from the wafer or panel.']
  step_count varchar [note: 'Total count of discrete steps defined in the flow.']
  underfill_material varchar [note: 'Material used to fill the gap between die and substrate after bonding.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the flow record.']

  Note: 'Master definition of the step-by-step assembly process flow for each package type and product combination. Captures flow revision, process steps (die attach, wire bond/flip-chip bump, underfill, molding, singulation, marking, final inspection), equipment class per step, material specifications, and DFM constraints. Linked to package type and product SKU. Enables traceability from assembly order to process specification.'
}

Table packaging.substrate_bom {
  substrate_bom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each substrate BOM record.']
  assembly_change_notice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ECO that introduced this BOM revision.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Substrate BOM is generated per IC design; linking enables BOM cost roll‑up per product.']
  package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type.package_type_id — Substrate BOMs are defined per package type. Required for material planning and assembly order material allocation.']
  substrate_package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type.package_type_id — Substrate BOMs are specific to a package type. Required for procurement and assembly material planning.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the substrate.']
  avl_status varchar [note: 'AVL qualification status of the substrate supplier.']
  bump_pitch_um decimal(5,2) [note: 'Center‑to‑center distance between bumps on the substrate, expressed in micrometers.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Classification indicating performance tier or special characteristics.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall regulatory compliance status of the substrate.']
  core_material varchar [note: 'Primary material of the substrate core (e.g., ABF, BT resin, silicon, glass).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BOM record was first created.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the unit cost.']
  density_g_per_cm3 decimal(5,2) [note: 'Material density of the substrate.']
  dielectric_constant decimal(5,3) [note: 'Relative permittivity of the substrate material.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this BOM revision is valid for procurement.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which this BOM revision remains valid (null if open‑ended).']
  is_obsolete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substrate is marked as obsolete.']
  last_eco_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent engineering change order affecting this BOM.']
  layer_count varchar [note: 'Number of material layers in the substrate stack.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle phase of the substrate product.']
  material_grade varchar [note: 'Grade or quality level of the substrate material.']
  max_operating_temp_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature at which the substrate can reliably operate.']
  min_operating_temp_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature for reliable substrate operation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the substrate BOM.']
  pad_finish varchar [note: 'Surface finish applied to substrate pads (e.g., ENIG, OSP, immersion tin, gold).']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned part number identifying the substrate.']
  rdl_spec varchar [note: 'Specification details of the redistribution layer (RDL) used on the substrate.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substrate complies with REACH chemical safety requirements.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision was released.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for the BOM version.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substrate complies with RoHS restrictions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system where the record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).']
  substrate_bom_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the substrate.']
  substrate_bom_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BOM record.']
  substrate_to_package_type varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.packaging_package_type.packaging_package_type_id — Substrate BOMs are defined for specific package types (CoWoS interposer, flip-chip BGA substrate, etc.).']
  substrate_type varchar [note: 'Category of substrate used in packaging (e.g., interposer, die, panel, wafer).']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number used by the supplier for this substrate.']
  thermal_conductivity_w_per_mk decimal(6,3) [note: 'Thermal conductivity of the substrate material.']
  thickness_um decimal(6,2) [note: 'Physical thickness of the substrate in micrometers.']
  unit_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Reference cost per unit of substrate (excluding taxes and freight).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the BOM record.']

  Note: 'Bill of Materials for packaging substrates and interposers used in advanced semiconductor packages. Captures substrate part number, layer count, core material (ABF, BT resin, silicon interposer, glass core), redistribution layer (RDL) specs, bump pitch, pad finish (ENIG, OSP, immersion tin), supplier, AVL status, unit cost reference, RoHS/REACH compliance flag, and revision history with ECO traceability. SSOT for substrate material specifications enabling procurement and assembly execution.'
}

Table packaging.assembly_lot {
  assembly_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the assembly lot record.']
  assembly_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.assembly_order.assembly_order_id — Assembly lots are created against assembly orders. This is the fundamental header-to-lot relationship enabling WIP tracking per order.']
  assembly_osat_vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.osat_vendor.osat_vendor_id — Each lot is processed at a specific OSAT site. Required for site-level yield analysis and capacity tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Lot-level cost accounting ties production expenses to the cost center that funds the lot, used in the Packaging Cost Summary.']
  customer_order_id varchar [note: 'External order number from the customer that triggered this assembly lot.']
  export_license_usage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license_usage. Business justification: Export license usage per assembly lot must be tracked for license utilization reporting.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_wafer_lot. Business justification: Lot Traceability Report links each packaging assembly lot to its originating wafer lot for yield, compliance, and audit tracking.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Yield and defect analysis per IC design needs the lot to reference its IC catalog entry.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Lot tracking reports require the originating design project to correlate yield and defect data with design intent.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Supports Lot Traceability workflow linking each assembly lot to the preceding inspection lot for defect tracking across packaging.']
  lot_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Lot manager is recorded for WIP ownership, yield accountability, and shift hand‑over documentation.']
  osat_vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.osat_vendor.osat_vendor_id — Each lot is physically at a specific OSAT site. Required for WIP-by-site reporting and site-level yield analysis.']
  packaging_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to packaging.packaging_line. Business justification: An assembly lot is processed on a specific packaging line; linking the lot to the line provides traceability of line usage for yield and defect analysis.']
  packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type']
  primary_assembly_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.assembly_order.assembly_order_id — Assembly lots are created under an assembly order. This is the primary parent-child relationship for WIP tracking.']
  process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_flow. Business justification: REQUIRED: Each assembly lot originates from a wafer lot produced by a specific fab flow; needed for traceability.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit Center Performance report allocates revenue and profit per packaging lot to the profit center responsible for the product line.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Lot‑level tracking report requires linking each assembly lot to the SKU it contains.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Logistics and Inventory Valuation require storing the location of each assembled package for warehouse management.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the lot was actually completed; may be null if not yet finished.']
  assembly_site varchar [note: 'Name of the OSAT vendor or internal fab where the lot is assembled.']
  cost_estimate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary cost of assembling the lot, expressed in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the assembly lot record was first created in the system.']
  cumulative_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall percentage of good dies produced from the lot after each process step.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values associated with the lot.']
  current_process_step varchar [note: 'The most recent manufacturing step the lot has completed or is currently executing.']
  defect_density decimal(6,3) [note: 'Number of defects per unit area (e.g., defects per cm²) observed on the lot.']
  die_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual dies contained in the assembly lot.']
  hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lot is currently on hold for any reason.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the lot was placed on hold.']
  inspection_fail_count varchar [note: 'Number of inspection points that failed during the latest quality check.']
  inspection_pass_count varchar [note: 'Number of inspection points that passed during the latest quality check.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the assembly lot record.']
  lot_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label for the assembly lot, often used in reports and dashboards.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the assembly lot, used for tracking across systems.']
  lot_package_type varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.packaging_package_type.packaging_package_type_id — Each assembly lot is for a specific package type. Required for WIP reporting and routing.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the lot, indicating whether it is active, completed, or terminated.']
  lot_to_order varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.pkg_assembly_order.pkg_assembly_order_id — Assembly lots are created against assembly orders. This is the fundamental WIP-to-order traceability link.']
  package_version varchar [note: 'Version or revision identifier of the package design used for this lot.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent quality inspection for the lot.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when assembly of the lot began.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the lot should be fully assembled.']
  wip_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the lot within the assembly workflow.']

  Note: 'Master tracking entity for an assembly lot progressing through OSAT or internal packaging operations. Captures lot number, parent wafer lot reference, die count, package type, assembly site (OSAT vendor), current process step, WIP status (queued, in-process, hold, complete), start/target/actual completion dates, cumulative yield, and hold flags. Central WIP identity that all step records, yield results, inspections, and defects reference. SSOT for assembly lot identity and real-time WIP status within the packaging domain. Equipment asset records are owned by the equipment domain; SPC charting is owned by the quality domain.'
}

Table packaging.assembly_yield {
  assembly_yield_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for assembly_yield']
  assembly_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.assembly_lot.assembly_lot_id — Yield results are recorded per lot. Essential for lot-level yield rollup and OSAT scorecard calculations.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Yield analysis ties scrap and rework costs to the cost center responsible for the assembly line.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator or automation system that performed the step.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment (e.g., die attach machine, wire bonder) used for this step.']
  primary_assembly_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent assembly run or batch to which this yield step belongs.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: REQUIRED: Yield per assembly step must reference the corresponding fab step to correlate SPC and defect data.']
  assembly_yield_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the step record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the yield record was first created in the system.']
  cumulative_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Running cumulative yield from the start of the assembly run to this step.']
  dppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Defective parts per million metric for the step.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Name of the specific packaging process step for which the yield data is recorded.']
  scrap_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why units were scrapped.']
  step_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the process step completed.']
  step_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the step within the overall assembly run.']
  step_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the process step began.']
  units_in varchar [note: 'Number of dies or packages entering the process step.']
  units_out varchar [note: 'Number of good dies or packages exiting the process step.']
  units_scrapped varchar [note: 'Number of dies or packages discarded during the step.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the yield record.']
  yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Yield for the step expressed as a percentage of units_out to units_in.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing assembly yield data at each major process step and at final assembly completion for each lot. Stores lot ID, process step, units in, units out, units scrapped, scrap reason codes, yield percentage, cumulative assembly yield, and DPPM. Also serves as the authoritative record for scrap quantities and loss reasons within the packaging domain. Feeds assembly yield trending, OSAT scorecard, cost of poor quality (COPQ) analysis, and product cost calculations. Distinct from wafer probe yield (owned by test domain).'
}

Table packaging.assembly_defect {
  assembly_defect_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for assembly_defect']
  assembly_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.assembly_lot.assembly_lot_id — Defects are recorded against a specific lot. Required for DPPM calculation and lot disposition.']
  capa_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective action record linked to this defect.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Defect tracking cost allocation assigns investigation and corrective action expenses to the owning cost center.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator who recorded the defect.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment used for the inspection.']
  primary_assembly_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer lot where the defect was observed.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: REQUIRED: Defect root‑cause analysis links packaging defects back to the fab process step that introduced them.']
  quality_defect_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.defect_record. Business justification: Enables Defect Traceability across wafer and package stages, required for Root Cause Analysis report that correlates wafer defects to package defects.']
  wafer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific wafer containing the defective unit.']
  assembly_defect_lot_fk varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.assembly_lot.assembly_lot_id — Defects are identified on specific lots. Required for lot disposition decisions and DPPM calculations.']
  assembly_defect_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the defect record.']
  comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the inspector.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the defect record was initially created.']
  defect_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level category describing the nature of the defect.']
  defect_image_uri varchar [note: 'Link to the image captured for the defect.']
  defect_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the defect observed during inspection.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the defect was recorded.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Decision taken for the defective unit.']
  dppm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated defect density for the lot (defects per million units).']
  fmea_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related Failure Mode and Effects Analysis record.']
  hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lot/unit is placed on hold for MRB escalation.']
  inspection_batch_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspection batch (transaction header) to which this defect belongs.']
  inspection_tool varchar [note: 'Name or model of the inspection tool used.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the defect is considered critical for product functionality.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the measurement value.']
  measurement_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Quantitative measurement associated with the defect (e.g., void size).']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause analysis result for the defect.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Line sequence number of the defect within the inspection batch.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the defect.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the defect was recorded.']
  unit_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the individual packaged unit (die) where the defect was detected.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the defect record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for assembly defects and non-conformances identified during in-process inspection, final visual inspection, or reliability testing. Captures defect type (delamination, voiding, wire sweep, solder bridge, package crack, marking error), detection step, lot ID, unit serial number, defect image reference, disposition (rework, scrap, use-as-is), corrective action reference, and hold flag for quality domain MRB escalation. Supports DPPM tracking, FMEA, and feeds quality domain for enterprise-wide non-conformance management.'
}

Table packaging.package_qualification {
  package_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for package_qualification']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Qualification approval requires a qualified engineer\'s sign‑off; the report lists the approving employee.']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.certification. Business justification: Package qualification results are tied to specific certifications; linking enables compliance verification.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Package Qualification Cost Tracking report assigns qualification expenses to the cost center overseeing the packaging program.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Package qualification must be tied to the design project to verify that the chosen package meets the design\'s electrical and mechanical requirements.']
  osat_vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.osat_vendor.osat_vendor_id — Qualifications are site-specific — a package type must be qualified at each OSAT site independently.']
  primary_packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type']
  qualification_plan_id varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the qualification plan used to certify the package.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.sku']
  tapeout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.tapeout. Business justification: Qualification results are linked to the specific tapeout to ensure the mask set used matches the qualified package specifications.']
  tertiary_package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type.package_type_id — Qualifications certify specific package types for production. Essential for determining which package types are production-released.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Package qualification requires a specific test program; linking provides traceability of which test program validated the package, required for qualification audit.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the qualification was formally approved for production release.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of any special compliance considerations (e.g., automotive, aerospace).']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the qualification cost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification record was first created in the system.']
  defect_rate_ppm varchar [note: 'Defective parts per million observed in the qualification samples.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the qualification becomes effective for manufacturing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Expiration date of the qualification, if applicable.']
  external_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the external audit, if required.']
  external_audit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an external audit is mandated for this qualification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the qualification team.']
  pass_fail_criteria varchar [note: 'Defined criteria that determine pass or fail outcomes for each test.']
  qualification_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for the qualification program, expressed in US dollars.']
  qualification_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the document that contains the full qualification report.']
  qualification_method varchar [note: 'Approach used for qualification testing.']
  qualification_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the person responsible for managing the qualification program.']
  qualification_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the qualification after evaluating all test criteria.']
  qualification_standard varchar [note: 'Standard or specification against which the package is qualified.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification program.']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Category of qualification: new package, package‑product combo, or OSAT site certification.']
  regulatory_compliance varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) the qualification adheres to.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number of the qualification record.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk category of the qualification based on test outcomes.']
  sample_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of sample units tested during qualification.']
  stress_test_matrix varchar [note: 'Set of stress tests applied during qualification (e.g., High Temperature Operating Life, Temperature Cycling).']
  test_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total duration of the qualification test cycle in hours.']
  test_equipment_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment used to perform the qualification tests.']
  test_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the qualification testing was conducted.']
  test_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature (in Celsius) applied during stress testing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the qualification record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version label for the qualification plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of good dies per wafer after qualification testing.']

  Note: 'Master record for package qualification programs executed to certify a new package type, package-product combination, or OSAT site for production release. Captures qualification plan ID, package type, product SKU, qualification standard (JEDEC JESD47, AEC-Q100/Q101, MIL-STD-883), stress test matrix (HTOL, TC, HAST, ESD, latch-up), pass/fail criteria, qualification status, and approval date. SSOT for package qualification certification.'
}

Table packaging.reliability_stress_test {
  reliability_stress_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each reliability stress test run.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Reliability Stress Test budgeting requires charging test expenses to the cost center that sponsors the product qualification.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer or technician who supervised the test.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the test equipment used for the run.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Reliability reports must be tied to the specific IC design under test.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Reliability tests are executed on a specific design; linking enables the reliability report to reference design parameters and version.']
  package_qualification_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_qualification.package_qualification_id — Stress tests are executed as part of qualification programs. This link enables qualification status rollup from individual test results.']
  packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the semiconductor package associated with this test run.']
  quality_reliability_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.reliability_test. Business justification: Integrates packaging stress test results with wafer‑level reliability data for the Reliability Consolidated Report used in product qualification.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Reliability stress test results are required for regulatory filing submissions; linking provides evidence.']
  reliability_package_qualification_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_qualification.package_qualification_id — Stress tests are executed as part of a qualification program. Required for qualification evidence chain.']
  cycles varchar [note: 'Number of electrical or mechanical cycles applied (relevant for cycling tests).']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that captured the test data (e.g., Camstar MES).']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the test run.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the test run in seconds.']
  failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of units that failed during the test.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Descriptive classification of the observed failure mechanism.']
  humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity level during the test, expressed as a percentage.']
  mttf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated mean time to failure derived from test results, expressed in hours.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the test operator.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test record was first captured in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the test record.']
  reliability_stress_test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reliability test.']
  result_metric varchar [note: 'Name of the primary quantitative result measured (e.g., failure_rate).']
  result_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the result value (e.g., failures_per_hour).']
  result_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the primary result metric.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of individual units subjected to the stress test.']
  stress_test_to_qualification varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.pkg_qualification.pkg_qualification_id — Reliability stress tests are executed as part of qualification programs. Each test run links to its parent qualification.']
  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature condition applied during the test, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  test_conditions_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the test environment and setup.']
  test_location varchar [note: 'Facility or lab identifier where the test was executed.']
  test_run_code varchar [note: 'External code or number assigned to the test run for traceability across systems.']
  test_spec_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the test specification document.']
  test_standard varchar [note: 'Industry or internal standard governing the test methodology.']
  test_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stress test started.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Type of reliability stress test performed (e.g., HTOL, TC, HAST, PRECON, ESD, Latch-up, Drop). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HTOL|TC|HAST|PRECON|ESD|Latch-up|Drop — promote to reference product]']
  voltage_v decimal(6,3) [note: 'Voltage applied to the device under test, expressed in volts.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for individual reliability stress test runs performed as part of package qualification or ongoing reliability monitoring. Captures test type (HTOL, TC, HAST, PRECON, ESD, latch-up, drop test), sample size, stress conditions (temperature, humidity, voltage, cycles), test duration, failure count, failure mode, MTTF estimate, and pass/fail disposition. Linked to pkg_qualification and pkg_defect_record.'
}

Table packaging.material_lot {
  material_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for material_lot']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Material Lot Cost Accounting attributes material procurement costs to the cost center that ordered the material.']
  material_substrate_bom_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.substrate_bom.substrate_bom_id — Material lots received are matched to substrate BOM line items for consumption tracking.']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.raw_material. Business justification: Material Traceability and Compliance reports need to link each packaging material lot to its raw material inventory record.']
  substance_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.substance_inventory. Business justification: Substance inventory tracks hazardous chemicals in material lots for REACH/ROHS compliance reporting.']
  substrate_bom_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.substrate_bom.substrate_bom_id — Material lots of substrates reference the substrate BOM specification they fulfill. Enables material traceability from lot to spec.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the external supplier providing the material lot.']
  certificate_of_conformance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the Certificate of Conformance document associated with the lot.']
  compliance_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to any additional compliance documentation attached to the lot.']
  compliance_itar_status varchar [note: 'ITAR export‑control status of the material lot.']
  compliance_reach_status varchar [note: 'REACH compliance status of the material lot.']
  compliance_rohs_status varchar [note: 'RoHS compliance status of the material lot.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard cost assigned to a single unit of the material.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost_per_unit.']
  humidity_storage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded relative humidity percentage for the lot\'s storage environment.']
  incoming_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the initial quality inspection performed on receipt of the lot.']
  inspection_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Textual reason for inspection failure, if applicable.']
  inspection_passed boolean [note: 'True if the lot passed all required inspections.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the material lot by the receiving facility.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the material, including any special characteristics.']
  material_lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the material lot within the packaging operation.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Category of the packaging material (e.g., substrate, lead frame, mold compound). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: substrate|lead_frame|mold_compound|bond_wire|solder_paste|underfill|marking_ink — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Standard part number that uniquely identifies the packaging material type across the enterprise.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical quality rating assigned after inspection (0‑100).']
  quantity_received decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount of material received in the lot, expressed in the unit defined by unit_of_measure.']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lot is currently placed in quarantine pending further review.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the material lot was logged into the system.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the material lot record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the material lot record.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date the lot was released from quarantine and made available for production.']
  shelf_life_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the material lot is considered out‑of‑spec for use.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage environment (e.g., temperature‑controlled, humidity‑controlled).']
  supplier_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number provided by the external supplier for traceability.']
  temperature_storage_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the lot.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for quantity_received (e.g., pieces, kilograms).']

  Note: 'Master record for incoming packaging material lots received at the assembly site, including substrates, lead frames, mold compounds, bond wires, solder paste, underfill, and marking inks. Captures material part number, supplier lot number, quantity received, incoming inspection status, Certificate of Conformance (CoC) reference, shelf life expiry, storage conditions, and quarantine/release status. SSOT for packaging-specific material lot identity and incoming quality status. General inventory management is owned by the inventory domain; supplier master data is owned by the supply domain; RoHS/REACH compliance declarations are owned by the compliance domain.'
}

Table packaging.inspection_result {
  inspection_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inspection_result']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inspection Result Cost Allocation links inspection labor and equipment expenses to the cost center responsible for quality control.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the operator who performed the inspection.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the inspection equipment used.']
  inspection_assembly_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wafer or package lot that was inspected.']
  primary_assembly_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.assembly_lot.assembly_lot_id — Inspection results are recorded per lot at each inspection gate. Required for lot release decisions.']
  spc_chart_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SPC chart that consumes this inspection result.']
  defect_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the defect type for this record.']
  defect_count_category varchar [note: 'Number of defects observed for the specified defect category.']
  defect_count_total varchar [note: 'Aggregate number of defects detected across all categories.']
  defect_density decimal(10,4) [note: 'Defects per unit inspected, calculated as total defects divided by units inspected.']
  defect_density_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for defect density.']
  defect_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level assigned to the defect(s) detected.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final accept/reject decision for the inspected lot.']
  inspection_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the inspection event, often used on shop floor reports.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the inspection.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by the inspector.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection record.']
  inspection_step varchar [note: 'Specific process step at which the inspection was taken.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the inspection was performed on the lot.']
  is_spc_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection result is used in SPC control charting.']
  quality_outcome varchar [note: 'Overall quality result derived from the inspection.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection result record was first captured in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the inspection result record.']
  units_inspected varchar [note: 'Number of individual units (dies, packages) inspected in this step.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for in-process and final visual/automated inspection results during assembly. Captures inspection step (post-die-attach, post-wire-bond, post-mold, final visual), inspection method (AOI, X-ray, SAM, manual), lot ID, unit count inspected, defect count by category, inspection equipment reference (FK to equipment domain), operator ID, and accept/reject disposition. SSOT for assembly-line inspection outcomes within the packaging domain. Enterprise-wide SPC analysis and control charting are owned by the quality domain.'
}

Table packaging.assembly_change_notice {
  assembly_change_notice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for assembly_change_notice']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer impacted by the change.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Change Notice financial impact is recorded against the cost center that bears the cost of re‑qualification or redesign.']
  customer_design_win_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_design_win. Business justification: Change notices must reference the affected design win to track impact on the specific product, supporting the Change Management report.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee identifier of the person responsible for the change.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Change notices reference the affected IC design for compliance and impact analysis.']
  osat_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Name or code of the substrate material supplier affected by the change.']
  packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'Type of package involved in the change. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wlcsp|info|cowos|tsv_3d|flip_chip|wire_bond|leadframe_bga — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Regulatory filing is triggered by assembly change notices; linking captures filing reference for audit.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documentation is attached to the change notice.']
  attachment_reference varchar [note: 'Path or identifier of the attached document(s) in the DMS.']
  change_approval_date date [note: 'Date the change was formally approved for implementation.']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature and rationale of the change.']
  change_originator varchar [note: 'Department or function that originated the change request.']
  change_owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the change owner.']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the change notice.']
  change_submission_date date [note: 'Date the change notice was submitted to the change control system.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Category of the change (e.g., material, process, OSAT site transfer, substrate supplier, design, other).']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance review was completed.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulatory/compliance review for this change.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the change notice record was first created in the system.']
  customer_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status from the customer regarding the change.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the customer must be notified of this change.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the change becomes binding for production.']
  impact_on_dppm decimal(7,2) [note: 'Estimated change in DPPM metric due to the change.']
  impact_on_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage change in wafer or die yield caused by the change.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  osat_site_transfer varchar [note: 'Target OSAT site code if the change involves moving production to a different assembly facility.']
  package_material varchar [note: 'Material composition of the package (e.g., epoxy, ceramic, organic).']
  pcn_number varchar [note: 'Unique PCN identifier assigned per JEDEC JESD46 guidelines.']
  process_step_changed varchar [note: 'Specific manufacturing process step that is being modified.']
  qualification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the change requires a qualification or qualification run before implementation.']
  regulatory_impact_details varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the regulatory impact, including standards affected.']
  regulatory_impact_flag boolean [note: 'True if the change has regulatory implications (e.g., RoHS, REACH, ITAR).']
  related_pcn_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of a related PCN, if this change is a follow‑on or amendment.']
  sku_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct SKUs listed in the affected_sku field.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the change notice record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the change notice.']

  Note: 'Master record for Product Change Notifications (PCN) and process change notifications originating from the packaging domain. Captures PCN number, change type (package material, process step, OSAT site transfer, substrate supplier change), affected product SKUs, change description, customer notification requirement, customer approval status, qualification requirement, effective date, and regulatory impact flag. Supports JEDEC JESD46 PCN standard and customer contractual PCN obligations. Regulatory impact assessment details are owned by compliance domain.'
}

Table packaging.customer_requirement {
  customer_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for customer_requirement']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the OEM/fabless/ODM customer associated with the requirement.']
  customer_osat_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the OSAT partner approved by the customer for assembly.']
  customer_package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type.package_type_id — Customer requirements specify required package types. Required for design-win to assembly execution linkage.']
  customer_packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'Target package format requested by the customer.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Customer Requirement matrix is defined per IC; linking enables requirement traceability.']
  primary_osat_vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.osat_vendor.osat_vendor_id — Customers approve specific OSAT sites for their products. Required for order routing compliance.']
  primary_package_type_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.package_type.package_type_id — Customer requirements specify required package types. Needed to validate assembly orders against customer specs.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards compliance constraints (e.g., RoHS, REACH) attached to the packaging.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the packaging requirement contract becomes binding.']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the packaging requirement contract ends; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requirement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary fields.']
  custom_marking varchar [note: 'Customer‑specified marking, labeling, or silkscreen details for the package.']
  customer_requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the packaging requirement.']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Customer‑specified destination for the packaged product.']
  dry_pack_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package must be shipped in a dry‑pack condition.']
  electrical_performance_requirements varchar [note: 'Electrical specifications such as impedance, capacitance, and signal integrity constraints.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control regime applicable to the package (EAR, ITAR, or None).']
  leadframe_material varchar [note: 'Material of the leadframe (if applicable) for the package.']
  moisture_sensitivity_level varchar [note: 'Moisture Sensitivity Level required for handling and storage.']
  package_height_mm decimal(8,3) [note: 'External height of the finished package in millimetres.']
  package_length_mm decimal(8,3) [note: 'External length of the finished package in millimetres.']
  package_width_mm decimal(8,3) [note: 'External width of the finished package in millimetres.']
  packaging_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of the packaging service in US dollars.']
  packaging_material varchar [note: 'Material used for the package encapsulation.']
  packaging_sla_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days from order receipt to package delivery as agreed with the customer.']
  packaging_test_method varchar [note: 'Primary test method used to qualify the package.']
  pcn_notification_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the customer requires advance notice for a Product Change Notification.']
  qualification_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific qualification tests or criteria the package must satisfy.']
  reliability_target_ppm varchar [note: 'Target reliability expressed as defective parts per million over the product life.']
  required_dppm varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed defective parts per million for the package.']
  required_mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target MTBF for the packaged component expressed in hours.']
  required_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable good‑die yield percentage for the package.']
  requirement_number varchar [note: 'External business reference number for the packaging requirement contract.']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the requirement lifecycle stage.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Preferred logistics method for delivering the packaged component.']
  test_specification varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed test specification document.']
  thermal_budget_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature rise for the package during operation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the requirement record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the requirement record for change tracking.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Length of the warranty period offered for the packaged component.']
  weight_gram decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of the packaged component in grams.']

  Note: 'Master record for customer-specific packaging requirements and design-in specifications agreed with OEM, fabless, or ODM customers. Captures customer ID, product SKU, required package type, custom marking requirements, special handling (MSL, dry pack), customer-approved OSAT sites, customer qualification requirements, PCN notification lead time, and contractual packaging SLAs. Bridges customer design-win commitments to packaging execution.'
}

Table packaging.assembly_step_record {
  assembly_step_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each assembly step execution record.']
  assembly_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.assembly_lot.assembly_lot_id — Every step execution record references the lot being processed. This is the fundamental traceability link from step to lot.']
  assembly_process_flow_id varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.assembly_process_flow.assembly_process_flow_id — Step records reference the process flow definition to validate correct sequence and parameters.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Step‑record cost capture links each process step’s labor and equipment spend to the cost center budgeting the lot.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the human operator or automation agent responsible for the step execution.']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the equipment (e.g., die bonder, wire bonder) that performed the step.']
