  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material lot (e.g., adhesive, underfill) consumed in this step.']
  primary_assembly_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the assembly lot (batch of packaged units) to which this step belongs.']
  process_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to process.process_process_step. Business justification: REQUIRED: Mapping each assembly step to the fab step enables integrated process control and reporting.']
  rework_assembly_step_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on assembly_step_record (rework_assembly_step_record_id)']
  assembly_step_record_lot_fk varchar [note: 'FK to packaging.assembly_lot.assembly_lot_id — Every step execution record references the lot being processed. This is the core traceability link from step-level data to lot identity.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard governing the step (e.g., SEMI, JEDEC).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the step record was initially created in the system.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Number of defects detected during inspection of this step.']
  defect_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of detected defects (e.g., particle, misalignment, void).']
  dppm varchar [note: 'Defect density expressed as parts per million for this step.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the step in seconds (calculated from start and end timestamps).']
  force_n decimal(10,2) [note: 'Force applied (e.g., during die attach or bonding) expressed in newtons.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the in‑process inspection for this step.']
  is_pass boolean [note: 'True if the step passed all quality criteria; false otherwise.']
  material_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of material used in the step, expressed in the unit of measure defined for the material (e.g., grams, milliliters).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text comments captured by the operator or system.']
  parameter_profile varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded set of detailed process parameters (ramp rates, dwell times, etc.) captured for the step.']
  pressure_pa decimal(12,2) [note: 'Pressure applied during the step, expressed in pascals.']
  process_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Yield percentage measured after this step (good units / units processed).']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Safety level assigned to the step based on material and process hazards.']
  step_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the step completed or was terminated.']
  step_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this step within the defined assembly process flow.']
  step_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the step began execution on the equipment.']
  step_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the step record.']
  step_type varchar [note: 'Categorical code indicating the type of packaging operation performed in this step (e.g., die attach, wire bonding).']
  temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target or measured temperature of the equipment during the step, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the step record.']

  Note: 'Consolidated transactional record for all assembly process step executions including die attach, wire bonding, flip-chip bumping, molding/encapsulation, TSV formation, underfill, singulation, and marking. Captures step type, lot ID, process parameters (material, temperature, pressure, force profiles as structured data), equipment ID, operator ID, measurement results, inspection outcomes, and pass/fail disposition. Supports full traceability from packaged unit back through every process step. Replaces individual step-level tables with a single flexible entity differentiated by step type.'
}

Table packaging.packaging_line {
  packaging_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for packaging_line']
  parent_packaging_line_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on packaging_line (parent_packaging_line_id)']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the line is located.']
  capacity_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum number of packages the line can produce per hour under nominal conditions.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification (e.g., "high_volume", "prototype", "R&D").']
  code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used in production planning and scheduling.']
  compliance_certification varchar [note: 'Relevant compliance certifications held by the line (e.g., ISO 26262, IEC 61508).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the packaging line record was first created in the system.']
  current_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed good‑part yield as a percentage of total units processed in the latest reporting period.']
  defect_rate_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Defects per million units measured on the line.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the packaging line became operational for the defined configuration.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the packaging line configuration is scheduled to be retired or replaced (null if open‑ended).']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total electricity consumption of the line per month.']
  equipment_count varchar [note: 'Number of major equipment units (e.g., bonders, die attachers) assigned to the line.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive maintenance activity.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Category of packaging technology employed on the line.']
  location varchar [note: 'Specific building/area within the plant (e.g., "Cleanroom 3A").']
  mtbf_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average operating time between unplanned line stoppages.']
  mttr_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average time required to restore the line after a failure.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the packaging line (e.g., "WLCSP Line A").']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next preventive maintenance activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the line.']
  operator_in_charge varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the primary operator responsible for the line.']
  osat_partner varchar [note: 'Name of the outsourced assembly service provider linked to this line, if any.']
  process_flow_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the process flow recipe used on the line.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification state of the line for the assigned package types.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Safety class assigned to the line based on hazard analysis.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Operational shift model used for the line.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the line.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the packaging line record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for packaging_line. Referenced by packaging_line_id.'
}

  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification indicating the sensitivity of the qualification plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification plan record was first created.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed qualification plan document stored in the document repository.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification plan expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification plan becomes effective.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the qualification plan is mandatory for the product family.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the qualification plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the plan.']
  osat_partner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the outsourced assembly service partner linked to this qualification plan.']
  pass_fail_threshold varchar [note: 'Threshold definition (e.g., yield ≥ 99.5%) used to determine pass or fail.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code used to reference the qualification plan across systems.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the qualification plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the qualification plan indicating its primary focus.']
  process_flow varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the assembly process flow used in the qualification.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Family or series of semiconductor products subject to qualification.']
  qualification_criteria varchar [note: 'Key performance or reliability criteria that must be met for plan approval.']
  qualification_scope varchar [note: 'Textual description of the products, packages, or processes covered by the plan.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the engineer accountable for the plan.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the qualification plan record must be retained for compliance.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for minor updates within a version.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the qualification plan.']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired minimum yield percentage for qualified units.']
  test_end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for the qualification testing.']
  test_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for the qualification testing.']
  test_suite_name varchar [note: 'Name of the test suite executed as part of the qualification.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the plan for change control.']
  yield_history_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the historical yield data set used during plan creation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the qualification plan record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for qualification_plan. Referenced by qualification_plan_id.'
}

Table supply.supplier {
  supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the supplier record.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the supplier\'s primary address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the supplier\'s primary address (optional).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier was approved for procurement.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the internal user who approved the supplier onboarding.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the supplier\'s primary address.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the supplier with internal and external regulations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the supplier\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier record was first created.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount approved for the supplier.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Default transaction currency used with the supplier.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet unique identifier for the supplier organization.']
  ear_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is subject to EAR export controls.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export classification code for the supplier\'s products or services.']
  financial_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating assigned to the supplier based on financial health.']
  global_footprint varchar [note: 'Scope of the supplier\'s operational presence.']
  industry_classification varchar [note: 'Broad industry segment to which the supplier belongs.']
  is_certified_kga boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds a KGA (Known Good Assembly) certification.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is subject to ITAR export controls.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent supplier quality or compliance audit.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of days from order placement to delivery.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal registered name of the supplier entity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or comments about the supplier.']
  parent_company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ultimate parent organization if the supplier is a subsidiary.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the supplier.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the supplier\'s primary address.']
  preferred_logistics_partner varchar [note: 'Logistics provider most frequently used for shipments from this supplier.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main contact person for the supplier.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary supplier contact.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number of the primary supplier contact.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Overall quality performance rating based on audits and defect data.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number of the supplier\'s legal entity.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk score derived from financial, geopolitical, and operational factors.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the supplier\'s primary address.']
  supplier_group varchar [note: 'Business grouping used for strategic sourcing and spend analysis.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Primary display name of the supplier used in business transactions.']
  supplier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supplier record.']
  supplier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supplier based on strategic importance and spend volume.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the supplier\'s environmental and social responsibility performance.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax registration number for the supplier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the supplier record.']
  website varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the supplier.']

  Note: 'Master record for all semiconductor supply chain vendors including raw material suppliers, chemical suppliers, gas suppliers, photomask vendors, OSAT partners, and subcontractors. Captures supplier identity, classification (Tier 1/2/3), business registration, DUNS number, geographic footprint, financial health rating, strategic importance, and ITAR/EAR export control classification. SSOT for supplier identity across the supply domain. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master.'
}

Table supply.approved_vendor {
  approved_vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for approved_vendor']
  approved_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Each approved vendor record belongs to a single supplier; linking provides parent‑child hierarchy and removes redundant supplier details that may exist in approved_vendor.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.material_master.material_master_id — AVL records approve a supplier FOR a specific material category. The material_master is the SSOT for what\'s being approved.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — AVL records authorize a specific supplier — this is the most fundamental FK in the domain. Cannot operate AVL without knowing which supplier is approved.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the vendor\'s street address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the vendor\'s street address (optional).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor was granted approved status.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification status of the vendor within the Approved Vendor List.']
  approved_commodity_scope varchar [note: 'List or description of material categories, chemical families, or process inputs the vendor is qualified to supply.']
  approved_vendor_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text providing additional context or notes about the vendor.']
  approved_vendor_status varchar [note: 'Overall operational status of the vendor within the enterprise.']
  audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical result of the latest audit (0‑100).']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the vendor\'s address.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of certifications (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949, RoHS) held by the vendor.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the vendor\'s primary location.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Currency code used for transactions with the vendor.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet unique identifier for the vendor organization.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor\'s approved status expires unless renewed.']
  financial_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating assigned to the vendor based on financial health.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent supplier audit.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of calendar days from order placement to material receipt for this vendor.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition agreed with the vendor.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the vendor\'s address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the vendor\'s primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main point of contact for the vendor.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number of the vendor\'s primary contact.']
  quality_tier varchar [note: 'Internal quality classification assigned to the vendor based on performance and compliance.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approved vendor record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the approved vendor record.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vendor with respect to applicable semiconductor regulations.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) derived from supply‑risk assessments.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the vendor\'s address.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s tax registration number for invoicing and compliance.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Internal alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify the vendor within the enterprise.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the supplier organization.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Category of the vendor based on its role in the semiconductor supply chain.']

  Note: 'Approved Vendor List (AVL) records establishing which suppliers are qualified and authorized to supply specific material categories, chemical families, or process inputs to the fab. Tracks qualification status, approval date, expiry date, approved commodity scope, quality tier, and the engineering/procurement authority who granted approval. Supports DFM and process qualification gating. Sourced from Oracle Agile PLM and SAP MM.'
}

Table supply.material_master {
  material_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each material master record.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Each material must have an ECCN for export‑control classification; used in licensing and reporting.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved vendor supplying this material.']
  base_uom varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure used for inventory and procurement transactions (e.g., kg, L, pcs).']
  batch_management boolean [note: 'True if the material is managed in batches for traceability.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating where the material originates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the material master record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency for the standard cost.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the material must not be used.']
  hazardous_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification indicating the material\'s hazardous status under RoHS, REACH, and TSCA.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from purchase order issuance to receipt of the material.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the material record.']
  lot_size_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard production or procurement lot size for the material.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the material, including its purpose and key characteristics.']
  material_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the material.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the material across the enterprise.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the material by its functional role in semiconductor manufacturing.']
  max_order_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Largest quantity that can be ordered in a single purchase order.']
  min_order_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from a supplier.']
  procurement_group varchar [note: 'Organizational group responsible for procuring the material.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'True if incoming or in‑process inspection is mandatory for the material.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the material meets the Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) requirements.']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the material complies with the Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) directive.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of material kept on hand to protect against demand or supply variability.']
  serial_management boolean [note: 'True if the material is tracked by serial numbers.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the material can be stored before it expires.']
  standard_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Default cost used for valuation and planning, expressed in the base currency.']
  storage_humidity_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest permissible relative humidity percentage for storage.']
  storage_humidity_min_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest permissible relative humidity percentage for storage.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest permissible storage temperature in degrees Celsius.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest permissible storage temperature in degrees Celsius.']
  tsca_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the material is compliant with the US Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the material master record.']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'Key for grouping materials with similar accounting treatment.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master record for all procured materials used in semiconductor manufacturing: silicon wafer substrates, process chemicals (slurries, etchants, photoresists), specialty gases (silane, nitrogen, argon), photomasks, packaging substrates, lead frames, bonding wire, and consumables. Captures material number, description, material type, base unit of measure, hazardous material classification (RoHS/REACH/TSCA), storage conditions, shelf life, procurement lead time, safety stock levels, reorder points, and lot sizing parameters. SSOT for material identity and procurement parameters within the supply domain.'
}

Table supply.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the purchase order record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Purchase order fulfills a specific customer account order; linking enables order‑to‑cash reconciliation and profitability analysis.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: PO accounting requires posting expenses to a cost center for internal budgeting and cost‑reporting; the finance cost_center entity holds the approved cost center hierarchy.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Purchase orders are raised for specific IC catalog items; linking enables order‑to‑item traceability and cost reporting.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: Required for Order‑to‑Purchase Order traceability in the order‑to‑cash process, enabling reporting of procurement cost per sales order.']
  primary_purchase_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal employee who created the PO request.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis allocates PO spend to a profit center, enabling product‑line P&L reporting; finance profit_center stores the responsible profit center codes.']
  purchase_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier (vendor) to which the purchase order is issued.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Every PO is issued to exactly one supplier. This is the most fundamental FK in procurement — cannot ship without it.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when goods/services were actually received.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the purchase order.']
  compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Semicolon‑separated list of compliance indicators (e.g., RoHS, REACH).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this purchase order record was first captured in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts on the PO.']
  delivery_schedule varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of delivery windows or milestones.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount applied.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount granted on the gross amount.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for goods/services to be delivered.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export classification code (e.g., EAR99, ITAR, ECCN) for the items on the PO.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of freight/shipping.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Responsibility for freight costs.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FOB|CFR|CIF|DAP|DDP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_ear_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PO is subject to U.S. Export Administration Regulations.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from PO issuance to expected receipt.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the material or service.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or service being procured.']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order was initially created/submitted.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment schedule and conditions for the purchase order.']
  plant varchar [note: 'SAP plant code where the goods are to be received.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the purchase order by the procurement system.']
  purchase_group varchar [note: 'Organizational group responsible for the procurement.']
  purchase_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the purchase order.']
  purchase_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PO based on its procurement strategy.']
  purchase_supplier_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Every PO is issued TO a supplier. This is a day-1 mandatory FK.']
  purchasing_org varchar [note: 'SAP purchasing organization that owns the PO.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units ordered for the material.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was released to the supplier.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage bin identifier for receipt.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction and rate applied.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, discounts, and freight.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final payable amount after taxes, discounts, and freight.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate tax amount applied to the purchase order.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the ordered quantity (e.g., EA, KG, L).']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price per unit of the material before taxes and discounts.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the purchase order record.']

  Note: 'Procurement purchase orders issued to qualified suppliers for raw materials, process chemicals, gases, photomasks, equipment spare parts, and subcontracted services (OSAT assembly/test). Captures PO number, supplier, line items, ordered quantities, agreed unit price, currency, delivery schedule, Incoterms, payment terms, and export control classification (EAR/ITAR). Represents the legally binding procurement commitment. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (EKKO/EKPO).'
}

Table supply.po_line {
  po_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the purchase order line record.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Each PO line references the exact IC part being ordered, supporting receipt matching and inventory control.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.material_master.material_master_id — Each PO line references a specific material being procured.']
  po_material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.material_master.material_master_id — Each PO line procures a specific material. Essential for material traceability and MRP consumption.']
  po_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent purchase order header to which this line belongs.']
  primary_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.purchase_order.purchase_order_id — Header-line pattern: every PO line belongs to exactly one PO. Critical structural relationship.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.purchase_order.purchase_order_id — Header-line relationship. PO lines cannot exist without their parent PO header.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier providing the material.']
  account_assignment_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific cost center, WBS element, or order linked to this line.']
  account_assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost object (cost center, WBS element, or internal order).']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to a framework contract governing this line, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the purchase order line record was created.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values on the line.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the supplier should deliver the material.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Total discount value applied to the line.']
  goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current status of goods receipt for this line.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities.']
  is_final_invoice boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line has been invoiced in its final form.']
  is_service_line boolean [note: 'True if the line represents a service rather than a material.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from order to delivery for this line.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the purchase order.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the purchase order line.']
  line_total_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Gross amount before tax and discount (ordered_quantity * net_price).']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the material.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Master data identifier for the material or component being procured.']
  net_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Net monetary value of the line after tax and discount.']
  net_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Net price per unit of the material before taxes and discounts.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the material requested on this line.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant or site receiving the material.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'Number of units that the net price applies to (e.g., price per 1000 pieces).']
  purchase_group varchar [note: 'Organizational group responsible for the procurement.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the goods were received.']
  receipt_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material that has been received against this line.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage bin where the material will be stocked.']
  supply_risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk rating (0‑100) assessing supplier and material availability risk.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Monetary tax amount calculated for the line.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code used to calculate tax for the line.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the ordered quantity is expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the line record.']
  vendor_material_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s own part number for the material.']

  Note: 'Individual line items within a purchase order, each representing a distinct material or service being procured. Captures line number, material number, ordered quantity, unit of measure, net price, delivery date, plant/storage location destination, account assignment (cost center or WBS element), and goods receipt status. Enables granular tracking of multi-material POs and partial deliveries. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (EKPO).'
}

Table supply.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the goods receipt record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Goods receipt is allocated to the customer account it fulfills, needed for inventory allocation and billing.']
  export_license_usage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license_usage. Business justification: Goods receipt must reference the export‑license usage that covered the inbound shipment for compliance verification.']
  goods_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the purchase order associated with the receipt.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Goods receipt ties received material to the IC catalog entry for quality verification and traceability.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.purchase_order.purchase_order_id — Goods receipts are posted against purchase orders for three-way matching. Critical operational link.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier providing the goods.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the production batch or lot of the received material.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material complies with applicable regulations (e.g., RoHS).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Detailed compliance status of the material.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods receipt record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Any external system reference linked to this receipt (e.g., logistics tracking ID).']
  goods_purchase_order_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.purchase_order.purchase_order_id — Goods receipts are posted against a purchase order. Core three-way match requirement.']
  goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receipt (e.g., posted, reversed, pending).']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the receipt before taxes and discounts.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality inspection lot created for this receipt.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between purchase order issuance and goods receipt.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the material.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Master data identifier for the material received.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code indicating the nature of the receipt (e.g., 101 = goods receipt).']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net monetary value after tax and discounts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or observations.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or fab where receipt is posted.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the receipt is posted to financial accounting.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Result of the incoming quality inspection for the received material.']
  quantity_received decimal(15,3) [note: 'Amount of material physically received, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the goods were physically received.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the receipt document (e.g., GR number).']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the receipt was recorded in the system.']
  received_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the person who logged the receipt.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing supply risk for the received material (higher = higher risk).']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or fab location where the material is stored after receipt.']
  supplier_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Reference supplied by the vendor for the batch (e.g., vendor lot number).']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the receipt.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the quantity is measured (e.g., each, kilogram, liter, meter).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the goods receipt record.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Valuation category for the material (e.g., S = standard, U = moving average, V = special).']

  Note: 'Records the physical receipt of materials and goods at the fab or warehouse against a purchase order. Captures GR document number, posting date, received quantity, unit of measure, storage location, batch number, quality inspection lot reference, and movement type. Triggers inventory update and initiates incoming quality inspection. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (MSEG/MKPF — movement type 101).'
}

Table supply.supplier_qualification {
  supplier_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each supplier qualification record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Supplier qualification is performed for a particular customer account\'s requirements, used in account‑specific compliance reporting.']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.certification. Business justification: Qualification programs are linked to a specific compliance certification required for supplier approval.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer or authority who approved the qualification.']
  primary_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier being qualified, linking to the supplier master record.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Qualification records are for a specific supplier. Without this FK, you cannot determine which supplier is being qualified.']
  tertiary_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Qualification records assess a specific supplier. Cannot exist without supplier reference.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification was formally approved.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the audit was performed.']
  audit_team varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of auditors and subject‑matter experts who conducted the audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit execution: initial qualification, routine surveillance, or for‑cause audit.']
  compliance_standards varchar [note: 'Semicolon‑separated list of regulatory or industry standards the qualification satisfies.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Date by which all corrective actions must be completed.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed plan describing corrective actions required to resolve audit findings.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification record was first created in the system.']
  findings_severity varchar [note: 'Highest severity level of audit findings identified during the assessment.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of audit observations, non‑conformances, and strengths.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information captured by the qualification team.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Result of the qualification audit after considering findings and corrective actions.']
  qualification_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the qualification program for tracking and audit purposes.']
  qualification_program_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualification program: initial onboarding, scheduled periodic review, or change‑triggered re‑qualification.']
  qualification_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the qualification assessment covering quality system audit, process capability, material certification, or a combination.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification record.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score (0‑100) derived from supplier risk assessment models.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the qualification record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier qualification expires unless re‑qualified.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier qualification becomes effective.']

  Note: 'Formal supplier qualification lifecycle records encompassing the full assessment, audit, and approval process for onboarding new suppliers or re-qualifying existing ones. Captures qualification program type (initial, periodic, change-triggered), assessment scope (quality system audit, process capability, material certification review), on-site and remote audit execution details (audit type — initial qualification, surveillance, for-cause; audit team composition; findings by severity — critical/major/minor; corrective action plans and closure tracking; overall audit rating), qualification criteria and pass/fail results, validity period, and approving engineer. SSOT for all supplier audit and qualification activity within the supply domain. Supports IATF 16949, ISO 9001, and SEMI standards supplier approval requirements.'
}

Table supply.supplier_audit {
  supplier_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the supplier audit record.']
  audit_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_event. Business justification: Supplier audits are recorded as compliance audit events, enabling unified audit tracking and reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name of the manager or authority who approved the audit results.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier being audited.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the audit was formally approved.']
  audit_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the audit focus.']
  audit_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for conducting the audit, expressed in US dollars.']
  audit_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the audit was performed.']
  audit_document_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the audit report document.']
  audit_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total time spent on the audit, expressed in hours.']
  audit_findings_document_reference varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the detailed findings document.']
  audit_method varchar [note: 'Method used to conduct the audit.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned audit number used for tracking and reference.']
  audit_report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored audit report document.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of standards and processes covered by the audit.']
  audit_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that originated the audit record.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit.']
  audit_team varchar [note: 'Names of the auditors or audit team members.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit purpose.']
  auditor_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the audit team.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance determination after the audit.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which all corrective actions must be completed.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  ear_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audited supplier is subject to EAR regulations.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification applicable to the supplier.']
  findings_critical_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical severity findings identified.']
  findings_major_count varchar [note: 'Number of major severity findings identified.']
  findings_minor_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor severity findings identified.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of audit findings across severity levels.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audited supplier is subject to ITAR regulations.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical site or facility where the audit was conducted.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the supplier\'s next audit.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating assigned to the audit based on findings.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score assigned to the supplier based on audit outcomes.']
  supplier_rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating assigned to the supplier after the audit.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the supplier\'s environmental and social performance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit record.']

  Note: 'Records of on-site and remote quality system audits conducted at supplier facilities. Captures audit type (initial qualification, surveillance, for-cause), audit date, audit team, scope of audit (ISO 9001, IATF 16949, SEMI standards), findings count by severity (critical, major, minor), corrective action plan status, and overall audit rating. Drives supplier performance management and AVL maintenance decisions.'
}

Table supply.risk_assessment {
  risk_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for risk_assessment']
  disruption_event_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for a recorded supply disruption event.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or component impacted.']
  primary_risk_alternate_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of an approved alternate supplier for the material.']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Project‑Level Supply Risk Assessment records risk scores per research project, supporting risk registers and mitigation planning.']
  risk_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Risk assessments assign an employee owner for accountability; the owner is recorded in the risk owner employee field.']
  risk_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Risk assessments evaluate specific suppliers or supplier-material combinations.']
  assessment_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the assessment, used for tracking and reporting.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the risk assessment was initially created.']
  assessment_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the risk assessment, typically includes supplier or material reference.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk assessment.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who performed the risk assessment.']
  buffer_stock_target_qty varchar [note: 'Target quantity of safety stock to buffer against the risk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk assessment record was first created.']
  disruption_end_date date [note: 'Date when the disruption was resolved or ended.']
  disruption_escalation_level varchar [note: 'Severity level for escalation and response.']
  disruption_event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the disruption that occurred.']
  disruption_report_date date [note: 'Date when the disruption was reported.']
  disruption_reported_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who reported the disruption.']
  disruption_start_date date [note: 'Date when the disruption began.']
  disruption_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the disruption record.']
  estimated_fab_impact_wafer_starts varchar [note: 'Projected loss of wafer starts due to the disruption.']
  impact_estimate_wafer_starts varchar [note: 'Estimated number of wafer starts that could be lost if the risk occurs.']
  impact_severity varchar [note: 'Potential impact level if the risk materializes.']
  impacted_po_quantity varchar [note: 'Total purchase order quantity affected by the disruption.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent risk assessment review.']
  mitigation_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Actions executed to mitigate the disruption impact.']
  mitigation_strategy varchar [note: 'Planned actions to reduce probability or impact of the risk.']
  probability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Likelihood of the risk event occurring, expressed as a percentage.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the disruption handling process.']
  review_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the risk assessment is reviewed.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the risk type being evaluated.']
  risk_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes and observations about the risk.']
  risk_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the risk assessment becomes effective.']
  risk_expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the risk assessment is no longer valid.']
  risk_last_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the risk record.']
  risk_owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department of the risk owner.']
  risk_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person accountable for managing the risk.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numeric score (0‑100) representing overall risk severity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Comprehensive supply risk management combining prospective risk assessments and actual disruption event records. Captures risk evaluations (risk category — single-source, geopolitical, financial, natural disaster, export control; probability, impact severity, risk score, mitigation strategy, buffer stock targets, alternate supplier identification, review cadence) and realized disruption incidents (disruption ID, event type — supplier factory shutdown, natural disaster, logistics delay, customs hold, quality hold, geopolitical trade restriction; affected supplier and materials, disruption start and end dates, impacted PO quantities, estimated fab impact in wafer starts at risk, escalation level, mitigation actions taken, resolution outcome). SSOT for all supply risk evaluation and disruption tracking within the supply domain. Supports supply chain resilience planning, CHIPS Act compliance reporting, and executive supply chain risk dashboards.'
}

Table supply.sourcing_contract {
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sourcing contract record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Sourcing contracts are negotiated to supply products for a specific customer account, critical for contract management and revenue forecasting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who approved the contract.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Contracts for controlled commodities must reference the export license that governs the supply terms.']
  sourcing_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier party associated with this contract.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Contracts are negotiated with specific suppliers. Essential commercial relationship link.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the contract within internal governance.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Internal or industry code (e.g., part number, SKU) that uniquely identifies the commodity.']
  commodity_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the material, component, or service being sourced.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned by the organization for tracking and reference.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of the sourcing contract indicating its business purpose.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in the contract.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'Incoterms governing responsibility and cost transfer between buyer and supplier.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract becomes legally binding.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract terminates unless extended; nullable for open‑ended agreements.']
  force_majeure_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing conditions under which performance may be excused due to extraordinary events.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard supplier lead time in calendar days for the commodity.']
  maximum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Largest quantity the supplier agrees to deliver in a single order.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity the supplier will accept per order under the contract.']
  pcn_notification_obligation varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s obligation to notify the buyer of Product Change Notifications.']
  price_escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing any price adjustment mechanisms (e.g., CPI‑linked escalations).']
  quality_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific quality standards, inspection criteria, and acceptance metrics required by the buyer.']
  rebate_tier varchar [note: 'Rebate level applicable based on purchase volume or other criteria.']
  sourcing_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract.']
  sourcing_supplier_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Contracts are negotiated with specific suppliers.']
  supply_continuity_obligation varchar [note: 'Commitments related to uninterrupted supply, safety stock, or backup sources.']
  target_quantity varchar [note: 'Planned total quantity to be procured under the contract.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of the contract (unit_price * target_quantity). Stored as raw data; derived metrics are calculated downstream.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the target quantity (e.g., pieces, kilograms).']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price per unit of the commodity, expressed in the contract currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the contract record.']

  Note: 'End-to-end sourcing lifecycle records from initial solicitation through executed supply agreement. Covers RFQ issuance (commodity description, target quantity, technical specifications, evaluation criteria, submission deadline), supplier quotation responses (quoted unit price, currency, delivery lead time, MOQ, technical deviations), comparative evaluation and award decisions, and executed contract terms (contract number, supplier, commodity scope, effective and expiry dates, minimum order quantities, volume rebate tiers, price escalation clauses, force majeure terms, PCN notification obligations, quality requirements, and supply continuity obligations). SSOT for the complete RFQ-to-contract sourcing lifecycle within the supply domain. Distinct from customer-facing sales contracts owned by the sales domain.'
}

Table supply.material_requirement_plan {
  material_requirement_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each material requirement plan record.']
  primary_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key referencing the material master record.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved supplier that will fulfill the planned order.']
  tertiary_material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.material_master.material_master_id — MRP outputs are calculated for specific materials.']
  batch_managed_flag varchar [note: 'Flag indicating if the material requires batch tracking (Y) or not (N).']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the MRP system generated this record.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency in which the planned cost is expressed.']
  demand_date date [note: 'Date on which the underlying demand (e.g., production start) is scheduled.']
  demand_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the material required by production orders or forecasts.']
  exception_message varchar [note: 'Textual description of any planning exception (e.g., shortfall, excess).']
  is_fixed_lot boolean [note: 'True if the lot size is fixed by engineering, otherwise False.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification (e.g., quantity change, status update).']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from order placement to receipt of material.']
  lot_sizing_procedure varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate the lot size for the planned order.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Customer‑visible material number (e.g., SKU) used in procurement and production.']
  material_requirement_plan_status varchar [note: 'Operational status indicating whether the line is still in planning, released to procurement, or flagged.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'Code of the person or system that owns the MRP parameters for the material.']
  mrp_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the planning method used for this line.']
  planned_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary cost of the planned quantity based on standard price.']
  planned_delivery_date date [note: 'Target date by which the material must be available for production.']
  planned_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material that the MRP run recommends procuring.']
  planning_horizon_end date [note: 'Last day of the time window considered by the MRP run.']
  planning_horizon_start date [note: 'First day of the time window considered by the MRP run.']
  planning_run_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the MRP calculation was performed.']
  planning_run_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the MRP execution that generated this plan.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fabrication plant or site for which the plan is created.']
  procurement_group varchar [note: 'Organizational unit within supply chain that owns the procurement process.']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a new procurement recommendation.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity kept as buffer to protect against demand or supply variability.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit in which the planned quantity is expressed.']

  Note: 'Material Requirements Planning (MRP) run outputs defining planned procurement quantities and timing for all fab materials based on production schedules, wafer start plans, safety stock levels, and reorder points. Captures planning run date, material, plant, planned order quantity, planned delivery date, MRP type, lot sizing procedure, and exception messages (shortfall, excess, rescheduling). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP (MDKP/MDTB).'
}

Table supply.inbound_shipment {
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inbound shipment record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.carrier. Business justification: Link inbound shipments to carrier entity for logistics; replace free-text carrier column with carrier_id FK.']
  experimental_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_lot. Business justification: Shipment Tracking links each inbound shipment to the specific experimental lot it supplies; required for logistics and receipt reconciliation.']
  inbound_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.purchase_order.purchase_order_id — Inbound shipments deliver against purchase orders. Required for logistics tracking and landed cost.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.purchase_order.purchase_order_id — Inbound shipments fulfill specific purchase orders. Critical for logistics-to-procurement traceability.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the material.']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Date on which the shipment was physically received.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the bill of lading document.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature‑controlled handling is required.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance assessment for the shipment (e.g., compliant, exception, pending).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inbound shipment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the freight cost.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customs entry for the shipment.']
  delivery_window_end date [note: 'End date of the agreed delivery window.']
  delivery_window_start date [note: 'Start date of the agreed delivery window.']
  destination_plant varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab or warehouse receiving the shipment.']
  ear_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains items subject to Export Administration Regulations.']
  estimated_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the shipment is expected to arrive.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification code (e.g., ECCN) for the shipment.']
  freight_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost charged by the carrier for transporting the shipment.']
  hazardous_goods_classification varchar [note: 'Classification code for hazardous materials per UN/ADR.']
  inbound_po_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.purchase_order.purchase_order_id — Inbound shipments deliver materials against purchase orders. Required for supply continuity monitoring and landed cost calculation.']
  inbound_shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inbound shipment.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial term defining responsibility and cost allocation.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains items subject to ITAR regulations.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from shipment dispatch to arrival.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions related to the shipment.']
  origin_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the shipment origin.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Description of the primary packaging used (e.g., pallet, container, drum).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk rating for the shipment based on supplier and logistics factors.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'External shipment reference number assigned by the carrier or logistics provider.']
  shipment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the primary shipment event (e.g., departure from supplier).']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature for the shipment when cold chain is required.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature for the shipment when cold chain is required.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking identifier provided by the carrier.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transport used for the inbound shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inbound shipment record.']
  volume_m3 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the shipment.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of the shipment in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Inbound logistics records tracking the physical movement of procured materials from supplier origin to fab or warehouse destination. Captures shipment number, carrier, mode of transport (air, sea, road, courier), origin country, destination plant, estimated and actual arrival dates, freight cost, customs declaration number, Incoterms, hazardous goods classification, and cold chain requirements for temperature-sensitive chemicals. Supports landed cost calculation and supply continuity monitoring.'
}

Table supply.supplier_scorecard {
  supplier_scorecard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier scorecard record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Supplier performance scorecards are evaluated per key customer account to drive strategic sourcing decisions.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal reviewer who approved the scorecard.']
  primary_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier to which this scorecard applies.']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Project‑Specific Supplier Scorecard evaluates supplier performance for a given research project; needed for project risk and quality reporting.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Scorecards measure a specific supplier\'s performance. Mandatory relationship for supplier performance management.']
  tertiary_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Scorecards rate a specific supplier\'s performance.']
  assessment_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the scorecard assessment period or initiative.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the scorecard was formally closed.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Primary internal contact responsible for the scorecard.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate actions taken to contain the defect.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned corrective action to resolve the defect.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scorecard record was created.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Description of the defect or non-conformance that prompted the SCAR.']
  incoming_quality_rejection_rate_dppm varchar [note: 'Defective parts per million observed on incoming material.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or observations.']
  on_time_delivery_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries received on or before the promised date.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Overall performance rating for the supplier in the period.']
  pcn_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier complied with required PCNs during the period.']
  preventive_action varchar [note: 'Preventive measures to avoid recurrence of the defect.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score based on supply risk factors.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause identified for the defect.']
  scar_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the supplier must complete corrective actions.']
  scar_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for a Supplier Corrective Action Request linked to this scorecard.']
  scar_trigger_event varchar [note: 'Event or issue that initiated the SCAR.']
  scorecard_type varchar [note: 'Frequency or nature of the assessment (e.g., annual, quarterly, monthly).']
  scoring_period_end date [note: 'End date of the scoring period.']
  scoring_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the scoring period.']
  strategic_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic importance tier assigned to the supplier.']
  supplier_scorecard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scorecard.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scorecard.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of verification of the corrective action implementation.']

  Note: 'Unified supplier performance management, corrective action, and continuous improvement records. Captures periodic scorecard assessments (scoring period, overall rating, on-time delivery rate, incoming quality rejection rate in DPPM, PCN compliance, strategic tier), plus formal Supplier Corrective Action Requests (SCARs) issued for quality escapes, inspection failures, delivery non-conformances, or audit findings. SCAR records include SCAR number, triggering event, defect description, 8D problem-solving steps (containment, root cause, corrective action, preventive action), supplier response due dates, verification status, and closure dates. SSOT for all supplier performance measurement and corrective action within the supply domain. Drives AVL status decisions, preferred supplier designations, FMEA-driven quality improvement, DPPM reduction programs, and annual supplier business reviews.'
}

Table supply.osat_work_order {
  osat_work_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the OSAT work order record.']
  approved_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the outsourced assembly and test (OSAT) vendor responsible for execution.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the work order record.']
  osat_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — OSAT work orders are issued to specific OSAT vendors (who are suppliers). Critical for subcontractor management.']
  primary_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — OSAT work orders are issued to a specific OSAT vendor (who is a supplier).']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal IC design project associated with this work order.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — OSAT work orders are issued to a specific OSAT vendor (who is a supplier). Mandatory for subcontractor management.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the work order received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the work order within internal governance.']
  assembly_process_spec varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the assembly steps, materials, and equipment to be used.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification result for export controls and regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the work order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary values in the work order.']
  delivery_commit_date date [note: 'Date by which the OSAT vendor must deliver the finished product.']
  die_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of individual dies to be assembled and packaged under this work order.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of export control regime applicable to the work order.']
  is_ear_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work order is subject to U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR).']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order was officially issued to the OSAT vendor.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the work order record.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Historical or contracted lead time for the OSAT process.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any supplemental information or special instructions.']
  nre_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'One-time engineering fee associated with the work order.']
  osat_work_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work order.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Type of semiconductor package to be produced (e.g., Wafer-Level Chip Scale Package, Integrated Fan-Out).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level for scheduling and resource allocation.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Quality tier assigned to the work order based on inspection results.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing supply risk for this work order (higher = higher risk).']
  shipment_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for the outbound shipment.']
  substrate_type varchar [note: 'Material of the substrate used for the package (e.g., silicon, glass).']
  target_cycle_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from work order issue to delivery commitment.']
  total_assembly_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount contracted for assembly (unit cost * quantity + NRE).']
  unit_assembly_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost to assemble a single die/package unit, excluding NRE.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the work order record.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the OSAT subcontractor.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the work order by the OSAT vendor or internal system.']

  Note: 'Work orders issued to OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) subcontractors for backend services including die packaging, wire bonding, flip-chip assembly, wafer-level packaging (WLCSP, InFO, CoWoS), and final test. Captures work order number, OSAT vendor, package type, die quantity, substrate type, assembly process specification, target cycle time, NRE charges, unit assembly cost, delivery commit date, and shipment tracking. Primary transactional record for managing outsourced backend operations within the supply domain.'
}

Table supply.supplier_corrective_action {
  supplier_corrective_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the supplier corrective action record.']
  related_defect_defect_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the defect record in the defect tracking system.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier to which the corrective action applies.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual monetary cost incurred after corrective action completion.']
  attached_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting documentation (e.g., inspection reports, photos).']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the corrective action meets regulatory compliance requirements.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate actions taken to contain the defect and prevent further impact.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned action(s) the supplier must implement to eliminate the root cause.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action implementation.']
  cost_justification varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the estimated/actual cost was incurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SCAR record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost fields.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the defect or non‑conformance discovered.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the supplier must submit a response or corrective plan.']
  ear_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the SCAR involves items subject to Export Administration Regulations.']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary cost to implement the corrective action.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification code (e.g., ECCN) applicable to the SCAR.']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SCAR was formally issued to the supplier.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the SCAR involves items subject to International Traffic in Arms Regulations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations related to the SCAR.']
  preventive_action varchar [note: 'Long‑term actions to prevent recurrence of similar defects.']
  preventive_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the preventive action implementation.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the SCAR for handling.']
  related_po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number associated with the material that triggered the SCAR, if applicable.']
  risk_assessment_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score representing the risk associated with the defect.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Analysis identifying the underlying cause of the defect.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the root cause (e.g., process, material, design).']
  scar_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the SCAR by the organization.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the defect impact.']
  supplier_corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the SCAR.']
  supplier_response varchar [note: 'Narrative response provided by the supplier to the SCAR.']
  supplier_response_date date [note: 'Date on which the supplier submitted their response.']
  triggering_event varchar [note: 'Description of the event (e.g., inspection failure, delivery non‑conformance) that initiated the SCAR.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SCAR record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the corrective action was verified as effective.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify corrective action (e.g., inspection, test, audit).']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the verification of the corrective action implementation.']

  Note: 'Formal Supplier Corrective Action Requests (SCARs) issued to suppliers following quality escapes, incoming inspection failures, delivery non-conformances, or audit findings. Captures SCAR number, triggering event, defect description, 8D problem-solving steps (containment, root cause, corrective action, preventive action), supplier response due date, verification status, and closure date. Supports FMEA-driven quality improvement and DPPM reduction programs.'
}
