Table supply.material_certification {
  material_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each material certification record.']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.certification. Business justification: Material certifications are a type of compliance certification; linking provides traceability to the certifying body.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name of the internal engineer or manager who approved the certification for use.']
  material_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier that issued the certification.']
  materials_research_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.materials_research. Business justification: Materials Research requires certification records for each researched material; linking certification to materials_research_id ensures compliance tracking.']
  primary_material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.material_master.material_master_id — Certifications (CoC/CoA) are issued for specific materials. Required for incoming quality release.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Certifications are issued by the supplying vendor. Required for traceability.']
  tertiary_material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.material_master.material_master_id — Material certifications (CoC/CoA) are issued for specific materials. Required for incoming quality release decisions.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the certification was approved for production release.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of certification document, such as Certificate of Conformance, Certificate of Analysis, RoHS compliance, REACH compliance, TSCA certification, or Product Change Notification.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard to which the certification attests compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the system.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Path or URL to the electronic certification document.']
  document_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the certification document.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the certification expires or becomes invalid.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the certification was issued by the supplier.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or department that issued the certification.']
  material_certification_supplier_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Certifications are issued BY a specific supplier for their material lots.']
  material_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier assigned to the batch of material covered by the certification.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the material as defined in the material master.']
  material_supplier_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Certifications are issued by a specific supplier. Required to trace compliance evidence to its source.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional remarks or observations.']
  pcn_change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the change communicated in the PCN.']
  pcn_change_type varchar [note: 'Category of change described in the PCN.']
  pcn_customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PCN must be communicated to customers.']
  pcn_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the PCN change becomes effective.']
  pcn_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Summary of the impact of the PCN on downstream processes and customers.']
  pcn_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PCN associated with this material certification, if applicable.']
  pcn_requalification_decision varchar [note: 'Decision on whether the material requires re‑qualification after the PCN.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating associated with the material based on certification and supplier data.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the compliance test associated with the certification.']
  test_units varchar [note: 'Units of measurement for the test value, such as ppm, mg/kg, or %.']
  test_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Quantitative result of the test (e.g., concentration, impurity level).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']

  Note: 'Supplier-originated material compliance documents including quality certifications and product change notifications. Captures Certificate of Conformance (CoC), Certificate of Analysis (CoA), RoHS compliance declarations, REACH SVHC declarations, TSCA certifications, and Product Change Notifications (PCNs). For certifications: certification type, issuing supplier, material lot number, test results, compliance standard, issue date, expiry date, and document reference. For PCNs: PCN number, supplier, affected material or component, change type (process, material, site, EOL), change description, effective date, impact assessment, required customer notification obligation, and re-qualification decision. SSOT for all supplier-originated material compliance and change documentation within the supply domain. Required for regulatory compliance, incoming quality release, process stability, and customer PCN pass-through obligations.'
}

Table supply.supply_forecast {
  supply_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each supply forecast entry.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.material_master.material_master_id — Forecasts specify quantities for specific materials.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Purchase forecasts are communicated TO specific suppliers for capacity planning.']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material (part number) for which the forecast is generated.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the strategic supplier receiving the forecast.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the material is subject to regulatory constraints (e.g., RoHS, REACH) during the forecast period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the forecast record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency used for price‑related attributes.']
  forecast_source varchar [note: 'Name of the ERP or planning system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM) that produced the forecast record.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the forecast (e.g., draft, released to supplier, approved, or rejected).']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Specifies if the forecast quantity is a firm commitment (firm) or an indicative estimate (indicative).']
  horizon_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date covered by the forecast horizon.']
  horizon_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date covered by the forecast horizon.']
  is_ltb boolean [note: 'True if the forecast is used for planning a final purchase of an end‑of‑life material.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the forecast was last reviewed by supply‑chain planners.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned supplier lead time in calendar days for the material during the forecast horizon.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, assumptions, or explanations related to the forecast.']
  period varchar [note: 'Defines whether the forecast is expressed per week or per month.']
  price_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Estimated cost per unit of material for the forecast horizon.']
  prior_forecast_quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Forecast quantity from the immediately preceding version, used for variance calculations.']
  quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Projected amount of the material to be supplied during the forecast horizon, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Indicates which regulatory regime applies to the material for the forecasted period.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk rating (0‑100) reflecting supply‑chain uncertainty for the forecasted material.']
  supply_material_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.material_master.material_master_id — Forecasts are for specific materials. Required to link forecast quantities to procurable items.']
  supply_supplier_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — Forecasts are communicated to specific suppliers for capacity planning. Required for supplier collaboration.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit (e.g., pcs, kg) used for the forecast quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the forecast record.']
  variance_quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Numeric difference between the current forecast quantity and the quantity from the previous forecast version.']
  version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the forecast, incremented on each revision.']

  Note: 'Forward-looking supply demand forecasts communicated to strategic suppliers as rolling purchase forecasts (typically 12-52 week horizon) to enable supplier capacity planning and material pre-positioning. Captures forecast period, material, supplier, forecasted quantity by week/month, firm vs. indicative horizon split, forecast version, and variance from prior forecast. Supports supplier capacity reservation and LTB (Last Time Buy) planning for EOL materials.'
}

Table supply.supplier_quotation {
  supplier_quotation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier quotation record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal buying organization requesting the quotation.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Supplier quotations are issued for specific IC parts; linking enables price and lead‑time analysis per part.']
  primary_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the quotation response.']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Research Project Quotation Request process records which project initiated a supplier quotation; enables project‑level cost analysis.']
  award_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for the awarded price.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date on which the quotation was awarded to the supplier.']
  award_decision varchar [note: 'Result of the quotation evaluation.']
  award_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Agreed delivery lead time after award.']
  award_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final agreed price per unit after award.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the material or service being sourced.']
  commodity_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the commodity requested.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the quotation meets regulatory compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quotation record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the quoted total price.']
  evaluation_criteria varchar [note: 'Criteria used to score and compare supplier quotations.']
  evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score assigned to the supplier quotation based on evaluation criteria.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification code (e.g., ECCN) for the quoted item.']
  is_ear_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the quotation is subject to U.S. Export Administration Regulations.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RFQ was issued to suppliers.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity the supplier is willing to sell.']
  net_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final net price after discounts and taxes.']
  quotation_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the RFQ/quotation.']
  quoted_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Supplier‑stated delivery lead time in days.']
  quoted_total_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total price (unit price multiplied by quoted quantity) before discounts and taxes.']
  quoted_unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit offered by the supplier.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk assessment score associated with this quotation.']
  submission_deadline date [note: 'Last date suppliers may submit their quotation.']
  supplier_quotation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quotation.']
  supplier_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier submitted their quotation.']
  target_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity the buyer intends to procure.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tax amount applicable to the quoted total price.']
  technical_deviation_notes varchar [note: 'Supplier‑provided notes on any deviations from the requested specifications.']
  technical_specifications varchar [note: 'Detailed technical requirements attached to the RFQ.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the target quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the quotation record.']

  Note: 'Sourcing solicitation and supplier response records covering the full RFQ-to-award lifecycle. Captures RFQ issuance (commodity description, target quantity, technical specifications, evaluation criteria, submission deadline), supplier responses (quoted unit price, currency, delivery lead time, MOQ, technical deviations), comparative evaluation scores, and award/decline decisions. Enables competitive bidding, side-by-side comparison for sourcing decisions, and forms the basis for PO price negotiation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (EKKO type AN).'
}

Table supply.product_change_notification {
  product_change_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the product change notification record.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material impacted by the change.']
  product_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier issuing the PCN.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: PCNs often trigger regulatory filings; linking enables automatic filing generation and status tracking.']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: PCN impact assessment links each product change notification to affected research projects to trigger re‑qualification and schedule updates.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — PCNs are issued by specific suppliers. Must trace which supplier initiated the change notification.']
  affected_component varchar [note: 'Specific component or part of the product impacted by the change.']
  affected_material_number varchar [note: 'Material number of the impacted component.']
  affected_process_step varchar [note: 'Specific process step (e.g., lithography, etch) impacted by the change.']
  affected_site varchar [note: 'Manufacturing site or fab location impacted by the change.']
  attachment_url varchar [note: 'Link to the PDF or electronic file containing the full PCN details.']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature of the change.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Category of the change being announced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the PCN record was first created in the system.']
  customer_notification_deadline date [note: 'Latest date by which the customer must acknowledge or act on the PCN.']
  ear_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the change is subject to Export Administration Regulations.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the change becomes effective in production.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification code (e.g., ECCN) associated with the change.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of how the change affects product performance, yield, and qualification.']
  impact_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the change impact.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the change is subject to International Traffic in Arms Regulations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  pcn_number varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the supplier for the change notification.']
  product_change_notification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product change notification.']
  product_pcn_supplier_fk varchar [note: 'FK to supply.supplier.supplier_id — PCNs are issued BY a specific supplier announcing changes to their materials/processes.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing body or standard organization to which the PCN is reported.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the PCN must be reported to a governing body (e.g., SEMI).']
  requalification_decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the re‑qualification decision was made.']
  requalification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the change triggers a re‑qualification of the affected product.']
  requalification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the re‑qualification effort.']
  required_customer_notification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier requires the customer to be notified of the change.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned to the PCN based on supply impact.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the PCN record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the PCN document, incremented on each amendment.']

  Note: 'Product Change Notifications (PCNs) received from suppliers announcing changes to materials, processes, packaging, or manufacturing sites that may impact semiconductor product performance or qualification status. Captures PCN number, supplier, affected material or component, change type (process, material, site, EOL), change description, effective date, impact assessment, required customer notification obligation, and re-qualification decision. Critical for maintaining process stability and customer PCN pass-through compliance.'
}

Table supply.disruption_event {
  disruption_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for disruption_event']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Disruption impact assessments are tied to affected customer accounts for risk mitigation and service level management.']
  affected_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier impacted by the disruption.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who logged the disruption.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material (part) impacted by the disruption.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Purchase order that is impacted by the disruption.']
  cause_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the root cause of the disruption.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the disruption record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the financial impact amount.']
  disruption_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the disruption domain.']
  disruption_duration_days varchar [note: 'Calculated duration of the disruption in whole days.']
  disruption_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disruption ended or was resolved.']
  disruption_event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the disruption event.']
  disruption_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the disruption event for tracking across systems.']
  disruption_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disruption began.']
  disruption_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the root cause of the supply disruption.']
  ear_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the disruption involves items subject to the U.S. Export Administration Regulations.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Severity level used to prioritize response actions.']
  estimated_fab_impact_wafer_starts varchar [note: 'Estimated number of wafer starts that could be delayed or lost due to the disruption.']
  estimated_financial_impact_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of the financial loss caused by the disruption.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification code (e.g., ECCN) relevant to the disrupted material.']
  impacted_po_quantity decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total quantity on the affected purchase order that is at risk.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the disruption involves ITAR‑controlled items.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Factory or site code where the disruption originated or was observed.']
  mitigation_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of actions performed to mitigate the disruption.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the disruption.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disruption was first reported in the system.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the disruption handling process.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk rating assigned to the disruption event.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, MES) that supplied the disruption data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the disruption record.']

  Note: 'Records of actual supply disruption events impacting material availability, including supplier factory shutdowns, natural disasters, logistics delays, customs holds, quality holds, and geopolitical trade restrictions. Captures disruption event type, affected supplier and materials, disruption start and end dates, impacted PO quantity, estimated fab impact (wafer starts at risk), escalation level, mitigation actions taken, and resolution outcome. Feeds supply risk assessment updates and executive supply chain reporting.'
}

Table supply.supply_agreement {
  supply_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the SupplyAgreement association']
  research_project_id varchar [note: 'Links to the R&D project consuming the supplied material or service']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Links to the supplier providing the material or service']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the contract between supplier and project']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Agreed lead time for delivery of supplies to the project']
  price_escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Clause describing how prices may be adjusted over the contract term']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Quality performance rating for the supplier in the context of the project']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the supplier for this project']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual relationship between a supplier and a research project. Each record captures the specific terms of that engagement, such as contract identifier, agreed lead time, risk assessment, quality rating, and any price‑escalation clauses.. Existence Justification: Suppliers provide materials, services, or equipment to many R&D projects, and each research project sources from multiple suppliers. The partnership is managed through formal contracts that capture lead times, risk scores, quality ratings, and pricing clauses, and these contracts are created, updated, and retired by business users.'
}

Table supply.consignment_agreement {
  consignment_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for consignment_agreement']

  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier party that owns the consigned inventory.']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the consignment agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the consignment agreement by the organization.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of consignment agreement indicating the fulfillment model.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the agreement received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved the consignment agreement.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the agreement with internal and external regulations.']
  consignment_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or facility code where the consigned inventory is stored.']
  consignment_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total quantity of the item placed on consignment.']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the agreement document (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consignment agreement record was first created in the system.']

  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the consignment agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the consignment agreement expires or is scheduled to end; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  inventory_item_sku varchar [note: 'Stock‑keeping unit of the consigned item as defined in the product master.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for additional remarks, special conditions, or comments.']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Agreed price for each unit of the consigned item.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement renews automatically, requires manual action, or does not renew.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Internal assessment of supply‑chain risk associated with the consignment agreement.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the consignment agreement.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the counter‑party must be notified prior to termination.']
  total_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Calculated total monetary value of the consignment (quantity × price per unit).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the consigned quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the consignment agreement record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for consignment_agreement. Referenced by consignment_agreement_id.'
}

Table supply.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for carrier']
  parent_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on carrier (parent_carrier_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the carrier\'s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the carrier\'s mailing address, if applicable.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Internal business code used to reference the carrier within the enterprise.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation provided by the carrier.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the carrier\'s mailing address.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of regulatory or industry certifications held by the carrier (e.g., ISO 9001, C‑TPAT).']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the carrier\'s primary contact.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person for the carrier.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number of the carrier\'s primary contact.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier agreement expires or is terminated; null if open‑ended.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier agreement became effective.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the carrier is headquartered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier record was first created in the system.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of cargo insurance coverage provided by the carrier.']
  insurance_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the insurance coverage amount.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of days from shipment booking to delivery.']
  max_volume_cbm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum cargo volume the carrier can accommodate per shipment, expressed in cubic meters.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum payload weight the carrier can handle per shipment, expressed in kilograms.']
  name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the logistics carrier.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any supplemental information about the carrier.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the carrier\'s mailing address.']
  rating_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Performance rating of the carrier on a 0‑5 scale.']
  rating_source varchar [note: 'Source or agency that provided the carrier rating.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Four‑character code assigned to the carrier by the Standard Carrier Alpha Code registry.']
  service_area varchar [note: 'Geographic regions or markets served by the carrier.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the carrier\'s mailing address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carrier record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the carrier record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for carrier. Referenced by carrier_id.'
}

Table research.research_program {
  research_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for program']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Program‑level budgeting consolidates expenses under a cost center, required for finance\'s program cost roll‑up reports.']
  director_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the program director, linking to HR systems for organizational reporting.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: PROGRAM TARGETING: Technology program focuses on a product family; needed for budgeting, KPI tracking, and strategic alignment.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Program budgeting reports tie each program to the sponsoring org unit for financial governance.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Program financial performance is tracked via a profit center to allocate licensing and product revenue.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the program was completed or terminated. Null if program is still active.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the program commenced execution. Null if program has not yet started.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for budget amounts (typically USD for semiconductor R&D programs).']
  budget_envelope_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget allocation for the program across all fiscal years, in USD. Includes NRE, capital equipment, materials, and labor costs.']
  charter_description varchar [note: 'Detailed charter describing program scope, deliverables, success criteria, and boundaries. Defines what is in-scope and out-of-scope for the program.']
  chips_act_award_number varchar [note: 'Official award or grant number assigned by the US Department of Commerce for CHIPS Act funded programs. Null if not a CHIPS Act program.']
  chips_act_program_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) whether this program is funded or governed under the US CHIPS and Science Act, requiring specific compliance reporting.']
  collaboration_partners varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of external research institutions, universities, consortia, or industry partners collaborating on this program (e.g., \'MIT, IMEC, Stanford University\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this program record was first created in the system.']
  current_phase varchar [note: 'Current execution phase of the program: concept (initial research), feasibility (proof of concept), development (engineering), integration (system-level), qualification (reliability testing), production_ramp (volume manufacturing readiness), closed (program complete). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|feasibility|development|integration|qualification|production_ramp|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  experimental_wafer_lot_count varchar [note: 'Total number of experimental wafer lots processed for this program across all process splits and iterations.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export Administration Regulations (EAR) classification code (ECCN) or ITAR category for technologies developed in this program. Null if not export-controlled.']
  gate_review_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage-gate review checkpoint: gate_0 (concept approval), gate_1 (feasibility), gate_2 (development authorization), gate_3 (integration readiness), gate_4 (qualification approval), gate_5 (production release), post_program_review (lessons learned). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gate_0|gate_1|gate_2|gate_3|gate_4|gate_5|post_program_review — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ip_filing_count varchar [note: 'Total number of patent applications filed as a result of this R&D program. Key metric for innovation output.']
  itar_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) whether this program involves ITAR-controlled defense articles or technical data requiring export control compliance.']
  key_performance_indicators varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of quantitative success metrics for the program (e.g., \'PPA improvement: 30% power reduction, 20% area reduction\', \'Yield target: >85% at qualification\').']
  last_gate_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal gate review conducted for this program.']
  last_gate_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Decision outcome from the most recent gate review: approved (proceed to next phase), conditional_approval (proceed with conditions), deferred (additional work required), rejected (program termination recommended).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this program record was last updated.']
  next_gate_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal gate review. Null if program is completed or no review scheduled.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, lessons learned, or special considerations not captured in structured fields.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date for program completion and achievement of all strategic objectives.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned date when the program is scheduled to begin execution (charter approval and resource allocation).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Relative priority ranking of this program within the R&D portfolio (1=highest priority). Used for resource allocation and executive attention.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the R&D program, used in roadmaps, budgets, and executive reporting.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the R&D program (e.g., \'2nm Gate-All-Around Technology Development\', \'1.4nm CFET Process Node Program\').']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the R&D program: planning (charter development), active (execution underway), on_hold (temporarily suspended), completed (objectives achieved), cancelled (terminated before completion), archived (historical record).']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the R&D program by technology domain: process node development (e.g., 2nm, 1.4nm), device architecture (FinFET, GAA, CFET), advanced packaging (CoWoS, InFO), materials research, IP core development, or design methodology.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall program risk assessment based on technical complexity, schedule pressure, resource constraints, and external dependencies: low (routine execution), medium (manageable challenges), high (significant obstacles), critical (jeopardy to success).']
  sponsoring_business_unit varchar [note: 'Name of the business unit or division sponsoring and funding the R&D program (e.g., \'Advanced Logic Technology\', \'Foundry Services\', \'Memory Solutions\').']
  strategic_objective varchar [note: 'High-level strategic goal and business rationale for the program (e.g., \'Enable 30% power reduction for mobile SoC applications\', \'Achieve 2x density improvement for AI accelerators\').']
  tapeout_milestone_count varchar [note: 'Number of design tapeouts (GDSII submissions for mask fabrication) completed within this program for test chips, proof-of-concept designs, or qualification vehicles.']
  target_application_domain varchar [note: 'Primary market or application domain this technology targets (e.g., \'Mobile SoC\', \'High-Performance Computing\', \'AI/ML Accelerators\', \'Automotive\', \'IoT Edge Devices\').']
  technical_lead_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the principal engineer or scientist responsible for technical execution and engineering decisions.']
  technology_generation_target varchar [note: 'Target technology node or generation for this program (e.g., \'2nm\', \'1.4nm\', \'N3\', \'N2\'). For non-node programs, describes the technology generation (e.g., \'Gen 5 CoWoS\', \'3rd Gen GAA\').']
  technology_platform varchar [note: 'Underlying technology platform or architecture family this program targets (e.g., \'FinFET\', \'GAA Nanosheet\', \'CFET\', \'FDSOI\', \'Hybrid Bonding\').']
  technology_readiness_level varchar [note: 'Current TRL score (1-9) indicating maturity: 1=basic principles, 3=proof of concept, 5=validation in relevant environment, 7=prototype demonstration, 9=actual system proven in production. Aligned with NASA/DOD TRL framework.']

  Note: 'Master record for R&D programs representing major multi-year technology development initiatives such as next-generation process node development (e.g., 2nm GAA, 1.4nm CFET), advanced packaging platforms, or novel device architecture programs. Captures program charter, technology generation target, strategic objectives, budget envelope, timeline with detailed milestone tracking (milestone name, type, planned/actual dates, completion criteria, gate review outcomes, action items, approving authority), milestone types (technical gates, management reviews, customer demos, tapeout milestones, silicon return, qualification gates), sponsoring business unit, program director, technology readiness level (TRL), and program lifecycle status. SSOT for all R&D program identity, governance, milestone, and gate review data. Supports technology roadmap execution tracking and CHIPS Act program reporting.'
}

Table research.project {
  project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for project']
  chips_act_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.chips_act_obligation. Business justification: CHIPS Act funding ties obligations (domestic production, workforce training) directly to funded research projects for reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Project cost accounting requires linking each R&D project to its primary cost center for budgeting and expense reporting.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Export Control process requires each research project using controlled technology to be linked to its approved export license for audit and reporting.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: PROJECT PRODUCT FOCUS: Research project develops a primary product; essential for milestone reporting and cost allocation.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Project profitability analysis assigns each R&D project to a profit center for revenue attribution and performance reporting.']
  project_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier or username of the person who last modified the research project record, for audit trail purposes.']
  project_principal_investigator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the principal investigator responsible for the research project, linking to Workday Human Capital Management (HCM) system.']
  research_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to research.program.program_id — Every research project is executed under an R&D program. This is the fundamental hierarchical relationship in R&D portfolio management — without it, you cannot roll up project status to program level ']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual completion date when the research project was closed or terminated, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Null if project is still active.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the project budget (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).']
  collaboration_partner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external organization, university, or consortium partner collaborating on the research project, if applicable.']
  collaboration_type varchar [note: 'Nature of collaboration for the research project: internal (within company), university partnership, industry consortium, government-funded program, or Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) partner collaboration.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data confidentiality classification level for the research project information: public (no restrictions), internal (company-only), confidential (restricted access), or highly confidential (executive/need-to-know only).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the research project record was first created in the system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  device_architecture_focus varchar [note: 'Primary device architecture or transistor technology being researched, such as FinFET (Fin Field-Effect Transistor), GAA (Gate All Around), CFET (Complementary FET), or other novel structures.']
  experimental_wafer_lot_count varchar [note: 'Total number of experimental wafer lots processed as part of this research project for proof-of-concept or feasibility testing.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification code or designation for the research project under International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) or Export Administration Regulations (EAR), if applicable.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Primary source of funding for the research project: internal Research and Development (R&D) budget, government grant (e.g., CHIPS Act), industry consortium funding, customer Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) investment, or venture capital.']
  ip_filing_count varchar [note: 'Number of patent or Intellectual Property (IP) applications filed as a result of this research project.']
  ip_filing_planned boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether patent or Intellectual Property (IP) filing is planned as an outcome of this research project.']
  itar_controlled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the research project involves technology or data subject to International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) export controls.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the research project record was last updated or modified, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  mpw_shuttle_participation boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this research project participated in a Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) shuttle run to share fabrication costs with other projects.']
  nre_budget_allocated decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) budget amount allocated to the research project in US dollars, covering design, prototyping, tooling, and development costs.']
  nre_budget_spent decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) budget amount spent to date on the research project in US dollars.']
  phase varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle phase of the research project: exploratory (initial concept), feasibility (viability assessment), development (active R&D), qualification (validation and testing), or closed (completed or terminated).']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Originally planned or target completion date for the research project, in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  plm_system_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier or link to the corresponding project record in Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) systems such as Siemens Teamcenter or Oracle Agile PLM.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the research project for tracking and reference across Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) systems including Siemens Teamcenter and Oracle Agile PLM.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the research and development project, describing the focus area or objective.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the research project indicating whether it is actively progressing, temporarily on hold, successfully completed, cancelled, or awaiting management approval to proceed.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the research project by its primary objective: proof-of-concept (PoC) demonstration, feasibility study, process integration experiment, materials research, Intellectual Property (IP) development initiative, or novel device architecture exploration.']
  research_domain varchar [note: 'Primary technical domain or area of focus for the research project: next-generation process node development, novel device architecture, advanced packaging technologies, materials research, Intellectual Property (IP) core development, lithography techniques, or interconnect technologies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: process_node|device_architecture|advanced_packaging|materials|ip_core|lithography|interconnect — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment level for the research project based on technical complexity, resource requirements, and probability of success: low, medium, high, or critical.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the research project scope, objectives, technical goals, and expected deliverables.']
  start_date date [note: 'Official start date when the research project was initiated and work began, in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  strategic_priority varchar [note: 'Strategic importance and priority level assigned to the research project by executive management: critical (top priority), high, medium, or low.']
  technical_objectives varchar [note: 'Specific technical objectives and success criteria for the research project, including performance targets, yield goals, or proof-of-concept milestones.']
  technology_node_target varchar [note: 'Target process technology node for the research project, expressed in nanometers (e.g., 3nm, 2nm, 1.4nm) or next-generation architecture designation (e.g., Gate-All-Around (GAA), Complementary FET (CFET)).']
  technology_readiness_level varchar [note: 'Technology Readiness Level (TRL) assessment of the research project outcome, ranging from TRL1 (basic principles observed) to TRL9 (actual system proven in operational environment), following NASA/DoD TRL framework. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TRL1|TRL2|TRL3|TRL4|TRL5|TRL6|TRL7|TRL8|TRL9 — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Individual research and development project records within an R&D program. Represents discrete proof-of-concept (PoC) projects, feasibility studies, process integration experiments, materials research efforts, and IP development initiatives. Tracks project scope, objectives, assigned principal investigator, start/end dates, project phase (exploratory, feasibility, development, qualification), funding source, NRE budget allocation, collaboration type (internal, university, consortium), and current project status. Supports PLM integration with Siemens Teamcenter and Oracle Agile PLM.'
}

Table research.research_technology_node {
  research_technology_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the semiconductor process technology node record.']
  fab_site_id varchar [note: 'FK to fabrication.fab_site']
  product_process_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: TECH NODE ALIGNMENT: Research node is mapped to a manufacturing process node for technology road‑mapping and capacity planning.']
  research_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to research.research_program']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this technology node record is currently active and available for use in design and manufacturing systems. False indicates archived or obsolete status.']
  collaboration_partners varchar [note: 'Names of external research institutions, universities, consortia, or industry partners collaborating on the development of this technology node (e.g., IMEC, SEMATECH, university research labs).']
  cost_per_wafer_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual manufacturing cost per wafer for this technology node, measured in US dollars. Used for economic analysis and pricing decisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this technology node record was first created in the system, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  design_rule_manual_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the design rule manual (DRM) document that specifies layout constraints, spacing rules, and design-for-manufacturability (DFM) guidelines for this technology node.']
  device_architecture_type varchar [note: 'The transistor architecture employed at this technology node. Planar for older nodes, FinFET (Fin Field-Effect Transistor) for 22nm-7nm, GAA (Gate-All-Around) for 3nm and below, CFET (Complementary FET) for future nodes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planar|FinFET|GAA|CFET|nanowire|nanosheet|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  environmental_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of this technology node with environmental regulations including RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances), REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals), and other environmental standards.']
  eol_announcement_date date [note: 'Date when the technology node was officially announced as end-of-life, indicating phase-out of new design starts and eventual manufacturing discontinuation.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification for this technology node under ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations), EAR (Export Administration Regulations), or other trade control regimes. Determines export licensing requirements.']
  first_tapeout_date date [note: 'Date of the first design tapeout (final design submission for manufacturing) using this technology node. Marks transition from development to production readiness.']
  foundry_name varchar [note: 'Name of the semiconductor foundry or fabrication facility that developed or manufactures this technology node (e.g., TSMC, Samsung, Intel, GlobalFoundries).']
  gate_pitch_nm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Distance between adjacent transistor gates, measured in nanometers. Critical dimension for density and performance characterization.']
  ip_filing_count varchar [note: 'Number of patent applications or IP disclosures filed related to innovations developed for this technology node. Tracks innovation output.']
  irds_roadmap_alignment varchar [note: 'Reference to the corresponding IRDS (formerly ITRS) roadmap generation or year that this technology node aligns with. Used for industry benchmarking and planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this technology node record was last updated or modified, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  lithography_generation varchar [note: 'Primary lithography technology used for patterning at this node. DUV (Deep Ultraviolet) for 28nm and above, EUV (Extreme Ultraviolet) for 7nm and below, hybrid for transitional nodes.']
  mask_count varchar [note: 'Total number of photomask layers required to manufacture a complete wafer at this technology node. Increases with node complexity and multi-patterning requirements.']
  metal_layer_count varchar [note: 'Number of metal interconnect layers available in the BEOL (Back End of Line) stack for this technology node. Typically ranges from 6 to 15+ layers for advanced nodes.']
  metal_pitch_nm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Minimum spacing between metal interconnect lines in the first metal layer, measured in nanometers. Defines routing density.']
  minimum_feature_size_nm decimal(10,3) [note: 'The smallest feature dimension that can be reliably manufactured at this technology node, measured in nanometers. Critical metric for node classification.']
  multi_patterning_technique varchar [note: 'Advanced lithography technique used to achieve sub-wavelength patterning (e.g., LELE - Litho-Etch-Litho-Etch, SADP - Self-Aligned Double Patterning, SAQP - Self-Aligned Quadruple Patterning). Required for nodes below 20nm with DUV lithography.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code for the technology node used across design, fabrication, and research systems (e.g., N7, N5, N3, N2).']
  node_maturity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the technology node. Research: early exploration; Development: process definition; Pilot: qualification runs; Production: volume manufacturing; Mature: stable high-volume; EOL (End of Life): phase-out.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the process technology node (e.g., 28nm, 16nm FinFET, 7nm EUV, 5nm, 3nm GAA, 2nm CFET). This is the primary business identifier for the node.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about this technology node, including special characteristics, limitations, or historical information.']
  nre_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total non-recurring engineering cost invested in developing this technology node, including R&D, tooling, mask sets, and qualification. Measured in US dollars.']
  pdk_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the PDK (Process Design Kit) associated with this technology node. PDK contains design rules, device models, and libraries required for IC design.']
  process_flow_complexity_score varchar [note: 'Numerical score representing the manufacturing complexity of this technology node based on number of process steps, critical layers, and advanced techniques required. Higher scores indicate greater complexity.']
  production_qualification_date date [note: 'Date when this technology node achieved production qualification status, meaning it met all yield, reliability, and performance targets for volume manufacturing.']
  reliability_qualification_status varchar [note: 'Status of reliability testing and qualification for this technology node, including stress tests, accelerated life testing, and failure analysis. Qualified status indicates the node meets long-term reliability requirements.']
  supply_voltage_v decimal(5,3) [note: 'Nominal core supply voltage for digital logic at this technology node, measured in volts. Decreases with node scaling to reduce power consumption.']
  target_area_scaling_factor decimal(5,3) [note: 'Expected die area reduction factor compared to the previous technology node generation (e.g., 0.5 represents 50% area reduction). Part of PPA (Power-Performance-Area) characterization.']
  target_performance_metric_ghz decimal(10,3) [note: 'Target maximum operating frequency for standard cells at this technology node, measured in gigahertz. Part of PPA (Power-Performance-Area) characterization.']
  target_power_metric_mw_per_mhz decimal(12,6) [note: 'Target power consumption metric for this technology node, measured in milliwatts per megahertz. Part of PPA (Power-Performance-Area) characterization.']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target manufacturing yield (percentage of good dies per wafer) for production-qualified wafers at this technology node. Typically 70-95% for mature nodes.']
  technology_roadmap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.technology_roadmap. Business justification: A research technology node is defined within a technology roadmap; linking provides hierarchy and eliminates redundant roadmap attributes in the node table.']
  transistor_density_mtransistors_per_mm2 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Number of transistors that can be integrated per square millimeter of silicon at this technology node, measured in millions. Key metric for node capability.']
  wafer_size_mm varchar [note: 'Diameter of silicon wafers used for this technology node, measured in millimeters. Standard sizes are 200mm (8-inch) or 300mm (12-inch). Advanced nodes typically use 300mm.']

  Note: 'Reference master for semiconductor process technology nodes and generations (e.g., 28nm, 16nm FinFET, 7nm EUV, 3nm GAA, 2nm CFET). Captures node name, lithography generation (DUV/EUV), device architecture type (planar, FinFET, GAA, CFET), minimum feature size, target PPA (Power-Performance-Area) metrics, process design kit (PDK) version, foundry/fab association, node maturity status (research, development, production, EOL), and ITRS/IRDS roadmap alignment. SSOT for technology node classification used across design, fabrication, and research domains.'
}

Table research.technology_roadmap {
  technology_roadmap_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the technology roadmap record. Primary key.']
  packaging_package_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to packaging.package_type. Business justification: Roadmap planning defines target package types; FK provides traceability from roadmap to the definitive package definition.']
  research_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to research.research_technology_node.research_technology_node_id — Technology roadmaps reference target technology nodes for each milestone. Essential for roadmap-to-execution traceability.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the technology roadmap was formally approved by executive leadership or steering committee.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive or committee that approved this technology roadmap.']
  area_improvement_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target die area reduction percentage or transistor density improvement compared to previous generation, part of PPA (Power Performance Area) metrics.']
  benchmark_confidence_level varchar [note: 'Confidence level in the accuracy and reliability of the competitive benchmark data.']
  benchmark_date date [note: 'Date when the competitive benchmark data was collected or published.']
  benchmark_methodology varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the methodology used to collect and analyze competitive benchmark data.']
  chips_act_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this roadmap is aligned with CHIPS and Science Act compliance reporting requirements for federal funding eligibility.']
  competitive_benchmark_source varchar [note: 'Source type of competitive benchmarking data used for strategic positioning analysis.']
  competitor_drive_current decimal(10,4) [note: 'Benchmarked competitor transistor drive current in microamperes per micrometer (µA/µm), indicating performance capability.']
  competitor_frequency_ghz decimal(6,3) [note: 'Benchmarked competitor maximum operating frequency in gigahertz (GHz).']
  competitor_leakage_current decimal(10,6) [note: 'Benchmarked competitor transistor leakage current in nanoamperes per micrometer (nA/µm), indicating power efficiency.']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Name of competitor or foundry being benchmarked for comparative technology performance analysis.']
  competitor_power_consumption decimal(10,4) [note: 'Benchmarked competitor power consumption in watts (W) or milliwatts per megahertz (mW/MHz).']
  competitor_transistor_density decimal(12,2) [note: 'Benchmarked competitor transistor density in millions of transistors per square millimeter (MTr/mm²).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this technology roadmap record was first created in the system.']
  device_architecture_type varchar [note: 'Target device architecture technology (e.g., \'FinFET\', \'GAA (Gate All Around)\', \'CFET (Complementary FET)\', \'Nanosheet\', \'Nanowire\'). Nullable for non-architecture-specific roadmaps.']
  estimated_nre_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total non-recurring engineering cost in USD required to achieve roadmap milestones.']
  investment_priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority level for capital and R&D investment allocation to support this roadmap execution.']
  key_enabling_technologies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of critical enabling technologies required for roadmap execution (e.g., \'EUV (Extreme Ultraviolet Lithography)\', \'High-NA EUV\', \'ALD (Atomic Layer Deposition)\', \'Selective Etch\').']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this technology roadmap record was last modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent executive review or update of this technology roadmap.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of key technology milestones and deliverables planned within this roadmap.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next executive review or update cycle of this technology roadmap.']
  performance_improvement_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target performance improvement percentage (frequency, speed) compared to previous generation, part of PPA (Power Performance Area) metrics.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'End date of the multi-year planning horizon covered by this roadmap.']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the multi-year planning horizon covered by this roadmap.']
  power_improvement_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target power consumption reduction percentage compared to previous generation, part of PPA (Power Performance Area) metrics.']
  risk_assessment_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of key technical, market, and execution risks identified for this technology roadmap.']
  roadmap_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the technology roadmap (e.g., \'Advanced Node Progression 2024-2030\', \'GAA Transition Roadmap\').']
  roadmap_references_nodes varchar [note: 'FK to research.technology_node.technology_node_id — Technology roadmaps plan the progression through technology nodes. Each roadmap milestone references target nodes for introduction.']
  roadmap_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the technology roadmap.']
  roadmap_targets_node varchar [note: 'FK to research.technology_node.technology_node_id — Technology roadmaps plan the progression through technology nodes. Essential for strategic planning alignment.']
  roadmap_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the technology roadmap, following semantic versioning convention (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1).']
  strategic_implication_assessment varchar [note: 'Executive assessment of strategic implications from competitive benchmarking, including positioning recommendations and investment priorities.']
  target_introduction_date date [note: 'Planned date for technology introduction to production or market availability.']
  target_process_node varchar [note: 'Target semiconductor process node for this roadmap generation (e.g., \'3nm\', \'2nm\', \'1.4nm\', \'A14\'). Nullable for non-node-specific roadmaps.']
  technology_focus_area varchar [note: 'Primary technology domain focus of this roadmap (e.g., process node progression, device architecture evolution, advanced packaging capability).']
  transistor_density_target decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target transistor density in millions of transistors per square millimeter (MTr/mm²) for this technology generation.']

  Note: 'Technology roadmap records defining the multi-year strategic plan for process node progression, device architecture evolution, and advanced packaging capability development. Captures roadmap version, planning horizon, technology milestones, target introduction dates, PPA improvement targets per node generation, key enabling technologies, and roadmap approval status. Includes integrated competitive benchmarking: competitor/foundry performance comparisons (transistor density, drive current, leakage, frequency, power), benchmark sources (public disclosure, teardown analysis, published papers, industry reports), benchmark methodology, confidence levels, benchmark dates, and strategic implication assessments. Supports executive technology strategy, competitive positioning, investment prioritization, and CHIPS Act compliance reporting.'
}

Table research.experimental_lot {
  experimental_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the experimental wafer lot. Primary key for research and development lot tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Experimental lot expenses are charged to a cost center to enable detailed R&D cost tracking in finance.']
  experimental_project_id varchar [note: 'FK to research.project.project_id — Experimental lots are created to execute specific research projects. This FK is essential for tracing which lots belong to which project, enabling project-level cost tracking and outcome assessment.']
  experimental_research_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent research and development project that this experimental lot supports. Links lot to technology roadmap and innovation initiatives.']
  fabrication_process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the non-standard, research-grade process flow recipe defining the sequence of fabrication steps for this lot. Distinct from production process flows due to exploratory parameters and novel techniques.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: PROTOTYPE TRACKING: Experimental lot is fabricated for a specific product; needed for yield and performance reporting.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the research team or organizational unit conducting this experiment. Links to workforce management and resource allocation systems.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Material Procurement Process assigns a supplier to each experimental wafer lot; experts track supplier_id on experimental_lot for sourcing traceability.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when all fabrication and testing activities for this experimental lot were completed. Null if lot is still in process.']
  actual_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual total cost in US Dollars incurred for processing this experimental lot, captured after completion. Business-confidential financial data.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when this experimental lot began fabrication processing. Captured from MES system when lot is released to the fab floor.']
  actual_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured yield percentage achieved after lot completion and testing. Calculated as the ratio of good dies to total dies, expressed as a percentage.']
  collaboration_partner varchar [note: 'Name of external research institution, university, or industry partner collaborating on this experimental lot. Null if internal-only research. Business-confidential due to competitive and IP considerations.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this experimental lot record. Audit trail for accountability and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this experimental lot record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  data_collection_plan varchar [note: 'Description of the metrology, inspection, and test data collection strategy for this experimental lot. Defines what measurements will be taken, at which process steps, and with what frequency to support research objectives.']
  estimated_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost in US Dollars for processing this experimental lot, including materials, equipment time, labor, and overhead. Business-confidential financial data.']
  experimental_outcome_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of the experimental lot results: pending (evaluation not yet complete), successful (met all research objectives), partially successful (met some objectives), failed (did not meet objectives), or inconclusive (results require further investigation).']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification for the technology and data associated with this experimental lot: EAR99 (Export Administration Regulations standard), ECCN (Export Control Classification Number for controlled items), ITAR-controlled (International Traffic in Arms Regulations), or not controlled. Restricted due to national security and compliance requirements.']
  fab_line_assignment varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fabrication line or pilot line where this experimental lot is processed. Research lots often use dedicated R&D fab lines or pilot production facilities separate from high-volume manufacturing.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason why this experimental lot is on hold, if applicable. Examples: equipment failure, material shortage, process excursion, awaiting test results, or engineering review. Null if lot is not on hold.']
  ip_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for any patent application, invention disclosure, or IP filing associated with the innovations tested in this experimental lot. Restricted due to pre-publication IP sensitivity.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this experimental lot record has been archived for historical reference. True if archived and no longer actively referenced in operational systems; False if active or recently completed.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this experimental lot record. Audit trail for accountability and compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this experimental lot record was last updated. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the experimental lot, typically assigned by the Manufacturing Execution System (MES). Used for tracking and referencing in fab operations.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the experimental lot: planned (scheduled but not started), released (authorized for fab processing), in-process (actively being processed), on-hold (temporarily suspended), completed (all processing finished), scrapped (terminated due to failure or obsolescence), or archived (historical record). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|released|in_process|on_hold|completed|scrapped|archived — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lot_to_project varchar [note: 'FK to research.research_project.research_project_id — Every experimental lot is created for a specific research project. This links physical fabrication activity to the project that authorized and funded it.']
  lot_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the experimental lot purpose: process development for new manufacturing steps, device validation for novel architectures, materials research for new substrates or films, split-lot for comparative studies, design of experiments (DOE) for statistical optimization, or proof-of-concept for feasibility assessment.']
  mes_system_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this experimental lot record to the corresponding lot record in the Camstar MES system for wafer tracking and real-time fab operations integration.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date for completing all fabrication and testing activities for this experimental lot. Used for project milestone tracking and Time-To-Market (TTM) planning.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when this experimental lot is planned to begin fabrication processing. Used for capacity planning and resource allocation.']
  principal_investigator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead researcher or engineer responsible for this experimental lot and its associated research objectives. Business-confidential to protect internal R&D organization structure.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Processing priority assigned to this experimental lot relative to other research and production lots. Critical priority for time-sensitive proof-of-concept or customer commitments; high for strategic roadmap projects; normal for standard R&D; low for exploratory research.']
  research_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing detailed research observations, experimental conditions, anomalies, and insights discovered during lot processing. Business-confidential to protect proprietary research findings.']
  scrap_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason why this experimental lot was scrapped, if applicable. Examples: catastrophic process failure, contamination, equipment damage, or project cancellation. Null if lot was not scrapped.']
  split_condition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of split-lot experimental conditions where different wafers within the lot receive different process parameters, materials, or sequences to enable comparative analysis. Null if lot is not split.']
  split_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct experimental splits or conditions applied within this lot. Value of 1 indicates uniform processing; values greater than 1 indicate split-lot design.']
  substrate_type varchar [note: 'Base material type of the wafers in this lot: silicon (standard CMOS), Silicon-On-Insulator (SOI), Gallium Arsenide (GaAs) for RF applications, Gallium Nitride (GaN) for power devices, Silicon Carbide (SiC) for high-temperature applications, sapphire for LED/optoelectronics, glass for display technologies, or other experimental materials. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: silicon|soi|gaas|gan|sic|sapphire|glass|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_device_structure varchar [note: 'Description of the novel device architecture or structure being developed in this experimental lot. Examples: FinFET (Fin Field-Effect Transistor), GAA (Gate-All-Around), CFET (Complementary FET), MRAM (Magnetoresistive RAM), or other emerging device types.']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected or target yield percentage for this experimental lot based on research objectives. May be lower than production yield targets due to exploratory nature of the process.']
  technology_node_nm varchar [note: 'Target process technology node in nanometers representing the minimum feature size or generation. Examples include 7nm, 5nm, 3nm, 2nm for advanced nodes, or larger nodes for mature process research.']
  wafer_count varchar [note: 'Total number of wafers included in this experimental lot. Typically smaller than production lots due to research constraints and cost considerations.']
  wafer_diameter_mm varchar [note: 'Diameter of the wafers in millimeters. Common sizes include 200mm (8-inch) and 300mm (12-inch) for mainstream production, or 150mm (6-inch) for specialized research.']

  Note: 'Experimental wafer lot records created specifically for R&D process development, device architecture validation, and materials research. Distinct from production wafer lots — these are research-grade lots with non-standard process flows, split-lot experimental conditions, and exploratory process parameters. Captures lot ID, associated research project, wafer count, substrate type, experimental process flow ID, split conditions, target device structures, fab line assignment, lot priority, and experimental outcome status. Integrates with Camstar MES for wafer tracking.'
}

Table research.process_integration_run {
  process_integration_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the process integration experiment run. Primary key for tracking individual experimental runs through the complete lifecycle from flow definition to results analysis.']
  experimental_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to research.experimental_lot.experimental_lot_id — Process integration runs are executed on experimental lots. This is the core operational link between the experiment definition and the physical wafers being processed.']
  fabrication_process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the process flow definition that specifies the complete sequence of FEOL, MOL, and BEOL fabrication steps executed in this integration run.']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target technology node (e.g., 7nm, 5nm, 3nm) for which this process integration experiment is being developed.']
  fabrication_wafer_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the experimental wafer lot used for this process integration run. Links to the physical wafers processed through the integration flow.']
  pdk_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PDK that this integration run supports or contributes to. Links experimental results to design enablement.']
  primary_process_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the process integration engineer responsible for designing, executing, and analyzing this experimental run.']
  process_experimental_lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to research.experimental_lot.experimental_lot_id — Process integration runs are executed on experimental lots. This is the core traceability link between process experiments and physical wafers.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the process integration run results and conclusions by technical leadership or review board.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the process integration run results were formally approved.']
  beol_step_count varchar [note: 'Number of BEOL process steps in the integration flow, covering metal interconnect layers and passivation.']
  collaboration_partner varchar [note: 'External research institution, university, or industry partner collaborating on this process integration experiment, if applicable.']
  contact_resistance_ohm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Measured contact resistance in ohms. Critical for device performance and interconnect reliability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this process integration run record was first created in the system.']
  device_architecture_type varchar [note: 'Target device architecture being developed in this integration run. FinFET (Fin Field-Effect Transistor), GAA (Gate All Around), CFET (Complementary FET), Planar, Nanowire, or Nanosheet structures.']
  doe_design_type varchar [note: 'Statistical experimental design methodology applied to this integration run. Defines the split condition strategy for systematic parameter variation and analysis.']
  drive_current_ua decimal(12,3) [note: 'Measured on-state drive current in microamperes. Key performance metric for transistor switching speed and circuit performance.']
  electrical_test_structure_type varchar [note: 'Type of test structures fabricated for electrical characterization (e.g., ring oscillators, SRAM arrays, parametric test structures, scribe line monitors).']
  electrical_test_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when electrical testing was performed on the processed wafers.']
  experiment_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name assigned to this process integration experiment for identification and tracking purposes.']
  feol_step_count varchar [note: 'Number of FEOL process steps in the integration flow, covering transistor formation from substrate preparation through source/drain formation.']
  gate_oxide_thickness_nm decimal(8,3) [note: 'Measured or target gate dielectric thickness in nanometers. Critical parameter for device electrostatics and reliability.']
