  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: NRE billing milestones are generated per design project; linking enables project‑level cost tracking and profitability analysis.']
  nre_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice generated for this milestone.']
  primary_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — When an NRE milestone triggers billing, the resulting invoice must be linked back to the milestone for revenue recognition and project accounting.']
  primary_nre_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the milestone billing.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the IC design project associated with the milestone.']
  test_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: NRE Milestone Billing – NRE milestones for test program development are billed; linking to test_program enables tracking milestone costs against the program.']
  actual_date date [note: 'Date the milestone was actually billed.']
  billing_formula varchar [note: 'Expression or rule used to calculate the milestone amount (e.g., fixed fee, percentage of NRE total).']
  billing_trigger_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism that initiates the billing event.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework that governs the billing (e.g., export control, environmental).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the billing amounts.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment for the milestone must be received.']
  evidence_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting documentation (e.g., sign‑off report, test results).']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, discounts, or adjustments.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing milestone repeats on a schedule.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'External code or number used to reference the billing milestone in contracts and invoices.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the billing milestone (e.g., "RTL Freeze", "GDS Tapeout").']
  milestone_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the milestone within the project billing schedule.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after tax and any adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or remarks about the billing milestone.']
  nre_billing_milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the billing milestone.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment condition associated with the milestone.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Date the milestone is scheduled to be billed.']
  recurring_interval varchar [note: 'Time interval for recurring billing when is_recurring is true.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the gross amount.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction or rule applied.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the milestone record.']

  Note: 'Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) billing milestone and planned billing schedule records for semiconductor IC design, tapeout, IP licensing, and long-term supply agreement projects. Each record represents a contractually defined billing event — either milestone-based (RTL freeze, GDS tapeout, first silicon, qualification sign-off) or schedule-based (calendar-periodic, shipment-triggered, subscription). Captures milestone/schedule name, sequence, planned and actual dates, billing amount or formula, billing trigger type, invoice linkage, approval status, and completion evidence. SSOT for all planned, milestone-driven, and schedule-based billing events including NRE project billing plans, IP licensing periodic billing, recurring supply agreement billing cadences, and subscription billing schedules. Subsumes all billing_schedule concepts.'
}

Table invoice.adjustment_memo {
  adjustment_memo_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for adjustment_memo']
  adjustment_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Adjustment memos (credit/debit/correction) must reference the original invoice being adjusted. Required for AR reconciliation and audit trail.']
  audit_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to an immutable audit log entry capturing the full change history.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user who approved or rejected the memo.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Link to the general ledger entry generated for this adjustment.']
  primary_adjustment_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice that this adjustment memo references.']
  related_contract_customer_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract or agreement underlying the original invoice.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order associated with the original invoice, if any.']
  tertiary_adjustment_applied_to_invoice_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the open receivable invoice to which this adjustment was applied.']
  adjustment_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category such as price_adjustment, return, overbilling_correction, yield_concession.']
  adjustment_memo_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the adjustment memo.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'External business number assigned to the adjustment memo for tracking and reference.']
  adjustment_subtype varchar [note: 'More granular subtype within the adjustment category, if applicable.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the memo reduces (credit), increases (debit), or formally corrects an invoice.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the adjustment amount that has been applied to the target invoice.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the approval workflow for the adjustment memo.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the approval decision was recorded.']
  approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount approved for the adjustment before tax or other charges.']
  audit_user_created varchar [note: 'System user who originally created the adjustment memo record.']
  audit_user_updated varchar [note: 'System user who performed the most recent update.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes entered by the creator or approver.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment memo record was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the adjustment amounts.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the adjustment becomes financially effective.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to external systems (e.g., ERP, CRM) or partner identifiers.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'True if the adjustment memo was entered manually rather than generated by an automated process.']
  is_tax_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax is already included in the approved amount.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Organizational unit or role that issued the adjustment memo (e.g., Finance, Sales).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User who performed the most recent modification to the memo.']
  last_modified_reason varchar [note: 'Reason text explaining why the memo was last modified.']
  original_invoice_number varchar [note: 'Customer‑visible invoice number that is being adjusted.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the adjustment memo is posted to the general ledger.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the business reason for the adjustment.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the adjustment memo was issued.']
  remaining_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding balance on the original invoice after applying this adjustment.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the adjustment was settled.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment has been settled against receivables.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the adjustment (e.g., SAP, custom billing).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the adjustment, if applicable.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (percentage) used to calculate tax_amount.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net financial impact of the adjustment (approved amount plus/minus tax).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the adjustment memo.']

  Note: 'Adjustment memo records (credit, debit, and correction) issued to customers to modify outstanding invoice balances. Credit memos reduce balances for price adjustments, RMA returns, overbilling corrections, yield-related concessions, and quality claim settlements. Debit memos increase balances for underbilling corrections, retroactive price adjustments, and expedite fees. Corrections cover formal invoice amendments requiring accounting reversal and reissuance. Captures original invoice reference, adjustment direction (credit/debit/correction), reason code, approved amount, corrected line items, net financial impact, issuing authority, approval workflow status, replacement invoice linkage, and application status against open receivables. SSOT for all post-invoice billing adjustments — subsumes all debit_memo and invoice_correction concepts.'
}

Table invoice.payment_receipt {
  payment_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payment receipt record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who made the payment.']
  audit_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_event. Business justification: Payment receipt compliance checks are recorded as audit events; linking enables audit trail reporting.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Cash receipt must be posted to a journal entry for cash accounting and audit trail.']
  payment_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary invoice associated with the payment (if single).']
  primary_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Payment receipts must reference the invoices they clear. This is the core cash application relationship enabling AR aging accuracy and payment reconciliation.']
  processed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Payment receipt processing is performed by a treasury employee; audit logs require linking receipt to processing employee.']
  reversal_reference_payment_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payment receipt being reversed.']
  to_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Payments must be applied against invoices for AR clearing. This is the fundamental cash application link. After merge with payment_allocation, this becomes the primary clearing relationship.']
  allocated_amount_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of amounts allocated to invoices from this payment.']
  bank_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number provided by the bank for the transaction.']
  compliance_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of compliance checks (e.g., sanctions, AML) for the payment.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when compliance check was performed.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the receipt record.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code of the payment.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Discount taken on the payment, e.g., early payment discount.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied if payment currency differs from functional currency.']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Payment amount converted to the company\'s functional currency.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax and discount.']
  number_of_allocations varchar [note: 'Count of invoice allocations linked to this payment receipt.']
  on_account_payment_flag boolean [note: 'True if the payment is recorded as an on-account credit not yet allocated to specific invoices.']
  overpayment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the payment amount exceeds the total invoiced amount.']
  partial_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the payment only partially covers the invoiced amount.']
  payer_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number of the payer.']
  payer_bank_code varchar [note: 'Identifier (e.g., SWIFT/BIC) of the payer\'s bank.']
  payer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity making the payment.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment was processed.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date the payment was received.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to make the payment.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the payment.']
  payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the payment was recorded in the system.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the payment is domestic or international.']
  receipt_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the receipt record was created in the system.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'External receipt number assigned by the finance system.']
  receipt_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the receipt record.']
  remittance_advice varchar [note: 'Textual remittance information supplied by the payer.']
  residual_open_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unallocated amount after allocations.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this receipt represents a reversal of a previously posted payment.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the payment, if applicable.']
  to_ar_invoice varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Payment receipts must link to the invoices they clear. Required for cash application, AR aging, and bank reconciliation.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount received before any deductions.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last updated the receipt record.']

  Note: 'Records of customer payments received, their line-level allocation against outstanding invoices, and cash application status. Captures payment header (date, method — wire/ACH/check/LC, amount, currency, bank reference, remittance advice) and line-level allocation details (allocated amount per invoice, allocation date, clearing document reference, partial payment flag, discount taken, residual open amount). Supports partial payments, overpayments, multi-invoice payment runs, on-account payments, payment reversals, and complex allocation scenarios. Enables accurate AR aging, cash application tracking, and bank reconciliation. SSOT for all customer payment receipts, their invoice clearing allocations, and cash application — subsumes all payment_allocation concepts.'
}

Table invoice.royalty_billing {
  royalty_billing_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the royalty billing record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or partner receiving the royalty bill.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Royalty expense is charged to a cost center for internal expense tracking and budgeting.']
  design_ip_core_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the licensed IP core (e.g., processor, PHY) for which royalties are calculated.']
  pricing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master licensing agreement governing the royalty terms.']
  primary_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Royalty billing records generate invoices. The linkage enables tracking from IP license royalty calculation to the customer-facing billing document.']
  royalty_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated financial invoice record.']
  to_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Royalty billing calculations must link to the generated invoice for payment tracking and audit trail.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of discounts, credits, or penalties applied to the gross royalty.']
  audit_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed audit report document.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Status of the royalty audit performed on the reported figures.']
  billing_date date [note: 'Date the royalty invoice was issued to the licensee.']
  billing_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the royalty invoice, e.g., RB-20230101.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the royalty invoice.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the royalty billing record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the royalty amounts.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'True if the royalty invoice is under dispute.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the reason for the royalty dispute.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the royalty payment must be received.']
  exchange_rate_to_usd decimal(12,6) [note: 'Conversion rate from the invoice currency to US dollars for reporting.']
  is_royalty_exempt boolean [note: 'True if the licensee is exempt from royalty for this period (e.g., promotional waiver).']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent royalty audit.']
  minimum_commitment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual minimum royalty amount the licensee must pay for the period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the royalty billing.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Means by which the royalty payment was made.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date the royalty payment was actually received.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or code provided by the payer for the royalty payment.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the royalty billing has been reconciled with payments.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the licensee\'s primary operating region.']
  royalty_amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total royalty calculated before any discounts, taxes, or adjustments.']
  royalty_amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final royalty amount after adjustments, before tax.']
  royalty_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to derive the royalty amount.']
  royalty_period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date of the royalty reporting period.']
  royalty_period_start date [note: 'First calendar date of the royalty reporting period.']
  royalty_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate applied to calculate royalty – either a per‑unit amount or a percentage (expressed as a decimal).']
  royalty_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the royalty is calculated per shipped unit or as a percentage of revenue.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the royalty invoice, if applicable.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction or rule applied.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Tier classification that determines the royalty rate based on volume or other criteria.']
  to_ar_invoice varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Royalty billing records must link to the invoice issued for the royalty period. Required for IP licensing revenue tracking and licensee reconciliation.']
  unit_shipment_volume varchar [note: 'Number of licensed units (e.g., chips, dies) reported by the licensee for the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the royalty billing record.']

  Note: 'IP licensing royalty billing records for semiconductor IP cores (processor IP, PHY IP, memory controllers, SerDes, etc.) licensed to fabless customers, design houses, and foundry partners. Captures royalty period, licensed IP reference, unit shipment volume reported by licensee, royalty rate tier, calculated royalty amount, minimum royalty commitment tracking, audit status, licensee self-report reconciliation, and invoice linkage. Supports volume-tiered royalty structures, per-unit and percentage-of-revenue models, and royalty audit dispute resolution.'
}

Table invoice.payment_term {
  payment_term_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payment term record.']
  applicable_to_customer_type varchar [note: 'Customer segment(s) to which this payment term applies.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the payment term.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment term was approved for use.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) that this payment term must satisfy.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the payment term record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment term record was first created.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure allowed under this payment term.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code applicable to the term.']
  early_payment_discount_days varchar [note: 'Number of days within which the early payment discount applies.']
  early_payment_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered for early payment.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the payment term becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the payment term expires; null for open‑ended terms.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Additional days after due date before penalties commence.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term is the default for new customers.']
  late_payment_penalty_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after due date when the penalty starts to accrue.']
  late_payment_penalty_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage penalty applied after the due date.']
  legal_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal document governing the payment term.']
  letter_of_credit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a letter of credit is mandatory for this term.']
  max_discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cap on the monetary value of early payment discounts.']
  max_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cap on the monetary value of late payment penalties.']
  net_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after invoice date when payment is due (e.g., 30 for Net 30).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary free‑text notes or remarks.']
  payment_method_allowed varchar [note: 'Payment instruments permitted for this term.']
  payment_term_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the payment term and its business rationale.']
  payment_term_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment term.']
  tax_inclusive boolean [note: 'Specifies whether the amounts are tax‑inclusive.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the payment term (e.g., NET30, 2/10NET30).']
  term_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment term.']
  term_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment term indicating its structure (net, discount, cash, etc.).']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the payment term record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment term record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the payment term for change management.']

  Note: 'Master reference for customer payment terms governing invoice due dates, early payment discount structures, and late payment penalty rules. Defines net payment days (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, Net 90), cash discount percentages and qualifying windows (e.g., 2/10 Net 30), letter of credit requirements, and currency-specific term variants. Assigned at customer account level with order-level override capability.'
}

Table invoice.customer_credit_limit {
  customer_credit_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated surrogate key uniquely identifying each credit limit record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer to whom the credit limit applies.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst responsible for managing this credit limit.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the customer relationship.']
  credit_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the credit limit amount.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s credit is currently on hold.']
  credit_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why a credit hold has been applied.']
  credit_insurance_coverage varchar [note: 'Level of insurance protection covering the credit exposure.']
  credit_insurance_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the insurer providing credit insurance, if any.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding accounts‑receivable exposure permitted for the customer, expressed in credit_currency.']
  credit_limit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit limit record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit_last_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the credit limit record.']
  credit_limit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the credit limit agreement, used in external communications and reporting.']
  credit_limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit limit record.']
  credit_limit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credit limit (e.g., standard, NRE milestone, royalty, project‑based).']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating from an external agency (e.g., Moody\'s, S&P).']
  credit_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal credit risk review.']
  credit_risk_classification varchar [note: 'Internal risk grade assigned to the customer based on financial health and payment history.']
  credit_utilization_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current total of outstanding receivables against the credit limit.']
  credit_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Utilization expressed as a percentage of the total credit limit.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the credit limit becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the credit limit expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended limits.']
  legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the legal entity within the corporate structure that owns the credit agreement.']
  limit_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for the most recent credit limit change.']
  limit_adjustment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the most recent credit limit adjustment was applied.']
  max_overdue_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days any invoice has been overdue for this customer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the credit limit.']
  overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of invoices currently past due.']
  payment_history_score varchar [note: 'Aggregated score reflecting the customer\'s historical payment performance.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country/region code where the customer is primarily located.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originally created the record (e.g., SAP S/4HANA FI).']

  Note: 'Credit limit master records for each customer account defining the maximum outstanding AR exposure permitted. Captures approved credit limit amount, credit currency, credit risk classification, credit review date, credit hold status, credit insurance coverage details, and credit analyst assignment. Supports dynamic credit limit adjustments based on payment history and financial health reviews. SSOT for credit management decisions.'
}

Table invoice.credit_hold {
  credit_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the credit hold record.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice that triggered the credit hold due to non‑payment.']
  primary_credit_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer to whom the credit hold applies.']
  primary_credit_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the credit analyst who authorized the hold.']
  primary_customer_credit_limit_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.customer_credit_limit.customer_credit_limit_id — Credit holds are triggered by credit limit breaches. The hold must reference the credit limit record that was exceeded for analyst review and release decisions.']
  block_level varchar [note: 'Scope of the hold: order creation, delivery release, or billing.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the analyst regarding the hold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit hold record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the credit hold.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount approved for the customer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the hold amount.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Optional date when the hold automatically expires if not released.']
  hold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount that is blocked due to the credit hold.']
  hold_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the hold based on its origin or purpose.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible alphanumeric identifier assigned to the credit hold event.']
  hold_placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the credit hold was placed.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason why the credit hold was placed (e.g., overdue invoice, exceeded limit).']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer was notified about the credit hold.']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has been notified of the hold.']
  overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding amount of the overdue invoice.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms associated with the customer account.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credit hold was lifted.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score used to assess the customer\'s creditworthiness at hold time.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the credit hold record.']

  Note: 'Records of credit hold events placed on customer accounts due to overdue invoices, exceeded credit limits, or financial risk triggers. Captures hold placement date, hold reason, blocking level (order block, delivery block, billing block), responsible credit analyst, customer notification status, and hold release date with authorization. Directly impacts order fulfillment and shipment release workflows.'
}

Table invoice.dispute {
  dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each invoice dispute record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who raised the dispute.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the original invoice linked to the dispute.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal owner handling the dispute.']
  actual_settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement amount was actually paid.']
  attached_documents_flag boolean [note: 'True if supporting documents are attached to the dispute record.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel used to submit the dispute.']
  close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was closed in the system.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to prevent recurrence of the issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the disputed amount.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the dispute for tracking and communication.']
  dispute_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the dispute entry within the organization.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the dispute.']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the dispute.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount being contested in the dispute.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating how many times the dispute has been escalated.']
  expected_settlement_date date [note: 'Target date for completing the settlement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional details, comments, or observations.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispute was initially recorded.']
  original_invoice_number varchar [note: 'External invoice number from the source billing system.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the dispute.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of why the dispute was raised.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the dispute after processing.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispute was resolved.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the underlying cause identified during investigation.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final monetary amount agreed upon to resolve the dispute.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for the settlement amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the dispute record.']

  Note: 'Customer invoice dispute records tracking formal challenges raised against billed amounts, pricing, quantities, or delivery conditions. Captures dispute type (pricing error, quantity discrepancy, quality claim, duplicate billing, delivery shortfall), disputed amount, dispute open date, responsible owner, resolution status, and final settlement amount. Supports dispute workflow management and root cause tracking for billing quality improvement.'
}

Table invoice.revenue_recognition_event {
  revenue_recognition_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the revenue recognition event record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer associated with the underlying billing transaction.']
  accounting_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Revenue recognition events are reviewed/approved by an accounting employee; required for SOX audit trails.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Revenue recognition events are triggered by billing events (invoices). The link to the source invoice is mandatory for ASC 606 compliance and audit.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue recognition events post amounts to a GL account, required for ASC 606 compliance.']
  performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the performance obligation to which this revenue recognition event is linked.']
  recognition_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the schedule that defines how revenue is spread over time.']
  revenue_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice that generated this revenue recognition event.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (e.g., FY2023Q2) to which the recognized revenue is booked.']
  cost_of_goods_sold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Direct cost attributable to the recognized revenue for this event.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  deferred_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the invoice amount that remains deferred after this recognition event.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the revenue recognition event (e.g., RRE-2023-000123).']
  is_reversed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the revenue recognition event has been reversed (true) or not (false).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or explanations about the revenue recognition event.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period over which revenue is recognized for this event.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period over which revenue is recognized for this event.']
  profit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross profit derived from the recognized revenue (revenue minus COGS).']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recognize revenue: point‑in‑time or over‑time.']
  recognition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue was recognized according to the schedule.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue recognition event record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the revenue recognition event record.']
  revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of revenue recognized in this event.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the revenue (e.g., product sale, service, IP licensing, royalty, maintenance).']
  revenue_recognition_event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the revenue recognition event.']
  revenue_rev_rec_event_to_ar_invoice varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Revenue recognition events must reference the billing document (invoice) that triggered recognition. Required for ASC 606/IFRS 15 compliance audit.']
  source_record_key varchar [note: 'Unique key of the source record in the originating system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system that created the revenue recognition event record.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the recognized revenue.']

  Note: 'Revenue recognition event records capturing when and how revenue from customer invoices is recognized under applicable accounting standards (ASC 606 / IFRS 15). Each record links a billing event (invoice, NRE milestone, royalty) to its revenue recognition schedule, performance obligation, recognition method (point-in-time vs. over-time), recognized amount, deferred revenue amount, and accounting period. SSOT for revenue recognition compliance.'
}

Table invoice.pricing_agreement {
  pricing_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each pricing agreement.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer that the pricing agreement applies to.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the semiconductor product or product line covered by the agreement.']
  accrual_method varchar [note: 'Financial treatment of rebates – either cash settlement or accrual accounting.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the pricing agreement, used in contracts and invoicing.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the pricing agreement, e.g., forward supply, rebate program, spot sale, design‑win, or volume discount.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Role or group that granted final approval for the agreement.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the agreement received formal approval.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Text describing any regulatory, export‑control, or industry‑specific compliance obligations attached to the agreement.']
  contract_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal employee responsible for the agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the pricing agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency used for pricing and rebate amounts.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied on top of the base price before rebate calculations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the pricing agreement terminates; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the pricing agreement becomes binding.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'True if the agreement terms are marked confidential for internal handling.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'True if the pricing agreement provides exclusive supply rights to the customer.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO‑3 country code indicating the legal jurisdiction for the agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|CHN|JPN|KOR|DEU|GBR|FRA|TWN — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the last rebate payment was settled.']
  maximum_commitment_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound on total spend covered by the agreement.']
  minimum_commitment_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lowest total spend the customer must achieve to keep the agreement active.']
  next_settlement_date date [note: 'Planned date for the upcoming rebate settlement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary remarks, comments, or special conditions.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Credit terms governing invoice payment, e.g., Net 30 days.']
  price_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary amount defining the base price per unit or per metric as stipulated in the agreement.']
  price_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Metric that the base price applies to, such as per die, per watt, or per unit.']
  pricing_agreement_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description providing context and purpose of the agreement.']
  pricing_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the agreement indicating whether it is in draft, active, suspended, terminated, or expired.']
  rebate_accrued_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total rebate amount that has been accrued but not yet paid.']
  rebate_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of rebate applied to qualifying purchases under the agreement.']
  rebate_settled_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative amount of rebate that has been paid to the customer.']
  rebate_type varchar [note: 'Category of rebate, such as volume‑based, growth‑based, design‑win, or market development fund.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often accrued rebates are settled with the customer.']
  tax_included boolean [note: 'True if the base price already incorporates applicable taxes.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statutory tax rate applied to the pricing agreement, expressed as a percent.']
  tier_end_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound quantity for a specific price tier.']
  tier_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary price applied to quantities falling within the tier range.']
  tier_start_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound quantity for a specific price tier within the agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the agreement record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version used to track amendments to the agreement.']
  volume_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity level at which the agreement\'s pricing or rebate conditions change.']
  volume_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the volume threshold, e.g., dies, units, or watt.']

  Note: 'Customer-specific pricing and rebate agreement master records defining negotiated commercial terms for semiconductor products and services. Covers forward pricing (long-term supply agreements, design-win pricing, distributor pricing, spot pricing) and retrospective rebate programs (volume rebates, growth rebates, design-win rebates, market development funds). Captures agreement type, effective date range, product scope, volume commitment thresholds, price break tiers, rebate rate schedules, accrual basis, settlement frequency, minimum thresholds, payment method, and approval authority. Tracks accrued rebate liability and settlement status for retrospective programs. SSOT for all customer pricing validation, rebate accrual, and rebate settlement — subsumes all rebate_agreement concepts.'
}

Table invoice.dunning_notice {
  dunning_notice_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the dunning notice record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer to whom the overdue invoice belongs.']
  collection_agent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Dunning notices are issued by a collection agent employee; needed for collection performance metrics and compliance reporting.']
  dunning_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the overdue invoice that triggered this dunning notice.']
  primary_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Dunning notices reference specific overdue invoices. The collections process requires traceability from notice to outstanding billing documents.']
  to_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Dunning notices reference the overdue invoices driving the collection action. Required for collections workflow and customer communication.']
  collection_agency_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external collection agency handling the case, if any.']
  collection_agency_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the collection agency contact.']
  collection_agency_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the collection agency contact.']
  contact_address varchar [note: 'Mailing address used when the delivery method is postal.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the customer contact point for this notice.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the customer contact point for this notice.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dunning notice record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'True if the customer\'s credit is suspended because of overdue balances.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Number of days the invoice has been past its due date at the time of the notice.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Channel used to deliver the dunning notice to the customer.']
  dunning_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee applied for issuing the dunning notice, per collection policy.']
  dunning_fee_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a dunning fee has been applied to this notice.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Escalation stage of the notice, indicating how far the collection process has progressed.']
  dunning_notice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dunning notice.']
  escalation_threshold_exceeded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the overdue amount or days overdue have crossed a predefined escalation threshold.']
  interest_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Interest accrued on the overdue amount as of the notice date.']
  interest_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annualized interest rate applied to the overdue amount, expressed as a percentage.']
  legal_hold_flag boolean [note: 'True if the account is placed on legal hold due to collection actions.']
  next_action_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next collection activity (e.g., phone call, legal notice).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for internal comments or special instructions related to the notice.']
  notice_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dunning notice was generated and sent to the customer.']
  notice_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the dunning notice, used for tracking and communication with the customer.']
  original_due_date date [note: 'The payment due date originally agreed for the invoice.']
  overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original invoice amount that remains unpaid at the time of the notice.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment term code associated with the invoice (e.g., NET30).']
  response_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer provided a response to the dunning notice.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has responded to the notice.']
  total_due decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of overdue principal, dunning charge, and interest, representing the total amount the customer must pay.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dunning notice record.']

  Note: 'Dunning notice records generated for overdue customer invoices as part of the structured collections process. Captures dunning level (first reminder, second reminder, final notice, legal referral, collection agency handoff), dunning date, overdue invoice references, total overdue amount, dunning charges and late payment interest applied, customer contact details, delivery method (email, postal, EDI), and customer response status. Supports escalating collections workflow management and integrates with credit hold triggers when dunning thresholds are breached.'
}

Table invoice.tax_determination {
  tax_determination_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each tax determination record.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Tax liability lines are posted to specific GL accounts for tax reporting and reconciliation.']
  ic_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or service that the tax is applied to.']
  primary_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Tax determination records are calculated per invoice. The FK links tax computation results to the billing document for tax reporting and audit.']
  tax_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice to which this tax determination belongs.']
  tax_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tax determination records must be signed off by a tax officer employee for regulatory compliance.']
  to_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Tax determination records must link to the invoice they apply to. Tax is calculated per invoice and must be traceable for tax authority audits.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the tax line within the invoice.']
  reverse_charge_mechanism varchar [note: 'Description of the reverse charge method used.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated tax amount for the line.']
  tax_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount on which the tax rate is applied.']
  tax_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to compute the tax amount.']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Classification of tax (standard, reduced, zero).']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to identify the specific tax rule applied.']
  tax_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the tax credit, if applicable.']
  tax_credit_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the line qualifies for a tax credit.']
  tax_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the tax amount.']
  tax_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of supporting tax document (e.g., certificate, exemption letter).']
  tax_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of supporting tax document.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction line is tax‑exempt.']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or code for tax exemption, if applicable.']
  tax_is_reverse_charge boolean [note: 'True if reverse charge mechanism applies.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the tax jurisdiction (e.g., ISO‑3166‑2 country‑state code).']
  tax_jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the tax jurisdiction.']
  tax_jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Level of the jurisdiction that the tax applies to.']
  tax_liability_party varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for remitting the tax.']
  tax_line_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the tax line.']
  tax_line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the tax line.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  tax_rate_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the tax rate is applicable.']
  tax_rate_expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the tax rate is no longer valid.']
  tax_record_created timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the tax determination record was created.']
  tax_record_updated timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the tax record.']
  tax_region_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax region within the jurisdiction.']
  tax_reporting_period date [note: 'Fiscal period for which the tax is reported.']
  tax_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP or tax engine that generated the record.']
  tax_source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the tax record in the source system.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Category of tax applied to the transaction line.']
  tax_withholding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether withholding tax applies to this line.']
  taxable_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of goods or services subject to tax.']
  to_ar_invoice varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Tax determination records must link to the invoice they apply to. Required for tax reporting, audit, and VAT/GST reconciliation.']
  withholding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated withholding tax amount.']
  withholding_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Withholding tax rate expressed as a percentage.']

  Note: 'Tax determination records for each invoice capturing applicable tax codes, jurisdictions, rates, and calculated tax amounts for semiconductor product sales across global markets. Covers VAT, GST, sales/use tax, withholding tax, and customs duty components. Captures determination inputs (ship-from/ship-to jurisdiction, product tax classification, customer tax status, free trade zone applicability, bonded warehouse status) and resulting tax line amounts. Supports withholding tax certificate tracking for Asian semiconductor markets (Taiwan, Korea, China, India) where WHT documentation is required for payment release.'
}

Table invoice.write_off {
  write_off_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the write‑off record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer whose invoice is being written off.']
  accounting_entry_id varchar [note: 'Link to the corresponding general‑ledger entry generated for the write‑off.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Write‑off transactions must be recorded against a GL account for financial statements compliance.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department owning the write‑off transaction.']
  primary_write_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or manager who approved the write‑off.']
  quaternary_write_reversal_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User who performed the reversal of the write‑off.']
  tertiary_write_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User who performed the most recent update to the write‑off record.']
  to_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Write-offs must reference the specific invoices being written off for accounting accuracy and potential recovery tracking.']
  write_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice that is the subject of the write‑off.']
  amount_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of tax, fees, or discounts applied to the write‑off.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original invoice amount before any adjustments.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount written off after adjustments; the amount removed from accounts receivable.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the write‑off received formal approval.']
  bad_debt_expense_account varchar [note: 'General Ledger account used to record the bad‑debt expense.']
  batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch in which this write‑off was loaded.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the write‑off is subject to regulatory compliance review.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center responsible for the write‑off expense.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the write‑off record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the write‑off amount.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Accounting period start date for the write‑off entry.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used by an external ERP or legacy system to reference this write‑off.']
  financial_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (year and quarter) to which the write‑off is allocated.']
  is_reversed boolean [note: 'True if the write‑off has been reversed after posting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or justification details entered by the approver.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Standardized reason why the invoice balance is being written off.']
  recovery_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of collection attempts made after the write‑off was posted.']
  recovery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the write‑off will be monitored for possible future recovery.']
  recovery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any post‑write‑off recovery effort.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code of the geographic region associated with the write‑off.']
  regulatory_reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used for statutory reporting (e.g., SEC, SOX, CHIPS Act).']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date on which the write‑off reversal was recorded.']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the write‑off within its processing batch.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating source system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Oracle ERP).']
  tax_effect_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the write‑off amount, if applicable.']
  to_ar_invoice varchar [note: 'FK to invoice.ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id — Write-off records must reference the specific invoices being written off. Required for bad debt accounting and recovery tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the write‑off record.']
  write_off_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business domain that triggered the write‑off.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'Date on which the write‑off was executed in the accounting system.']
  write_off_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the write‑off was entered manually or generated by an automated rule.']
  write_off_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the write‑off transaction for tracking and audit.']
  write_off_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the write‑off record.']
  write_off_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the write‑off based on its financial nature.']

  Note: 'Bad debt write-off records for uncollectable customer invoice balances that have been approved for removal from accounts receivable. Captures write-off date, written-off invoice references, write-off amount, write-off reason (customer bankruptcy, unresolvable dispute, statute of limitations), approval authority, bad debt expense account, and recovery tracking flag for subsequent collections efforts.'
}

Table invoice.performance_obligation {
  performance_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for performance_obligation']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer to whom the performance obligation applies.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent contract that contains this performance obligation.']
  parent_performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on performance_obligation (parent_performance_obligation_id)']
  actual_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity actually delivered or consumed against the target.']
  billing_end_date date [note: 'Date of the final billing occurrence; null if billing continues until obligation end.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'How often billing events are generated for the obligation.']
  billing_start_date date [note: 'Date of the first billing occurrence for the obligation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the performance obligation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose and scope of the performance obligation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the performance obligation terminates or expires; null for open‑ended obligations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the performance obligation becomes binding.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for target and actual quantities.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or comments entered by users regarding the performance obligation.']
  obligation_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the performance obligation, used in contracts and invoicing.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the performance obligation defining the nature of the deliverable.']
  performance_metric varchar [note: 'Metric used to measure fulfillment of the obligation (e.g., units delivered, usage hours).']
  recognition_end_date date [note: 'Date when revenue recognition ends for the obligation.']
  recognition_start_date date [note: 'Date when revenue recognition begins for the obligation.']
  recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative revenue amount that has been recognized to date for this obligation.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recognize revenue for the obligation.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the performance obligation.']
  target_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantitative target that must be achieved to satisfy the obligation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the obligation\'s monetary amounts.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the tax regime applicable to the obligation.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual monetary amount to be recognized for the obligation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the performance obligation record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for performance_obligation. Referenced by performance_obligation_id.'
}

Table invoice.recognition_schedule {
  recognition_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for recognition_schedule']
  revised_recognition_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on recognition_schedule (revised_recognition_schedule_id)']
  amount_per_period decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount allocated to each recognition period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the schedule record was initially inserted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency identifier for monetary amounts.']
  description varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining the business purpose and rules of the schedule.']
  effective_from date [note: 'First date on which the schedule may be applied to revenue.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Last date on which the schedule is valid; null if open‑ended.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Interval at which revenue is recognized (e.g., monthly).']
  is_prorated boolean [note: 'True if the schedule allows prorated revenue for partial periods.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which the schedule was reviewed for compliance or accuracy.']
  period_count varchar [note: 'Total count of recognition periods defined by the schedule.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code that identifies the schedule across systems.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the revenue recognition schedule.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate revenue over time (e.g., straight‑line, milestone, percentage‑of‑completion, custom).']
  status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule is currently in use, pending, or retired.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate revenue amount that the schedule will recognize over its lifetime.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the last modification to the schedule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version identifier used for change management.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for recognition_schedule. Referenced by recognition_schedule_id.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cost center.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who owns the cost center responsibility.']
  parent_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent cost center in the hierarchy.']
  primary_cost_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for the cost center.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate costs (e.g., headcount, fab utilization, revenue, custom).']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes capturing audit trail information for the cost center.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned budget amount allocated to the cost center for the fiscal year.']
  budget_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the budget amount applies.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Controlling area to which the cost center belongs for internal reporting.']
  cost_center_category varchar [note: 'Category classification such as expense, investment, or overhead.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the cost center in financial postings.']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the cost center.']
  cost_center_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of cost centers for reporting (e.g., fab group, R&D group).']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the cost center.']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center (e.g., fab, R&D, corporate, support).']
  cost_element varchar [note: 'Cost element code associated with the cost center for expense categorization.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the cost center operates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost center record was created in the source system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for budgeting and reporting.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code of the department to which the cost center belongs.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center in external systems (e.g., legacy ERP).']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth level of the cost center within the organizational hierarchy.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'True if the cost center is currently active; false otherwise.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost center contains confidential financial information.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center was last reviewed for relevance and accuracy.']
  legal_entity varchar [note: 'Legal entity to which the cost center belongs.']
  natural_account varchar [note: 'General ledger natural account linked to the cost center.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant associated with the cost center.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Associated profit center code for revenue attribution.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter region identifier (e.g., APJ, EMEA, AMER).']
  reporting_line varchar [note: 'Organizational reporting line identifier for the cost center.']
  responsibility_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the responsibility center linked to the cost center.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of cost center reviews.']
  subdepartment_code varchar [note: 'Code of the sub‑department within the department.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applicable to the cost center for tax reporting.']
  to_company_code varchar [note: 'FK to finance.company_code.company_code_id — Cost centers belong to a specific company code/controlling area. Required for organizational hierarchy and reporting boundaries.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost center record.']
  validity_flag boolean [note: 'True if the cost center is within its validity period; false otherwise.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date when the cost center becomes effective.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date when the cost center ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']

  Note: 'Authoritative master record for all SAP CO cost centers used in semiconductor operations. Captures fab cost centers (FEOL, BEOL, CMP, lithography), R&D cost centers, engineering support centers, and corporate overhead pools. Includes cost center hierarchy, responsible manager, controlling area, currency, validity periods, and budget allocation type. SSOT for cost center identity used across all financial postings.'
}

Table finance.gl_account {
  gl_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the GL account record.']
  account_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification such as operating, non‑operating, or statutory.']
  account_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used in the chart of accounts; often alphanumeric (e.g., 4000, 5-200).']
  account_group varchar [note: 'Group identifier used for reporting aggregation (e.g., 4‑Revenue, 5‑COGS).']
  account_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the account within the chart of accounts (e.g., 1‑5).']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose of the account.']
  account_subgroup varchar [note: 'Sub‑group within an account group for finer granularity.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the account for financial statement mapping.']
  amortization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is subject to amortization (e.g., intangible assets).']
  closing_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance of the account at the end of the fiscal year.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the account is charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the GL account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the functional currency for the account.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate depreciation for asset‑type accounts.']
  depreciation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual depreciation rate expressed as a percentage.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the account in external systems (e.g., legacy ERP code).']
  financial_statement varchar [note: 'Financial statement where the account is reported.']
  gl_account_description varchar [note: 'Extended textual description of the account purpose and usage.']
  gl_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the account record.']
  is_budget_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is subject to budgetary controls.']
  is_consolidation_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account participates in legal entity consolidation.']
  is_reconciliation_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is used for automatic reconciliation of sub‑ledger postings.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance of the account at the start of the fiscal year.']
  posting_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'True if posting to the account is restricted by policy.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center associated with the account for profitability analysis.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for consolidated financial reporting.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Business segment identifier (e.g., product line, geography) used for reporting.']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Tax treatment applicable to the account.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the account, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 7.25).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the GL account record.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date when the account becomes effective.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date when the account is retired or becomes inactive; null if open‑ended.']

  Note: 'General ledger chart of accounts master for the semiconductor enterprise. Covers P&L accounts (wafer cost, NRE revenue, R&D expense, depreciation), balance sheet accounts (fab equipment assets, WIP inventory, receivables), and statistical accounts. Includes account type, financial statement version assignment, tax category, reconciliation account flag, and SOX control classification. Sourced from SAP FI chart of accounts.'
}

Table finance.profit_center {
  profit_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the profit center.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for the profit center.']
  parent_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent profit center in the hierarchy.']
  actual_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual expenditures recorded against the profit center.']
  allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of corporate overhead allocated to this profit center.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned budget allocated to the profit center for the fiscal period.']
  budget_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the budget amount, e.g., USD.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing why the profit center record was changed.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Controlling area code to which the profit center belongs in SAP CO.']
  cost_allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate costs to the profit center.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Associated cost center code for internal cost allocation.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the profit center record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the profit center record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the profit center was retired or closed (nullable).']
  fab_location varchar [note: 'Fabrication facility location associated with the profit center.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Regional market or sales region covered by the profit center.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth level of the profit center within the organizational hierarchy.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the profit center is included in corporate consolidation.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent financial or operational review.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Product family or line that the profit center supports.']
  profit_center_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category used for reporting and analysis.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Business code used in SAP and reporting to identify the profit center.']
  profit_center_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context and business purpose.']
  profit_center_group varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of profit centers for consolidated reporting.']
  profit_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the profit center, e.g., "Advanced Logic ASICs".']
  profit_center_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the profit center.']
  profit_center_type varchar [note: 'Category that defines the nature of the profit center.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the profit center reports financial results.']
  responsibility_center varchar [note: 'Internal responsibility center code for cost allocation.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of scheduled reviews for the profit center.']
  sox_compliant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating SOX compliance for financial reporting.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the profit center became effective.']
  subgroup varchar [note: 'Secondary grouping within a profit center group.']
  technology_node varchar [note: 'Process technology node (e.g., 5nm, 7nm) linked to the profit center.']
  to_company_code varchar [note: 'FK to finance.company_code.company_code_id — Profit centers are assigned to company codes for legal entity-level profitability reporting.']
  transfer_pricing_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the profit center participates in internal transfer pricing.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last updated the profit center record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the profit center record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between budgeted and actual spend (budget - actual).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change tracking.']

  Note: 'SAP EC-PCA profit center master representing autonomous business segments for internal profitability reporting. Covers product lines (SoC, ASIC, FPGA, discrete), technology nodes (5nm, 7nm, 28nm), and geographic segments. Includes profit center hierarchy, responsible manager, controlling area assignment, and transfer pricing indicator. Enables margin analysis by product family and technology generation.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the journal entry.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer involved in the transaction.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entries are posted against cost center, profit center, internal order and WBS element; FK replaces ambiguous code fields.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee (e.g., payroll or expense entry).']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Export‑controlled sales require each journal entry to reference the export license authorizing the shipment for compliance reporting.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Link journal entry to internal order for cost allocation and tracking.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Journal entries need a reliable link to profit center for reporting.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project linked to the entry.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: Revenue journal entries must reference the originating sales order for audit trails and financial reporting.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor involved in the transaction.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Journal entries should reference the WBS element they belong to.']
  amount_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the entry converted to the corporate group currency.']
  amount_local decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the entry in the document\'s currency.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'Fixed‑asset identifier for depreciation or acquisition postings.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of profit centers for reporting.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Organizational unit identifier for legal entity reporting.']
  consolidation_group varchar [note: 'Group identifier for consolidation adjustments.']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the consolidation entry.']
