  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the journal entry record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the document.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the line is a debit or credit.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the journal entry document.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the financial document (e.g., vendor invoice, intercompany).']
  exchange_rate decimal(15,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert document currency to group currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Two‑digit period within the fiscal year (01‑12).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the entry belongs.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Functional area code for internal reporting.']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the entry is part of an intercompany transaction.']
  journal_entry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the journal entry purpose.']
  ledger_account varchar [note: 'General Ledger account number associated with the entry.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax and adjustments.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the accounting period for the entry.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'SAP posting key that determines debit/credit logic.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the journal entry.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the entry was posted to the ledger.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the original entry being referenced.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date on which the reversal was posted.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the entry reverses a previous posting.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Market or product segment for analytical reporting.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the entry.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code determining tax calculation rules.']
  to_company_code varchar [note: 'FK to finance.company_code.company_code_id — Every journal entry is posted within a specific legal entity (company code). Required for legal entity reporting and consolidation.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the journal entry record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the journal entry record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the journal entry record.']

  Note: 'Core SAP FI general ledger journal entry capturing all financial postings across the semiconductor enterprise including consolidation adjustments. Document types include vendor invoice, customer payment, asset depreciation, accrual, intercompany, and consolidation entries (intercompany elimination, minority interest adjustment, currency translation adjustment, goodwill amortization). Captures posting date, fiscal period, company code, currency, exchange rate, consolidation unit/group (for group entries), consolidation method, and SOX-relevant reversal indicator. Covers fab cost postings, NRE revenue recognition, R&D capitalization entries, period-end accruals, and group-level consolidation adjustments for SOX-compliant financial reporting under US GAAP or IFRS. Primary transactional record and audit trail for all financial activity.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry_line {
  journal_entry_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the journal entry line record.']
  booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.booking. Business justification: Revenue recognition report requires each journal entry line to map to its originating sales booking for reconciliation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center.cost_center_id — Cost center assignment on journal entry lines is required for management accounting, cost allocation, and P&L reporting by organizational unit.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.gl_account.gl_account_id — Every journal entry line posts to a GL account. This is the core of double-entry bookkeeping and is required for trial balance, financial statements, and all reporting.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Line‑level journal entries need the order line ID to allocate revenue and cost precisely per SKU.']
  primary_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent journal entry header to which this line belongs.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.profit_center.profit_center_id — Profit center assignment enables profitability analysis by business segment, technology node, and product line - core semiconductor finance requirement.']
  to_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.journal_entry.journal_entry_id — Fundamental header-line relationship. Every journal entry line must reference its parent journal entry document. This is the most critical FK in the entire domain - without it, line items are orphaned']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.wbs_element']
  amount_document_currency decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the line expressed in the document\'s currency.']
  amount_local_currency decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the line converted to the company\'s local currency.']
  assignment_field varchar [note: 'User‑defined field for additional cost allocation or tagging.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the document currency.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the line is a debit or credit.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the line amount becomes effective for reporting.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Functional area classification for reporting (e.g., R&D, Manufacturing).']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the line item.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the journal entry.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the line.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the line is posted to the ledger.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity associated with the line (e.g., number of units, hours).']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this line reverses a previous entry.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system that generated the journal entry line.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code determining tax treatment for the line.']
  to_journal_entry varchar [note: 'FK to finance.journal_entry.journal_entry_id — Header-line relationship is the most fundamental FK in any GL system. Every line item must reference its parent journal entry document. Without this, you cannot reconstruct a balanced journal entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the line record.']

  Note: 'Individual debit/credit line items within a SAP FI journal entry document. Each line captures GL account, cost center, profit center, WBS element, functional area, amount in document and local currency, tax code, and assignment field. Supports granular cost allocation to fab process steps, equipment depreciation lines, and NRE milestone revenue lines. Essential for drill-down financial analysis and SOX audit trails.'
}

Table finance.capex_request {
  capex_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the capital expenditure request.']
  capex_fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.fixed_asset.fixed_asset_id — Approved capex requests result in fixed asset creation. Links investment approval to the resulting asset for lifecycle tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capex requests are tied to a cost center; using an FK removes the ambiguous code column.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal employee who originated the capex request.']
  export_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Capital equipment purchases subject to export controls must be tied to the relevant export license for financial tracking and audit.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the capital project or program linked to the request.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external supplier providing the equipment.']
  to_fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.fixed_asset.fixed_asset_id — Approved capex requests result in fixed asset creation. This FK links the investment decision to the resulting asset record, enabling capex tracking from request through asset lifecycle.']
  approval_stage varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow stage of the request.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent approval action.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the request was last approved or rejected.']
  approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount approved after any adjustments.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget amount allocated for the request.']
  business_case_summary varchar [note: 'Brief narrative describing the strategic rationale and expected ROI.']
  capex_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capex request.']
  chips_act_funding_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request qualifies for CHIPS Act financial incentives.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the request record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the requested amount (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the asset is fully depreciated.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate the capital asset.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation of the asset begins.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the fab equipment being requested.']
  equipment_model varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model number or SKU of the equipment (e.g., NXE:3400).']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of capital funds for the investment.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Detailed technical justification, including technology node applicability and capacity impact.']
  request_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount requested for the investment before any adjustments.']
  request_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the capex request was initially submitted.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable request number assigned by the finance system (e.g., CR‑2023‑00123).']
  requester_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee requesting the investment.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk assessment rating for the investment request.']
  technology_node varchar [note: 'Process technology node the equipment will support.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the request record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated economic useful life of the asset in years for depreciation.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the equipment supplier.']

  Note: 'Capital expenditure request and approval record for semiconductor fab equipment investments. Captures investment type (lithography scanner, CMP tool, CVD/PVD/ALD system, ATE platform, metrology tool), requested amount, business justification, technology node applicability, expected useful life, depreciation method, and multi-level approval workflow status. Tracks NRE vs. recurring capex classification and CHIPS Act funding eligibility. Links to SAP IM investment program.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each fixed asset record.']
  asset_custodian_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Asset register requires assigning a custodian employee for accountability and audit of each fixed asset.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center.cost_center_id — Every fixed asset is assigned to a cost center for depreciation posting and cost allocation. Critical for fab equipment cost tracking.']
  fab_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab (fabrication facility) where the asset resides.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation recorded to date for the asset.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to acquire the asset, including purchase price, freight, installation, and commissioning.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the asset was purchased or otherwise acquired.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level classification (e.g., Equipment, Facility, IT Infrastructure).']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the asset (e.g., EUV Lithography Scanner 1).']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric tag or barcode assigned to the asset for physical identification.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Category of the asset such as Lithography Scanner, CVD Tool, CMP Equipment, Cleanroom Infrastructure, or IT Asset.']
  capitalized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset has been capitalized on the balance sheet (True) or remains expense (False).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the asset record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fixed asset record was first created in the system.']
  depreciation_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional depreciation amount applied due to changes in utilization or technology node retirement.']
  depreciation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Depreciation expense posted for the most recent fiscal period.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Projected date when the asset will be fully depreciated or disposed.']
  depreciation_key varchar [note: 'Code that determines the depreciation area (e.g., book, tax, IFRS) and rules applied.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate periodic depreciation.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation calculations begin for the asset.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date the asset was removed from service and disposed of, sold, or transferred.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the asset was disposed.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary proceeds received from the disposal of the asset.']
  fixed_asset_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the asset.']
  grant_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the grant allocated to the asset.']
  grant_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which the grant amount is recognized in financial statements.']
  grant_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for CHIPS Act or other government grant associated with the asset.']
  impairment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount by which the asset\'s carrying value is reduced due to impairment.']
  impairment_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether an impairment test has been performed and resulted in a loss.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity performed on the asset.']
  location_bay varchar [note: 'Specific bay or cleanroom area within the building housing the asset.']
  location_building varchar [note: 'Name or code of the building where the asset is installed.']
  maintenance_contract_number varchar [note: 'Contract identifier for the service agreement covering preventive maintenance.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Asset\'s carrying amount after subtracting accumulated depreciation.']
  next_maintenance_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled maintenance.']
  remaining_useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated remaining economic life of the asset, expressed in years.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns or manages the asset.']
  technology_node_nm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Process node (in nanometers) the equipment is qualified for (e.g., 7.0, 5.0).']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the asset record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fixed asset record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Planned economic life of the asset expressed in years for depreciation purposes.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average operational utilization of the asset expressed as a percentage of its capacity.']

  Note: 'SAP FI-AA fixed asset master and full depreciation lifecycle for semiconductor manufacturing equipment and infrastructure. Covers EUV/DUV lithography scanners, deposition systems (CVD/PVD/ALD), etch tools, CMP equipment, ATE platforms, cleanroom infrastructure, and IT assets. Master attributes include asset class, acquisition cost, capitalization date, useful life, depreciation key, location (fab building/bay), technology node assignment, and CHIPS Act grant tracking. Depreciation records capture each periodic posting: run date, fiscal period, depreciation area (book/tax/IFRS), depreciation amount, accumulated depreciation, net book value, and remaining useful life. Supports fab utilization-based depreciation adjustments, technology node retirement accounting, asset impairment testing, and disposal/transfer processing. SSOT for the complete asset register from acquisition through disposal including all depreciation schedules. Generated by SAP FI-AA depreciation run (AFAB).'
}

Table finance.asset_depreciation {
  asset_depreciation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each depreciation posting record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Asset depreciation records should reference the cost center responsible for the asset.']
  depreciation_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the depreciation run (header) that generated this posting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who approved or triggered the posting.']
  fab_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab facility that owns the asset.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or fab site where the asset resides.']
  primary_fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the fab equipment or infrastructure asset being depreciated.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation accumulated on the asset up to the posting date.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow status of the depreciation posting approval.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the posting was approved or rejected.']
  asset_depreciation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the depreciation line.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch job that performed the depreciation calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the depreciation record was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts (e.g., USD).']
  depreciation_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional amount added/subtracted to the base depreciation due to utilization or revaluation.']
  depreciation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Depreciation expense posted for the period in the selected currency.']
  depreciation_area varchar [note: 'Accounting area for the depreciation amount – book (GAAP), tax, or IFRS.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate depreciation for the asset.']
  depreciation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual depreciation rate expressed as a percentage.']
  external_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to external documentation or audit trail (e.g., audit report ID).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Period number within the fiscal year (typically month 1‑12).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year of the depreciation posting (e.g., 2024).']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the depreciation run.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or explanations for the posting.']
  posted_date date [note: 'Date on which the depreciation entry was posted to the ledger.']
  remaining_useful_life_months varchar [note: 'Estimated remaining service life of the asset in months after this posting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP or system that generated the depreciation run (e.g., SAP FI‑AA).']
  technology_node varchar [note: 'Process technology node associated with the asset (e.g., 7nm, 14nm).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the depreciation record.']
  utilization_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Factor reflecting actual equipment utilization used to adjust depreciation.']

  Note: 'Periodic depreciation posting records for semiconductor fab equipment and infrastructure assets. Captures depreciation run date, fiscal period, asset, depreciation area (book, tax, IFRS), depreciation amount, accumulated depreciation to date, and remaining useful life. Supports fab utilization-based depreciation adjustments and technology node retirement accounting. Generated by SAP FI-AA depreciation run (AFAB).'
}

Table finance.internal_order {
  internal_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the internal order.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset‑under‑construction record created from capitalized costs.']
  internal_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center responsible for the order\'s expenses.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee accountable for the order execution.']
  to_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center.cost_center_id — Internal orders are assigned to responsible cost centers for settlement and organizational reporting.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual cost incurred against the internal order to date.']
  auditor_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the auditor provided approval for capitalization.']
  auditor_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the internal order has been approved by the external auditor for capitalization.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for the internal order expressed in the order currency.']
  capitalization_date date [note: 'Date on which eligible costs were capitalized to an intangible asset.']
  capitalization_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order meets criteria for R&D cost capitalization.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the internal order was closed and settled.']
  commitment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Committed cost amount that cannot be exceeded without re‑approval.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the order reached technically complete status.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  internal_order_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the purpose and scope of the internal order.']
  internal_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the internal order.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent status transition for the order.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after any adjustments, taxes, or discounts applied to the order.']
  order_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the internal order was initially created in the source system.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the internal order.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order purpose (e.g., research & development, equipment maintenance, non‑recurring engineering).']
  real_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the order records real, incurred costs.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the internal order record.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the internal order was released for execution.']
  settlement_rule varchar [note: 'Rule that determines how costs are settled (to an asset, cost center, or WBS element).']
  statistical_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the order is used for statistical cost allocation rather than actual cost tracking.']
  technology_node varchar [note: 'Process technology node (e.g., 5nm, 7nm) associated with the R&D effort.']
  to_cost_center varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center.cost_center_id — Internal orders have a responsible cost center for settlement and organizational assignment. Required for cost collection and settlement processing.']
  trl varchar [note: 'Readiness level of the technology (1‑9) at the time of order creation.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'Work Breakdown Structure element code to which the order costs are assigned.']

  Note: 'SAP CO internal order master for collecting, tracking, settling, and capitalizing costs of specific semiconductor initiatives. Covers R&D capitalization orders (process node development, IP core development, EDA tool evaluation), fab equipment maintenance campaign orders, customer-specific NRE execution orders, and corporate initiative orders. Includes order type, status lifecycle (created/released/technically complete/closed), responsible cost center, settlement rule (to asset, cost center, or WBS), overall budget, commitment value, actual cost, and statistical/real indicator. Full R&D capitalization lifecycle: tracks technology readiness level, capitalization eligibility assessment per ASC 730/IAS 38, original expense amounts, capitalized amounts, capitalization date, asset under construction reference, and auditor approval. Enables end-to-end cost collection from initial R&D expense through capitalization event to intangible asset creation. Sourced from SAP CO-OM/CO-PC.'
}

Table finance.wbs_element {
  wbs_element_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the WBS element record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: WBS elements belong to a cost center; an FK provides a reliable link.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager accountable for the WBS element.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.internal_order.internal_order_id — WBS elements in R&D projects link to internal orders for cost collection and capitalization. Required for project cost tracking and R&D capitalization workflow.']
  parent_wbs_wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent element in the WBS hierarchy.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Real monetary cost incurred to date for the WBS element.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Real calendar date when work on the element actually completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Real calendar date when work on the element actually started.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the WBS element was approved for execution.']
  billing_element_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the WBS element is billable for NRE or customer invoicing.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned monetary budget allocated to the WBS element.']
  cost_allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate costs from this element to downstream entities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the WBS element record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems (e.g., PLM, ERP) to reference this WBS element.']
  financial_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the element\'s financial data belongs.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Quarter within the financial year for reporting.']
  is_capital_expenditure boolean [note: 'True if costs incurred under this element are treated as capital expenditures.']
  is_template boolean [note: 'True if the element is defined as a reusable template for future projects.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the WBS element record.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date when work on the element is expected to finish.']
  project_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the overall project to which the element belongs.']
  r_and_d_capitalization_flag boolean [note: 'True if the element\'s costs are eligible for R&D capitalization under accounting standards.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date when work on the element is scheduled to begin.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the WBS element record.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'Hierarchical alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the WBS element within the project hierarchy.']
  wbs_element_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work scope represented by the WBS element.']
  wbs_element_level varchar [note: 'Depth level of the element within the WBS tree (root = 1).']
  wbs_element_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the WBS element.']
  wbs_element_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the WBS element.']
  wbs_element_type varchar [note: 'Category of the WBS element indicating its granularity within the project hierarchy.']
  wbs_hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Delimited string representing the full path from root to this element (e.g., 1.2.3).']
  wbs_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status of the WBS element.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the WBS element record.']

  Note: 'Work Breakdown Structure element master from SAP PS for semiconductor R&D and capital projects. Structures complex multi-phase projects such as new process node development (PDK creation, process qualification, yield ramp), fab expansion programs, and EUV lithography integration. Captures WBS code, project definition, responsible manager, planned/actual dates, budget, and billing element flag for NRE milestone invoicing. Enables hierarchical project cost tracking and R&D capitalization.'
}

Table finance.cost_allocation {
  cost_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each cost allocation record.']
  allocation_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the allocation cycle (header) to which this record belongs.']
  primary_sender_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation records identify the sending cost center; the FK replaces the code and enforces referential integrity.']
  tertiary_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center.cost_center_id — Cost allocation cycles have sender cost centers (overhead pools) that distribute costs. Without this FK, allocation cycles cannot identify their source.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount allocated to the receiver cost center.']
  allocation_base_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the allocation basis (e.g., total machine hours).']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Primary driver used as the allocation base (e.g., machine hours, wafer passes).']
  allocation_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or notes for the allocation.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the allocation (e.g., activity‑based, statistical, fixed percentage).']
  allocation_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Rate applied per unit of the allocation base (e.g., cost per machine hour).']
  cost_allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the allocation record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the allocation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the allocated amount.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the allocation becomes effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the allocation is no longer valid (nullable).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period for which the allocation is recorded, expressed as YYYYQ#.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1‑4) of the allocation.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year of the allocation.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the allocation line within its allocation cycle.']
  receiver_cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the cost center receiving the allocated cost.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this allocation record reverses a prior allocation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the allocation record.']

  Note: 'Periodic cost allocation, assessment, and distribution cycle records for semiconductor fab overhead and shared service costs. Captures allocation cycle ID, sender cost center (fab overhead pool, shared services), receiver cost centers or orders, allocation method (activity-based using machine hours or wafer passes, statistical key figure, fixed percentage), allocation base quantity, allocated amount, fiscal period, and reversal indicator. Supports wafer cost modeling by distributing equipment depreciation, utilities, cleanroom maintenance, and indirect labor to process steps based on fab utilization rates and activity drivers. Enables accurate product costing and profitability analysis.'
}

Table finance.wafer_cost_model {
  wafer_cost_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each wafer cost model record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Wafer cost models are allocated to cost centers; replace code with FK.']
  fabrication_process_flow_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing process flow associated with this cost model.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Wafer cost models are scoped to a profit center; the FK provides proper ownership and reporting.']
  chemicals_gases_cost_usd_per_wafer decimal(12,2) [note: 'Direct material cost for chemicals and gases consumed per wafer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost model record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values in the model.']
  die_size_um2 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Area of a single die on the wafer in square micrometres.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the cost model is retired or superseded; null if still active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the cost model becomes valid for planning and reporting.']
  equipment_depreciation_usd_per_wafer decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allocated equipment depreciation cost for each wafer pass.']
  fab_location varchar [note: 'Code representing the fab site (e.g., "US1", "SG2").']
  fab_overhead_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Factory overhead rate applied to the wafer cost, expressed as a percent.']
  labor_hours_per_wafer decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total labor hours required to process a single wafer.']
  labor_rate_usd_per_hour decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hourly labor rate applied to wafer processing activities.']
  mask_set_amortization_wafers decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of wafers over which the mask set cost is amortized.']
  mask_set_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of the photomask set required for the process flow, expressed in US dollars.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the cost model in finance and planning systems.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the cost model (e.g., "7nm High‑Yield Model").']
  silicon_wafer_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost of the raw silicon wafer per unit, in US dollars.']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned percentage of good dies per wafer used for cost allocation.']
  technology_node varchar [note: 'Process technology node expressed in nanometers (e.g., "7nm").']
  total_cost_per_wafer_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fully‑loaded cost of a wafer after aggregating all direct and indirect cost components.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost model record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for the cost model, incremented on each change.']
  wafer_cost_model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost model.']
  wafer_diameter_mm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Diameter of the silicon wafer in millimetres.']

  Note: 'Semiconductor-specific wafer cost model master defining the standard cost build-up for a wafer lot at a given technology node and process flow. Captures process node, wafer diameter, die size, target yield, mask set cost amortization, direct material cost (silicon wafer, chemicals, gases), direct labor rate, equipment depreciation per wafer pass, fab overhead rate, and fully-loaded cost per wafer out. Used for product pricing, NRE recovery planning, and fab utilization-based cost allocation. Unique to semiconductor manufacturing finance.'
}

Table finance.finance_nre_agreement {
  finance_nre_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the NRE agreement record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer party to whom the NRE agreement applies.']
  chips_act_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.chips_act_obligation. Business justification: NRE agreements can trigger CHIPS Act obligations; linking enables mandatory regulatory‑financial reporting of those obligations.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: NRE agreements are associated with a cost center; FK enforces this relationship.']
  ic_design_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IC design project associated with the NRE agreement.']
  primary_finance_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the NRE agreement.']
  sales_nre_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_nre_agreement. Business justification: Financial NRE agreement must reference the matching sales NRE agreement to align revenue recognition with the sales contract.']
  tertiary_finance_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the agreement record.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned to the NRE agreement.']

  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the agreement was approved.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes or remarks about the NRE agreement.']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the NRE contract document.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the NRE amount.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the NRE agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the NRE agreement ends, if applicable.']
  finance_nre_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the NRE agreement.']
  legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code of the legal entity within the corporation that owns the NRE agreement.']
  payment_milestone_schedule varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of payment milestones and amounts.']
  recovery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recover NRE costs from the customer.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region of the agreement.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method for recognizing revenue from the NRE agreement.']
  tax_regime varchar [note: 'Tax treatment applicable to the NRE agreement.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for termination if the agreement status is terminated.']
  total_nre_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the NRE agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']

  Note: 'Non-Recurring Engineering cost recovery agreement master tracking NRE contracts with fabless customers for ASIC/SoC design services, mask set costs, and process qualification. Captures NRE type (mask NRE, design NRE, qualification NRE), total NRE amount, payment milestone schedule, recovery method (upfront, volume-based royalty, hybrid), customer, product design reference, and revenue recognition method per ASC 606/IFRS 15. SSOT for NRE financial obligations distinct from recurring product sales.'
}

Table finance.finance_nre_milestone {
  finance_nre_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the NRE milestone record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: NRE milestones inherit the cost center from their parent agreement; FK makes the link explicit.']
  finance_nre_agreement_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.finance_nre_agreement.finance_nre_agreement_id — NRE milestones are child records of NRE agreements. Fundamental header-line relationship for NRE billing and revenue recognition.']

  project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated IC design project.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Each NRE milestone is owned by a responsible engineer; the milestone record must reference that employee.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone was actually completed.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross monetary amount for the milestone before taxes or adjustments.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary amount after tax and any adjustments.']
  amount_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the milestone amount.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the milestone for billing.']
  billing_trigger_event varchar [note: 'Event that triggers billing for the milestone.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone record complies with SOX reporting requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  expense_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the expense for accounting purposes.']
  finance_nre_milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the milestone.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the invoice associated with the milestone.']
  is_revenue_recognized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether revenue has been recognized for this milestone.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the milestone (e.g., M001).']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the milestone (e.g., RTL Freeze, Tapeout Submission).']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Category of the milestone within the NRE project lifecycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the milestone.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Date the milestone is scheduled to be completed.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which revenue is recognized for the milestone per ASC 606.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recognize revenue for the milestone.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the milestone record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the milestone record.']

  Note: 'Individual billing milestone records within an NRE agreement tracking completion of design and engineering deliverables. Captures milestone name (RTL freeze, tapeout submission, first silicon, qualification pass), planned and actual completion date, milestone amount, billing trigger event, invoice status, and revenue recognition date. Enables ASC 606-compliant revenue recognition at point of performance obligation satisfaction for NRE contracts.'
}

Table finance.budget_plan {
  budget_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the budget plan record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budget plans are defined per cost center, profit center, and WBS element. Adding explicit FK columns replaces the string codes and enables proper joins for reporting and allocation.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Budget plans belong to a profit center; the FK enables drill‑down of profit‑center level budgeting.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Budget plans are linked to a WBS element for project‑level budgeting; the FK replaces the free‑text WBS code.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the budget plan within the governance workflow.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget (e.g., operating expense, capital expenditure, R&D investment, headcount).']
  chips_act_funding_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the budget includes funding that is aligned with the US CHIPS Act grant requirements.']
  compliance_regulation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the budget plan complies with applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., SOX, CHIPS Act).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the budget plan record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the local amount.']
  financial_reporting_standard varchar [note: 'Accounting framework used for the budget plan (e.g., IFRS, US‑GAAP).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year to which the budget plan applies.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the budget plan record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or explanations about the budget plan.']
  organization_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the cost or profit center, division, or legal entity responsible for the budget.']
  plan_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget plan received final approval.']
  plan_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget period ends.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the budget plan, used for tracking and reporting.']
  plan_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget period begins.']
  planned_amount_group decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted monetary amount expressed in the corporate group reporting currency.']
  planned_amount_local decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted monetary amount expressed in the local operating currency.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned count or volume associated with the budget line (e.g., wafer starts, FTEs, machine hours).']
  planning_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to develop the budget (e.g., top‑down, bottom‑up, rolling forecast, zero‑based).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the planned quantity.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last updated the budget plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the budget plan record.']
  variance_to_prior_year_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between the planned amount and the actual amount of the prior fiscal year, expressed in currency.']
  variance_to_prior_year_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage difference between the planned amount and the prior year actual amount.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the plan to support revisions and audit trails.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who initially created the budget plan.']

  Note: 'Annual and multi-year financial budget plan with granular line-item detail for the semiconductor enterprise. Header captures planning version, fiscal year, budget type (operating expense, capital expenditure, R&D investment, headcount), organizational unit (cost center, profit center, company code), planning method, and approval status. Line items (owned within this product) specify GL account/cost element, cost center or WBS element, fiscal period distribution, planned quantity (wafer starts, headcount FTEs, machine hours), planned amount in local and group currency, and variance to prior year. Supports fab capacity-based opex planning, technology node R&D investment roadmaps, CHIPS Act grant-aligned capex budgets, rolling forecasts, and period-level budget vs. actual variance analysis. Enables drill-down from plan header to individual cost element allocations. Sourced from SAP BPC/CO planning cycles.'
}

Table finance.budget_line {
  budget_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the budget line record.']
  budget_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent budget plan to which this line belongs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Replace string codes with proper foreign keys to enable joins and enforce referential integrity.']
  cost_center_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Budget line approvals are signed off by the cost‑center manager employee, linking budget lines to that manager.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Replace string codes with proper foreign keys to enable joins and enforce referential integrity.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Budget lines for compliance activities reference specific regulatory filings to track allocated spend.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Replace string codes with proper foreign keys to enable joins and enforce referential integrity.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate the budget amount to the line.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow state of the budget line.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the approver.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line was approved.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the budget line (e.g., capital expenditure, operating expense).']
  budget_line_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context for the budget line.']
  budget_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the budget line.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates regulatory compliance relevance (e.g., SOX, SEC).']
  cost_center_manager varchar [note: 'User identifier of the manager responsible for the cost center.']
  cost_element_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the cost element represented by the line.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency identifier for the monetary values.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Optional date when the budget line ceases to be effective.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the budget line becomes effective for accounting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Month or period identifier within the fiscal year (01‑12). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|02|03|04|05|06|07|08|09|10|11|12 — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit year to which the budget line is allocated.']
  is_frozen boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line is locked from further edits.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the budget document.']
  planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted monetary amount for the line, expressed in the line\'s currency.']
  planned_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Forecasted amount of the resource (e.g., wafer starts, FTEs) for the period.']
  planned_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the planned quantity.']
  related_project_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project or program associated with the line.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version counter for changes to the budget line.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating enterprise system that supplied the budget data.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the budget line.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between actual spend and planned amount (actual – planned).']
  variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance calculated as (variance_amount / planned_amount) * 100.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Individual line items within a budget plan capturing granular cost element-level budget allocations. Each line specifies GL account/cost element, cost center or WBS element, fiscal period distribution, planned quantity (e.g., wafer starts, headcount FTEs), planned amount, and variance to prior year. Enables period-level budget vs. actual variance analysis for fab operations, R&D programs, and SG&A functions.'
}

Table finance.rd_capitalization {
  rd_capitalization_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each R&D capitalization event record.']
  amortization_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the amortization schedule applied to the capitalized amount.']
  design_ip_core_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IP core whose development costs were capitalized.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset‑under‑construction record that receives the capitalized cost.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the R&D project that generated the expense.']
  reversal_of_capitalization_rd_capitalization_id varchar [note: 'If this record is a reversal, references the original capitalization record.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.wbs_element.wbs_element_id — R&D capitalization events reference the WBS element whose costs are being capitalized. Required for project-to-asset reclassification audit trail.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the auditor approved the capitalization.']
  auditor_approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the internal or external audit review of the capitalization.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the auditor who approved or rejected the capitalization.']
  capitalization_date date [note: 'Date on which the expense was officially capitalized.']
  capitalization_event_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the capitalization event for tracking across systems.']
  capitalized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the original expense reclassified as a capitalized asset.']
  capitalized_asset_type varchar [note: 'Category of the asset created by the capitalization.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the capitalization (e.g., ASC 730, IAS 38).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for the original expense.']
  cost_element varchar [note: 'Accounting cost element used for the original expense.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially loaded the record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the amounts.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to depreciate the capitalized asset.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation of the capitalized asset begins.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the capitalized asset is retired or fully amortized (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the capitalized asset becomes effective for accounting purposes.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the capitalization event was recorded in the source system.']
  external_audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an external auditor has reviewed the event.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the capitalization belongs.']
  internal_audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an internal audit has reviewed the event.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record reverses a prior capitalization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations.']
  original_expense_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount originally expensed before capitalization, in the transaction currency.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center associated with the expense, if applicable.']
  rd_capitalization_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the capitalization event and rationale.']
  rd_capitalization_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the capitalization event.']
  related_ip_core_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the related IP core.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP or PLM system that supplied the data.']
  technology_readiness_level varchar [note: 'Readiness level of the technology at the time of capitalization (e.g., TRL 1‑9). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trl_1|trl_2|trl_3|trl_4|trl_5|trl_6|trl_7|trl_8|trl_9 — promote to reference product]']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life of the capitalized asset in years.']

  Note: 'R&D capitalization event record tracking the reclassification of qualifying R&D expenditures to intangible assets or capitalized development costs per ASC 730/IAS 38. Captures project WBS element, cost element, original expense amount, capitalized amount, capitalization date, asset under construction reference, technology readiness level at capitalization, and auditor approval. Specific to semiconductor companies capitalizing process node development and IP core creation costs.'
}

Table finance.intercompany_transaction {
  intercompany_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the intercompany transaction record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Intercompany transactions are posted to a cost center for internal reporting; the FK replaces the free‑text code.']
  intercompany_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the master intercompany agreement governing this transaction.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.journal_entry.journal_entry_id — Every intercompany transaction generates journal entries in both sending and receiving company codes. This FK enables reconciliation and elimination during consolidation.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Intercompany transactions also affect a profit center; the FK enables profit‑center level analysis.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, fees, or adjustments, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax and adjustments, the amount to be settled.']
  amount_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the transaction amount.']
  audit_documentation_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting audit documentation for the transaction.']
  country_of_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the tax jurisdiction governing the transaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the transaction.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the transaction\'s validity period.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date of the transaction\'s validity period (null if open‑ended).']
  elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction should be eliminated during financial consolidation.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the transaction is booked.']
  interco_to_sending_company varchar [note: 'FK to finance.company_code.company_code_id — Intercompany transactions must identify sending and receiving company codes. Required for elimination and transfer pricing compliance.']
  intercompany_transaction_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the transaction purpose or notes.']
  intercompany_transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the transaction.']
  receiving_entity_code varchar [note: 'Company code or legal entity identifier of the receiver in the intercompany transaction.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction must be included in country‑by‑country (CbC) reporting.']
  sending_entity_code varchar [note: 'Company code or legal entity identifier of the sender in the intercompany transaction.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record (e.g., SAP S/4HANA) that originated the transaction.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applicable to the transaction for VAT/GST purposes.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible transaction number assigned by the source ERP system.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the intercompany transaction was recorded in the source system.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of the intercompany transaction.']
  transfer_pricing_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the supporting OECD transfer‑pricing documentation.']
  transfer_pricing_margin decimal(12,4) [note: 'Margin applied when the cost‑plus method is used, expressed as a percentage.']
  transfer_pricing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to determine the arm\'s‑length price for the intercompany transaction.']
  transfer_pricing_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Rate (percentage or per‑unit) applied under the selected transfer pricing method.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the transaction record.']

  Note: 'Intercompany financial transaction records with embedded transfer pricing governance for semiconductor enterprises with multi-entity structures (design center subsidiaries, fab entities, OSAT affiliates, IP holding companies). Transaction records capture sending/receiving company codes, transaction type (intercompany loan, transfer pricing charge, royalty, shared service allocation, intercompany sale of wafers/dies), amount, currency, elimination flag, and transfer pricing documentation reference. Transfer pricing master configuration embedded within: pricing method (cost-plus, CUP, resale price, TNMM), applicable entity pair, product/service category, price or margin rate, validity period, arm\'s length documentation, and OECD documentation reference. Supports financial consolidation elimination, OECD transfer pricing compliance, country-by-country reporting under BEPS Action 13, and tax authority audit defense.'
}

Table finance.consolidation_entry {
  consolidation_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the consolidation adjustment entry.']

  related_entry_consolidation_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a related entry, such as the original entry for a reversal.']
  amount_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net adjustment amount (e.g., elimination, translation gain/loss) applied to the gross amount.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before any adjustments or netting, expressed in the entry currency.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Resulting amount after applying adjustments, the amount that impacts the consolidated financial statements.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the entry received formal approval.']

  consolidation_entry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context or rationale for the entry.']
  consolidation_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the entry.']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used for the consolidation (e.g., full consolidation, proportionate, equity method, cost method).']
  consolidation_to_company varchar [note: 'FK to finance.company_code.company_code_id — Consolidation entries reference the consolidation unit (company code) being consolidated. Required for group reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consolidation entry record was first created in the system.']

  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the consolidation adjustment becomes effective for reporting.']
  entry_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible sequential number assigned to the consolidation entry.']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Category of the consolidation adjustment (e.g., elimination of intercompany profit, minority interest, currency translation, goodwill amortization).']
