  scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational scope of the obligation.']
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the source regulation, standard, or contract document.']

  Note: 'Master record defining individual regulatory and standards compliance obligations applicable to the organization, specific sites, or product lines, including periodic compliance assessment history. Captures obligation source (regulation, standard, customer contract, industry code, CHIPS Act award), obligation description, applicable scope (global, regional, site-specific, product-specific), responsible function, compliance control owner, assessment frequency, current compliance status, next review date. For periodic assessments: assessment date, method (self-assessment, desk review, site inspection), assessor, compliance status (compliant, partially compliant, non-compliant), gap description, risk rating, remediation plan, and target remediation date. Serves as the unified compliance obligation register and assessment tracker underpinning the ISO 37301 compliance management framework.'
}

Table compliance.technology_control_plan {
  technology_control_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the Technology Control Plan.']
  authorized_personnel_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Technology Control Plans list employees authorized to access controlled technology for security compliance.']
  eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'FK to compliance.eccn_classification.eccn_classification_id — Technology Control Plans govern access to specific ECCN-classified technology. The TCP must reference the ECCN classification it protects to validate scope and applicability.']
  fabrication_technology_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fabrication.technology_node. Business justification: Technology Control Plans are defined per technology node to ensure export/ITAR compliance; the plan must reference the specific node used in fab.']
  process_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: Technology Control Plans are defined per process node; linking ensures the plan applies to the correct manufacturing node.']
  superseded_technology_control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on technology_control_plan (superseded_technology_control_plan_id)']
  technology_eccn_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Technology control plan references an ECCN; FK avoids duplication of ECCN data.']
  access_control_measures varchar [note: 'Summary of physical and logical controls required to protect the technology.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON or text log of audit events related to the plan.']
  change_history varchar [note: 'Chronological record of major changes to the plan.']
  classification_rationale varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the technology received its export classification.']
  clean_team_protocol_description varchar [note: 'Procedures governing clean‑team members who may access the controlled data.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the plan.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance assessment of the plan.']
  controlled_technology_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the technology, process, or data that is controlled.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan record was first created in the system.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the plan record must be retained for compliance.']
  deminimis_value_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary threshold below which export licensing may be waived.']
  design_methodology varchar [note: 'Design flow or methodology (e.g., RTL, gate‑level) covered by the plan.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed plan documentation stored in the document repository.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the Technology Control Plan becomes active.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the Technology Control Plan expires or is superseded.']
  export_license_required boolean [note: 'True if an export license is mandatory for the controlled technology.']
  foreign_national_exclusions varchar [note: 'List of nationalities or countries excluded from access under the plan.']
  ip_block varchar [note: 'Identifier of the IP block or core that is subject to export control under this plan.']
  is_deemed_export boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan involves a deemed export under U.S. regulations.']
  it_access_restriction_description varchar [note: 'Details of network, system, and file‑level restrictions applied to the technology.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan was last reviewed.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the plan record.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the export license associated with the plan.']
  mitigation_measures varchar [note: 'Planned actions to mitigate identified compliance risks.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance review of the plan.']
  physical_barrier_description varchar [note: 'Description of any physical segregation (e.g., locked rooms, clean‑team areas).']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the Technology Control Plan used for reference and reporting.']
  plan_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the plan\'s coverage (e.g., specific modules, processes, or IP blocks).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the plan indicating the kind of controlled technology it governs.']
  process_node varchar [note: 'Technology node or process generation (e.g., 7nm, 5nm) to which the plan applies.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory regime governing the plan.']
  responsible_officer varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the officer accountable for the plan.']
  restricted_country_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of countries to which export is prohibited.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between mandatory plan reviews.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent plan review.']
  risk_assessment_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of risks identified for the controlled technology.']
  technology_control_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the plan.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether personnel must complete export‑control training.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the plan for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record for Technology Control Plans (TCPs) governing access to export-controlled semiconductor technology, source code, and technical data within facilities employing foreign nationals. Captures TCP scope (process node, IP block, design methodology), controlled technology description, ECCN classification, access control measures (physical barriers, IT access restrictions, clean-team protocols), authorized personnel list, foreign national nationalities excluded, TCP effective date, review cycle, and responsible export control officer. Critical for EAR deemed export compliance in multinational semiconductor R&D and FAB environments.'
}

Table compliance.declaration_substance {
  declaration_substance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the declaration_substance association']
  reach_svhc_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance declaration']
  substance_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the substance inventory record']
  measured_concentration decimal [note: 'Actual concentration of the substance measured in the product for this declaration']
  threshold_value decimal [note: 'Regulatory concentration limit for the substance applicable to this declaration']

  Note: 'Association product representing the link between a compliance declaration and a chemical substance. Each record captures the measured concentration of the substance in the product and the regulatory threshold value applicable to that substance.. Existence Justification: Each reach_svhc_declaration lists multiple chemical substances, and each substance can appear in many product declarations. The relationship is actively managed by compliance engineers who record measured concentration and regulatory threshold values for each substance within a declaration. This many‑to‑many association carries its own data, so it is modeled as an association entity.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each employee record.']
  employee_position_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.position.position_id — Employees fill positions. This is the core link between the person and the authorized headcount slot.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the direct manager or supervisor.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.position.position_id — Employees must reference their assigned position for headcount tracking.']
  address varchar [note: 'Employee\'s residential address for payroll and tax purposes.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Employee\'s bank account for direct deposit.']
  certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of professional certifications held.']
  clearance_level varchar [note: 'Level of security clearance granted to the employee.']
  compensation_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base annual compensation amount before bonuses.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which the employee\'s labor is charged.']
  currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for compensation (e.g., USD).']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Employee\'s birth date, required for benefits eligibility and legal compliance.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department where the employee works.']
  disability_status boolean [note: 'True if the employee has a recognized disability.']
  ear_eligibility boolean [note: 'True if the employee is eligible to access EAR-controlled technology.']
  education_level varchar [note: 'Highest completed education level of the employee.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary corporate email address used for official communication.']
  employee_org_unit varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.org_unit.org_unit_id — Employee must reference their org unit for management hierarchy and cost center assignment.']
  employee_position varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.position.position_id — Employee must reference their assigned position for headcount tracking and organizational placement.']
  employee_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment relationship.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s given (first) name as recorded in HR.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Concatenated legal name of the employee (first + middle + last).']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee officially started employment with the company.']
  itar_eligibility boolean [note: 'True if the employee is eligible to access ITAR-controlled data.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official title or role assigned to the employee.']
  language_proficiency varchar [note: 'Languages spoken and proficiency levels.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s family (last) name as recorded in HR.']
  location varchar [note: 'Primary site or fab where the employee performs duties.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued personal identifier (e.g., SSN, SIN) used for tax reporting.']
  organization_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level within the corporate structure (e.g., L1, L2).']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is eligible for overtime compensation.']
  passport_number varchar [note: 'Passport identifier for employees requiring international travel clearance.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the employee (mobile or office).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the employee record.']
  salary_grade varchar [note: 'Internal salary band or grade assigned to the employee.']
  security_clearance_expiration date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s security clearance expires.']
  skills varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of core technical and soft skills.']
  social_security_number varchar [note: 'U.S. Social Security Number for payroll tax reporting.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Tax ID used for payroll tax withholdings.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended, if applicable.']
  union_member_flag boolean [note: 'True if the employee is a member of a labor union.']
  veteran_status varchar [note: 'Indicates military veteran status of the employee.']
  work_shift varchar [note: 'Scheduled shift pattern for the employee.']

  Note: 'Master record for all semiconductor workforce personnel including fab operators, process engineers, IC design engineers, EDA specialists, metrology technicians, and corporate staff. SSOT for employee identity, employment status, job classification, cost center assignment, and organizational placement. Captures hire date, termination date, employment type (full-time, contractor, intern), work location (fab site, design center, office), clearance/export control eligibility (ITAR, EAR), and position assignment. Integrates with Workday HCM as the system of record. Central entity referenced by all workforce transactions.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the position record.']
  internal_job_id varchar [note: 'Legacy or alternate identifier used by internal HR systems.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.org_unit.org_unit_id — Positions must be assigned to an org unit for headcount planning.']
  position_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department master record.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical site record.']
  budget_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Approved monetary budget allocated for this position.']
  budget_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the position\'s budget.']
  certification_requirements varchar [note: 'Professional certifications mandatory for the role.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the role is exempt or non‑exempt under labor regulations.']
  compensation_band varchar [note: 'Compensation band or salary range classification for the position.']
  competency_requirements varchar [note: 'Key competencies and skill levels expected for the position.']
  compliance_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if mandatory compliance training is required for the position.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which the position\'s expenses are charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was first created.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for salary and budget amounts.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department to which the position reports.']
  education_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for the role.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the position is scheduled to be retired or closed.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the position becomes active in the organization.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the employment contract.']
  fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standardized FTE value representing the workload of the position.']
  grade_level varchar [note: 'Organizational grade or band indicating seniority and compensation tier.']
  headcount_filled varchar [note: 'Number of currently filled slots for the position.']
  headcount_required varchar [note: 'Number of employees approved for this position.']
  is_remote_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position permits remote or hybrid work arrangements.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to uniquely identify the job family or role across the enterprise.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad functional area to which the position belongs.']
  location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab site, design center, or office where the position is based.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the position is eligible for overtime compensation.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of responsibilities, scope, and expectations for the role.']
  position_org_unit varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.org_unit.org_unit_id — Position must reference the org unit it belongs to for headcount planning.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current staffing status of the position.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'High‑level categorization of the role\'s functional focus.']
  profile varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.job_profile.job_profile_id — Positions reference their job profile template for role specification.']
  required_security_clearance varchar [note: 'Level of security clearance required to perform the role.']
  salary_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the salary range for the position, in the specified currency.']
  salary_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the salary range for the position, in the specified currency.']
  shift_end_time timestamp [note: 'Planned end time of the shift (HH:mm, 24‑hour).']
  shift_start_time timestamp [note: 'Planned start time of the shift (HH:mm, 24‑hour).']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Standard shift classification for the position.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status (e.g., hiring freeze, project delay).']
  title varchar [note: 'Official title of the position as displayed in job postings and internal directories.']
  to_job_profile varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.job_profile.job_profile_id — Positions are created from job profile templates. Links the headcount slot to the role specification.']
  travel_required boolean [note: 'True if the role requires regular travel beyond the primary location.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the position record.']

  Note: 'Authorized headcount position master record defining approved roles within the semiconductor organization, including the job profile template specifications that define each role. Captures position title, job family (Fab Operations, Process Engineering, IC Design, EDA, Quality, Supply Chain), grade level, required headcount, filled/vacant status, fab site or design center assignment, budget approval status, job code, exempt/non-exempt classification, required education, standard competency requirements, and compensation benchmarking parameters. Supports headcount planning, workforce deployment across FAB sites and design centers, and serves as the template for hiring requisitions. Sourced from Workday HCM position management and job catalog.'
}

Table workforce.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the organizational unit.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent unit in the hierarchy.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the unit\'s physical address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the unit\'s physical address, if applicable.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Approved budget for the unit for the fiscal year, in the specified currency.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the unit is located.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) that govern the unit\'s operations.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost‑center identifier linked to the unit for financial reporting.']
  country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the unit\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the org unit record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  data_classification_level varchar [note: 'Enterprise data classification assigned to the unit\'s data assets.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the unit was retired or merged; null if still active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the unit became operational.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems (e.g., legacy PLM) to reference the unit.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Primary business function of the unit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Design|Fabrication|Quality|SupplyChain|Finance|HR|R&D|Sales — promote to reference product]']
  headcount_actual varchar [note: 'Current number of active employees assigned to the unit.']
  headcount_planned varchar [note: 'Number of employees planned for the unit for the current fiscal period.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the unit in the org chart (root = 0).']
  is_cost_center boolean [note: 'True if the unit functions as a cost center.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'True if the unit operates globally rather than at a single site.']
  is_profit_center boolean [note: 'True if the unit functions as a profit center.']
  is_shared_service boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the unit provides shared services across the enterprise.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent governance or compliance review of the unit.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the primary site or fab where the unit operates.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours (e.g., "08:00‑17:00").']
  org_chart_path varchar [note: 'Delimited string representing the full hierarchy path (e.g., "Corporate>Engineering>Design").']
  org_unit_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the unit in ERP and PLM systems.']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the unit\'s purpose and scope.']
  org_unit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the organizational unit.']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the unit.']
  org_unit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the unit within the corporate hierarchy.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the unit\'s address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the unit\'s main point of contact.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the unit\'s main point of contact.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit‑center identifier associated with the unit.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region of the unit.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the unit\'s primary location.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the org unit record.']

  Note: 'Organizational unit hierarchy master record representing the management structure of the semiconductor enterprise. Captures business unit, department, cost center, division, and team nodes across fab operations, design engineering, process engineering, quality, supply chain, and corporate functions. Supports multi-level hierarchy traversal for reporting, headcount allocation, and workforce planning. Maps to SAP CO cost center hierarchy and Workday HCM supervisory organization.'
}

Table workforce.competency {
  competency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for competency']
  certification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the certification linked to the skill, if applicable.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee (person) to whom the skill record belongs.']
  skill_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the competency or skill defined in the skill catalog.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent assessment was performed.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to evaluate the employee\'s proficiency.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date the certification expires and must be renewed.']
  certification_issue_date date [note: 'Date the certification was originally issued to the employee.']
  certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the skill requires a formal certification to be considered valid.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the employee\'s certification for the skill.']
  last_assessed_date date [note: 'Date of the last time the skill was formally assessed.']
  proficiency_level varchar [note: 'Declared level of skill mastery for the employee.']
  proficiency_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric rating (0.00‑5.00) representing the employee\'s skill proficiency.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee‑skill record was first created.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee‑skill record.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this employee‑skill record.']
  skill_attainment_date date [note: 'Date when the employee first achieved the recorded proficiency level.']
  skill_change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason recorded for the most recent change to the skill definition.']
  skill_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the skill definition was last changed.']
  skill_criticality varchar [note: 'Business impact rating of the skill for operations.']
  skill_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the skill proficiency is considered expired (e.g., for time‑limited certifications).']
  skill_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the skill definition.']
  skill_owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for maintaining the skill definition.']
  skill_owner_team varchar [note: 'Team within the department that curates the skill.']
  skill_required_for_role varchar [note: 'Job role or function for which the skill is a prerequisite.']
  skill_source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for the skill definition.']
  skill_training_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of training hours logged for this skill.']
  skill_training_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to deliver training for the skill.']
  skill_validated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who validated the skill proficiency.']
  skill_validated_date date [note: 'Date when the skill proficiency was validated.']
  skill_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the skill definition (e.g., v1.2).']

  Note: 'Unified competency and skill management product for semiconductor workforce. Combines the skill catalog (defining all technical and functional competencies tracked across the organization) with individual employee skill proficiency records. Catalog entries capture skill name, category (EDA Tools, Process Technology, Lithography, CMP, CVD/PVD/ALD, Ion Implantation, Wafer Probing, Packaging, Quality/SPC, ATPG/DFT, RTL Design, Physical Design), proficiency scale definitions, and certification linkages. Employee records capture skill attainment date, proficiency level (Novice, Practitioner, Expert, Master), assessment method, last assessed date, and expiry for time-limited qualifications. Enables workforce capability mapping, skills gap analysis, and deployment decisions for specialized fab and design roles.'
}

Table workforce.workforce_qualification {
  workforce_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for qualification']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee (from Workday HCM).']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Status of the internal audit for the certification record.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate number assigned by the issuing body.']
  certification_code varchar [note: 'Short code or identifier for the certification.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Name of the certification as defined in the certification catalog.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the certification is mandatory, optional, or internal.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the certification meets regulatory compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was initially created.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the digital copy of the certification document.']
  expiration_notice_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an expiration notice has been sent to the employee.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the certification expires.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the certification was issued to the employee.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the certification.']
  last_training_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent training session for the certification.']
  last_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes related to the certification.']
  regulatory_mandate_flag boolean [note: 'True if the certification is mandated by regulatory bodies (e.g., ITAR, IATF16949).']
  renewal_notice_date date [note: 'Date when a renewal notice was sent to the employee.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certification requires renewal.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the certification (e.g., equipment qualification, cleanroom access).']
  trainer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the trainer or instructor who provided the certification training.']
  training_hours varchar [note: 'Number of training hours completed to obtain the certification.']
  validity_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the certification is valid from issue date.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the certification record.']
  workforce_qualification_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the certification purpose and scope.']
  workforce_qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the certification for the employee.']

  Note: 'Unified certification and qualification management product for semiconductor workforce compliance. Combines the certification catalog (defining all mandatory and optional certifications) with individual employee certification attainment records. Catalog entries capture certification name, issuing body (SEMI, JEDEC, IEEE, IATF 16949, ISO 9001, internal fab qualification), type, validity period, renewal requirements, and regulatory mandate flag. Employee records capture issue date, expiry date, certificate number, status (Active, Expired, Suspended, Pending Renewal), and compliance flag. Critical for fab operator equipment qualification, cleanroom access authorization, ITAR/EAR export control training compliance, and IATF 16949 quality auditor certification management.'
}

Table workforce.shift_schedule {
  shift_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shift_schedule']
  fab_site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab site where the shift is scheduled.']
  primary_shift_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee assigned to the shift.']
  shift_pattern_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shift pattern (template) applied to this assignment.']
  assignment_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code used to reference the shift assignment in operational systems.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the shift assignment becomes effective for the employee.']
  break_schedule varchar [note: 'Description of scheduled break times within the shift (e.g., 2×15 min).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost‑center to which the labor cost of the shift is charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift assignment record was first created in the system.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date‑time of the assigned shift.']
  labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Labor cost allocated to the shift assignment (currency unit implied by corporate policy).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or special instructions for the shift assignment.']
  overtime_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether overtime work is permitted for this shift assignment.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours approved for the shift.']
  shift_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shift assignment.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Category of the shift pattern (e.g., Day, Swing, Night).']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date‑time of the assigned shift.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the shift assignment record.']
  work_area varchar [note: 'Functional area within the fab where the employee will work during the shift.']

  Note: 'Unified shift scheduling product for semiconductor fab operations combining shift pattern definitions with individual employee shift assignments. Pattern definitions capture shift templates (Day, Swing, Night, 12-hour Continental, 4-on-4-off), start/end times, duration, break schedules, and site applicability. Assignment records capture employee-to-shift-pattern mapping, effective dates, fab site and work area (FEOL, BEOL, Metrology, Packaging), shift supervisor, assignment status, and overtime authorization. Supports 24/7 FAB operations staffing, shift handover management, and labor cost allocation to production cost centers.'
}

Table workforce.talent_acquisition {
  talent_acquisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for talent_acquisition']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab site or design centre where the role is based.']
  nre_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Non‑Recurring Engineering project linked to the hire, if applicable.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the standardized job profile (e.g., Fab Operator, Process Engineer).']
  primary_talent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who owns the requisition.']
  primary_talent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department requesting the hire.']
  budget_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Approved budget for the hiring effort.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requisition was closed (filled, cancelled, or otherwise completed).']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Any regulatory or export‑control constraints attached to the position (e.g., ITAR, EAR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requisition record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|JPY|CNY|GBP|CAD|AUD|CHF|HKD|SGD — promote to reference product]']
  headcount_filled varchar [note: 'Number of positions already filled from this requisition.']
  headcount_requested varchar [note: 'Number of positions requested in this requisition.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role is classified as confidential (e.g., security‑sensitive projects).']
  job_profile_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the job profile.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the requisition.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requisition was made visible to candidates.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the requisition.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the requisition, used in reports and communications.']
  requisition_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the hiring need: new headcount, backfill, or contractor.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Origin of the requisition request.']
  talent_acquisition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of duties, responsibilities, and required qualifications.']
  talent_acquisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the requisition.']
  target_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date for contract or temporary assignments.']
  target_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for the new hire.']
  title varchar [note: 'Title of the position being recruited.']

  Note: 'Unified talent acquisition product for semiconductor workforce covering the full hiring pipeline from approved requisition through candidate tracking to hire. Requisition data captures hiring request details including job profile, hiring manager, target org unit, fab site or design center, requisition type (New Headcount, Backfill, Contractor), priority, and NRE project linkage. Application data captures candidate pipeline including source channel, application status (Applied, Screening, Phone Interview, Technical Interview, Offer, Hired, Rejected, Withdrawn), interview stages, and offer details. Supports talent acquisition tracking for specialized semiconductor roles. Sourced from Workday HCM recruiting module.'
}

Table workforce.employment_event {
  employment_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the employment lifecycle event.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or HR representative who approved the employment event.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Employment lifecycle events must reference the employee they pertain to.']
  employment_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Employment lifecycle events must reference the employee. Required for SOX audit trail.']
  primary_employment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee to whom this event applies.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the event received final approval.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes or remarks captured by HR during processing of the event.']
  compensation_change_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the compensation adjustment associated with the event (positive for increase, negative for decrease).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employment event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the compensation change amount.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the employment change becomes effective.']
  employment_event_employee varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Employment lifecycle events must reference the employee they describe.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the event.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the employment change (e.g., hire, transfer, promotion, demotion, job change, location change, leave of absence, return from leave, termination). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hire|transfer|promotion|demotion|job_change|location_change|leave_of_absence|return_from_leave|termination — promote to reference product]']
  new_compensation_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade or band applicable to the employee after the event.']
  new_location varchar [note: 'Physical fab site or office location assigned to the employee after the event.']
  new_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit, department, or division assigned to the employee after the event.']
  new_position varchar [note: 'Job title or position assigned to the employee after the event.']
  previous_compensation_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade or band applicable to the employee before the event.']
  previous_location varchar [note: 'Physical fab site or office location where the employee was assigned before the event.']
  previous_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit, department, or division where the employee worked before the event.']
  previous_position varchar [note: 'Job title or position held by the employee before the event.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the employment change (e.g., business_need, performance, restructuring).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation of why the employment event was initiated.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the employment event record (e.g., Workday, SAP SuccessFactors).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the employment event record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing all significant employment lifecycle events for semiconductor employees throughout their tenure. Covers hire, transfer, promotion, demotion, job change, location change (fab site transfer), leave of absence, return from leave, and termination events. Captures event type, effective date, previous and new values for changed attributes (position, org unit, compensation grade, location), reason code, and approver. Provides the complete employment history audit trail required for SOX compliance and workforce analytics.'
}

Table workforce.compensation {
  compensation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the compensation record.']
  compensation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee receiving the compensation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Compensation records must reference the employee. Core payroll/HR FK.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compensation plan template applied.']
  to_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Compensation records must reference the employee. Required for labor cost allocation.']
  base_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base salary or hourly rate amount before bonuses or variable components.']
  bonus_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual bonus amount paid for the period.']
  bonus_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target bonus as a percentage of base compensation.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the latest change to the compensation record.']
  compensation_code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the compensation record.']
  compensation_employee varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Compensation record must reference the employee it applies to.']
  compensation_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the compensation plan or record.']
  compensation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compensation record.']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'Category of compensation applicable to the employee.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center to which labor cost is allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compensation record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Department to which the employee belongs.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the compensation ends or is superseded; null if open-ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the compensation becomes effective.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment relationship.']
  equity_grant_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of equity granted.']
  equity_grant_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is eligible for equity grants.']
  health_insurance_eligibility boolean [note: 'Eligibility for health insurance benefits.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this record reflects the employee\'s current compensation.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Employee\'s job title associated with this compensation.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the fab or site where the employee works.']
  nre_project_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the NRE project associated with this compensation, if applicable.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is eligible for overtime pay.']
  overtime_rate_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base rate for overtime hours.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which base compensation is paid.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Internal pay grade or band assigned to the employee.']
  payroll_group varchar [note: 'Payroll group classification for processing.']
  retirement_plan_eligibility boolean [note: 'Eligibility for company retirement plan.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compensation review.']
  shift_differential_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional pay amount for non-standard shifts.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift for which differential applies.']
  tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates if compensation is tax-exempt for the employee.']
  union_member boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is a union member.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compensation record.']
  variable_compensation_actual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual amount paid for variable compensation.']
  variable_compensation_target decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target amount for variable compensation components.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the compensation record.']

  Note: 'Employee compensation master record capturing the current and historical compensation details for semiconductor workforce. Tracks base salary, pay grade, pay frequency, currency, effective date, compensation plan type (salary, hourly, variable), bonus target percentage, equity grant eligibility, and shift differential pay for fab operators on non-standard shifts. Supports labor cost allocation to FAB cost centers, NRE project costing, and compensation benchmarking against semiconductor industry surveys.'
}

Table workforce.time_entry {
  time_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each time entry record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Every time entry must reference the employee who worked the hours. Fundamental for labor cost allocation.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab or site where the work was performed.']
  primary_time_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who logged the time.']
  tertiary_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Time entries must reference the employee who worked the hours. Critical for labor cost allocation.']
  time_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Time entries must reference the employee who worked the hours. Critical for labor cost allocation to fab cost centers.']
  absence_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours attributed to the recorded absence.']
  absence_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the absence recorded for the employee.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the time entry in the approval process.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the entry received approval.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current financial posting state of the labor cost.']
  clock_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment the employee started the shift or activity.']
  clock_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment the employee ended the shift or activity.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes or remarks about the time entry.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the entry meets applicable labor law and safety compliance.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which labor cost is allocated.']
  entry_source varchar [note: 'Method by which the time entry was captured.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'True if labor cost should be charged to a customer project.']
  labor_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of labor for the entry, used for cost posting.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Classification of labor type for cost allocation and reporting.']
  labor_grade varchar [note: 'Pay band or grade associated with the employee for compensation rules.']
  labor_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard hourly cost rate for the employee\'s labor category.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours worked, subject to overtime multiplier.']
  overtime_multiplier decimal(3,2) [note: 'Factor (e.g., 1.5) used to calculate overtime pay.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering or NRE project to which the labor is charged.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the time entry record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the time entry.']
  regular_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of standard (non‑overtime) hours worked.']
  scheduled_shift_end timestamp [note: 'Planned end time for the employee\'s shift.']
  scheduled_shift_start timestamp [note: 'Planned start time for the employee\'s shift.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shift schedule (e.g., A‑shift, B‑shift).']
  shift_differential_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked during a shift that carries a differential premium.']
  time_entry_type varchar [note: 'Denotes whether the record represents regular work, an absence, or an adjustment.']
  union_member boolean [note: 'True if the employee is a member of a labor union, affecting overtime rules.']
  work_date date [note: 'Calendar date for which the time entry applies.']
  work_order_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing work order or production order associated with the labor.']

  Note: 'Transactional time, attendance, and absence management record capturing all hours worked and not worked by semiconductor employees and contractors. Tracks work date, clock-in/clock-out timestamps, regular hours, overtime hours, shift differential hours, work order or project code (for NRE project labor tracking), cost center, and approval status. Also captures planned and unplanned absences (Vacation, Sick Leave, FMLA, Parental Leave, Military Leave) with absence type, duration, and impact on shift coverage. Critical for FAB labor cost allocation to wafer lot WIP, NRE project billing, 24/7 shift coverage planning, and compliance with labor regulations. Integrates with SAP CO for cost center labor posting.'
}

Table workforce.training {
  training_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for training']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the training course from the catalog.']
  primary_training_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who is enrolled in the training.']
  training_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  training_trainer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trainer or instructor delivering the course.']
  assessment_max_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum possible score for the assessment.']
  assessment_pass_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to pass the assessment.']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved on the training assessment, expressed as a percentage.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of assessment used to evaluate the employee\'s learning.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date the employee completed the training (if completed).']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the employee must complete the training to satisfy regulatory or internal requirements.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance regime that mandates the training.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which the training is delivered to the employee.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the employee was enrolled in the training.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the enrollment, used for tracking and reporting.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the training certification expires and must be renewed.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the enrollment record.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the training was delivered.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training is required for the employee\'s role or compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the enrollment.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Result of the assessment indicating whether the employee passed, failed, or has not yet completed the assessment.']
  recurrence_interval varchar [note: 'How often the training must be repeated to stay current (e.g., Annual).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the enrollment data (e.g., Workday, LMS).']
  training_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the training (e.g., Technical, Safety, Leadership).']
  training_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the enrollment record.']

  Note: 'Unified training management product for semiconductor workforce combining the training course catalog with employee enrollment and completion records. Course catalog captures course definitions, training categories (Technical/Process, EDA Tools, Safety/EHS, Quality/SPC, Export Control/ITAR, Leadership, Compliance), delivery modes, passing criteria, mandatory flags, regulatory linkages, and recurrence intervals. Enrollment records capture employee enrollment date, completion date, assessment score, pass/fail status, delivery location, and compliance deadline. Critical for ITAR/EAR export control compliance, ISO 9001/IATF 16949 quality system requirements, and fab operator process qualification sign-off.'
}

Table workforce.fab_operator_qualification {
  fab_operator_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for each fab operator qualification record.']
  certification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital or physical certification document attached to the record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Fab operator qualifications must reference the operator/employee. Gates MES work order assignment.']
  fab_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  fab_tool_id varchar [note: 'Restricted identifier of the fab tool or equipment for which the operator is qualified.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab site or cleanroom where the qualification applies.']
  primary_fab_employee_id varchar [note: 'Restricted identifier of the fab operator or technician who holds the qualification.']
  tertiary_fab_operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  certification_document_name varchar [note: 'File name or title of the attached certification document.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the qualification (e.g., ISO 9001, ITAR).']
  equipment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the equipment (e.g., "EUV Scanner 1A").']
  fab_qualified_operator varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Fab operator qualifications must reference the employee/operator. Gates MES work order assignment.']
  last_requalification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent re‑qualification, if any.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, observations, or special instructions related to the qualification.']
  process_step_code varchar [note: 'Standard code for the semiconductor process step (e.g., "EUV_EXPOSE", "CMP_POLISH").']
  process_step_description varchar [note: 'Verbose description of the process step associated with the qualification.']
  qualification_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the qualification record for reference in MES and PLM systems.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date the operator successfully completed the qualification.']
  qualification_level varchar [note: 'Level indicating depth of competence: Trainee, Qualified, or Senior Qualified.']
  qualification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the qualification, e.g., "EUV Lithography Exposure – Level 2".']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the qualification.']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Category of qualification – e.g., Process, Equipment, Safety, or Quality.']
  qualification_validity_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the qualification remains valid from the qualification date.']
  quinary_fab_qual_employee varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Fab operator qualification must reference the qualified operator. Gates MES work order assignment.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the qualification record.']
  related_competency varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee_skill.employee_skill_id — Fab operator qualifications relate to the broader competency/skill framework for the operator.']
  requalification_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the operator must be re‑qualified to retain authorization.']
  shift_qualification varchar [note: 'Indicates which work shifts the operator is qualified for.']
  skill_category varchar [note: 'Broad skill area associated with the qualification (e.g., Lithography, CMP, Metrology).']
  suspension_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the qualification is currently suspended.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why the qualification was suspended.']
  training_hours varchar [note: 'Total number of training hours completed for this qualification.']

  Note: 'Semiconductor-specific operational qualification record certifying that a fab operator or technician is authorized to perform specific process steps or operate specific equipment in the cleanroom. Captures operator employee reference, qualified process step (e.g., EUV Lithography Exposure, CMP Polish, CVD Deposition, Ion Implantation), qualified equipment tool ID, qualification date, qualified-by supervisor, qualification level (Trainee, Qualified, Senior Qualified), requalification due date, and suspension flag. Directly gates MES work order assignment in Camstar/SmartFactory.'
}

Table workforce.site_assignment {
  site_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for site_assignment']
  primary_site_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee (person) assigned to the site.']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shift schedule applied to this assignment.']
  site_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Site assignment must reference the employee assigned to the site.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the fab, design centre, or office location.']
  access_card_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical access card issued for site entry.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Portion of the employee\'s time allocated to this site (0‑100%).']
  assignment_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to reference the assignment in HR and operational systems.']
  assignment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the assignment (e.g., "Fab A – Process Engineer").']
  assignment_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for the assignment (e.g., project support, capacity fill, skill development).']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assignment (permanent, temporary rotation, or project‑based secondment).']
  cleanroom_access_level varchar [note: 'Authorized cleanroom access tier for the employee at this site.']
  compliance_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required compliance training has been finished for this site.']
  compliance_training_date date [note: 'Date on which compliance training was completed.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost‑center to which labor costs for this assignment are charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the assignment ends or is scheduled to end; null for open‑ended assignments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the assignment becomes active.']
  hours_per_week decimal(4,1) [note: 'Planned weekly work hours for the assignment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks or special conditions.']
  payroll_grade varchar [note: 'Payroll grade or band applicable to the employee for this assignment.']
  primary_site_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site is the employee\'s primary work location (True) or a secondary assignment (False).']
  remote_work_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the employee may perform duties remotely for this assignment.']
  safety_induction_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee has completed site‑specific safety induction.']
  safety_induction_date date [note: 'Date on which the safety induction was completed.']
  site_assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assignment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assignment record.']
  work_shift varchar [note: 'Standard shift designation for the assignment.']

  Note: 'Master record capturing the assignment of semiconductor employees to specific fab sites, design centers, or office locations. Tracks primary work site (fab name, design center, office), secondary site assignments for multi-site roles, effective start and end dates, assignment type (Permanent, Temporary Rotation, Project-Based Secondment), cleanroom access authorization level, and site-specific safety induction completion status. Supports multi-site workforce deployment for engineers supporting multiple FAB nodes or design centers.'
}

Table workforce.contractor_engagement {
  contractor_engagement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the contractor engagement record.']
  contractor_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Contractor engagements are assigned to an organizational unit; linking contractor_engagement to org_unit provides hierarchy and reporting, eliminating the free‑text assigned_org_unit column.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.org_unit.org_unit_id — Contractor engagements must reference the org unit they are assigned to for cost allocation and management.']
  primary_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.org_unit.org_unit_id — Contractors are engaged by specific org units. Required for contingent workforce management and cost allocation.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staffing or recruiting agency providing the contractor.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the contractor\'s background screening.']
  billing_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Hourly or daily billing rate agreed for the contractor.']
  cleanroom_access_authorization varchar [note: 'Authorization status for contractor to access cleanroom environments.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any additional compliance-related observations.']
  contract_effective_from timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the contract became effective.']
  contract_effective_until timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the contract expires or is terminated.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contractor engagement is scheduled to end.']
  contract_manager varchar [note: 'Name of the internal employee responsible for managing the contractor agreement.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the contractor agreement.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contractor engagement becomes effective.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contractor agreement.']
  contractor_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the contractor for correspondence.']
  contractor_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contractor communication.']
  contractor_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the contractor individual or organization.']
  contractor_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the contractor.']
  contractor_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor is an individual consultant or a corporate entity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contractor engagement record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for billing.']
  engagement_type varchar [note: 'Category of contractor engagement model.']
  export_control_status varchar [note: 'Status of ITAR/EAR export control screening for the contractor.']
  fab_site varchar [note: 'Location/fab where the contractor will work.']
  headcount_classification varchar [note: 'Whether the contractor headcount is recorded on or off the company\'s balance sheet.']
  invoice_frequency varchar [note: 'How often invoices are issued for the contractor services.']
  is_ear_contract boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract is subject to EAR regulations.']
  is_it_ar_contract boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract is subject to ITAR regulations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contractor engagement record.']
  nda_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Non-Disclosure Agreement with the contractor.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Terms governing payment schedule and invoicing for the contractor.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order linked to the contractor engagement.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Indicates if the contract includes an automatic renewal clause.']
  risk_assessment_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score representing the risk level of the contractor engagement.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for early termination of the contractor engagement.']
  total_contract_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of the contract over its term.']

  Note: 'Master record managing contingent workforce and contractor engagements for semiconductor operations. Captures contractor name, agency name, contract start and end dates, engagement type (Staff Augmentation, SOW-Based, Managed Service), assigned org unit and fab site, billing rate, purchase order reference, NDA status, background check status, export control screening status (ITAR/EAR), cleanroom access authorization, and headcount classification (on-balance-sheet vs off-balance-sheet). Tracks the significant contractor population in semiconductor fabs and design centers.'
}

Table workforce.cleanroom_access {
  cleanroom_access_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cleanroom access record.']
  cleanroom_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee or contractor granted cleanroom access.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Cleanroom access authorization must reference the employee/contractor granted access.']
  fab_site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab site where the cleanroom is located.']
  to_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Cleanroom access records must reference the employee/contractor granted access.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Access level granted defining permissible activities within the cleanroom.']
  cleanroom_access_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cleanroom access record.']
  cleanroom_employee varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee.employee_id — Cleanroom access authorization must reference the employee/contractor granted access.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the access record was initially created.']
  ehs_clearance_status boolean [note: 'Indicates if Environmental Health and Safety clearance is approved for the employee.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cleanroom access expires.']
  export_control_category varchar [note: 'Export control category applicable to the individual under ITAR/EAR.']
  foreign_national boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is a foreign national subject to export control regulations.']
  grant_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cleanroom access was granted.']
  is_temporary boolean [note: 'Indicates if the granted access is temporary (e.g., for a specific project or time window).']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit for this access record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments related to the cleanroom access.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Code of the project or design requiring the cleanroom access.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Gowning qualification status of the personnel for cleanroom entry.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for revoking the cleanroom access.']
  revocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cleanroom access was revoked, if applicable.']
  safety_induction_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee has completed the required safety induction.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when required cleanroom training was completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the access record.']
  zone varchar [note: 'Designated cleanroom zone for the access grant.']

  Note: 'Transactional record managing cleanroom access authorization for semiconductor fab personnel. Captures employee or contractor reference, fab site, cleanroom zone (FEOL, BEOL, MOL, Metrology, Packaging), access level granted, gowning qualification status, access grant date, expiry date, access revocation date and reason, safety induction completion, and EHS clearance status. Cleanroom access is strictly controlled in semiconductor fabs due to contamination risk, safety requirements, and export control restrictions on foreign nationals.'
}

Table workforce.export_control {
  export_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for export_control']
  primary_export_employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the employee or contractor being screened.']
  authorized_technology_areas varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of technology domains the individual is cleared to access.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the individual under US export control rules.']
  ear_license_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an EAR export license is required for the individual\'s access.']
  ear_license_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required EAR license.']
  employee_phone varchar [note: 'Contact telephone number for the employee or contractor.']
  export_control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the screening record.']
  itar_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current ITAR authorization state for the individual.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Country of citizenship or permanent residence, expressed as ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the screening.']
  periodic_rescreening_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next mandatory export control rescreening must be completed.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the screening record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the screening record.']
  screening_conducted_by varchar [note: 'Name of the compliance officer or system that performed the screening.']
  screening_date date [note: 'Date on which the export control screening was performed.']
  screening_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the export control screening.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the screening data.']
  technology_access_restrictions varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any technology access limitations imposed on the individual.']
  visa_type varchar [note: 'Type of work visa held by the employee, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Export control compliance screening record for semiconductor workforce personnel tracking ITAR and EAR authorization status. Captures employee or contractor nationality, visa type, export control classification (US Person, Foreign National, Deemed Export Risk), ITAR authorization status, EAR license requirement flag, technology access restrictions, screening date, screening outcome, authorized technology areas, and periodic re-screening due date. Critical for semiconductor companies handling controlled technology under ITAR and EAR regulations governing advanced IC design and fab processes.'
}

Table workforce.safety_event {
  safety_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for safety_event']
  affected_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee directly impacted by the incident.']
  fab_site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fab site where the incident took place.']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work shift during which the incident occurred.']
  cleanroom_zone varchar [note: 'Specific cleanroom zone or area within the fab where the incident occurred.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed corrective actions to prevent recurrence.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost associated with the incident (medical, lost productivity, etc.).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident record was first created in the system.']
  exposure_chemical varchar [note: 'Name of the chemical involved in a chemical exposure incident.']
  exposure_level varchar [note: 'Severity level of chemical exposure.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the incident record.']
  incident_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the safety incident occurred.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the safety incident.']
  injury_body_part varchar [note: 'Body part(s) injured, if applicable.']
  injury_type varchar [note: 'Medical classification of the injury (e.g., laceration, burn).']
  investigation_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investigation was concluded.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the incident investigation.']
  lab_name varchar [note: 'Name of the laboratory (if the incident occurred in a lab environment).']
  lost_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of work days lost due to the injury.']
  medical_treatment_provided varchar [note: 'Details of medical treatment administered after the incident.']
  near_miss_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record represents a near‑miss event.']
  osha_recordable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident is recordable under OSHA regulations.']
  regulatory_reporting_status varchar [note: 'Status of required regulatory reporting (e.g., OSHA, ISO 45001).']
  report_date date [note: 'Date when the incident was formally reported.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who reported the incident.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause analysis result identifying why the incident occurred.']
  safety_event_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of what happened, including context and observations.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity of the incident based on impact to employee health and operations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that captured the incident (e.g., Workday HCM, MES).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the incident record.']

  Note: 'Occupational health and safety incident record for semiconductor workforce capturing workplace injuries, near-misses, chemical exposures, and EHS events in fab and design center environments. Tracks incident date and time, location (fab site, cleanroom zone, lab), incident type (Chemical Exposure, Ergonomic Injury, Electrical Hazard, Slip/Fall, Near Miss), affected employee, severity level (First Aid, Recordable, Lost Time, Fatality), root cause, corrective actions, OSHA recordable flag, and regulatory reporting status. Supports ISO 45001 and OSHA compliance.'
}

Table workforce.compensation_plan {
  compensation_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for compensation_plan']
  superseded_compensation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on compensation_plan (superseded_compensation_plan_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the plan within the governance workflow.']
  base_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount associated with the plan (e.g., base salary).']
  bonus_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Factor applied to the base amount to calculate bonus payouts.']
  compensation_frequency varchar [note: 'How often compensation under the plan is paid or awarded.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compensation plan record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department to which the plan applies.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended plans.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Textual description of the rules that determine employee eligibility for the plan.']
  eligibility_grade varchar [note: 'Job grade or level required for an employee to qualify for the plan.']
  is_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is currently eligible for assignment to employees.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad job family classification (e.g., engineering, manufacturing, R&D) for plan applicability.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the plan was last reviewed for compliance or market alignment.']
  location varchar [note: 'Geographic site or fab location code where the plan is valid.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the compensation plan.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of plans (e.g., executive, staff, hourly).']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or code used to reference the plan.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the compensation plan.']
  plan_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational scope of the plan (global, regional, site‑specific).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the plan indicating the compensation mechanism.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the plan to track revisions.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  target_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target monetary amount for variable components such as bonuses or incentives.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the compensation plan record.']
  vesting_schedule varchar [note: 'Description of equity or bonus vesting timeline and conditions.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for compensation_plan. Referenced by plan_id.'
}

Table workforce.skill {
  skill_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for skill']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for maintaining the skill definition.']
  parent_skill_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on skill (parent_skill_id)']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used to reference the skill across systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the skill record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the skill, its purpose and typical tasks.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the skill becomes valid for assignment.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the skill is retired or no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  is_core_skill boolean [note: 'True if the skill is considered core to semiconductor operations.']
